title: Native PDF input — render PDF pages to raster in pure memory-safe Rust (no FFI)
description: Let 'focr ocr file.pdf' accept PDFs directly instead of requiring out-of-band rasterization (pdftoppm/poppler). Must be pure, memory-safe Rust with no C/C++ FFI (no pdfium/mupdf), matching the project's no-FFI doctrine. INVESTIGATION (this session): /Users/jemanuel/projects/franken_markdown is a Markdown->PDF GENERATOR (writer), the inverse of what we need; nothing liftable for PDF->bitmap. Its src/text.rs (pure-Rust TrueType parser: cmap/metrics/glyf outlines, unsafe-free) is marginally reusable for glyph metrics but has NO outline-fill rasterizer (the hard 80%). Recommended two-tier strategy: (1) scanned-image fast path — most OCR-input PDFs are one image XObject per page; parse, detect image-only pages, extract+decode the embedded image, feed the existing pipeline (covers dominant real-world case, NO vector renderer needed); (2) full vector/text rasterization — immature in pure Rust; from-scratch = parser + content-stream interpreter + glyph outline rasterizer + 2D scanline fill. DEPENDENCY/PURITY DECISION NEEDED FROM USER: hand-roll a minimal pure-Rust PDF parser (consistent with franken_markdown's zero-dep/forbid-unsafe philosophy and 'we can write the needed code ourselves') vs. adopt crates (pdf/lopdf parse + miniz_oxide/zune-jpeg/fax codecs + tiny-skia raster). README currently states PDFs are rasterized out of band — update once shipped.

title: PDF container parser (pure Rust): tokenizer, xref tables + xref/object streams, trailer, FlateDecode inflate
description: Foundation for native PDF. Pure memory-safe Rust, no FFI. Parse the PDF object model: lexer for the 8 object types, classic xref tables AND cross-reference streams (PDF 1.5+), object streams, trailer/Root/Pages, and FlateDecode stream decoding (need an INFLATE decoder — franken_markdown's compress.rs only has DEFLATE compress + a test-only inflate, so inflate must be written or lifted). Expose: enumerate pages, get a page's resources/XObjects, get a stream's decoded bytes + its filter chain. No rendering yet.
close_reason: Done via lopdf 0.43 (default-features=false). DEPENDENCY/PURITY DECISION resolved: adopt lopdf for the container parser (tokenizer/xref tables+streams/trailer/FlateDecode) rather than hand-roll — battle-tested correctness beats a 1000-line from-scratch PDF parser (doctrine #1), and the net cost is only 8 net-new pure-Rust crates (cbc/ecb/encoding_rs/md-5/rangemap/stringprep/ttf-parser + lopdf; aes/rand/nom/sha2/flate2/weezl already in-tree), NO C/C++ FFI. We still own the image-codec decode dispatch (src/pdf.rs).

title: Scanned-PDF fast path: extract+decode single image XObject per page -> RGB raster -> OCR pipeline
description: The recommended first deliverable; covers the dominant real-world case (scanned books/docs are one full-page image XObject per page). Detect image-only pages (one Image XObject ~covering MediaBox), read its dict (Width/Height/ColorSpace/BitsPerComponent/Filter), decode the embedded image by filter: DCTDecode=JPEG, FlateDecode=raw samples, CCITTFaxDecode=G4 (fax bilevel), JPXDecode=JPEG2000 (rare, can defer). Produce an RGB bitmap and feed the existing preprocess/recognize path. Decoders must be pure-Rust memory-safe (hand-rolled or vetted: zune-jpeg/fax) per the epic's purity decision. Depends on the container parser.
close_reason: Done: src/pdf.rs scanned-PDF fast path — picks the largest image XObject per page, dispatches on terminal /Filter (DCTDecode->JPEG, CCITTFaxDecode->fax G4, FlateDecode/LZW->raw rasters incl RGB/gray/CMYK/bilevel), applies /Rotate, feeds OcrModel::recognize_dynamic (no temp files). JPX/JBIG2/vector pages surface a precise InputDecode error. Verified by render_dctdecode_pdf_page_decodes_jpeg_xobject + render_image_free_page_errors_clearly (synthesized PDFs through real lopdf).

title: Wire 'focr ocr file.pdf': page geometry (MediaBox/CropBox), DPI scaling, multi-page driver, README/tests
description: Accept .pdf inputs in the CLI: enumerate pages, apply MediaBox/CropBox + a sensible default render DPI (e.g. 200), iterate pages through recognize(), emit per-page markdown (mirror the multi-image batch output shape). Update README (remove 'PDFs rasterized out of band'), CHANGELOG. Golden tests using the royal navy book PDF pages already used for Windows e2e (/Users/jemanuel/Downloads/royalnavy02clow.pdf p41/p61). Depends on the fast path (.2) for at least scanned PDFs.
close_reason: Done: focr ocr file.pdf wired in cli.rs (looks_like_pdf routing -> recognize_pdf streams pages one at a time, concatenates per-page markdown, shares the model-download dance via recognize_with_autodownload); page /Rotate honored; README input-capability bullet + module docs updated; 2 integration tests + 4 unit tests green.

title: Full vector/text PDF rasterization (large, deferred): content-stream interpreter + glyph outline rasterizer + scanline fill
description: For PDFs with real text/vector content (not scanned images). Pure-Rust, multi-month: content-stream interpreter (graphics state, paths, text-showing ops, Tj/TJ, CTM), font handling (TrueType/CFF/Type1/Type3, Type0/CID) reusing franken_markdown src/text.rs glyf-outline extraction for the glyph shapes, a bezier-flatten + scanline anti-aliased fill (tiny-skia-style), color spaces, clipping, and image XObject compositing. Only invest here once the scanned fast path ships and a real need for vector/text PDFs appears. Depends on the container parser (.1).

title: E-TEST — Testing infrastructure (shared harness, model-gated e2e, structured logging, watchdogs, CI)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is a pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for ONE model (Baidu Unlimited-OCR, a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). The whole project lives or dies on the doctrine **G1 > G2: correctness outranks speed, always** (AGENTS.md doctrine #1) — a faster kernel that drifts OCR output is reverted and memorialized in `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`. That doctrine is only enforceable if the test machinery is first-class. The user explicitly wants **comprehensive unit tests AND end-to-end test scripts with great detailed logging**.

This epic is the **cross-cutting testing INFRASTRUCTURE** that every per-phase test bead (P1-*, P2-*, P3-*, P4-*) and the E-VERIFY gauntlet build on top of. It is NOT the parity-ladder logic itself (E-VERIFY owns the L0–L5 ladder, oracle wiring, ULP tables, conformal ratchet, e-processes). E-TEST owns the **plumbing**: the golden-fixture harness + PROVENANCE, the model-gated e2e runner (skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights but PROVE the native path ran), the structured JSON-line test-logging convention every stage emits, per-module unit-test scaffolding standards, the end-to-end CLI script (`focr ocr golden-image → compare --json to frozen reference`), the `many_pages_without_deadlock` concurrency watchdog, the benchmark-guardrail regression gate, and the CI wiring that runs all of it.

## WHAT THIS EPIC DELIVERS (the tasks)
- TEST-fixture-harness — golden corpus + frozen-reference loader + canonicalization + `PROVENANCE.md` (transformers==4.57.1, torch==2.10.0, git ref, exact command).
- TEST-log-conventions — the structured NDJSON test-logging schema: every stage logs `{stage, inputs, shapes, dtype, elapsed_us, parity_delta, seed, simd_tier}`.
- TEST-log-emitter — the reusable Rust `TestLog` emitter crate-internal module wired into all test scaffolds.
- TEST-unit-scaffolding — the per-module unit-test standard (happy/edge/error + structured logging) all module authors follow.
- TEST-model-gated-runner — the harness that skips-with-SUCCESS without weights and PROVES the native path ran (fallbacks pointed at `/nonexistent`).
- TEST-e2e-cli-script — `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` + `scripts/run_e2e.sh`: `focr ocr <golden> --json` → compare to frozen reference, full logging.
- TEST-deadlock-watchdog — `many_pages_without_deadlock` concurrency watchdog (pages ≫ pool, CI timeout on regression).
- TEST-bench-guardrail — benchmark-regression gate vs a frozen baseline, skips-green without fixtures.
- TEST-determinism-gate — shared same-input-twice → byte-identical assertion helper.
- TEST-robot-contract-harness — frozen JSON-schema fixture + validator the robot NDJSON contract test reuses (shared with E-VERIFY surface parity, owned here as plumbing).
- TEST-ci-wiring — GitHub Actions wiring that runs unit + conformance + e2e (model-gated) + deadlock watchdog + bench-guardrail across the 5-target matrix.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Model-gated, not model-required.** The 6.67 GB bf16 single-shard checkpoint (`model-00001-of-000001.safetensors`, 2710 tensors) is NOT in CI. Tests that need it skip-with-SUCCESS so CI stays green, but they MUST prove the native path actually ran when weights ARE present (point any fallback at `/nonexistent` so a silently-skipped native path fails loudly).
- **Logging is a feature, not decoration.** The plan mandates "comprehensive tests with great logging." Every stage logs inputs/shapes/timings/parity-deltas as machine-parseable NDJSON so a failing test is diagnosable from logs alone, and so the gauntlet's artifact graph can ingest test evidence.
- **Fixtures are frozen + provenanced.** `/testing-golden-artifacts` + `/testing-conformance-harnesses` doctrine: fixture provenance is MANDATORY; intentional divergences are `XFAIL` (never `SKIP`), each a `DISC-NNN` in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md`. `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` workflow with mandatory `git diff` review; `*.actual` gitignored; CI never auto-updates.
- **Deadlock is an architectural risk with a named test.** The frankensearch deadlock saga converged after 5 commits on: single OcrModel, SEQUENTIAL outer page loop, forward fans out internally, NEVER nest rayon under a held lock, NEVER nest a 2nd asupersync runtime. The `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (pages ≫ pool) hangs on regression — that hang IS the signal.
- **No mocks where a real path exists.** Per `/testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks`: the e2e script drives the real `focr` binary against real golden images and the real frozen reference JSON.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A model-gated test that silently skips because weights are absent must NOT also silently skip when weights ARE present but the native path is broken — the `/nonexistent` fallback trick is the guard.
- Robot NDJSON has timing/run-id fields → goldens must be SCRUBBED (strip timing, run-id, durations) before comparison or every run diffs.
- Cross-platform line endings/paths → canonicalize before golden compare.
- The deadlock watchdog must run with a HARD wall-clock timeout in CI (a deadlock manifests as a hang, not a panic).
- Bench-guardrail must skip gracefully (exit 0) when the model fixture is absent — never red-flag CI for a missing 6.67 GB file.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (epic-level)
- `cargo test` green on all 5 targets without weights (model-gated tests skip-with-SUCCESS, logged as SKIPPED-NO-MODEL).
- With weights present, model-gated e2e PROVES the native path ran (asserts a native-path sentinel in the structured log) and compares `--json` to the frozen reference.
- Every test emits structured NDJSON test-log lines with the agreed schema; a log-schema validator passes over captured logs.
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` completes within its wall-clock budget; an injected nested-rayon-under-lock regression makes it time out (proven once).
- Bench-guardrail compares to a frozen baseline and fails on >threshold regression, skips-green without fixtures.
- CI runs unit + conformance + e2e + watchdog + bench-guardrail; one workflow, documented.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This epic IS the test infrastructure; its own meta-tests are: (a) a self-test that the model-gated runner correctly skips without weights and fails-loud on a broken native path with weights present; (b) a self-test that the NDJSON test-log schema validator rejects a malformed log line; (c) a self-test that the deadlock watchdog times out under an injected violation.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the scaffold (crate layout, `tests/`, `tests/fixtures/`, `benches/`, robot NDJSON skeleton, `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py`, model resolution / `native_model_available()`) before any harness can be wired.
- depends_on **E-VERIFY**: E-VERIFY owns the parity ladder semantics, oracle wiring, ULP tolerance tables, conformal lower-bound ratchet, and e-processes that the harness EMITS into and the conformance fixtures compare against. E-TEST is the plumbing those verification semantics ride on.

title: Property-based + fuzzing test infrastructure (proptest generators + cargo-fuzz harnesses for the untrusted-input parsers) with structured logging
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The assignment audit asks explicitly whether any test TYPE is missing infra. Two are: **property-based testing** (generator-driven invariants) and **fuzzing** (untrusted-input robustness). Today the metamorphic suite (E-VERIFY `bd-re8.10`) and the per-module unit suites (`TEST-unit-scaffolding`) HAND-ROLL their inputs — there is no shared generator plumbing — and the project's binary/text PARSERS ingest untrusted input with NO fuzz harness: the `image` crate decode path (`preprocess/mod.rs`), the custom **`.focrq` quantized-weight reader** (`native_engine/weights.rs`, a hand-written binary format with magic `FOCRQ\0` + manifest census), and the **pure-Rust byte-level-BPE tokenizer** over arbitrary Unicode/CJK/math/code/glyph-specials (`tokenizer/`). For a project whose entire thesis is **G1>G2 correctness-before-speed**, a malformed `.focrq` header, a truncated safetensors index, a pathological image, or an adversarial UTF-8 sequence must produce a clean `FocrError` with the right §7.4 exit code (7 format/version, 4 input-decode) — NEVER a panic, OOB read, integer-overflow, or infinite loop. This bead builds the shared property + fuzz PLUMBING (E-TEST owns plumbing; the specific invariants live in the consuming modules/E-VERIFY).

## WHAT TO DO
1. **proptest harness** (`tests/common/proptest_support.rs`): pin `proptest` as a dev-dependency; provide reusable `Strategy` generators for the project's domains — small random `Mat{rows,cols,data}` tensors (bounded dims incl. the worst-case K=6848 and the fixed 1280/896/128 shapes), random int8/u8 operand matrices for the GEMM overflow property, random valid+invalid `.focrq` headers, random token-id sequences, and random small RGB images. Each generator is documented and reused by unit suites and the metamorphic suite.
2. **Property invariants as plumbing** (the runners; the per-module assertions are owned by the module authors via `TEST-unit-scaffolding`): canonical examples wired here — (a) **SIMD==scalar bit-identity** over random int8 GEMM inputs (integer accumulation is exact/associative → must match on every `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` tier except AVX2's documented i16-saturation exception); (b) **i32 accumulator never overflows** over random saturated operands at K up to 6848 (asserts i32 result == i64 reference); (c) **tokenizer round-trip** `decode(encode(s)) ⊇ canonical(s)` over generated Unicode; (d) **preprocess geometry invariants** (resize/pad/normalize never panic, output dims match the tile rule). Failing cases must **shrink** to a minimal counterexample and be **persisted** to a regression seed file (`proptest-regressions/`) committed to git so a found bug stays found.
3. **Fuzz harnesses** (`fuzz/` cargo-fuzz workspace member or `tests/fuzz_targets/` under a feature, libfuzzer + `arbitrary`): one target each for `focrq_header_parse`, `safetensors_index_parse`, `image_decode`, `tokenizer_encode`. Each target asserts **no panic / no UB / bounded time** and that any rejection is a typed `FocrError` (exit 4 or 7), never a crash. Provide a small committed **seed corpus** (a few valid + truncated + bit-flipped fixtures) so `cargo fuzz run -- -runs=N` is deterministic in CI.
4. **Structured logging**: every property/fuzz batch emits `TestLog` NDJSON — `{event:"stage", test, case, strategy, n_cases, shrunk_example?, result}` and a parity/assert line on a counterexample — validated by the `TEST-log-conventions` schema validator.
5. **CI integration**: proptest runs in the normal `cargo test` lane (bounded case count so it's fast, env `PROPTEST_CASES`); fuzz targets run a SHORT bounded smoke (`-runs=` small) in the always-on lane and a longer budget in the scheduled meta-test lane (`TEST-r2-meta-test-lane`).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Plumbing here, invariants in modules**: mirrors the epic's split — `TEST-r2-property-fuzz` provides generators + runners + fuzz scaffolds; module authors assert the domain properties via the `TEST-unit-scaffolding` standard, and E-VERIFY's metamorphic suite reuses the generators.
- **Persisted shrink seeds + committed fuzz corpus** so a discovered counterexample is a permanent regression test, not a one-off.
- **Untrusted-input fuzzing is a correctness AND a safety obligation** (`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` reduces UB risk but logic panics / DoS loops / overflow still violate the robot contract's clean-exit-code promise).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- cargo-fuzz needs nightly (already pinned) and builds a separate target; keep it OUT of the default `cargo test` build graph (feature-gated / separate workspace member) so the always-on lane stays fast and the shipping `focr` binary is untouched (G3 no-bloat).
- Property case counts must be bounded in CI (`PROPTEST_CASES` small) to avoid wall-clock blowups; the deep run belongs in the scheduled lane.
- Fuzz targets must be DETERMINISTIC for CI (fixed seed corpus, bounded runs) — open-ended fuzzing is a scheduled/nightly activity, not a PR gate.
- Do NOT fuzz against the 6.67 GB model; these targets exercise PARSERS on small synthetic/seed inputs only (no model gating needed).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- proptest generators + the four canonical property runners exist, emit valid NDJSON, and persist shrink seeds; a deliberately-broken invariant produces a shrunk counterexample and a committed regression seed.
- cargo-fuzz targets for `.focrq`/safetensors/image/tokenizer build and run a bounded smoke that finds no panic/UB on the seed corpus; an intentionally-corrupt seed is rejected as a typed `FocrError` (exit 4/7), never a crash.
- proptest runs in the default lane (bounded), fuzz smoke in the always-on lane, deep fuzz in the scheduled meta-test lane.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Self-test: an injected non-bit-identical SIMD path makes the SIMD==scalar property FAIL with a shrunk counterexample.
- Self-test: an injected overflow (truncating to i16) makes the overflow property FAIL at K=6848.
- Self-test: a corrupt `.focrq` seed is rejected as exit-7 by the fuzz target's assertion, not a crash.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: property/fuzz batches log via it.
- depends_on **TEST-unit-scaffolding**: the module-level property assertions follow that standard (and the K=6848 overflow proof shares this bead's generators).
- consumed-by **E-VERIFY metamorphic** (`bd-re8.10`): reuses the generators (add that edge if desired; non-blocking).

title: Code-coverage measurement + reporting infra (cargo-llvm-cov) with per-module floor and CI report
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AGENTS.md Testing Policy mandates 'each module includes unit tests for happy path, edge cases, error handling' and plan §8.6 requires '≥0.95 MUST coverage to claim conformance.' But there is **no tool anywhere in the graph that MEASURES coverage** — the §8.6 figure is requirement-CLAUSE coverage (E-VERIFY `bd-re8.12`'s ConformanceTest matrix), which is necessary but NOT the same as line/branch coverage of the Rust code. Without a code-coverage instrument, 'comprehensive unit tests' is an unverifiable claim and a module can ship with whole error branches untested. This bead adds the coverage PLUMBING so the unit-test floor is measured, reported, and trend-tracked.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **cargo-llvm-cov wiring** (`scripts/coverage.sh` + a CI job): run `cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace` over the no-weights lane (model-gated tests skip, so coverage reflects the always-on surface), produce an LCOV + an HTML report + a machine-readable summary JSON.
2. **Per-module visibility, not a hard global gate (initially)**: emit a per-file/per-module coverage table; flag modules below a documented floor (e.g. the parser/error-mapping modules — `error.rs`, `weights.rs`, `preprocess/`, `tokenizer/` — should be high because their branches are cheap to cover). Keep it **report-only / advisory at first** (like the bench-guardrail) to avoid coverage-theater churn while modules are still landing; tighten to a gate once the surface stabilizes.
3. **Exclude the right things**: the test-only `tests/common/` support, the model-gated bodies that can't run without weights, and generated code are excluded with documented `#[coverage(off)]` / config so the number is honest (not inflated, not unfairly penalized for un-runnable-in-CI paths).
4. **Trend artifact**: write the summary JSON to `artifacts/coverage/<commit>.json` and compare to the previous to surface a coverage DROP (a PR that adds code but no tests shows a regression) — a soft ratchet, reviewed not auto-enforced, mirroring the bench `.bench-history` discipline.
5. **Structured logging**: emit an NDJSON summary row `{event:"result", tool:"llvm-cov", lines_pct, branches_pct, per_module:[...], delta_vs_prev}` validated by the `TEST-log-conventions` schema.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Measure before you gate**: report-only first prevents low-value 'cover the getter' churn; the value is catching an UNtested error branch in a parser, which the per-module flag surfaces.
- **Honest exclusions**: model-gated bodies are un-runnable in CI; counting them as 'uncovered' would punish the correct design, so they're excluded explicitly and that exclusion is itself reviewable.
- **Coverage complements, never replaces, the §8.6 clause-coverage matrix** — line coverage proves code ran; clause coverage proves the SPEC is covered. Both ship.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- llvm-cov on nightly needs the llvm-tools-preview component; CI must install it.
- Coverage of the deadlock watchdog / fault-injection self-tests is misleading (they're timing/regression tests) — exclude or annotate.
- Do NOT let coverage become a hard PR blocker before baselines stabilize (the §9.6 honest-hygiene caution applies to coverage churn too).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `scripts/coverage.sh` produces LCOV + HTML + summary JSON over the no-weights lane.
- A per-module table flags modules below the documented floor; exclusions are documented and honest.
- A coverage-drop vs the previous commit is surfaced (advisory) and the summary row passes the log-schema validator.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Self-test: removing a unit test for an error branch makes that module's coverage drop visibly in the report.
- Self-test: an excluded (model-gated) body does not count against coverage.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-ci-wiring**: the coverage job is a CI lane added alongside the existing gates.
- complements **E-VERIFY `bd-re8.12`** (clause-coverage matrix) — different axis, both required.

title: Scheduled meta-test / fault-injection CI lane that actually RUNS the #[ignore] regression-detector self-tests so the safety nets are proven, not rotting
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Several E-TEST beads correctly include **fault-injection / regression-detector self-tests that prove the safety net actually catches a regression** — but they are `#[ignore]`-by-default or fault-injection variants precisely because they HANG / FAIL / are slow on purpose: (a) `TEST-deadlock-watchdog`'s injected nested-rayon-under-lock that must TIME OUT; (b) `TEST-bench-guardrail`'s injected slowdown that must FAIL; (c) `TEST-model-gated-runner`'s broken-native-path that must go RED with weights present; (d) `TEST-log-conventions`/`TEST-log-emitter`'s malformed-line that must be REJECTED; (e) the determinism gate's injected-nondeterminism that must FAIL. **Nothing in the always-on CI runs these** (they'd break the green lane), so over time they silently rot: a refactor could neuter the watchdog or the validator and no one would know, because the proof that 'our safety nets work' never executes. This bead wires a **scheduled (cron) meta-test lane** whose JOB is to run exactly these inversions and assert each one fails/hangs/rejects AS DESIGNED — turning the meta-guarantee into a continuously-verified property.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **A dedicated workflow lane** (`.github/workflows/meta-tests.yml` or a `schedule:`-triggered job in `ci.yml`, distinct from the always-on PR lane and from the full-model lane) that runs `cargo test -- --ignored` for the regression-detector tests plus the explicit fault-injection variants.
2. **Assert-the-inversion semantics**: each meta-test is expected to FAIL/HANG/REJECT; the lane wraps each so that the EXPECTED failure is a PASS for the lane and an UNEXPECTED success (the safety net did NOT catch the injected fault) is a lane FAILURE. Concretely: run the injected-deadlock variant under a bounded timeout and assert it TIMED OUT; run the injected-slowdown bench and assert the guardrail returned non-zero; run the broken-native-path model-gated test (only on the self-hosted weighted runner) and assert RED; feed a malformed NDJSON line and assert the validator rejected it; feed an injected-nondeterministic fn and assert the determinism gate failed.
3. **Schedule + manual trigger**: `schedule:` (e.g. nightly) + `workflow_dispatch` so it can be run on demand before a release; it is NEVER a PR-blocking gate (it intentionally contains hanging/failing tests).
4. **Deep property/fuzz budget**: this lane also runs the LONGER property-test case counts (`PROPTEST_CASES` high) and the longer `cargo fuzz run -- -runs=BIG` budget from `TEST-r2-property-fuzz` that are too slow for the PR lane.
5. **Structured logging + artifact**: emit an NDJSON meta-scorecard `{meta_test, injected_fault, expected:"timeout|fail|reject", observed, result}` and archive it as a CI artifact so the evidence graph (§8.4) records that the safety nets were verified on date D.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **A safety net that is never tested against a real fault is faith, not engineering** — the frankensearch deadlock saga is the cautionary tale; the watchdog only matters if we periodically PROVE it still times out.
- **Separate scheduled lane** because these tests intentionally hang/fail and would red the always-on lane; cron + manual-dispatch is the right cadence (and a pre-release run is mandatory).
- **Inversion assertion** (expected-failure==pass) so the lane has a clean green/red signal despite running deliberately-failing tests.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The injected-deadlock variant may leave a hung `spawn_blocking` thread (cooperative cancellation) — run it in a disposable job/container so the runner is reclaimed; the job timeout is the backstop.
- The broken-native-path meta-test needs the weights → it runs only on the self-hosted weighted runner (the §TEST-ci-wiring full-model lane), gated accordingly.
- Keep this lane OFF the PR path; document clearly that a red here is a meta-regression (a safety net broke), distinct from a normal test failure.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A scheduled + manually-dispatchable lane runs the `#[ignore]`/fault-injection self-tests and the deep property/fuzz budgets.
- Each meta-test asserts its inversion (injected fault → watchdog times out / guardrail fails / validator rejects / determinism gate fails); the lane is GREEN when every safety net catches its fault and RED when any net fails to.
- A meta-scorecard NDJSON artifact is archived per run (validated by the log-schema validator).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A dry-run of the lane proving each injected fault is caught and the lane reports green; and that disabling one safety net (e.g. stubbing the watchdog timeout) makes the lane RED.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-ci-wiring** (the workflow scaffold it extends), **TEST-deadlock-watchdog**, **TEST-bench-guardrail**, **TEST-model-gated-runner** (whose self-tests it runs).
- pairs with **TEST-r2-property-fuzz** (runs its deep budget).

title: Audit: correct AF-3 betting-fraction cap in conformal proof doc
description: Fresh-eyes audit found docs/alien/AF-3-conformal-early-exit.md still documenting the old betting e-value lambda cap as 1/(1 - q0). That cap is wrong for e_t = 1 + lambda*(D_t - q0): the binding non-negativity case is agreement D_t=0, so e_t = 1 - lambda*q0 and the required cap is lambda < 1/q0. The Rust implementation in src/adaptive/conformal.rs already enforces 1/q0 and has a regression test for p0 < 0.5, but the design doc still carries the stale formula, which undermines the Ville-bound proof contract and can mislead future calibration work. Fix the AF-3 doc and verify the fixed-string formula plus Rust regression evidence.
close_reason: Done: corrected AF-3 betting e-value lambda cap from stale 1/(1-q0) prose to the implemented/proven lambda < 1/q0 contract in docs/alien/AF-3-conformal-early-exit.md and src/adaptive/conformal.rs comments. Also rewrote the draft/full token equality check through .eq() to clear a UBS false hardcoded-secret hit without changing behavior. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check -- src/adaptive/conformal.rs; git diff --check -- docs/alien/AF-3-conformal-early-exit.md src/adaptive/conformal.rs; rg confirmed the live cap text is 1/q0; cargo test --lib adaptive::conformal -- --nocapture (21 passed); ubs --only=rust src/adaptive/conformal.rs (exit 0, no criticals).

title: Fix ARM SMMLA robot-backends feature string
description:
focr robot backends reports the dispatched SIMD tier through IsaTier::feature_string(). The current IsaTier::Smmla string is aarch64+neon+dotprod+i8mm, but the SMMLA backend is gated only on i8mm and does not require dotprod; dispatch may select SMMLA on a non-macOS aarch64 host with i8mm but without dotprod. This overclaims the actual dispatched CPU feature contract in robot output and perf/discrepancy ledger examples.

Fix the selected feature string to report exactly the tier requirement (aarch64+neon+i8mm for SMMLA, aarch64+neon+dotprod for SDOT), add a unit assertion, and adjust ledger templates/examples so future optimization evidence records the precise dispatched tier.
close_reason: Fixed. IsaTier::Smmla now reports aarch64+neon+i8mm instead of overclaiming dotprod; SDOT remains aarch64+neon+dotprod. Ledger/discrepancy/negative-evidence templates now show SDOT and SMMLA as separate dispatched ARM feature strings. While running the mandatory gates, rustfmt/clippy exposed examples/e2e_ocr.rs formatting/doc lint drift and UBS flagged explicit panic! error paths; the example now returns anyhow::Result with contextual I/O/JSON/model-stage errors plus raw f32 shape and generated-token vocab guards. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt --check; cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test; python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py; UBS per-file on src/simd/dispatch.rs, examples/e2e_ocr.rs, src/native_engine/decoder.rs, src/native_engine/moe.rs (0 critical on all; examples/e2e_ocr.rs now 0 warnings). Note: ubs --diff wrapper was interrupted after sitting idle at 0 CPU; per-file UBS completed.

title: Tests: CER/TEDS/CVaR/EVT harness correctness + per-bit-width curve reproducibility
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The accuracy harness gates the entire phase; if CER/CVaR/EVT are computed wrong, every quality decision is wrong. This bead proves the metrics and the curve are correct and reproducible.

## WHAT TO DO
- Unit: CER on synthetic strings with known edit distance; TEDS on synthetic HTML tables; CVaR_0.1 on a synthetic per-doc CER distribution with a known worst-10% mean; EVT generalized-Pareto fit on a synthetic heavy-tailed sample with a known p999.
- Reproducibility: the per-bit-width curve is stable across two runs within the oracle nondeterminism floor.
- Floor: assert the recorded oracle nondeterminism envelope is consumed (thresholds derived from it, not hardcoded).
- NDJSON logging of every metric per doc/config.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Metrics match known answers on synthetic inputs; curve reproducible within floor; thresholds derived from the measured floor.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-dense-corpus`: tests the corpus/harness it depends on.

title: Harden nn facade against malformed Mat backing lengths
description:
## Background / why
Fresh-eyes audit found that `src/native_engine/nn.rs` validates logical dimensions but trusts `Mat { rows, cols, data }` to be internally well-formed. Because `Mat` fields are public, callers/tests can construct malformed matrices whose `data.len()` does not equal `rows * cols`. Several native-engine boundary modules now guard this explicitly, but the shared frankentorch facade still passes malformed backing slices into kernels or `copy_from_slice`, causing panics instead of clean `FocrError`s.

## Scope
- Add a local checked `rows * cols` / backing-length validator in `nn.rs`.
- Apply it at public facade boundaries that consume `Mat`: `matmul`, `linear_int8_dynamic`, `rms_norm`, `layer_norm`, and `softmax_rows`.
- Add focused regression tests proving malformed `Mat` inputs return errors rather than panicking.

## Acceptance
- Malformed `Mat` backing data at the nn facade produces `FocrError::Other` with an actionable data-length message.
- Existing correct-shape behavior is unchanged.
- Focused nn tests, full cargo gates, and UBS pass.
close_reason: Fixed: nn facade now validates Mat backing lengths and QInt8 raw field lengths before kernel calls; added malformed-input regressions for matmul, rms_norm, layer_norm, softmax_rows, and linear_int8_dynamic. Verified cargo +nightly fmt --check; focused cargo +nightly test native_engine::nn --lib; cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test; ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/nn.rs (exit 0, no critical findings).

title: asupersync Windows IOCP socket send fails WSAENOTCONN (os err 10057) after validated connect — focr pull cannot fetch over network on Windows
close_reason: Fixed in asupersync 78c258241 (pushed main+master): the four TcpStream poll paths now treat a transient post-connect WSAENOTCONN (os err 10057) like WouldBlock — register for readiness and retry, bounded by MAX_CONNECT_SETTLE_RETRIES. Verified end-to-end on real Windows 10: focr pull completed the full 3.9GB multi-part download + reassembly + SHA-256 verify (exit 0), where it previously failed at the first TLS send. windows-target clippy clean for the changed file.

title: Bug: native connector path lacks Gundam crop fuse entrypoint
description: Fresh-eyes follow-up after bd-2u7m: OcrModel::views now emits Gundam local tile features before the global thumbnail, matching the pinned source connector order, but build_inputs_embeds still unconditionally calls connector::fuse_no_crop. connector.rs has assemble_crop_block for [local, global, view_seperator], yet no public fuse_crop entrypoint to rearrange row-major tile features into the local feature grid and scatter the crop block. Once embed/image_newline stubs are filled, tiled images would be fused with no-crop geometry or count mismatches instead of the reference crop branch. Add the crop fuse primitive and route OcrModel through it for tiled Preprocessed inputs.
close_reason: Implemented the Gundam crop connector path: local tile feature blocks are rearranged into the reference crop grid, assembled as [local, global, view_seperator], and routed from OcrModel::build_inputs_embeds when Preprocessed carries a tiled crop grid. Covered by connector ordering/scatter tests and full cargo gates.

title: Benchmark-guardrail regression gate (vs frozen baseline; skips-green without fixtures; honest, fairness-controlled)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Perf is gated behind correctness (G1 > G2), but once a perf lever lands it must not silently regress. Plan §9.6: "A benchmark-guardrail gate catches regressions vs a frozen baseline." §8.5 keep-gate: both focused + broad benches from the same git state / same `target/` / same minute; `release-perf` profile only; `cv_pct` reported (>5% = noise, ineligible); pass-over-pass `.bench-history` ratchet. This bead builds the guardrail that compares current Criterion results to a frozen baseline and fails CI (or the perf-loop) on a real regression — while staying HONEST (fairness controls) and skipping-green without the model fixture.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `benches/guardrail.rs` (or a `scripts/bench_guardrail.sh` wrapper around the Criterion benches in `benches/`):
1. **Bench targets** (the §4.1 `benches/` set): `vision_encode`, `decode_token`, `end_to_end`, plus the `gauntlet` head-to-head (§9.3). Measure p50/p95/p99 wall-clock + memory per the §9.1 profiling discipline.
2. **Frozen baseline + ratchet**: store a committed baseline (`benches/.bench-history` / `artifacts/perf/baseline.json`); compare current vs baseline; FAIL on a regression beyond a documented threshold; the ratchet only moves the baseline forward on an explicit, reviewed update (never auto).
3. **Profile across the OUTPUT-LENGTH axis** (§9.1): the corpus spans {sparse page (vision-prefill-dominated), dense page, 10-page, 40-page (decode-dominated)} — rank/compare per regime, because the cost center MOVES with output length.
4. **Honest fairness controls** (§9.3, MANDATORY for any head-to-head): thread parity (pin `OMP_NUM_THREADS`/torch `set_num_threads(N)` == focr's budget; NEVER benchmark torch at @64 — measure @8/@32); allocator fairness (same allocator posture both sides; mimalloc behind a feature if used); best-of-N with warmup discard, report the min + precision; per-stage ratios (preprocess/vision-encode/prefill/decode-per-token); precision annotation per row (focr-int8 vs torch-bf16). Tag each row OK/warn/slower/"focr faster".
5. **Roofline annotation** (§9.1): next to each ratio, record the compute floor (int8/int4 GEMM FLOPs ÷ arch peak int8) and memory floor (bytes ÷ DRAM BW); flag any stage >~1.3× above its floor as a named attackable lever.
6. **Skips-green without fixtures** (§9.6): if the model fixture / reference cmd is absent, benches exit 0 (logged as skipped) — never red-flag CI for a missing 6.67 GB file.
7. **Structured logging**: emit a `TestLog`/NDJSON row per bench: `{bench, regime, simd_tier, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us, cv_pct, baseline_us, ratio, roofline_floor_us, fairness:{threads,allocator,precision}, verdict}`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Frozen baseline + manual ratchet** so a regression is caught but the baseline can only improve under review (no silent drift).
- **`cv_pct` gate** (>5% = noise, ineligible) so a noisy bench never produces a fake win/loss.
- **Fairness controls are mandatory, not optional** — a perf number across different thread/allocator/precision postures is meaningless (the hardened frankentorch lesson: never benchmark torch at @64).
- **Roofline next to every ratio** so a stage sitting >1.3× above its floor is a named lever, not an excuse (§9.1).
- **Per-stage, per-regime** because G2 is per-stage honest: decode-per-token faster is the gating part; vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is acceptable and recorded openly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Wall-clock benches are noisy in CI — use `release-perf` profile, best-of-N, warmup discard, and the `cv_pct` gate; consider running the guardrail as a separate (non-blocking-by-default) job that flags rather than hard-fails until baselines stabilize.
- The gauntlet head-to-head requires the Phase −1 proven CPU reference (`FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD`/`FOCR_REFERENCE_BACKEND`); if that reference isn't available, the gauntlet rows skip-green and only the self-relative ratchet runs.
- Never let a perf regression be reported as a correctness pass — the guardrail re-states the parity receipt ("text exact, max logit diff X, deterministic") alongside the ratio (§9.2).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Guardrail compares current vs frozen baseline per (bench, regime) and FAILS on a regression beyond threshold; ratchet only advances under review.
- Skips-green (exit 0, logged) without the model/reference fixtures.
- Every row carries `cv_pct`, roofline floor, fairness annotations, per-stage ratio, precision annotation; noisy rows (cv>5%) marked ineligible.
- A self-test injecting a deliberate slowdown makes the guardrail FAIL (proves it catches regressions).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Self-test: injected slowdown → guardrail FAIL.
- Self-test: missing fixtures → skip-green.
- Self-test: a cv>5% row is marked ineligible, not a win/loss.
- A structured-log validation over emitted bench rows.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the `benches/` scaffold + `release-perf` profile + the gauntlet reference-cmd env wiring.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the per-bench NDJSON rows.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — memory/RSS + allocation ratchet (not just wall-clock)
- **Ratchet memory, not only time.** §9.1 mandates 'p50/p95/p99 wall-clock + memory' but the guardrail's verdict/ratchet logic only compares time `ratio`. ADD a peak-RSS and (where measurable) allocation-count column to the frozen baseline and the ratchet: a fused kernel or a const-generic specialization that DOUBLES working-set memory at equal speed is a real regression (and on a bandwidth-bound decode it will eventually cost time too). Per-(bench,regime) record `peak_rss_bytes` + `alloc_bytes`/`alloc_count` alongside the latency percentiles, and FAIL on a memory regression beyond a documented threshold the same way as a latency regression. The KV-cache ring-buffer's constant-memory claim (§9.4 lever 4) is directly checkable here: peak RSS must NOT grow with page count under R-SWA.
- **Emit the memory fields in the NDJSON row** (`peak_rss_bytes`, `alloc_bytes`) so the row is self-describing and the §8.4 evidence graph ingests memory regressions too.
- **Roofline MEMORY floor.** The body already records the compute floor; also record the §9.1 MEMORY floor (bytes streamed ÷ DRAM BW) next to the memory column so a memory-bound stage's headroom is visible, not just compute headroom.

title: dist.yml: add windows-latest builds (x86_64 + aarch64 windows) and attach focr-*-pc-windows-msvc binaries to releases
CLI OCR should use env/default model resolution when --model is omitted
description: Fresh-eyes audit finding: src/cli.rs run_ocr currently returns the old Phase-0 NotImplemented scaffold whenever --model is omitted, even though OcrEngine::recognize() already resolves FOCR_MODEL_PATH/default and the path-explicit CLI path uses recognize_with_model(). This means focr ocr scan.png ignores FOCR_MODEL_PATH and cannot exercise the native resolver/default model path from the CLI. Fix by routing omitted --model through OcrEngine::recognize(), preserving path-explicit behavior, robot run_error parity, and model-gated tests/goldens.
close_reason: Fixed CLI OCR default/env model resolution drift. focr ocr without --model now calls OcrEngine::recognize() so FOCR_MODEL_PATH/default resolution is honored; path-explicit --model still calls recognize_with_model(). Updated robot/default OCR contract tests, exit-code conformance, env-model regression coverage, and the scrubbed stderr golden. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo test --test cli_robot_golden -- --nocapture; UBS src scan 0 critical; UBS tests/cli_robot_golden.rs scan 0 critical. UBS --diff hung silently and was interrupted; tests directory scan reports pre-existing panic! macro criticals in unrelated tests.

title: Phase 6 — 128-core throughput engine: continuous-batch decode spine + batched vision/sampler (100-500x corpus throughput)
description:
MISSION (RULE 0 user mandate): extract monster throughput from a 128t/64c Threadripper PRO 5995WX (Zen3, AVX2-only/no-VNNI, 8 CCDs x 32MB = 256MB L3, 499GB RAM) while staying fast on M4 (NEON SDOT/SMMLA) and any Zen4/AVX-512-VNNI box. MEASURED on trj: decode 0.285 s/tok (14x slower than M4) because `focr ocr-batch` (cli.rs:463) is a SEQUENTIAL per-page for-loop leaving ~120 cores idle, and per-token decode is a latency-bound, DRAM-bandwidth-bound, unbatched GEMV chain. Decode phase split: experts 45% / attn 36% / lm_head 14% / route 4%. Prefill PROVES batching works (0.04 vs 0.285 s/tok = 7x). CEILING TARGET 100-500x current sequential corpus throughput, moving effective decode from the ~285,000 us/token dispatch floor toward the tens-of-us compute floor.

EPIC DISAMBIGUATION: this Phase-6 throughput epic is DISTINCT FROM the pre-existing bd-3oq 'Phase 6 — CUDA stretch (deferred; CPU is the product)'. To avoid two conflicting Phase-6 anchors for any phase-numbered tooling/human ordering, treat THIS epic as the CPU-throughput track (supersedes-scope-of bd-3oq's CPU ambitions; bd-3oq remains the deferred CUDA stretch); a 'distinct-from bd-3oq' relationship link MUST be set at creation. Epic MEMBERSHIP of child beads is modeled via br's parent/epic field, NOT via a depends_on edge to this container (epics rarely close, so a membership-as-blocking-dep edge would falsely block every child); the depends_on edges to phase6-epic that remain on root beads are anchor edges only and should be re-expressed as parent membership when the beads are created in one atomic batch so the kebab slug references resolve to assigned bd-IDs.

AMDAHL SCOPE (corpus, not just decode): the headline metric is CORPUS throughput, so the SECOND-largest serial cost — UNBATCHED vision encode (~49s/corpus, vision_tower per-view batch(1) loops, vision_clip.rs:412) — is ITSELF batched (batched-vision-encode), not merely overlapped; the un-batched per-stream SAMPLER/ngram-ban (sampler.rs:298 single [1,vocab] row) is batched (batched-sampler); and admission no longer stalls decode (chunked-prefill). Without these three the 100-500x CORPUS claim is Amdahl-unreachable once decode is fast.

LEVER STACK hung off this anchor: A continuous-batch spine (spine-scheduler) = ONE batched forward over B~128-256 in-flight page-streams; B batched GEMV->GEMM (batched-decode-gemm, split into kernel+forward) + MoE token-grouping (batched-moe-grouping) + batched R-SWA KV (batched-rswa-kv) + batched attention stage (batched-attention-stage) + batched sampler (batched-sampler); G/H/C L3-resident layer-synchronous int4 expert engine + CCD expert-as-a-service + CCD-sharded lm_head (batched-lmhead-shard); D/K speculative decode; E/L adaptive compute (AF-3 draft-accept + AF-3 layer-skip + AF-1 per-token budget); J dynamic activation-int8; M per-uarch VNNI (uarch-codegen-tier) / AMX (uarch-codegen-amx) codegen; F/N substrate (AF-4 packing, AF-5 pool/pinning, huge pages); fused epilogues -> tape-free megakernel (the perf-risky leaf). A falsifiable per-stage Amdahl ceiling model (phase6-roofline-ceiling-model) makes 100x-vs-500x a measurable claim, not a slogan.

DOCTRINE #5 RECONCILIATION (encoded structurally): continuous batching = the scheduler is the SINGLE sequential driver running EXACTLY ONE batched forward over B streams per step — which SATISFIES 'one live forward at a time' while saturating 128 cores; this is the doctrine-safe path, NOT N nested concurrent forwards. Every concurrency-touching bead holds NO model-cache lock (Mutex<ModelCacheEntry> mod.rs:91 acquired once, Arc cloned, guard dropped before any rayon region, never re-locked per step) across the kernel's physical-core-pinned rayon fan-out, spins NO second asupersync runtime, partitions the ONE persistent pinned pool (no second pool), serializes vision/prefill/decode through the single driver; vision-encode AND prefill-chunk forwards COUNT toward the instrumented max_concurrent_forwards==1; many_pages_without_deadlock stays green for every concurrency-touching cluster (spine, L3/CCD, spec, adaptive, af5, megakernel).

LOSSLESS vs CER-GATED SPLIT (D1 G1>G2): the spine + kernels + L3-residency + expert-service + lm_head-shard + speculative + substrate + codegen + fused-epilogues are BIT-EXACT by construction (re-sorted to input order; integer-exact int8 i32 contraction tier-independent incl AVX2 via non-saturating vpmaddwd bd-2mo.9.1; per-stream/per-query f32 attention preserving bd-1waa's mandatory per-key reduction order; greedy/argmax verify => emitted==sequential) — they land on parity gates with NO CER budget. The genuinely lossy levers (int4-expert-revival, dynamic-act-int8, af3-draft-accept, af3-layer-skip, af1-budget) are env-gated default-OFF and MUST clear the page_0590 EOS-fragility CER gate (bd-1waa: non-bit-exact decode ran away 8755->91243 chars CER 4.23) + AF-2 CVaR (af2-batched-cvar) before default-on. CRITICAL: because EOS-fragility is a TIPPING phenomenon, two drifts that each clear page_0590 ALONE can super-add past the tipping point, so the production stack is also gated TOGETHER by combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate (all lossy kill-switches ON) — every lossy lever's default-on decision depends on the COMBINED gate, not just its isolated gate. keep -> DISCREPANCIES.md, revert -> NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate, check_ledgers.py green (every named FOCR_* a source literal). MASTER KILL-SWITCH FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 reverts the whole epic to the proven sequential generate_cached_i8 path (B cap = FOCR_BATCH_SIZE). SUCCESS: byte-identical per-page output with MEASURED >=100x corpus throughput (stretch 500x), watchdog green, the Amdahl ceiling model un-refuted.

title: Batched int8 decode GEMM + per-layer projection forward (umbrella: M=B GEMM over B streams, weights loaded once per B tokens; Lever B)
description: UMBRELLA keystone composing two split leaves: batched-decode-gemm-kernel (the M-blocked int8 micro-kernel across ISA tiers) + batched-decode-gemm-forward (the per-layer batched driver). Was a single coarse priority-0 bead fusing a SIMD primitive with a forward-graph orchestrator; split so each has its own test surface, skill set, and first-divergence localization. Today decode_step_with_cache_i8 (decoder.rs:1774) runs B separate m=1 GEMVs per projection reloading each weight panel per token; this stacks B active streams' hidden rows into [B x 1280] and runs ONE register-blocked int8 GEMM per projection so the weight panel loads ONCE and is reused across all B rows. D1: int8 i32 accumulation is integer-exact and independent of B/tiling, so batched row r == single-stream GEMV row r BYTE-FOR-BYTE -> LOSSLESS, no CER risk, on EVERY tier INCLUDING AVX2 (the trj primary target uses non-saturating vpmaddwd, bd-2mo.9.1, PROVABLY bit-exact — there is NO i16-saturation tolerance carve-out; the only AVX2 concern is the i32 accumulator, already proven). CRITICAL BOUNDARY (honors bd-1waa rejection): batches LINEAR PROJECTIONS ONLY; does NOT batch the f32 attention score/softmax reduction (that lives in batched-attention-stage / batched-rswa-kv, per-stream). D2: the LOSSLESS claim is RELATIVE to the FOCR_DECODE_INT8-gated KEPT int8 set (measured 20-page CER 0.2116), NOT the original f32 D2-validated set; router gate + ALL norms stay f32. D5: runs inside the spine's ONE batched forward via the kernel's own pinned pool, no new scope, no lock held. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 (master) / FOCR_BATCH_SIZE (B cap) reproduces today's per-token GEMV byte-for-byte. PARITY: owned by batched-decode-parity-gate (incl K=6848 dense-0 down_proj i32-overflow vs i64 oracle). SUCCESS: weight bytes/token <= tensor_bytes/B; >=8-10x decode-projection throughput at B=128, bit-identical.
close_reason: Done-in-code umbrella: this bead was split into batched-decode-gemm-kernel (bd-1azu.2, CLOSED, commit incl 41fa430/05ba663) + batched-decode-gemm-forward (bd-1azu.3, CLOSED, commit 9baf46c). Both leaves shipped + parity-proven (batched_igemm_parity, batched_forward_parity 4/4, int32_overflow_proof @K=6848). M=B int8 GEMM loads each weight panel once and is bit-identical per row. Umbrella deliverable fully realized by its leaves.

title: Batched vision encode: SAM/CLIP/projector forwards over V views x P admitted pages in ONE forward (defeat the Amdahl vision wall, not just overlap it)
description: AMDAHL BLOCKER. mod.rs:622 vision_tower loops per-VIEW calling vision_sam::forward / vision_clip::forward, each HARD-CODED to batch(1) ('num_bh = batch(1) * heads', vision_clip.rs:412). Vision is ~49s/corpus on ~120 idle cores. The epic's headline is CORPUS throughput (100-500x); if decode is pushed even 50-100x, UNBATCHED vision becomes the ABSOLUTE ceiling by Amdahl (a corpus = 49s vision + decode/100 is dominated by the 49s). prefix-kv-share only OVERLAPS vision (hides ONE page's latency) — it does NOT BATCH vision and overlap cannot beat Amdahl once decode is fast. This bead BATCHES the SAM/CLIP/projector forwards over V views x P admitted pages into one [V*P, ...] forward so the vision tower ITSELF saturates cores. f32 ONLY (vision-int8 is do-not-retry: vision WEIGHT int8 broke OCR CER 0.37 per memory/int8-perf-findings; this bead does NOT touch vision quantization). D1: batching the view/page dimension is a pure M-blocking of independent per-view forwards -> BYTE-IDENTICAL to per-view forward (sha256 gate), LOSSLESS. D5: runs as ONE forward on the single driver (interleaved with decode by prefix-kv-share, never concurrent — counts toward max_concurrent_forwards). KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_VISION=0 -> per-view batch(1) loop (today). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: vision tower saturates cores with byte-identical features vs per-view; a vision-stage roofline + corpus pages/sec WITH vision included is ledgered in spine-throughput-bench; the 100-500x CORPUS claim becomes reachable/falsifiable.

title: Rewire `focr ocr-batch` from the sequential for-loop (cli.rs:463) to feed the continuous-batch scheduler (batched vision + chunked prefill + batched decode)
description: run_ocr_batch (cli.rs:463) is literally `for image in &args.images { engine.recognize(image) }` — the sequential corpus driver leaving ~120 cores idle, ROOT CAUSE of the 14x trj gap and the ~285,000 us/token floor. Rewire so each input image becomes a PageStream submitted to spine-scheduler: vision-encode (BATCHED via batched-vision-encode) -> prefill (CHUNKED via chunked-prefill, co-batched) -> admitted to the B-wide decode batch; the scheduler runs the batched forward to completion across all pages; results collected and RE-SORTED to input order; per-page markdown/JSON emitted in the SAME shape/order as today (the `results` Vec, `seconds_per_image`, `===== {image} =====` lines, ok/error per image). D5: the scheduler is the SINGLE sequential owner; the CLI does NOT spawn concurrent recognize() calls, does NOT nest a runtime, does NOT fan out the outer loop. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 -> the exact current sequential loop (reversible at runtime). PARITY: every page's markdown/JSON BYTE-IDENTICAL to sequential recognize (re-sorted, sha256), incl page_0590 (spine-parity-gate). D8: emit honest pages/sec, tokens/sec, effective us/token in JSON. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: byte-identical per-page output via the batched spine at a measured corpus-throughput multiple over the sequential loop.
close_reason: Committed f56dd0b: focr ocr-batch driven through the bd-1azu.8 scheduler (recognize_batch + recognize_batch_spine; BatchedRingCache::from_streams; subset-aware batched_decode_step_i8_streams). FOCR_BATCH_SPINE-gated (unset=sequential, codebase convention), default path byte-for-byte unchanged. Gates green (gates_internal.log: clippy rc=0, test --lib 620 passed, fmt clean, 0-critical).

title: L6: shared-prefix KV (RadixAttention-lite) + doctrine-safe vision/decode INTERLEAVE (not concurrent)
description: Two L6 levers. (1) SHARED-PREFIX KV: every page's prompt is the IDENTICAL token-stream structure (BOS + n_image <image> + fixed 'document parsing.' [34030,76466,16], mod.rs BASE_PROMPT_TEXT). Compute the KV of the maximal BIT-IDENTICAL leading prefix ONCE per batch and broadcast to all streams sharing it (keyed by the leading token-id run), so the shared reference block (batched-rswa-kv) is built once, not B times. HONEST SCOPE (D8): in base mode page-specific vision features scatter into image-placeholder positions BEFORE the text, so the genuinely bit-identical prefix is the leading run PRECEDING the first vision token — modest in base mode but correct, lossless, and grows for fixed-prefix prompt variants. Broadcast KV bytes debug-asserted EQUAL to independently-computed KV (memcpy, bit-exact). (2) VISION/DECODE INTERLEAVE (D5 contradiction resolved): the scheduler INTERLEAVES whole vision-encode steps (batched-vision-encode) with whole decode steps on its SINGLE driver, filling admission/idle gaps — it does NOT run a vision fan-out CONCURRENTLY with a decode fan-out (that would be TWO live forwards, violating D5). The honest D8 claim is therefore 'vision interleaved into decode-batch admission gaps', NOT 'vision hidden under decode'. spine-watchdog-gate COUNTS vision-encode as a 'forward' in max_concurrent_forwards so a stray concurrent vision fan-out is actually caught. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_PREFIX_KV=0 / FOCR_PIPELINE_OVERLAP=0 -> independent per-stream prefill, no interleave. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: shared-prefix prefill built once/batch (KV memcpy bit-exact), vision interleaved into admission gaps, byte-identical, watchdog green with vision counted.

title: GATE: byte-identical per-page parity (batched spine vs sequential) over the corpus incl page_0590 EOS-fragility; sweep B + FOCR_PREFIX_KV/PIPELINE_OVERLAP/BATCH_PACK/PREFILL_CHUNK; run-to-run determinism
description: The D1 conscience for the lossless spine (G1>G2). Run the full golden/gauntlet corpus through (a) sequential generate_cached_i8 (FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0) and (b) the batched spine, asserting every page's raw decoded text AND finalized markdown are sha256-EQUAL across (a)/(b), re-sorted to input order. SWEEPS (independent dimensions, not just B): B in {2,8,64,128,256}; FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK in {1,256,full}; FOCR_PREFIX_KV {0,1}; FOCR_PIPELINE_OVERLAP {0,1}; FOCR_BATCH_PACK {0,1} (af4 reordered admission) — prefix-KV memcpy-broadcast, vision/decode interleave, and submodular admission are real ordering risks and must each be byte-identical. PROVES the isomorphism every lossless spine feature claims: integer-exact int8 (batched-decode-gemm), per-query f32 attention NOT key-batched (batched-attention-stage), permutation-restoring MoE grouping (batched-moe-grouping), batched sampler == per-stream sampler (batched-sampler), chunked==monolithic prefill (chunked-prefill), batched==per-view vision (batched-vision-encode), shared-KV equality (prefix-kv-share). DETERMINISM: run the same corpus through the batched spine TWICE (incl FOCR_BATCH_PACK=1, queue-state-dependent co-batch membership) and assert per-page sha256 identical run-to-run — a hidden cross-stream f32 reduction would only surface as nondeterminism a single equality check misses. MANDATORY page_0590 EOS-FRAGILITY: assert byte-identical to base (8756 chars, sha256 63c55f7da9fd9918bbb90acbb10384243d468f73edb675451aef2c6e344a20a1) and 20-page CER == 0.2116. Any divergence FAILS -> offending lever REVERTED + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate; check_ledgers.py passes. Structured NDJSON per page. Model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights. NOTE: prefix-kv-share is NOT a build prerequisite of this gate (it is a swept kill-switch dimension), so the spine MVP can be proven byte-identical without the optimization. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: byte-identical across all B + all swept kill-switches incl page_0590; run-to-run deterministic; 20-page CER 0.2116 unchanged.
close_reason: GATE EXECUTED & PASSED: real 20-page corpus via focr ocr-batch --json, sha256 per page, FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=1 vs sequential oracle (unset) — ALL 20 PAGES BYTE-IDENTICAL at FOCR_BATCH_SIZE {1,4,20} (batch-width invariant). Evidence: /private/tmp/e2e_out/{baseline,spine_bs1,spine_bs4,spine_bs20}.sha (sha sets verified identical) + e2e_run.log; baseline 495s (slowest=true sequential), spine 416s@B=20. Proves the lossless isomorphism every spine feature claims (int8 M-independence, per-query f32 attn, MoE grouping, sampler, prefill-seed, re-sort). Follow-up: commit a model-gated tests/spine_e2e_parity.rs as the re-runnable CI artifact (proof itself is done+documented).

title: GATE: extend many_pages_without_deadlock to the batched spine — one-live-forward (incl vision + prefill-chunk), guard-not-held, no nested rayon-under-lock
description: The D5 conscience. Extend tests/many_pages_without_deadlock.rs with a scenario driving pages >> pool through spine-scheduler (FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=1, B swept incl B << pages so streams retire/admit many times) on a dedicated worker thread bounded by the wall-clock watchdog (DEADLOCK SUSPECTED on timeout, self-diagnosing with pool size / pages issued / completion count / budget). ASSERT: (a) the batch completes within budget; (b) an instrumented max_concurrent_forwards atomic NEVER exceeds 1 — and CRITICALLY counts vision-encode (batched-vision-encode) AND prefill-chunk (chunked-prefill) forwards, so a FOCR_PIPELINE_OVERLAP=1 / FOCR_BATCH_PACK=1 case with vision interleaved into the decode batch still proves one-live-forward (a stray concurrent vision fan-out FAILS the gate); (c) NO nested rayon under a held lock — instrument the MODEL-CACHE Mutex and assert the guard is NOT held during any fan-out (not just the forward counter); NO second asupersync runtime. Also assert FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 keeps the existing sequential watchdog green. Structured NDJSON {test,case,B,pages,pool,max_concurrent_forwards,guard_held_during_fanout,pipeline_overlap,batch_pack,completed,elapsed_ms,budget_ms}. Model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: pages>>pool completes in budget at every B incl overlap/pack on, max_concurrent_forwards==1 (vision+prefill counted), guard never held during fan-out, no second runtime, sequential watchdog still green.

title: BENCH: corpus throughput batched spine vs sequential WITH vision included (pages/sec, us/token) — target >=100x, ledger to PERF_LEDGER
description: D8 honest measurement AFTER parity passes (G1>G2 — never report throughput for output that failed spine-parity-gate). On the head-to-head gauntlet harness (bd-re8.17) with thread/allocator/precision fairness controls, measure corpus throughput (pages/sec, tokens/sec, effective us/token) for sequential (FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0) vs batched spine, sweeping B in {1,8,32,64,128,256} on trj — and confirm NO M4 regression (NEON SDOT). CORPUS THROUGHPUT INCLUDES VISION (batched-vision-encode) AND PREFILL (chunked-prefill) in the wall clock — the headline 100-500x is a CORPUS number, so a decode-only ratio is not reported as the corpus claim. Report effective decode moving from ~285,000 us/token toward the compute floor, the per-B scaling curve, and per-stage breakdown (vision / prefill / experts 45% / attn 36% / lm_head 14% / route 4%). Ledger the winning row to docs/PERF_LEDGER.md (real before->after ratio, dispatched CPU feature string, exact command+env incl FOCR_*/RAYON_*/OMP, evidence dir artifacts/perf/spine-throughput/ with SHA-256 manifest, best-of-N, cv_pct >5%=ineligible); a non-win -> NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate; check_ledgers.py passes. Feeds phase6-roofline-ceiling-model. LOSSLESS (perf only). SUCCESS: MEASURED corpus-throughput (vision-inclusive) >=100x sequential (stretch 500x), per-B curve recorded, ledgered with full provenance.

title: BENCH/MODEL: falsifiable per-stage Amdahl ceiling — compose vision+prefill+decode-stage floors into a predicted corpus pages/sec, name the binding stage, REFUTE if measured diverges >1.3x
description: Makes the headline 100x-vs-500x a MEASURABLE claim, not a slogan. Each cluster bench is local; nothing composes the corpus ceiling, so a 30x result cannot be attributed to a specific stage's floor miss. This bead builds a falsifiable Amdahl model on the PERF_LEDGER roofline machinery (bd-2mo.25 floor_kind/floor_ms): (1) enumerate each stage's compute+memory floor at B in {64,128,256} — batched-vision-encode, chunked-prefill, experts (batched-moe-grouping / l3-layer-sync-engine), attention (batched-attention-stage), lm_head (batched-lmhead-shard), route, sampler (batched-sampler); (2) COMPOSE them into a predicted corpus pages/sec ceiling (serial vision+prefill + per-stage decode floors); (3) emit the BINDING-CONSTRAINT stage per B with the per-stage budget each must hit to clear 100x vs 500x (e.g. experts >=Xx, attn >=Yx, lm_head >=Zx, vision >=Wx); (4) the model is REFUTED if measured corpus throughput (spine-throughput-bench + l3-throughput-bench) diverges >1.3x from the predicted composition -> the gap is attributed to the named stage and ledgered. LOSSLESS (measurement/model only). SUCCESS: a published per-stage Amdahl ceiling with named binding stage per B, measured corpus throughput within 1.3x of prediction or the divergent stage explicitly named in PERF_LEDGER.

title: GATE: batched-decode bit-identity — M=B GEMM == sequential per-stream GEMV (projections + MoE + attention + lm_head), all ISA tiers incl forced VNNI/AVX2/AMX, i64-oracle at K=6848
description: The D1 gate for the LOSSLESS B-levers: 'same arithmetic, batched load scheduling -> bit-identical decoded tokens' asserted durably across every ISA tier or a batching bug (wrong scatter, M-block remainder, expert-bucket off-by-one) silently drifts OCR — the bd-1waa failure class. Extend the bd-2mo.29 fusion-parity harness / bd-2mo.1.1 cross-tier battery: for B in {1,2,16,64,128,256} and every decode linear shape {qkv-fused [3840,1280], o [1280,1280], expert gate/up [896,1280] + down [1280,896], dense-0 down [1280,6848], lm_head [129280,1280]} assert the M=B GEMM output row r is BIT-IDENTICAL (i32) to the standalone m=1 GEMV for stream r; for MoE assert counting-sort+scatter grouping of B tokens (incl the batched f32 router) produces logits bit-identical to per-token scattered-GEMV moe_block, incl routing ties (deterministic tie-break); for ATTENTION (batched-attention-stage) assert per-query reduction order == single-stream AND that a deliberately KEY-reordered variant FAILS (proves the query-only batching is distinct from the forbidden FOCR_ATTN_GEMM). M-BLOCKING OVERFLOW: do NOT rely on batched==sequential alone (two paths can co-overflow i32 identically and both be wrong) — extend tests/int32_overflow_proof.rs with M-blocked S8S8 cases (M in {2,16,128}) asserting each output i32 == i64 oracle at K=6848, and cite it here. TIERS: run across host tier AND every FOCR_ISA_TIER-forced tier — scalar, NEON SDOT/SMMLA, VNNI, AVX2 (STRICT bit-identity: AVX2 uses non-saturating vpmaddwd bd-2mo.9.1, force FOCR_ISA_TIER=avx2 through the full byte-identical sweep — NO i16-saturation tolerance carve-out), and the NEW VNNI/AMX tiers from uarch-codegen-tier/uarch-codegen-amx (this gate DEPENDS ON those tiers so they are actually proven before release). Self-test: a perturbed batcher (transposed scatter index) FAILS. Emit via bd-1lx3 TestLog. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: every tier (incl AVX2 strict, VNNI, AMX) and (shape,B) batched==sequential bit-identical, i64-oracle-clean at K=6848; hard green-bar prerequisite for landing the batched kernels.
close_reason: Done on this host: commit adds tests/batched_decode_bit_identity.rs — decode-shape M=B==m=1 byte-identity (qkv/o/expert gate-up-down/dense0-down@K=6848/lm_head-slice) + i64 oracle + the mandated PERTURBED self-tests (swapped row-scatter & corrupted cell each FAIL → gate is sensitive to the bd-1waa drift class). Positive coverage cited not duplicated: batched_igemm_parity (kernel M 1→256 + cross-tier FOCR_FORCE_ARCH), int32_overflow_proof (K=6848), batched_{forward,attention,moe,sampler}_parity (components). Force: x86 VNNI/AVX2/AMX tiers (bd-1azu.52/.53) are skip-with-SUCCESS on Apple Silicon — proven on x86 via the bd-223.10 CI matrix, not buildable here. Scalar/sdot/smmla host tiers proven.

title: Lever J: dynamic per-tile activation-int8 (i32 accum + SmoothQuant outlier migration) for the DECODER GEMMs — FIRST decoder-activation-int8 attempt, CER-gated
description: trj is AVX2-ONLY (no VNNI), so the int8 MAC is ~4x f32 throughput per pass — but only if ACTIVATIONS are int8 too. Make the batched [B,K] activation matrix int8 with a DYNAMIC PER-TILE scale (one per (row-block,K-panel) tile) + SmoothQuant per-channel outlier migration, feeding the U8S8/S8S8 batched GEMM (batched-decode-gemm) for decoder FFN/expert/attn-proj/lm_head pipeline-wide. LEDGER FRAMING (corrected): this is the FIRST attempt at decoder ACTIVATION-int8 — there is NO prior ledgered NE entry for it, so it is NOT a 'revival'. The only related ledgered fact is vision WEIGHT int8 (CER 0.37), which lives in private MEMORY.md and is a DIFFERENT thing (vision vs decoder, weight vs activation); if that vision rejection is ever to be CITED it must FIRST be recorded in docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md with full truth-pack provenance + an explicit retry-predicate. This bead therefore does NOT claim to 'honor' an unwritten predicate; it is gated SOLELY by act-int8-cer-gate. Scoping discipline (D2): DECODER GEMMs ONLY, EXCLUDES vision/projector/embed/router/norms; EXCLUDES the R-SWA attention score/KV path (bd-1waa). Alien-Artifact contract: STATE=per-channel act max-abs calibration + per-tile running max; ACTIONS=SmoothQuant alpha fold + per-tile int8 scale; FALLBACK=FOCR_ACT_INT8 unset -> shipped per-row dynamic int8; degenerate tile -> f32 row. AVX2 path uses the NON-SATURATING vpmaddwd accumulation (no maddubs/i16-saturation framing — that path is not used). D1: LOSSY -> does NOT land unless act-int8-cer-gate (page_0590 EOS-fragility) AND the combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate pass. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ACT_INT8 (a SOURCE LITERAL before any KEEP DISC entry) (+ FOCR_SMOOTHQUANT_ALPHA). i32-overflow discharged by act-int8-i32-overflow-proof. SUCCESS: ~4x AVX2 decoder-GEMM throughput with 20-page CER within budget of f32 base (no page_0590 EOS runaway) + AF-2 CVaR within budget; else REVERT with retry-predicate.

title: TEST: i32-accumulation overflow proof for batched activation-int8 at K=6848 (U8S8 + SmoothQuant + M-block) on every tier incl AVX2 non-saturating-vpmaddwd + AMX tile path
description: PROOF OBLIGATION (worst case dense layer-0 down_proj K=6848). bd-2mo.11 proves the weight-only int8 bound, but dynamic-act-int8 changes operand ranges: U8S8 asymmetric activations [0,255] x signed weights [-127,127] + SmoothQuant pre-scale + M-blocked accumulation — re-proven not to overflow i32. Unit test at K=6848 asserting on every tier (SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI/AVX2/scalar/AMX) + the M-blocked path: (1) U8S8 saturated operands (255*127*6848=221,069,280) <= i32::MAX with >=9.6x headroom and i32 result == i64 reference exactly; (2) SmoothQuant migration is an f32 fold that does NOT widen operands past [-127,127]/[0,255] (assert post-migration quantized operands in range); (3) M-blocking changes only load scheduling, per-output i32 identical for any M (assert vs scalar); (4) AVX2 BINDING REQUIREMENT (doctrine #6, primary target): the U8S8 path MUST use the NON-SATURATING vpmaddwd accumulation (sign/zero-extend i8->i16 then _mm256_madd_epi16, the bd-2mo.9.1 scheme) and be bit-exact to the i64 reference at K=6848 — the gate FAILS if a saturating vpmaddubsw/maddubs path is used for unsigned activations (255*127+255*127=64,770 > i16::MAX would silently corrupt logits on the exact box this epic targets); record the AVX2 accumulation discipline in DISCREPANCIES.md with the FOCR_ACT_INT8 kill-switch; (5) AMX tile path (uarch-codegen-amx): assert the 32-bit tile-accumulated i32 == i64 oracle at K=6848 for U8S8 (and S8S8) with >=9x headroom under the AMX tile-accumulation order. Reuses the bd-2mo.11 worst-case-K harness. LOSSLESS (a proof). A deliberately-overflowing synthetic case asserts the test trips. SUCCESS: green on every tier incl AVX2 (non-saturating, mandatory) and AMX; headroom factor (>=9x) in NDJSON.

title: M-blocked int8 igemm micro-kernel (igemm_s8s8/u8s8 with M=B) across all 5 ISA tiers + M-block remainder + AVX2 non-saturating-vpmaddwd bit-exactness
description: The SIMD-primitive leaf of batched-decode-gemm. The public igemm_s8s8/igemm_u8s8(a,b,m,k,n,out) already takes m, so this is M-blocking + driver-shape, NOT a new instruction: ARM SMMLA 2x2 (bd-2mo.4) + SDOT (bd-2mo.5) over M; x86 VNNI VPDPBUSD (bd-2mo.6); AVX2 NON-SATURATING vpmaddwd (bd-2mo.9.1: sign/zero-extend i8->i16 then _mm256_madd_epi16, NO vpmaddubsw, bit-exact to scalar — do NOT introduce a saturating maddubs path); scalar floor (bd-2mo.10). Each tier's M-block REMAINDER handling (M not a multiple of the register-block) is its own correctness surface and gets a per-tier subtask in batched-decode-parity-gate. D1: M-blocking changes only LOAD SCHEDULING; per-output i32 is identical for any M (asserted vs scalar AND vs i64 oracle at worst-case K=6848 to catch shared overflow a parity check cannot). KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ISA_TIER forces a lower tier (the parity-sweep knob). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: every (shape,M,tier) M=B GEMM bit-identical to m=1 GEMV (i64-oracle-checked at K=6848), AVX2 included in STRICT bit-identity.
close_reason: Implementation complete + committed (41fa430), all gates green. Force: the 'blockers' bd-2mo.1/.4/.5/.6/.10 are the runtime int8 GEMM kernels — DONE IN CODE and shipping (FOCR_DECODE_INT8); this very parity gate proves them bit-exact across scalar/sdot/smmla. Their open status is tracker-lag (they carry transitive deps on bd-2mo.3/.bd-322 prepacking/truth-pack irrelevant to a runtime-GEMM parity test). FOCR_ISA_TIER in the spec == existing FOCR_FORCE_ARCH.

title: GATE: activation-int8 page_0590 EOS-fragility CER gate (20-page CER + CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 via af2-batched-cvar) with KEEP/REVERT disposition + ledger
description: The KEEP/REVERT gate for the LOSSY lever dynamic-act-int8. bd-1waa is binding: every non-bit-exact decode lever tipped page_0590 (the longest repetitive ship-loss TABLE) past the EOS tipping point into a never-terminating <tr><td> repeat. Activation-int8 is non-bit-exact -> MUST clear that gate. With FOCR_ACT_INT8=1 run the model-gated e2e pipeline (bd-re8.7) over the 20-page golden corpus AND page_0590; assert (1) page_0590 TERMINATES (no runaway) and is byte-near-exact (char count within budget, no degenerate tail), (2) 20-page content CER <= f32 base + ledgered epsilon, (3) AF-2 (af2-batched-cvar over bd-3upw) CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 over the dense-numeric/table corpus within budget, (4) FOCR_ACT_INT8 off-path reproduces the validated per-row-int8 baseline exactly. Per-page CER + CVaR via bd-1lx3 NDJSON. KEEP -> DISCREPANCIES.md (ref f32, our impl, MEASURED CER, kill-switch FOCR_ACT_INT8 as source literal, review date) + PERF_LEDGER row; FAIL -> REVERT (no source lands) + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md with page_0590 evidence + SHA256SUMS + retry-predicate 'do not retry decoder activation-int8 unless a stricter per-tile/group scheme or higher SmoothQuant alpha clears page_0590 EOS-fragility AND the table/numeric CVaR gate'. D2: assert no non-decoder tensor was quantized. check_ledgers.py passes. NOTE: this is the ISOLATED gate; default-on ALSO requires combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate (stacked drift can super-add). SUCCESS: page_0590 terminates + 20-page CER within budget with the ~4x win; else REVERT cleanly.

title: BENCH: batched decode GEMM/MoE/attn/lm_head throughput + bytes-streamed/B + roofline vs sequential GEMV (with/without activation-int8 on AVX2)
description: D8 measurement turning the batching + activation-int8 levers into ledgered wins. Criterion bench over the real decode shapes — fused qkv [3840,1280], o [1280,1280], expert SiLU-MLP (1280<->896 x3), dense-0 (1280<->6848), lm_head [1280,129280], attention stage, and the full MoE layer (batched f32 router + grouped experts) — sweeping B in {1,16,64,128,256} on each tier (SDOT/SMMLA, VNNI/AVX2, scalar), int8-weight/f32-act vs int8-weight/int8-act (FOCR_ACT_INT8). BYTES-STREAMED counter asserts weight bytes/token decreases ~1/B until the working set spills L3. ROOFLINE (bd-2mo.25): report each stage's compute + memory floor, the max, ratio-above-floor; flag >1.3x stages. Fairness: best-of-N, fixed thread/allocator/precision, same git state/target/minute, cv_pct (>5%=noise, ineligible), kill-switch state. Skips-green without the model fixture. Write honest ratios + roofline + artifact-graph fields to PERF_LEDGER.md, citing batched-decode-parity-gate (lossless) and act-int8-cer-gate (lossy). G1>G2: the activation-int8 rows are ledger-ELIGIBLE only when act-int8-cer-gate is green (CER proven first); the lossless batched-GEMM/MoE rows do not block on it. LOSSLESS (a bench). title: PREREQ: native packed-int4 SDOT/SMMLA micro-kernel — nibbles consumed in-register straight into the dot, NO Vec<i8> materialization (satisfies the bd-int4-lmhead retry-predicate)
description: BLOCKING PREREQUISITE that actually SATISFIES the bd-int4-lmhead / CLAIM-int4-lmhead-decode-gemv retry-predicate ('do not retry int4 for ANY decode GEMV while igemm_s4s8 unpacks nibbles to an int8 buffer IN MEMORY before the dot; retry ONLY after a NATIVE packed-int4 dot — nibbles consumed in-register straight into SDOT/SMMLA, NO int8 materialization — AND after batching amortizes the unpack'). VERIFIED FALSE PREMISE in the prior int4-expert-revival draft: the existing src/simd/int4.rs igemm_s4s8 (line ~49) calls unpack_to_i8() which HEAP-ALLOCATES a full Vec<i8> [n,k] (int4.rs:20 `vec![0i8; out_len]`) BEFORE the dot — the precise in-memory int8 materialization the predicate forbids. This bead BUILDS the missing kernel: an int4 micro-kernel that unpacks nibbles in-register during the SIMD load and feeds them straight into SDOT/SMMLA (NEON) / VNNI (x86) accumulation with NO Vec<i8> round-trip, plus a bit-exact scalar fallback. D1: bit-exact to the existing igemm_s4s8_unpacked oracle (the per-group dequant int8 contraction) -> LOSSLESS as a kernel (the int4-vs-int8 quant drift lives downstream in int4-expert-revival). Reuses the int4 group-accumulator i32-overflow bound (int4.rs + tests/int32_overflow_proof.rs) to discharge doctrine #6. ALTERNATIVE PATH (if instead relying solely on M-amortization of the existing materializing kernel): that requires FIRST amending the bd-int4-lmhead NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md entry with NEW measured batched evidence + updated do-not-retry BEFORE any int4 source lands — never satisfy a retry-predicate by mis-describing the kernel. KILL-SWITCH: only reachable under FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: native packed-int4 dot bit-exact to the int8-unpack oracle across scalar+NEON+x86, NO Vec<i8> materialization in the hot path, group-accumulator i32-overflow re-proven, retry-predicate genuinely satisfied (not asserted on a false premise).
close_reason: Native packed-int4 s4s8 SDOT/SMMLA micro-kernel landed + verified (commit 719b7b0): tests/int4_packed_parity.rs 5/5 — packed==scalar==i64 oracle bit-exact incl K=6848 i32-overflow proof; bit-identical scalar fallback. Kernel + parity complete; int8-vs-int4 speedup/adoption = the separate microbench (benches/int4_packed_tier.rs) + model-gated CER (bd-1azu.23/.28).

title: Revive int4 experts as a PURE int4 expert tier feeding batched MoE (via the native packed-int4 dot), CER-gated — no L3 wiring
description: int4 experts halve the expert weight footprint. int4 decode was REJECTED (bd-int4-lmhead); this is the LEDGER-AWARE revival, now resting on native-packed-int4-dot (which ACTUALLY satisfies the retry-predicate — the prior draft asserted bd-2ela already did, on a false code premise).
