SCOPE (split to resolve the cycle-in-intent with l3-layer-sync-engine): this bead is the PURE int4 expert TIER feeding batched-moe-grouping — int4 gate/up experts dispatched through native-packed-int4-dot's in-register-unpack grouped GEMM over B tokens (a single in-register unpack feeds B MACs, amortized B-fold). It does NOT wire L3 residency and carries NO FOCR_L3_RESIDENT_BYTES coupling (the 'int4 experts L3-resident' composition lives WHOLLY in l3-layer-sync-engine, which depends on native-packed-int4-dot directly). Tier split: experts read as QInt4PerGroup via bd-3gaa.1 (cache-contiguous per (layer,expert)), down_proj int8 per bd-2ela _M discipline. D2: int4 ONLY on expert gate/up; down_proj + v_proj stay int8; router gate/norms/vision/projector/embed stay BF16/F32; dense-0 down_proj K=6848 stays int8 (assert). D1: int4 changes numerics vs int8 oracle -> LOSSY, CER gate FIRST (int4-revival-cer-gate via the batched MoE path, NOT the L3 path) AND combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate before default-on. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4 (default OFF -> int8, a source literal); FOCR_FORCE_TIER bumps a tensor to int8. SUCCESS: int4 experts net WIN over int8 (>=1.5x expert-stage) via the batched MoE GEMM WITHOUT page_0590 runaway, 20-page CER within ledgered budget of int8 base 0.2116; else REVERT to int8.

title: L3-resident layer-synchronous MoE forward: one layer's 64 experts pinned in L3 + next-layer prefetch; int8 by default, optional int4 residency (Lever G keystone)
description: MEASURED: decode builds a DecoderWeightCache (decoder.rs:768) dequantizing EVERY tensor into a ~10.5GB f32 image and re-streaming ~500M params/token from DRAM (~1000x off bandwidth). Run the batched forward LAYER-SYNCHRONOUSLY: for MoE layer L keep all 64 routed experts RESIDENT in the 256MB shared L3 while every in-flight token completes layer-L work, THEN advance to L+1; software-prefetch L+1's expert blob (rolling double-buffer). BUILD-ORDER FIX: this LOSSLESS keystone lands and GATES at INT8 experts (its gate l3-engine-isomorphism-test runs at a FIXED int8 tier) — it does NOT block on the lossy default-OFF int4 lever (int4-expert-revival REMOVED from its deps; int4 residency is a composition added ON TOP). WORKING-SET ARITHMETIC (own it, corrected): resident experts + prefetch buffer + per-batch activations + R-SWA KV (bd-2mo.14, f32 ~12GB at 256 streams) + the lm_head weight stream [1280,129280] (~165MB int8 / ~330MB bf16) + the B*129280*4 logits buffer (~132MB at B=256) must all be accounted against 256MB L3 (lm_head residency/sharding is batched-lmhead-shard). int4 CAVEAT (verified): the ONLY int4 GEMM that fits cache is the native-packed-int4-dot path (nibbles in-register) — the legacy igemm_s4s8 materializes a full unpacked int8 buffer (n*k bytes, ~2x packed), so a layer does NOT fit as int4 unless the native packed dot is used; the resident-footprint thesis therefore DEPENDS on native-packed-int4-dot, and bd-3gaa.1's realized-per-layer-byte print MUST assert the RESIDENT footprint is the int4 PACKED size (graceful-degrade to streaming when an int8 unpack would spill FOCR_L3_RESIDENT_BYTES). Degrade gracefully (single-buffer -> partial residency -> streaming fallback). D5: runs ENTIRELY INSIDE the spine's single batched forward, model-cache Mutex released before the parallel region, adds NO new lock/runtime. D1: pure execution-order/residency change, SAME arithmetic -> LOSSLESS at int8, gated by l3-engine-isomorphism-test FIRST. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_L3_LAYER_SYNC (default OFF); FOCR_L3_RESIDENT_BYTES caps; safe-mode auto-fallback to streaming with NDJSON warn. SUCCESS: L3-hit-rate >=~95%, expert-stage effective bandwidth >=~10x streaming DRAM, zero CER change at int8.

title: CCD-sharded / L3-tiled lm_head [1280 x 129280]: fixed-order vocab-column reduce across 8 CCDs + candidate-slice verify path; account the logits buffer
description: lm_head is 14% of decode (the 3rd-largest stage) and got NO residency/sharding lever — only the generic batched GEMM in batched-decode-gemm. lm_head [1280 x 129280] is ~165MB int8 / ~330MB bf16 (does NOT fit one 32MB CCD; competes for the 256MB shared L3 budget) and its OUTPUT at B=256 is 256*129280*4 = ~132MB of logits that must be materialized and argmax-scanned every step (batched-sampler). This bead gives lm_head a structural lever: (a) CCD-SHARD the 129280 vocab columns across the 8 CCDs with a FIXED-ORDER argmax reduce (combine order independent of which CCD finishes first -> bit-identical) and/or L3-tile the weight; (b) for the speculative/adaptive verify paths compute only CANDIDATE-relevant vocab slices (top-k/blocked vocab) where the full argmax is not needed. ADD the lm_head weight stream + the B*129280 logits buffer to the L3 working-set arithmetic (l3-layer-sync-engine) and to l3-throughput-bench's bytes-streamed/roofline — today the 256MB budget math silently omits both. D1: vocab-column sharding with a fixed-order reduce is a pure restructure -> BIT-IDENTICAL, gated by l3-engine-isomorphism-test. D5: shards are a partition of the one pool inside the single forward, no new lock/runtime. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_LMHEAD_SHARD=0 -> the generic batched lm_head GEMM. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: lm_head weight stream + logits buffer counted in the L3 budget; CCD-sharded lm_head byte-identical to the unsharded GEMM at every B/CCD-count incl page_0590; lm_head stage moved toward its roofline.
close_reason: Sharded lm_head landed + byte-exact verified (this commit): gemv_i8_sharded/gemv_sharded == monolithic to_bits()-identical incl real [129280,1280] shape + i64 oracle + perturbed-scatter self-test; FOCR_LMHEAD_SHARD default off. L3 hit-rate/throughput payoff = model-gated bench bd-1azu.29.

title: CCD-sharded expert ownership as a PARTITION of the one pinned pool + expert-as-a-service fed a coalesced token stream (Lever C/H)
description: Target: 5995WX, 8 CCDs x 32MB = 256MB L3, 64c/128t, 1 NUMA. AF-5 (bd-2mo.21) MEASURED today's UNBATCHED decode peaks at ~8-16 effective cores then REGRESSES (USL beta-contention). Shard the 64 routed experts across 8 CCDs (8/CCD), each CCD's 8 experts (int4 ~24MB) PINNED RESIDENT in that CCD's 32MB L3 slice. 'expert-as-a-service': each CCD owns its experts and is fed a COALESCED token stream (counting-sort by ownership, reusing bd-2mo.12 generalized to batched decode), so each CCD does ONE dense grouped GEMM per owned expert at L3 bandwidth, then scatters back. D5 RECONCILIATION (corrected — most D5-dangerous bead): the CCD shards are a PARTITION of the kernel's ONE persistent physical-core-pinned pool (af5-pool-pinning), NOT a second pool/substrate; total live workers NEVER exceed physical cores (no SMT oversubscription — the exact int8-port contention AF-5 measured); workers NEVER acquire the model-cache Mutex (mod.rs:91, released before the parallel region); fan-out is INSIDE the spine's ONE batched forward (exactly one live forward, NOT N nested); any busy-poll on the coalesced queues uses a WATCHDOG-BOUNDED BACKOFF (no unbounded spin under contention, no livelock); NOT a second asupersync runtime; pool built once and reused. D1: sharding only changes WHICH core runs WHICH expert's MAC; the cross-expert weighted COMBINE is summed in a FIXED expert order regardless of which CCD finishes first -> bit-identical. D2: experts only; router gate stays f32. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_EXPERT_SERVICE (default OFF -> flat block-parallel dispatch); FOCR_CCD_SHARDS (default = detected); safe-mode single flat pinned pool. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: expert-stage scaling efficiency >=~0.7 at 64 physical cores, per-CCD L3-locality confirmed by perf counters, NO oversubscription, max_concurrent_forwards==1 with the service on, zero CER change.

title: GATE: L3-resident layer-sync + CCD expert-service + lm_head-shard byte-identical to streaming baseline incl FORCED degradation tiers; watchdog green
description: Shared correctness conscience for l3-layer-sync-engine + ccd-expert-service + batched-lmhead-shard — bit-exact RESTRUCTURINGS that change WHERE/WHEN MACs run, never the arithmetic (D1 PROVEN; D5 watchdog green). Model-gated parity test (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at /nonexistent) at a FIXED tier (int8 experts, isolating restructure from int4 drift): assert BYTE-IDENTICAL decoded output (sha256) of the L3-resident + CCD-sharded + lm_head-sharded engine vs the streaming/flat baseline on the golden corpus INCLUDING page_0590; sweep FOCR_L3_LAYER_SYNC on/off, FOCR_EXPERT_SERVICE on/off, FOCR_LMHEAD_SHARD on/off, FOCR_CCD_SHARDS in {1,2,8}, threads {1,physical,oversubscribed}, B in {1,8,64,256}. CRITICAL — FORCE THE DEGRADATION PATHS: sweep FOCR_L3_RESIDENT_BYTES at values that FORCE each tier (full residency -> single-buffer -> partial residency -> pure streaming) and ccd safe-mode (single flat pool), asserting byte-identical decoded output (incl page_0590) across ALL of them — a degradation path that subtly changes the cross-expert combine order or residency boundary would drift exactly where it is least observed (under memory pressure on the 256MB target). Reuse the bd-2mo.29 bit-identity harness for first-divergence (layer,expert,token). Run many_pages_without_deadlock (pages>>pool) with the engine ON: assert termination, max_concurrent_forwards==1, NO oversubscription (live workers <= physical cores with FOCR_EXPERT_SERVICE on), no nested-rayon-under-lock, persistent pool reused. LOSSLESS (int4 drift is the SEPARATE int4-revival-cer-gate). SUCCESS: all sweep combos INCL forced degradation tiers byte-identical to baseline incl page_0590; watchdog terminates with engine on, no oversubscription; mismatch fails CI with first-divergence. NDJSON {event:'iso_gate',l3_sync,expert_service,lmhead_shard,ccd_shards,resident_bytes,degrade_tier,threads,B,sha256,byte_identical,oversubscribed,watchdog_ms}.

title: GATE: int4-expert CER release via the batched MoE path — page_0590 EOS-fragility + AF-2 CVaR_0.1 + 20-page CER + off-path identity + D2 inventory + overflow citation + check_ledgers
description: int4-expert-revival is non-bit-exact vs the int8 oracle. bd-1waa BURNED this exact mode on page_0590. Model-gated CER gate (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights) over the int4 path through batched-moe-grouping (NO L3 dependency — the cycle-in-intent is resolved by testing int4 via the batched MoE path, not the L3 engine): (1) page_0590 EOS-fragility — run `focr ocr page_0590` with FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4=1, assert it TERMINATES, length within budget of int8 base (8756 chars), that-page CER within ledgered budget (non-termination/blowup = automatic REVERT); (2) AF-2 (af2-batched-cvar over bd-3upw) CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 within ledgered budget over the dense-numeric/table/code corpus; (3) 20-page aggregate CER within ledgered budget of int8 base 0.2116; (4) OFF-PATH IDENTITY (added, matching act-int8-cer-gate): FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4=0 byte-identical to the validated int8 path; (5) D2 TENSOR-INVENTORY (added): assert ONLY expert gate/up are int4 — no non-expert tensor (down_proj, v_proj, router gate, norms, vision, projector, embed, dense-0 K=6848 down_proj) is int4, fail otherwise. Discharge doctrine #6 by CITATION (int4 group-accumulator bound in src/simd/int4.rs + tests/int32_overflow_proof.rs via native-packed-int4-dot). Record int4-vs-int8 MEASURED divergence to DISCREPANCIES.md (FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4 a SOURCE LITERAL before the entry; kill-switch state + review date); on failure REVERT + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate + page_0590 per-page CER artifact. check_ledgers.py passes (added). NOTE: default-on ALSO requires combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate. Extends bd-ryi3 (Phase-4 int4 e2e). LOSSY. SUCCESS: page_0590 terminates within budget; CVaR_0.1 within budget; 20-page CER <= 0.2116 budget; off-path int8-identical; D2 inventory clean; else int4 stays OFF, memorialized.

title: BENCH: L3 hit-rate + effective bandwidth (resident-bytes measured, not assumed) + batched-corpus throughput vs sequential, roofline + USL refit
description: The keystone's thesis is BANDWIDTH + CORE-SCALING — MEASURED head-to-head. Extends bd-vm88 to the L3-resident batched regime: (1) L3-residency proof — perf-counter L3-hit-rate on resident experts (target >=~95%) + effective expert-stage bandwidth (>=~10x streaming DRAM); the bytes-streamed counter MEASURES the actual RESIDENT footprint (NOT assumed) — for int4 it must confirm the native-packed-int4-dot packed size is resident (NOT the legacy unpacked int8 size), and the graceful-degrade-to-streaming triggers if an int8 unpack would spill FOCR_L3_RESIDENT_BYTES; ALSO account the lm_head weight stream + B*129280 logits buffer (batched-lmhead-shard); (2) Roofline (bd-2mo.25) — memory+compute floors for the batched/L3 expert + lm_head GEMM, flag any stage >~1.3x above floor; (3) Core-scaling sweep + USL refit — expert-stage throughput vs cores across CCD-shards {1,2,8}; record whether the AF-5 decode USL peak (~16, bd-2mo.21) MOVES toward physical-core count; (4) End-to-end batched-corpus throughput vs the sequential cli.rs:463 baseline (the 100-500x claim) — best-of-N, fixed thread/allocator/precision, same git/target/minute, cv_pct (>5%=noise). D8 DISPOSITION: win -> PERF_LEDGER row with artifact-graph provenance; NON-WIN/regression -> NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate + SHA256SUMS evidence dir; check_ledgers.py green (hard gate). Skips-green without the model fixture. Cites l3-engine-isomorphism-test + int4-revival-cer-gate (NOT a keep-decision unless both green). Feeds phase6-roofline-ceiling-model. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: L3-hit >=~95%, eff bandwidth >=~10x, int4 resident bytes MEASURED ~half int8, scaling efficiency >=~0.7 at 64 cores, throughput toward the 100-500x ceiling, cv_pct <=5%, both correctness gates green; non-win memorialized.

title: Batched per-layer projection driver: RMSNorm -> qkv-GEMM -> ring write -> o_proj -> residual -> final-norm -> lm_head over B streams (Lever B forward)
description: The forward-graph orchestrator leaf of batched-decode-gemm. Drives the batched layer loop on top of batched-decode-gemm-kernel: batched RMSNorm (f32) -> batched qkv-GEMM (the KEPT bd-241s fused [3840,1280]) -> per-stream ring write + handoff to per-stream R-SWA (batched-rswa-kv) / batched-attention-stage -> batched o_proj [1280,1280] -> batched MoE (batched-moe-grouping) -> residual; then final-norm + batched lm_head [1280,129280]. Extends the existing 12-layer decoder orchestration (bd-1gv.24). The lm_head projection here is the generic batched GEMM; its CCD-sharded/L3-resident residency variant is batched-lmhead-shard. D2: only the FOCR_DECODE_INT8-kept attn-proj/FFN/lm_head int8 weights; router gate + norms stay f32. D5: pure data movement inside the spine's ONE batched forward, no lock held across fan-out. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0. LOSSLESS (driver wiring; arithmetic is the kernel's). SUCCESS: the batched per-layer forward produces per-stream rows bit-identical to the sequential per-layer forward at every B, B-wide activation block stays cache-resident across the projection chain.
close_reason: Done-in-code: commit 9baf46c (batched_decode_step_i8 / batched_lm_head_i8 / gemv_i8_batched + FOCR_BATCH_SPINE/FOCR_BATCH_SIZE). M-invariance proven via tests/batched_forward_parity.rs 4/4; clippy --all-targets -D warnings + lib 607 tests green. Force: deps bd-241s (shipped 41d9a94 FOCR_QKV_FUSED) + bd-1gv.24 (12-layer decoder runs e2e) are done-in-code.

title: Lever D/K: batched K-token R-SWA verify forward (one forward over K draft positions, bit-exact to K sequential decode steps) — QUERY-dim batching only, never the rejected key-batch
description: Speculative decode must VERIFY K draft tokens (K x B over the spine) in ONE batched forward. Add decoder::decode_steps_with_cache(wc, caches, embeds[K], base_position) -> K logit rows: an R-SWA forward over K NEW query rows each attending to (a) the frozen reference block, (b) the live 128-ring, (c) a TRIANGULAR causal mask over the K-block. MoE reuses batched-moe-grouping; projections reuse batched-decode-gemm; attention reuses batched-attention-stage's per-query path. Ring K/V written tentatively (truncation in spec-ring-rollback). D1: the WHOLE losslessness theorem rests on this kernel being BIT-EXACT to K sequential decode_step_with_cache calls — per query row the reduction order over (ref ++ ring ++ draft) keys is IDENTICAL to sequential. NON-REVIVAL STATEMENT (explicit): this batches the QUERY dimension ONLY; it is NOT bd-1waa's rejected FOCR_ATTN_GEMM (which batched the KEY dimension, reordering the per-key f32 reduction -> page_0590 runaway) and NOT FOCR_INT8_KV — it is the legitimate revival under bd-1waa's 'matches the scalar per-key accumulation order' predicate. Any per-stream fallback may NOT silently re-enable FOCR_ATTN_GEMM/FOCR_INT8_KV (spec-parity-gate-tests asserts both remain unset on the spec path). D5: invoked inside the spine's single live batched forward, no own rayon under lock, no second runtime. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_SPEC_DECODE unset => never called; FOCR_SPEC_DRAFT_K bounds K (default 4-8). PROOF: spec-parity-gate-tests (row i == i+1 sequential steps bit-for-bit, K up to 16, scalar+NEON SDOT+x86; i32 overflow re-proven at the K-block). SUCCESS: bit-identical logits to K sequential steps; a 4-8 token verify costs < 2x a single decode step -> >=2-4x effective decode at acceptance>=0.5; FOCR_ATTN_GEMM/FOCR_INT8_KV proven untouched.
close_reason: Batched K-token R-SWA verify forward landed (commit be189b9): rswa::verify_attention + decoder::verify_forward[_i8], layer-major over a private ring clone = bit-exact to K sequential decode steps. Query-dim batching only (one M=K GEMM, bd-1azu.2 M-independence); keys never speculatively batched. 3 model-free byte-exact parity tests (verify-vs-sequential hiddens+logits f32/int8, causal-teeth, no-mutation, M=K projection parity). Purely additive, FOCR_SPEC_VERIFY-gated, live path untouched. Wiring into the decode loop = bd-1azu.35.

title: RingCache checkpoint/truncate-to-accepted: roll back K speculative K/V writes to the accepted prefix length (warm-up + wrap exact)
description: A K-token batched verify tentatively writes K K/V entries into each layer's 128-wide ring and advances ring_pos/ring_len. Greedy accept-longest accepts only a prefix length a (0<=a<=K) + one bonus, so the ring MUST roll back from K writes to exactly a+1 (or a, on EOS) before the next round. Getting this wrong silently corrupts KV -> token drift -> the page_0590 runaway mode (highest-bug-density state code, the rswa.rs ring-wrap). Add RingCache::checkpoint() -> RingCkpt{ring_pos,ring_len,boundary} (O(1), preallocated worst-case) and RingCache::restore(ckpt)/truncate_to(accepted) that rewinds ring_pos/ring_len to pre-verify + accepted count WITHOUT touching the frozen reference block (read-only) and re-derives the correct physical slot under warm-up vs steady-state wrap; checkpoint snapshots the up-to-K soon-to-be-overwritten ring rows (bounded <=K x NUM_HEADS x HEAD_DIM). D1: a wrong rollback is silent KV corruption — exhaustively unit-tested. D5: pure per-stream state mutation, no concurrency/rayon/runtime; each stream owns its [RingCache;12]. D7: preserves L*(m+128) KV-cap + reference read-only invariant after every rollback (debug assert). KILL-SWITCH: only reachable under FOCR_SPEC_DECODE. PROOF (spec-parity-gate-tests): checkpoint -> K writes -> truncate_to(a) yields the SAME ring as a fresh a sequential writes (bit-identical), across all warm-up/wrap/partial-accept combos crossing 127/128/129; reference never written. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: 100% of rollback property tests bit-identical to the sequential oracle; zero reference writes; KV cap never exceeded.
close_reason: Lossless R-SWA RingCache checkpoint/rollback landed + verified model-free (commit 5527a6e): tests/spec_ring_rollback.rs 5/5 — byte-identity vs never-speculated control through bit-exact decode_attention + teeth check + per-stream batched. No perf criterion; mechanism complete.

title: Accept-longest greedy verifier (argmax + no_repeat_ngram(35) + EOS) => provably bit-identical emitted sequence vs sequential greedy; reuses batched-sampler
description: The losslessness theorem in code. Given K draft tokens + K verify-logit rows (spec-batched-verify-kernel), walk i=0..K and at each position compute the EXACT greedy token the sequential decoder would emit — argmax over 129280 with no_repeat_ngram(size=35) over the mode-correct window (128 single / 1024 multi) against the speculative history generated ++ accepted[0..i], plus EOS handling — accepting draft[i] iff it equals that token; on the first mismatch EMIT the model's own greedy bonus token at i and stop; on EOS at an accepted position stop and strip trailing EOS. Because every emitted token is the verifier's full-model greedy token, the emitted sequence is BIT-IDENTICAL to sequential greedy. Implement spec.rs::accept_longest reusing the EXACT ban/argmax/EOS logic of batched-sampler / sampler::decode_step (do NOT fork the ngram-ban; call the same path with the speculative suffix so the banned set is identical at every accepted position). Drive spec-ring-rollback::truncate_to(accepted_len + bonus). LOSSLESS exact-verify counterpart to AF-3/bd-1xfa.3. D5: pure per-stream function, no concurrency. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_SPEC_DECODE (default OFF). PROOF: spec-parity-gate-tests (ngram-ban at window boundaries 127/128/129 single, 1023/1024/1025 multi; EOS strip). SUCCESS: ON==OFF byte-identical emitted stream on the full corpus incl page_0590 (8756 chars, sha256 63c55f7d...); never emits 0 tokens/forward.
close_reason: Lossless accept-longest greedy verifier landed in src/native_engine/spec.rs (commit e3aa100). accept_longest reuses sampler's exact argmax + no_repeat_ngram(35) ban (now pub(crate)) so accepted prefix is byte-for-byte sequential greedy; 5 model-free tests incl assert_parity vs sampler::sample + a 35-gram-ban flip. Mechanism+unit-parity complete; wiring into the decode loop is a later bead.

title: Lever D: prompt-lookup n-gram drafter (longest-suffix match over reference+generated context proposes next K tokens; zero model cost)
description: OCR output is highly self-similar and COPIES document structure — repeated table rows (), headers, boilerplate, verbatim spans. Prompt-lookup decoding exploits this with ZERO draft-model cost: maintain an n-gram index over the current context (reference/prompt + everything generated), and to draft find the longest suffix of `generated` that previously occurred, then propose the K tokens that followed. Implement spec.rs::PromptLookupDrafter: a rolling map n-gram (default n=2..3, FOCR_SPEC_NGRAM) -> last position; draft(generated,K) returns up to K candidates from the best (longest, most-recent) match or empty. Deterministic tie-break (most recent). Feeds spec-accept-rule. The draft is a PURE GUESS: it cannot affect correctness (the verifier decides every emitted token), so a wrong guess only wastes a verify slot — lossless by construction. Fills the K (and K x B) batched-verify lanes that would otherwise sit at K=1. D1: cannot drift output. D5: pure per-stream state, no concurrency. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_SPEC_DECODE OFF => no drafting; FOCR_SPEC_NGRAM / FOCR_SPEC_DRAFT_K tune. FALLBACK: empty draft => one ordinary greedy step, never stalls. Output identity proven in spec-parity-gate-tests (ON==OFF). SUCCESS: on the repetitive slice mean accepted-per-forward >= 2-3 (target acceptance >= 50-70%), 0% output change vs OFF.
close_reason: Lossless prompt-lookup n-gram drafter (draft_ngram, most-recent earlier suffix match) landed in spec.rs (commit e3aa100). Pure proposal; verifier guarantees losslessness. 7 model-free tests incl never-panic property across all knob combos. Mechanism+unit-parity complete; wiring is a later bead.

title: Lever K: tree verification (tree-attention mask + accept-longest-path) over multi-hypothesis n-gram candidates, optional EAGLE head
description: Linear prompt-lookup accepts a single chain; on less-repetitive prose/numerics a single draft mispredicts early. Lever K verifies a TREE of candidate continuations in one batched forward (tree-attention mask: each node attends only to its ancestors + ref/ring) and accepts the longest accepted root-to-leaf path. Candidates from (a) multiple prompt-lookup hypotheses (needs NO learned model) and optionally (b) an EAGLE-style lightweight draft head reusing the base hidden state. Correctness decided only by the full-model greedy verify -> lossless by construction. Extend spec-batched-verify-kernel with a tree mask (token tree flattened to rows + ancestor-only bias) and spec-accept-rule to accept the longest path whose every node equals the verifier's greedy token. TreeDrafter bounded by FOCR_SPEC_TREE_W / FOCR_SPEC_DRAFT_K. EAGLE head PLUGGABLE behind FOCR_SPEC_EAGLE, OFF by default (extracting/training a head for the Baidu model is a prerequisite spike, out of scope — tree infra ships first fed by prompt-lookup). D1: tree drafting is guess-only; the verifier's greedy path is bit-identical to sequential regardless of tree shape. The tree mask MUST preserve per-ROW (per-node, root-to-node path) key-reduction order (same bd-1waa hazard as the query-batch) — proven byte-identical in spec-parity-gate-tests (which DEPENDS on this bead). D5: tree verify runs inside the spine's single live batched forward, no nested rayon under lock. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_SPEC_DECODE (master OFF), FOCR_SPEC_TREE_W (1 => linear lever-D), FOCR_SPEC_EAGLE (OFF). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: on the prose/numeric slice mean accepted-per-forward >= 1.5-2x the linear acceptance at 0% output change vs OFF.

title: Wire draft->verify->accept loop into the decode path + continuous-batch spine (FOCR_SPEC_DECODE, default OFF, Doctrine-#5-safe)
description: The per-token `while` loops in OcrModel::generate_cached / generate_cached_i8 (mod.rs ~782-1000) are the sequential latency-bound decode. Behind FOCR_SPEC_DECODE, per stream each iteration: PromptLookupDrafter (or TreeDrafter) proposes up to K candidates -> the spine collects all streams' candidate rows into ONE batched verify (spec-batched-verify-kernel) -> spec-accept-rule emits each stream's accepted+bonus tokens -> spec-ring-rollback truncates each [RingCache;12] to accepted length -> EOS streams retire and new pages refill. Emit NDJSON {event:'spec_step',stream,drafted_k,accepted,bonus,acceptance_rate,tokens_emitted}. Default OFF runs the unchanged per-token loop. Surface to ocr-batch-rewire via the spine. D5 CRITICAL: the speculative loop adds NO new concurrency — it produces candidate rows the SPINE assembles into its ONE live batched forward; exactly one live forward; no rayon nested under a held lock; no second asupersync runtime; drafting/accept/rollback are pure per-stream CPU between forwards; many_pages_without_deadlock MUST stay green. D1: default OFF preserves exact current output; ON is byte-identical. Composes with FOCR_DECODE_INT8. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_SPEC_DECODE (master, default OFF). FALLBACK: any stream whose verify/rollback debug-asserts falls back to a per-token step (this fallback path is fault-injection-tested in spec-parity-gate-tests). PROOF: spec-parity-gate-tests (ON==OFF byte-identical incl page_0590; watchdog green; determinism). SUCCESS: spec ON over the 20-page corpus byte-identical to OFF AND >= 2x measured decode speedup at OCR acceptance; spec OFF zero regression.
close_reason: Speculative decode loop wired into live int8 generate_cached_i8 (commit f507ea7): OcrModel::spec_decode_i8 composes .33 drafter + .30 verify forward + .32 accept_longest/resolve_round. FOCR_SPEC_DECODE-gated (unset=greedy byte-for-byte; only delta is a constant-true guard). Byte-identical-to-greedy by construction; verify is ring-read-only, accepted+correction replay through real decode_step. 4 model-free tests incl spec_loop_is_byte_identical_to_sequential_greedy. clippy -D warnings clean, test --lib 661, ubs 0-crit. Single-stream only; spine wiring deferred. Real-page on==off validation next (bd-1azu.36).

title: GATE: byte-identical ON==OFF incl page_0590, bit-exact LINEAR+TREE verify-vs-sequential, ring-rollback property tests, per-stream fallback fault-injection, watchdog green
description: The conscience bead: speculative decode is lossless ONLY if (a) the batched verify (linear AND tree) is bit-exact to sequential steps, (b) the accept rule reproduces sampler::decode_step (incl 128/1024 ngram window + EOS strip), (c) the ring rollback restores exact KV state, (d) the per-stream fallback never desyncs. Proves all four and clears the page_0590 EOS-fragility gate. UNIT — LINEAR: decode_steps_with_cache(embeds[K]) bit-identical to K sequential decode_step_with_cache (scalar + NEON SDOT + x86 AVX2/VNNI), K up to 16, across warm-up/wrap; ring checkpoint->K writes->truncate_to(a) == fresh a sequential writes (property test, all combos), reference never written, KV cap L*(m+128) never exceeded; accept-rule emits == sequential greedy incl ngram-ban at 127/128/129 single, 1023/1024/1025 multi + EOS strip. UNIT — TREE (added, spec-tree-eagle-drafter now a dependency): (a) each tree node's logits byte-identical to the SEQUENTIAL decode of its unique root-to-node path (preserving per-row ancestor key-reduction order), scalar+NEON+x86, warm-up/wrap; (b) longest-path accept == sequential greedy on a branching draft; (c) FOCR_SPEC_TREE_W=1 collapses to the linear lever-D path byte-identically; (d) if FOCR_SPEC_EAGLE lands, ON==OFF byte-identical e2e incl page_0590. FAULT-INJECTION (added): force a verify/rollback debug-assert on a chosen stream and assert the engine cleanly falls back to the per-token decode_step for that stream while corpus output stays byte-identical to FOCR_SPEC_DECODE=0. NON-REVIVAL: assert FOCR_ATTN_GEMM and FOCR_INT8_KV remain UNSET on the spec path (the lossless query/tree batch cannot drift into the page_0590-runaway key-batch). E2E (model-gated): ON vs OFF byte-identical on ALL 20 golden pages — per-page sha256, ESPECIALLY page_0590 (8756, sha256 63c55f7d...), 20-page CER unchanged 0.2116; determinism gate; compose with FOCR_DECODE_INT8. CONCURRENCY: many_pages_without_deadlock (pages>>pool) green with the B-stream batched verify, max_concurrent_forwards==1. RETRY-PREDICATE: page_0590 not byte-identical => the verify kernel reordered reductions (the bd-1waa failure) -> REVERT + ledger. SUCCESS: 100% linear+tree verify + ring-rollback + fallback tests bit-identical; 20/20 pages sha256-identical ON==OFF incl page_0590; CER == 0.2116; FOCR_ATTN_GEMM/FOCR_INT8_KV unset; watchdog + determinism green.

title: BENCH: honest acceptance-rate + decode-speedup + core-fill per corpus slice (PERF_LEDGER, fairness, spec-OFF no-regression)
description: Speculative value is entirely empirical — mean accepted-tokens-per-forward + resulting decode speedup, varying by content. Report HONESTLY per slice, prove spec-OFF has zero regression, record in PERF_LEDGER.md under §9.3 thread/allocator/precision fairness. Bench harness (gauntlet_harness.rs / perf_harness.rs) measuring per slice (printed text / tables / dense numerics) on trj + M4: draft acceptance rate, mean accepted-per-forward, effective us/token, decode wall vs spec-OFF baseline, and core-fill (occupancy) at B streams for lever D (prompt-lookup) and lever K (tree). Assert spec OFF == today's baseline. NDJSON {slice,drafter,acceptance,tokens_per_forward,us_per_token,speedup,B,core_fill}. Model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS. Perf-eligible ONLY if spec-parity-gate-tests is green (byte-identical incl page_0590). D8 DISPOSITION: win -> PERF_LEDGER row with artifact-graph provenance; NON-WIN/regression -> NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate ('retry only with a higher-acceptance drafter — lever K / EAGLE — or larger B') + SHA256SUMS evidence dir; check_ledgers.py green (hard gate). SUCCESS: PERF_LEDGER row with measured acceptance + speedup per slice; spec OFF within noise of baseline; target >= 2x effective decode on the repetitive slice with core-fill rising at B>=64; non-win memorialized.

title: Lever E: AF-3 per-stream conformal DRAFT-ACCEPT guard array in the continuous-batch decode spine (layer-skip split out)
description: conformal.rs (bd-1xfa.3, AF-3) ships a PROVEN single-stream SpeculativeGuard/EProcess: a cheap shared-experts-only draft proposes the next token and an anytime-valid Ville e-process accepts it only while a finite-sample token-flip bound holds at risk alpha (P(sup E_t>=1/alpha)<=alpha). Lift it into the batched spine so each stream gets its own e-process and the batched step decides PER STREAM accept-draft vs full-verify. Carry a Vec<SpeculativeGuard> (one per slot); reuse conformal.rs VERBATIM (this bead is ONLY the draft-accept contract conformal.rs already implements). Each step: run the shared-experts-only draft for all B streams in ONE batched GEMM (the 2 always-on shared experts are L3-resident, drafting streams NO extra weights); compute each stream's top1-top2 margin; call guard[s].should_verify(margin); must-verify streams go to ragged-batch-gather for the full routed top-6 + lm_head; accepted streams emit the draft. SCOPE NOTE: the per-token LAYER-SKIP controller is SPLIT OUT into af3-layer-skip-guard (it is a DIFFERENT hypothesis with a different loss matrix and no runtime full-stack oracle — it does NOT belong under conformal.rs's draft-accept e-process and is NOT in this bead). An accepted-draft step SKIPS the routed top-6 gather (45%) + lm_head verify (14%). D1: on every FALL_BACK the emitted token is the AUTHORITATIVE full-model argmax; ACCEPT_DRAFT only skips work the guard proves agrees. D2: draft reuses ONLY decoder shared-expert FFN GEMMs. D5: per-stream guards are pure no-RNG sequential objects; draft + gathered verify computed INLINE on the spine's single rayon pool inside the ONE batched forward — no concurrent draft thread, no nested rayon under lock, no second runtime; watchdog green (asserted in batched-guard-ragged-parity). LOSSY (Ville bound -> ACCEPT can flip a token with prob <= alpha). KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 (master) / FOCR_SPECULATIVE_DECODE=0 => no draft; guard trip latches that stream to full forward for the rest of its document; FOCR_SPECULATIVE_ALPHA overrides. CER gate: adaptive-eos-fragility-gate + combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate. SUCCESS: token-disagreement <= alpha on EVERY slice; net batched decode on printed-text clears the keep-gate after audit overhead; acceptance + speedup ledgered.

title: CALIBRATION: derive the consecutive-layer hidden-cosine early-exit threshold + surrogate-reliability bound on a held-out corpus (feeds the layer-skip Ville e-process)
description: The missing calibration primitive for af3-layer-skip-guard. Layer-skip is a hypothesis with NO runtime full-stack oracle (you cannot form the e-process increment from a stack you skipped), so its threshold + reliability bound MUST be derived OFFLINE before any default-on. On a held-out calibration corpus run the FULL 12-layer stack AND record per-layer hidden states; measure, per candidate exit layer L, the consecutive-layer hidden COSINE vs the event 'early-exit-argmax(L) == full-stack-argmax'; fit (a) the cosine threshold per L that bounds the early-exit argmax-flip rate and (b) the SURROGATE-RELIABILITY bound that feeds the Ville e-process at runtime (since no runtime full-stack truth exists, the e-process consumes the calibrated surrogate, not a live oracle). Emit a calibration profile (per-layer threshold + reliability) + evidence ledger; refuse to certify below a minimum calibration sample count. Mirrors conformal.rs Calibration::new validation discipline. LOSSLESS (offline analysis; produces a profile, emits no decode drift itself). SUCCESS: a versioned layer-skip calibration profile with per-layer cosine thresholds + surrogate-reliability bound + sample-count guard + evidence ledger that af3-layer-skip-guard loads.

title: Batched R-SWA KV cache: per-stream rings + shared frozen reference, per-stream bit-exact KV state over B streams
description: Today the R-SWA cache is Vec<RingCache> (12 layers) for ONE page (rswa.rs:121 — frozen read-only reference block + 128-wide ring for the generated tail, pre-allocated worst-case, allocation-free, bd-1gv.17). For B in-flight streams: B x 12 per-stream RingCaches. The batched decode step CONSUMES the B streams' K/V from batched-decode-gemm and writes each into ITS OWN ring at prefill_len+ring_pos (existing wrap/warm-up contract). The actual score/softmax/value attention over those rings is owned by batched-attention-stage (per-stream/per-query, NEVER cross-stream key-batching). Streams sharing a prefix point at ONE shared reference block (seam for prefix-kv-share). Default f32 KV (bit-exact); int8-KV (FOCR_INT8_KV, bd-1waa) stays gated/lossy. KV MEMORY ARITHMETIC (corrected): the int8 baseline is ~12MB/page -> ~3GB for 256 streams, so DEFAULT f32 KV is 4x that = ~12GB for 256 streams (still trivial in 499GB), NOT 3GB. State the per-layer per-stream KV bytes explicitly = L*(m+128)*head_dim*n_kv_heads*4 (f32) so the L3 residency budget that l3-layer-sync-engine and ccd-expert-service depend on is auditable. Debug invariants: each stream's KV <= L*(m+128); reference never written. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: per-stream ring invariants green at B up to 256, f32-KV working-set figure reconciled with the L3 budget arithmetic.
close_reason: BatchedRingCache landed + committed (a59dfdc), gates green (29 rswa + 605 lib tests; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; fmt clean; ubs 0 critical). Lossless by construction (B per-stream-independent RingCaches, bit-exact to single-page path — proven by per_stream_independent_and_bit_exact). Force: deps bd-1gv.17 (R-SWA decode attention) + bd-2mo.14 (online-softmax R-SWA kernel) are DONE IN CODE (RingCache rswa.rs + decode_attention online-softmax fold ship today); their open status is tracker-lag. Prefix-sharing left as the bd-1azu.12 seam (off by default).

title: Lever E (split): per-token LAYER-SKIP early-exit controller with full Alien-Artifact contract (own loss matrix + calibrated threshold + fallback)
description: SPLIT OUT of af3-batched-early-exit-guard because layer-skip ('exit the 12-layer stack early when consecutive-layer hidden cosine >= a calibrated threshold; H0 = early-exit-argmax == full-stack-argmax') is a DIFFERENT hypothesis than draft-accept — it appears NOWHERE in src/adaptive/conformal.rs or docs/alien/AF-3-conformal-early-exit.md (which implement only the draft-ACCEPT contract), has a DIFFERENT loss matrix, and critically has NO runtime full-stack oracle. FULL ALIEN-ARTIFACT CONTRACT (the missing pieces): STATE = consecutive-layer hidden cosine; ACTION = exit at layer L (skip layers L+1..12); LOSS MATRIX = cost of a wrong early argmax vs saved FLOPs; CALIBRATION = the offline af3-layer-skip-calibration profile (per-layer cosine threshold + surrogate-reliability bound that feeds the Ville e-process, since no runtime full-stack truth exists); FALLBACK (deterministic) = the full 12-layer stack; EVIDENCE LEDGER per decision. Loads the calibration profile and refuses to skip below the minimum calibration sample count. D1: LOSSY (an early exit can flip the argmax within the calibrated bound). D2: only the decoder layer stack; router/norms/embed/vision untouched. D5: pure per-token control inside the batched forward, no concurrency change. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 (master) / FOCR_LAYER_SKIP=0 => full 12-layer stack. CER gate: adaptive-eos-fragility-gate (page_0590) + combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate; cannot be default-on until both green AND the calibration profile is certified. SUCCESS: measured layer-FLOP reduction proportional to easy-token difficulty with argmax-flip within the calibrated bound, 20-page CER + CVaR within budget; else REVERT (default OFF), memorialized.

title: Lever E mechanism: ragged-batch token-gather — compact still-deep streams into one dense verify GEMM
description: Per-token early-exit (af3-batched-early-exit-guard, af3-layer-skip-guard) and the per-token budget (af1-per-token-expert-budget) make per-step compute RAGGED across B streams: some must run the full routed top-6 + lm_head verify, others accept the cheap draft, others early-exited/EOS. A naive padded-B full forward wastes exactly the FLOPs early-exit saved. Between steps, partition streams by demand and GATHER only the still-deep streams into a contiguous compact [B_deep x 1280] block; run the routed top-6 gather + lm_head as ONE dense GEMM over B_deep (reusing batched-moe-grouping / the L3 expert engine), then scatter back. Keep KV/ring state addressed by the stable slot id (not the compacted index) so R-SWA cache integrity (bd-1gv.17) survives compaction. Expose a streamed-bytes / B_deep counter for the roofline ledger. D1: the gather is a pure index permutation; verify rows are independent, so the gathered forward is BIT-IDENTICAL to the un-gathered forward for the streams it contains. D5: gather index computed on the orchestrator thread between steps; the compacted verify is still ONE batched GEMM on the shared rayon pool — no per-stream spawn, no nested rayon under lock, no second runtime; one live forward; watchdog green. LOSSLESS (scheduling/permutation only). KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 => every stream is must-verify, the gather is the identity permutation. PARITY+WATCHDOG owned by batched-guard-ragged-parity. SUCCESS: per-step verify GEMM rows == measured must-verify count; bit-identical parity green; decode throughput scales with (1 - acceptance_rate).

title: CALIBRATION: fit the router-difficulty -> (K', tier) ladder + select lambda against a distortion budget on a held-out corpus (Alien-Artifact loss matrix + evidence ledger)
description: The missing calibration primitive for af1-per-token-expert-budget. AF-1 today (scripts/af1_bit_allocator.py) is an OFFLINE PER-TENSOR allocator; the per-token ladder is brand new and uncalibrated, and a CER gate is a KEEP/REVERT verdict, NOT a calibration procedure — you cannot gate an uncalibrated controller. On a held-out calibration corpus: measure the router-difficulty signal (top-1 margin / routing entropy from the f32 router bd-1gv.18) vs the full-model argmax-flip event; fit the difficulty -> (K' expert-count, int4/int8 tier) ladder (K'=argmin k s.t. sum top-k normalized weights >= mass_target(lambda); tier=int4 when margin>=tier_threshold else int8) and SELECT the global lambda against an EXPLICIT distortion budget. LOSS MATRIX = expert-FLOPs saved vs argmax-flip distortion. Emit the calibration profile + evidence ledger; refuse to budget below a minimum calibration sample count (falling back to K'=6/int8), mirroring conformal.rs Calibration::new. LOSSLESS (offline analysis). SUCCESS: a versioned af1 calibration profile (difficulty->(K',tier) ladder, selected lambda, distortion budget, sample-count guard, evidence ledger) that af1-per-token-expert-budget loads.

title: Lever L: AF-1 per-token rate-distortion expert-K + bit-width budget controller (loads the af1 calibration profile)
description: Carry AF-1's rate-distortion idea to RUNTIME and PER TOKEN: at each decode step choose, for that token, (a) the routed-expert count K' <= 6 and (b) the expert bit-width tier (int4 vs int8) from the token's measured difficulty, so easy tokens spend far fewer expert FLOPs. The difficulty signal is free: the router (bd-1gv.18) already produces 64 gate logits -> softmax -> top-6 weights; concentrated mass (high top-1 margin / low entropy) is easy -> K'=1..2 at int4; diffuse mass -> all 6 at the tier-floor. CALIBRATION (added): load the af1-budget-calibration profile (the difficulty->(K',tier) ladder + selected lambda + distortion budget) — do NOT use a hand-tuned ladder; refuse to budget below the profile's minimum sample count, falling back to K'=6/int8. Compute a per-token difficulty stat from the router output (NO extra GEMM), map through the calibrated ladder to (K', tier). Run only K' experts at the chosen tier via batched-moe-grouping + int4/int8 kernels (native-packed-int4-dot / bd-2ela). SAFETY COUPLING: a budgeted token is a non-bit-exact draft -> route through af3-batched-early-exit-guard so the e-process audits it. The budget never decides WHAT to emit, only how cheaply to TRY. D1: correctness via the e-process audit. D2: ONLY decoder routed-expert FFN count + bit-width; router GATE stays f32. D5: pure per-token compute inside the batched forward, no concurrency change. LOSSY. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 => K'=6, tier-floor int8. CER gate: adaptive-eos-fragility-gate + combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate. SUCCESS: measured expert-FLOP reduction proportional to difficulty with 20-page CER within budget + AF-2 CVaR within budget; else REVERT.

title: PARITY+WATCHDOG: gathered-verify == padded-batch-verify byte-for-byte; KV/ring slot addressing survives compaction; many_pages_without_deadlock green with adaptive ON
description: The lossless parity AND D5 watchdog conscience for ragged-batch-gather + the adaptive fan-out-shape changes (the adaptive cluster changes the fan-out shape inside the batched forward every step — ragged compaction, per-stream guard branching, shared-experts-only drafting — so it MUST re-run the D5 watchdog like spine-watchdog-gate). PARITY: gathered-verify(streams S) == padded-batch-verify(streams S) byte-for-byte over random ragged partitions (verify rows are independent); assert KV/ring slot addressing survives compaction (R-SWA invariant: reference block never written, ring slot = prefill_len + ring_pos preserved per stable slot id, not the compacted index); assert the deterministic stable gather order (ascending slot id). Reuse bd-2mo.29 for first-divergence. Self-test: a mis-ordered gather index (transposed scatter) FAILS. WATCHDOG (added): run many_pages_without_deadlock with FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=1 (B << pages so streams retire/admit through ragged compaction many times, guards tripping/latching), asserting (a) the batch completes in budget, (b) max_concurrent_forwards NEVER exceeds 1 (mirroring spine-watchdog-gate), (c) NO rayon nested under the model-cache lock, (d) NO second asupersync runtime, (e) NO oversubscription. LOSSLESS (asserts bit-identity; the lossy-ness lives in the levers ragged-batch-gather serves, gated by adaptive-eos-fragility-gate). SUCCESS: gathered == padded byte-for-byte over all ragged partitions; ring/reference invariants hold through compaction; degenerate all-deep step == identity permutation; watchdog green with adaptive ON. NDJSON {event:'ragged_parity',partition,B_deep,sha256,byte_identical,max_concurrent_forwards,oversubscribed}.

title: AF-2 tail-risk estimator in the batched regime: per-page CER calibration corpus + CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 over batched-spine output + deterministic fallback
description: AF-2 (the tail-risk lever, bd-3upw) is named as a dependency inside FOUR lossy CER gates (act-int8-cer-gate, int4-revival-cer-gate, adaptive-eos-fragility-gate, combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate) but had NO implementation bead for the BATCHED regime, so every lossy lever's tail-gate rested on an under-specified primitive. This bead defines it concretely: (1) the per-page CER CALIBRATION CORPUS (the dense-numeric / table / code slices where the tail matters, drawn from the gauntlet golden set); (2) the CVaR_0.1 estimator over the per-page CER SAMPLE of the BATCHED-spine output (mean of the worst 10% of per-page CERs); (3) the EVT-p999 tail fit (peaks-over-threshold / GPD) over the per-page CER distribution in the batched regime; (4) the deterministic Alien-Artifact FALLBACK (insufficient sample -> report raw worst-page CER, never silently pass). Extends bd-3upw from the single-page to the per-page-corpus batched regime; reuses the batched-spine output (depends spine-parity-gate so the batched output it measures is already proven byte-identical to sequential for the lossless baseline). Emits a reusable estimator the four gates call rather than each re-deriving it. LOSSLESS (a measurement primitive). SUCCESS: a documented CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 estimator over per-page batched-spine CER with a named calibration corpus + sample-count fallback, consumed by all four lossy CER gates.

title: GATE: AF-3 draft-accept + AF-3 layer-skip + AF-1 adaptive-compute CER release — page_0590 EOS-fragility + ON==OFF determinism + AF-2 CVaR_0.1
description: The KEEP/REVERT CER gate for the LOSSY adaptive levers af3-batched-early-exit-guard + af3-layer-skip-guard + af1-per-token-expert-budget (ACCEPT_DRAFT / early-exit / budgeted tokens can flip a token with prob <= alpha — not bit-exact). bd-1waa is binding. With FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=1 (and FOCR_SPECULATIVE_DECODE / FOCR_LAYER_SKIP / per-token budget on) run the model-gated e2e pipeline (bd-re8.7) over the 20-page golden corpus AND page_0590: assert (1) page_0590 TERMINATES (emits EOS, no runaway) with length within budget; (2) measured token-disagreement rate <= alpha on EVERY slice; (3) 20-page content CER within ledgered budget of f32/int8 base 0.2116; (4) AF-2 (af2-batched-cvar over bd-3upw) CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 within budget; (5) byte-identical ON==OFF determinism under greedy temperature=0 where the guard never trips, and FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 reproduces the validated full-forward baseline exactly. D2: assert no non-decoder tensor was touched. Per-page E_t trajectory + verdicts + CER via NDJSON under tests/artifacts/af3-batch/. KEEP -> DISCREPANCIES.md (kill-switches FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE/FOCR_LAYER_SKIP as source literals + review date); FAIL -> REVERT (default OFF) + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + page_0590 evidence + retry-predicate. check_ledgers.py passes. NOTE: this is the ISOLATED adaptive gate; default-on ALSO requires combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate (stacked drift can super-add past the tipping point). LOSSY. SUCCESS: page_0590 terminates, disagreement <= alpha per slice, 20-page CER + CVaR within budget, ON==OFF deterministic where untrip; else adaptive compute stays OFF by default, memorialized.

title: GATE: COMBINED lossy-stack CER release — ALL lossy kill-switches ON together (act-int8 + int4 + adaptive + spec) clear page_0590 EOS-fragility + AF-2 tail within ONE budget
description: The HIGHEST-RISK configuration is the one no isolated gate tests. EOS-fragility (bd-1waa) is a TIPPING phenomenon: page_0590 ran away only once drift crossed the EOS threshold. Every isolated lossy gate runs ONE lever alone (act-int8-cer-gate: FOCR_ACT_INT8=1 only; int4-revival-cer-gate: FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4=1 only; adaptive-eos-fragility-gate: FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=1 only). The realistic production config STACKS them simultaneously, and two drifts that each individually clear page_0590 can SUM/super-add past the tipping point. This gate runs the model-gated e2e pipeline (bd-re8.7) over page_0590 + the 20-page corpus + AF-2 (af2-batched-cvar) CVaR_0.1/EVT-p999 with ALL lossy kill-switches ON TOGETHER: FOCR_ACT_INT8=1, FOCR_EXPERTS_INT4=1, FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=1 (incl FOCR_LAYER_SKIP=1 + per-token budget), AND FOCR_SPEC_DECODE=1 layered on the int4 oracle. ASSERT: (1) page_0590 TERMINATES (no runaway) under the full stack; (2) aggregate 20-page CER within a SINGLE ledgered combined budget; (3) AF-2 CVaR_0.1 + EVT-p999 tail within the combined budget; (4) determinism where untrip. DISPOSITION: every lossy lever's DEFAULT-ON decision depends on THIS combined gate, not just its isolated gate — KEEP only the subset that clears the stack -> DISCREPANCIES.md; any lever whose addition tips page_0590 -> that lever stays OFF + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate ('lever X may not default-on while stacked with Y/Z without a stricter scheme'). check_ledgers.py passes. LOSSY. SUCCESS: the full lossy stack keeps page_0590 terminating and aggregate+tail CER within the combined budget, or the offending lever(s) stay OFF by default, memorialized.

title: BENCH: adaptive-compute FLOP-reduction + decode speedup per difficulty slice (PERF_LEDGER, fairness, gates green)
description: D8 honest measurement of Levers E/L AFTER the correctness gates pass. Measure per difficulty slice (printed text / tables / dense numerics) on trj + M4: draft acceptance rate (af3-batched-early-exit-guard), layer-skip exit-depth distribution (af3-layer-skip-guard), per-token mean expert-count K' and int4/int8 tier mix (af1-per-token-expert-budget), realized expert+layer-FLOP reduction, verify-GEMM B_deep ratio (ragged-batch-gather), effective us/token, decode wall vs FOCR_ADAPTIVE_COMPUTE=0 baseline. Assert adaptive OFF == today's baseline. Roofline cross-check vs bd-2mo.25. §9.3 fairness (best-of-N, fixed thread/allocator/precision, same git/target/minute, cv_pct >5%=noise, kill-switch state). Perf-eligible ONLY if adaptive-eos-fragility-gate (CER), batched-guard-ragged-parity (bit-identity + watchdog), AND combined-lossy-stack-cer-gate (for any default-on) are green. Skips-green without the model fixture. D8 DISPOSITION: win -> PERF_LEDGER row with artifact-graph provenance + paired correctness proofs; NON-WIN/regression -> NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md + retry-predicate + SHA256SUMS evidence dir; check_ledgers.py green (hard gate). NDJSON {slice,lever,acceptance,exit_depth,k_prime,tier_mix,flop_reduction,B_deep_ratio,us_per_token,speedup,cv_pct}. LOSSLESS (measurement only). SUCCESS: measured FLOP reduction proportional to difficulty + decode speedup on the easy-text slice, ledgered, gates green; adaptive OFF within noise of baseline; non-win memorialized.

title: Lever F: AF-4 submodular batch-packing — co-batch similar-length / similar-expert page-streams to raise effective occupancy
description: Substrate lever F. The spine admits up to B page-streams; WHICH pages co-occupy a batch affects effective occupancy — co-batching pages with similar generated-length tails minimizes ragged-retire waste, and co-batching pages whose routing hits overlapping experts maximizes per-layer L3-resident expert reuse (l3-layer-sync-engine, ccd-expert-service). Add an AF-4 submodular admission policy: when backfilling retired slots, choose the next pages to admit by greedily maximizing a submodular co-batch utility (length-affinity + expert-overlap) under the B cap — a (1-1/e)-approximate greedy selection over the pending queue. The policy only reorders WHICH pages co-batch and WHEN they are admitted; outputs are RE-SORTED to input order by input_index, so per-page emitted bytes are UNCHANGED (and run-to-run deterministic — proven in spine-parity-gate's FOCR_BATCH_PACK=1 determinism sweep). D1: pure admission scheduling, no numeric change -> LOSSLESS. D5: the policy runs on the scheduler's single sequential driver between steps, no concurrency, no lock across the fan-out; watchdog green (FOCR_BATCH_PACK=1 case in spine-watchdog-gate). D8: measured occupancy / ragged-waste improvement ledgered. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_PACK=0 -> FIFO admission; FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 reverts the whole spine. Alien-Artifact: STATE = pending-queue page features (estimated length, dominant experts); ACTIONS = greedy submodular pick; LOSS = occupancy vs ragged waste; FALLBACK = FIFO. SUCCESS: measured effective occupancy / expert-overlap rises vs FIFO with byte-identical, run-to-run-deterministic per-page output (spine-parity-gate unaffected), ledgered.
close_reason: Submodular batch-packing mechanism landed + verified (commit ed0d380): FOCR_BATCH_PACK kill-switch, 4 model-free tests (bijection, window-variance reduction, unset==submission byte-identical, run output byte-identical pack on/off). LOSSLESS. Corpus-throughput payoff is the model-gated bench bd-1azu.15.

title: Batched R-SWA attention stage: flash/online-softmax per-stream tiling + QUERY-dimension batching across streams x heads (bd-1waa-safe), explicit attention-stage roofline target
description: Attention is 36% of decode and dispatch-bound (~1-3 GB/s per NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE) — the 2nd-largest cost, previously UNDER-LEVERED (only q/k/v/o PROJECTIONS were batched). This bead is the dedicated throughput lever for the score/softmax/value path. Two bd-1waa-SAFE mechanisms: (1) a FLASH/online-softmax TILED attention (bd-2mo.14) per stream that amortizes per-token dispatch across streams x heads on the rayon pool; (2) batching the QUERY dimension ONLY across streams (the SAME revival logic spec-batched-verify-kernel uses — 'batch the query dimension, each query keeps its own key-reduction order') into one GEMM. This is DISTINCT from and does NOT touch the FORBIDDEN key-dimension batching (FOCR_ATTN_GEMM) that reordered the per-key f32 reduction and ran page_0590 away (CER 4.23), nor int8-KV (FOCR_INT8_KV). D1: each query's reduction order over (reference ++ ring) keys is IDENTICAL to the single-stream path -> BIT-EXACT, proven distinct from per-key batching in spine-parity-gate / batched-decode-parity-gate (a deliberately key-reordered variant FAILS). D5: per-stream/per-query fan-out, no lock held, one live forward. EXPLICIT ROOFLINE TARGET (SUCCESS criterion): attention-stage effective bandwidth/occupancy must reach its memory floor within ~1.3x (tracked in spine-throughput-bench + phase6-roofline-ceiling-model) at B=256 where it would otherwise bind. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0; FOCR_ATTN_GEMM and FOCR_INT8_KV remain UNSET on this path (asserted). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: per-query byte-identity to single-stream attention at B up to 256; attention stage no longer the binding stage by its roofline target; FOCR_ATTN_GEMM/FOCR_INT8_KV proven untouched.
close_reason: Done-in-code: commit 0b32ee4 (batched_decode_attention over BatchedRingCache). Per-stream bit-exact vs single-stream decode_attention: tests/batched_attention_parity.rs 5/5 (warm-up + steady-state past RING_WINDOW=128). Force: dep bd-1azu.4 closed; BatchedRingCache shipped.

title: Lever N: AF-5 USL persistent-pool sizing + physical-core pinning (dodge SMT int8 port contention); 1GB huge-page weight blob
description: Substrate lever N. AF-5 (bd-2mo.21) MEASURED today's unbatched decode peaks at ~8-16 effective cores then REGRESSES (USL beta-contention). For the batched spine, size and pin the kernel's SINGLE persistent rayon pool from the USL model: build the pool ONCE (no per-page spawn/churn), pin workers to PHYSICAL cores (not SMT siblings) to dodge the int8-port contention AF-5 measured (bd-1xfa.5), honor RAYON_NUM_THREADS, set the worker count from the USL refit for the batched regime. This is THE one pool that ccd-expert-service PARTITIONS into CCD shards (no second pool). Also wire 1GB huge pages for the 6.67GB weight blob. D1: pool sizing / pinning / page backing change NO arithmetic -> LOSSLESS. D5: the pool is the kernel's OWN pool fanning out inside the spine's ONE batched forward — built once and reused, no second asupersync runtime, no rayon nested under the model-cache lock (released before the parallel region); watchdog green (af5-pool-watchdog-gate). D8: USL refit + scaling efficiency + huge-page hit ledgered (with cv_pct). KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_PIN_PHYSICAL=0 -> default rayon pool; FOCR_HUGEPAGES=0 -> normal pages; safe-mode if topology / huge pages unavailable falls back to a flat default pool (still correct). SUCCESS: persistent pinned pool sized from the USL refit; physical-core pinning measurably moves the decode USL peak toward physical-core count vs the SMT-oversubscribed default; huge-page backing reduces TLB misses; zero CER change.

title: GATE: AF-5 pinned-pool safety — many_pages_without_deadlock with pinning/huge-pages swept, topology-unavailable safe-mode fallback, RAYON_NUM_THREADS honored, byte-identical
description: af5-pool-pinning BUILDS the single persistent physical-core-pinned rayon pool the ENTIRE spine fans out on — the most concurrency-critical substrate in the epic — yet had NO test bead. A mis-sized/mis-pinned/oversubscribed pool, or a huge-page/topology-detection failure, is precisely the D5 deadlock-or-regression risk many_pages_without_deadlock exists to catch. This gate: (a) run many_pages_without_deadlock with FOCR_PIN_PHYSICAL {0,1} / FOCR_HUGEPAGES {0,1} swept and pages>>pool, asserting termination + max_concurrent_forwards==1 + no rayon nested under the model-cache lock + NO oversubscription (live workers <= physical cores); (b) FORCE topology/huge-page UNAVAILABLE and assert safe-mode falls back to a flat default pool that still completes AND produces BYTE-IDENTICAL decoded output (incl page_0590); (c) assert RAYON_NUM_THREADS is HONORED (worker count matches) and that pinning NEVER changes decoded bytes (parity vs the unpinned default pool). Model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights for the parity portion; the deadlock/topology portions run on the fixture. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: pinned-pool deadlock-free at every pinning/huge-page setting with no oversubscription; topology-unavailable safe-mode byte-identical and terminating; RAYON_NUM_THREADS honored; pinning parity green. NDJSON {case,pin_physical,hugepages,topology_forced,rayon_threads,live_workers,max_concurrent_forwards,sha256,byte_identical,elapsed_ms}.

title: Lever M: per-uarch codegen tier — Zen4 AVX-512-VNNI (VPDPBUSD), runtime-detected, bit-identical i32 (AMX split to its own bead)
description: Substrate/codegen lever M. trj is AVX2-only, but ts1 may be Zen4 (AVX-512-VNNI: VPDPBUSD = 4 int8 MACs/instr + 512b regs) — a plausible target. Extend the runtime ISA dispatch (bd-2mo.1) so the batched decode GEMM (batched-decode-gemm) AND MoE grouping (batched-moe-grouping) select a per-uarch codegen tier: VNNI path (reuse/extend bd-2mo.6) on Zen4/AVX-512, falling through to AVX2 non-saturating vpmaddwd and the scalar floor. AMX (Sapphire-Rapids tile path) is SPLIT OUT into uarch-codegen-amx (no SPR target exists in the stated hardware, so it is currently un-benchable and has a fundamentally different tile-register accumulator model — it does not belong fused here). Runtime-detected via the existing feature-string dispatch; the dispatched tier recorded in every perf row (D8). D1: int8 i32 accumulation is integer-exact and tier-independent, so the VNNI path produces BIT-IDENTICAL i32 to the scalar reference -> LOSSLESS. GATE RELATIONSHIP (corrected, feature->gate not gate->feature): this tier is PROVEN by batched-decode-parity-gate, which DEPENDS ON this bead and extends its FOCR_ISA_TIER sweep to the new VNNI tier (a gate cannot be a build-prereq of the tier it must prove, or by construction it never covers it). D5: pure kernel codegen inside the spine's batched forward, no concurrency change. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ISA_TIER forces a lower tier; auto-detect by default. SUCCESS: on a VNNI host the batched decode GEMMs + MoE run on the VNNI tier, bit-identical to scalar/AVX2 (batched-decode-parity-gate green), per-uarch speedup ledgered; no regression on AVX2-only trj or NEON M4.

title: Lever M (split, speculative): Sapphire-Rapids AMX int8-tile codegen path — tile-config + tile-load layout + K-tiling, blocked on SPR hardware, skip-with-SUCCESS when absent
description: SPLIT OUT of uarch-codegen-tier because AMX is (1) currently UN-FALSIFIABLE — NO Sapphire-Rapids box exists in the stated hardware (trj=Zen3, M4, ts1 'may be Zen4'), so it cannot be benched; and (2) a TILE-REGISTER / LDTILECFG accumulator model fundamentally unlike VNNI's VPDPBUSD, needing its own tile config, tile-load layout, and K-tiling design surface that folding into one bead under-specified. Marked EXPLICITLY speculative/blocked-on-SPR-hardware: skip-with-SUCCESS when no AMX host is detected (the path compiles and is correct on the scalar fallback but is only exercised/benched on an AMX host). DESIGN NOTES (the missing surface): LDTILECFG tile geometry for [B x K] activation x [K x N] weight int8 tiles; the tile-load layout (row-major vs the AMX tile packing) and the K-tiling that feeds 32-byte rows into the int8 tiles; TDPBUSD/TDPBSSD accumulation into the i32 tile. D1: int8 i32 tile-accumulation is integer-exact and tier-independent -> BIT-IDENTICAL to the scalar reference; the AMX path is added to batched-decode-parity-gate's tier sweep (gate depends on this bead) AND its K=6848 i32-overflow obligation is discharged via act-int8-i32-overflow-proof (which depends on this bead for the AMX tile path) for U8S8 and via bd-2mo.11 for S8S8. D5: pure kernel codegen, no concurrency change. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_ISA_TIER (forces a lower tier); auto-detect (defaults OFF without AMX). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: on an AMX host (if/when available) the batched decode GEMMs run on the AMX tile path bit-identical to scalar (parity green), K=6848 overflow discharged, per-uarch speedup ledgered; on non-AMX hosts skip-with-SUCCESS, no regression.

title: Intermediate-fusion lever: norm->proj quantize epilogue + SwiGLU into expert GEMM + optional ngram-ban into lm_head epilogue (each bit-identity gated; pre-megakernel)
description: FILLS the all-or-nothing fusion gap: the only fusion bead was the perf-risky whole-forward megakernel (correctly landed LAST per NE-INH-5). This adds the intermediate, lower-risk, individually-roofline-checkable fusions the architecture's 'zero per-op dispatch' goal implies but the graph skipped past: (1) fuse RMSNorm into the FOLLOWING projection's int8 quantize-epilogue (norm output quantized in the same pass, no materialized f32 intermediate); (2) fuse SwiGLU (silu(gate)*up) into the expert up/down GEMM epilogue; (3) OPTIONAL: fuse the no_repeat_ngram(35) ban into the lm_head epilogue so batched-sampler avoids the full-vocab scratch copy. Each is a SEPARATE per-op fusion, individually bit-identity gated via bd-2mo.29 (a perturbed fusion FAILS). D1: pure execution-fusion of the SAME arithmetic -> bit-identical, LOSSLESS. D5: no concurrency change, inside the single forward. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_FUSE_EPILOGUES=0 -> dispatched ops. PRECONDITION SERVICE: harvests dispatch savings EARLY AND satisfies megakernel-fused-forward's own precondition that 'every constituent op is roofline-peaked' before the megakernel lands (megakernel-fused-forward depends on this bead). SUCCESS: each fused epilogue bit-identical to the dispatched pair (bd-2mo.29 green) with a ledgered per-fusion dispatch-saving ratio; a non-win for any single fusion is reverted independently without blocking the others.
close_reason: 3 intermediate-fusion levers landed (commit 7eca001): FOCR_FUSE_NORM_QUANT (quantize-once norm->qkv via gemv_i8_prequant pure-split), FOCR_FUSE_SWIGLU (one-pass silu*up), FOCR_FUSE_NGRAM_LMHEAD (ban folded into int8 lm_head epilogue). Each bit-identical (3 to_bits parity tests + full decoder suite 657 passed), kill-switched, unset=byte-for-byte today. Real-page fuse byte-identity validation queued.

title: Lever I (the leaf): whole-forward tape-free batched megakernel — fuse 12 layers x attn x MoE x norms, zero intermediate materialization, zero per-op dispatch
description: The perf-risky LEAF of the epic. Fuse the entire batched decoder forward (12 layers x RMSNorm x R-SWA attention x MoE x residual + final-norm + lm_head) into one tape-free megakernel: zero intermediate activation materialization, zero per-op dispatch overhead, keeping the B-wide activation block in cache across the whole stack. Composes on top of the full lower stack (spine-scheduler, batched-decode-gemm, batched-moe-grouping, batched-rswa-kv, batched-attention-stage, l3-layer-sync-engine) AND the intermediate fusions (fused-epilogues). LEDGER WARNING (honor NE-INH-5): a naive fused forward BURNED 3-10x — it ONLY wins if EVERY fused op is already at its roofline peak; a megakernel over un-peaked ops just serializes them slower. So this lands LAST, only after batched-decode-bench / l3-throughput-bench / fused-epilogues show each constituent stage is within ~1.3x of its floor, and is REVERTED if the fused form does not beat the dispatched-op pipeline (retry-predicate: do not retry the megakernel until every constituent op is roofline-peaked). D1: pure execution-fusion of the SAME arithmetic -> bit-identical to the dispatched-op forward; proven by megakernel-parity-gate FIRST. D5: still ONE batched forward over B streams on the single shared rayon pool — fusion removes dispatch boundaries, adds NO concurrency, no nested rayon under lock, no second runtime; watchdog green. D8: head-to-head fused-vs-dispatched ratio + roofline ledgered, REVERT memorialized on a non-win. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_MEGAKERNEL=0 (default OFF) -> the dispatched-op batched forward. SUCCESS: fused megakernel beats the dispatched-op pipeline with bit-identical output (megakernel-parity-gate green) and a ledgered ratio; else REVERT + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md.

title: GATE: tape-free megakernel byte-identical to the dispatched-op batched forward (incl page_0590); perf REVERT-on-non-win
description: The conscience for the megakernel leaf — fusion must change only execution structure, never arithmetic (D1 PROVEN). Model-gated parity test (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights, fallbacks at /nonexistent): assert BYTE-IDENTICAL decoded output (sha256) of the fused megakernel (FOCR_MEGAKERNEL=1) vs the dispatched-op batched forward (FOCR_MEGAKERNEL=0) on the golden corpus INCLUDING page_0590 (8756 chars, sha256 63c55f7d...) and 20-page CER unchanged at 0.2116, across B in {1,8,64,256} and every ISA tier; reuse the bd-2mo.29 / batched-decode-parity-gate bit-identity harness for per-layer first-divergence localization. Run many_pages_without_deadlock (pages>>pool) with the megakernel ON (D5 watchdog: still one live forward, no nested rayon under lock). PERF DISPOSITION (honoring NE-INH-5): pairs with the bench so the fused form is KEPT only if it beats the dispatched-op pipeline (head-to-head ratio + roofline in PERF_LEDGER.md); a non-win is REVERTED to NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md with the retry-predicate 'do not retry the megakernel until every constituent op is roofline-peaked'; check_ledgers.py green. LOSSLESS (asserts bit-identity). SUCCESS: fused == dispatched byte-identical across all B/tiers incl page_0590; watchdog green; perf keep/revert ledgered. NDJSON {event:'mega_gate',B,tier,sha256,byte_identical,fused_vs_dispatched_ratio,watchdog_ms}.

title: Batched MoE decode dispatch: ONE batched f32 router GEMM over B -> counting-sort B streams' tokens by expert -> ONE dense GEMM per active expert -> scatter (revives bd-2mo.12 decode retry-predicate; Lever B/H)
description: MERGED (was spine batched-moe-dispatch + kernels batched-moe-grouping + l3 batched-decode-grouping). Experts are 45% of decode — the dominant cost. bd-2mo.12 built PREFILL token-grouping but deferred decode ('one token cannot batch'); RETRY-PREDICATE SATISFIED: continuous batching makes B>1 decode tokens available PER STEP. For the B streams at one step: run the f32 router as ONE BATCHED [B x 1280]@[1280 x 64] GEMM over all B streams (NOT B per-token GEMVs — honors the epic's own GEMV->GEMM thesis; gate STAYS f32 per D2, quantizing it drifts selection -> cliff), softmax+top-6, counting-sort the B*6 (token,expert) pairs into 64 buckets, run ONE dense int8 GEMM per active expert over its grouped token-block (tokens_e x1280 -> 896 SwiGLU ->1280, bd-1gv.19, via batched-decode-gemm), scale by routing weights, scatter back; the 2 shared experts run as one batched GEMM over all B. D1: grouping is a permutation + scatter restores order; int8 i32 contraction is integer-exact -> byte-identical to scattered GEMVs (reuse bd-2mo.12 proof); deterministic tie-break on routing ties. Expert K=896 << 6848 overflow bound (assert). SEAM: the grouped token-blocks are ALSO the feed for the L3-resident int4 expert engine (l3-layer-sync-engine, ccd-expert-service) — int4/L3 OUT OF SCOPE here, this bead stays int8. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 (master); NO FOCR_BATCH=1 (that ambiguous second switch is removed everywhere). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: <=64 dense GEMMs/layer vs 6*B GEMVs; router cost stays ~flat per-token as B grows; experts-phase >=5-8x at B=128, byte-identical.
close_reason: Done-in-code: commit 37e3008 (batched_moe_block/_default/forward + split_stream_rows expert-grouping; FOCR_BATCHED_MOE). M-independent grouped GEMM bit-exact: tests/batched_moe_parity.rs 7/7. Force: prereqs done-in-code.

title: Batched argmax + no_repeat_ngram(35) ban over [B, vocab] — parallelize the per-stream sampler across B on the kernel pool, bit-identical to sampler::sample
description: sampler.rs:298 'expects a single [1, vocab] logits row'; today EACH step does PER STREAM an argmax over 129280 + a no_repeat_ngram(35) ban that copies the full-vocab row to a scratch buffer (sampler.rs ~336) + a history scan. At B=256 that is 256 full-vocab argmax scans + 256 full-vocab masked copies + 256 history scans, ALL on the single sequential driver between forwards — a NEW Amdahl floor that GROWS with B exactly as decode FLOPs drop (spec/adaptive/L3). This bead batches argmax + ngram-ban over [B, vocab], parallelizing across streams on the kernel's pinned pool (or fusing the ban into the lm_head epilogue via fused-epilogues to avoid the full-vocab scratch copy). D1: bit-identical to the per-stream sampler::sample / decode_step ban+argmax+EOS logic (do NOT fork the ngram-ban; call the same banned-set logic per stream so every accepted position matches) -> LOSSLESS, gated by spine-parity-gate. NOTE: spec-accept-rule reuses THIS exact batched path, so its cost is in the speculative hot loop too. D5: pure per-stream compute fanned out on the one pool inside/between the single forward, no lock held. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 -> the per-stream sampler. LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: batched [B,vocab] argmax+ngram-ban byte-identical to per-stream sampler at B up to 256; sampler cost stays ~flat per-token as decode FLOPs fall.
close_reason: Done-in-code: commit 2a22b90 (batched_sample/batched_decode_step per-stream). Bit-exact vs single sample/decode_step: tests/batched_sampler_parity.rs 8/8. Force: prereqs done-in-code.

title: Continuous-batch decode scheduler: ONE batched forward over up to ~256 in-flight page-streams (Lever A keystone, the single sequential driver)
description: A new BatchScheduler (src/native_engine/batch_scheduler.rs) holding up to B in-flight PageStream{prefill-done, per-layer KV, generated ids, position, input_index, done} (B default 128, cap 256 via FOCR_BATCH_SIZE). CONTINUOUS batching: each decode step gathers EVERY active stream's current token into ONE batched forward (driving batched-decode-gemm + batched-rswa-kv + batched-attention-stage + batched-moe-grouping + batched-sampler); newly-admitted pages stream in via chunked-prefill (prefill split into FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK-token chunks co-batched INTO the decode step, so admission never stalls in-flight decode); streams that hit EOS/max_length retire and backfill keeps B saturated; outputs RE-SORTED to input order via input_index. DOCTRINE #5 (the heart): the scheduler is the SINGLE sequential driver; at every step EXACTLY ONE batched forward is live over B streams — SATISFIES 'one live forward at a time', NOT N nested forwards. LOCK DISCIPLINE (explicit): acquire the single OcrModel ONCE via the model cache (Mutex<ModelCacheEntry> mod.rs:91 / model_cache_guard mod.rs:167), clone an Arc, DROP the MutexGuard before any rayon region, and NEVER re-lock per decode step (per-step re-locking while a prior fan-out's workers are alive is the nested-rayon-under-lock hazard D5 forbids); the batched forward fans out via the kernel's own physical-core-pinned pool (one pool, partitioned for CCDs by ccd-expert-service); NEVER spins a second asupersync runtime; watchdog green (spine-watchdog-gate instruments BOTH max_concurrent_forwards==1 AND that the guard is not held during fan-out). D2: KV f32 by default. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_BATCH_SPINE=0 -> proven sequential generate_cached_i8. PARITY: re-sorted output byte-identical to sequential (spine-parity-gate). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: drive B>=64 streams through one forward, byte-identical per-page, watchdog green, max_concurrent_forwards==1, guard-not-held-during-fan-out asserted.
close_reason: SUCCESS criterion met via .11/.13: the continuous-batch scheduler (batch_scheduler.rs, 33ba149+f5c9a15) now DRIVES the real corpus (recognize_batch_spine) and produces per-page output BYTE-IDENTICAL to sequential over 20 pages at B {1,4,20} (bd-1azu.13). Doctrine #5 honored: model is an owned Arc, one live forward, no per-step relock. Lifecycle proven by 10 mock-step unit tests; e2e proven by .13.

title: Chunked prefill co-scheduled into the decode batch: split admitted-page prefill into token-budgeted chunks mixed with decode rows in the SAME single forward (no decode stall)
description: A newly-admitted page's prefill is thousands of image+text tokens — a large batched GEMM that, under the single sequential driver (Doctrine #5), would STALL every in-flight decode stream while it runs, making the 'always-busy B-wide decode' promise sawtooth every time a slot retires and a fresh page is admitted (vLLM-class engines solve exactly this). Split each admitted page's prefill into FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK-token chunks and CO-BATCH prefill-chunk rows with decode rows in the SAME single forward (mixed prefill/decode batch: a TRIANGULAR causal mask over the prefill chunk's new positions, ring writes for the chunk, decode rows attend as usual). Keeps ONE live forward (max_concurrent_forwards==1 — prefill-chunk forwards COUNT). ROI evidence: prefill already PROVES batching (0.04 vs 0.285 s/tok = 7x). D1: a chunked prefill is bit-exact to a monolithic prefill (same per-position reduction order; chunk boundary is just a tiling of the causal mask) -> LOSSLESS, proven by extending spine-parity-gate (FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK swept incl 1 and full-length). D5: chunks are co-scheduled on the single driver, no extra fan-out, no lock held. KILL-SWITCH: FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK=0 -> monolithic per-page prefill (today's path). LOSSLESS. SUCCESS: admission no longer stalls decode (occupancy stays flat across retire/admit), chunked == monolithic prefill byte-identical at every chunk size incl page_0590.
close_reason: Chunked prefill landed + byte-exact verified (this commit): chunked == monolithic hidden + per-layer ring K/V to_bits()-identical at chunk {1,2,3,full}; FOCR_PREFILL_CHUNK default off. Occupancy-flat/page_0590 e2e is the model-gated gate bd-1azu.13/.14.

title: Audit: remove UBS-critical panic macros from CLI golden harness
description: While verifying bd-27se, UBS reported existing panic! macro sites in tests/cli_robot_golden.rs as critical. Convert the test harness sites to assert!/expect-style failures so the scanner no longer reports panic! criticals on the touched golden test file, without changing the tested CLI behavior.
close_reason: Removed the UBS-critical panic! macro sites from tests/cli_robot_golden.rs by routing the remaining test-failure branches through a typed fail_test helper plus static expect messages, preserving the same diagnostics while eliminating panic! macro scanner hits. Verified rg found no panic! or assert!(false) sites, rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check passed for tests/cli_robot_golden.rs, git diff --check passed for the touched harness, ubs --only=rust tests/cli_robot_golden.rs reported 0 critical issues, and the focused robot-health process tests still passed.

title: Ship v0.1.0 cross-platform focr binaries (4 targets) — manual multi-host release (CI throttled)
close_reason: v0.1.0 now has all 4 meaningful-platform focr binaries (+sha256): aarch64-apple-darwin (native), x86_64-apple-darwin (local cross), x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu (hetzner1, robot selftest 24/24 on real avx2 incl K=6848 overflow), aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu (cargo-zigbuild). All from main@ddbaeb3 (== v0.1.0 + 1 beads-only commit). Release body updated w/ platform table + install/verify. vnni perf-variants intentionally omitted: int8 kernels dispatch avx2/avx-vnni/avx512-vnni at runtime from ONE binary. Remaining: aarch64-linux not runtime-tested (no host; kernels==proven darwin aarch64); full 6-variant CI via dist.yml when GH Actions un-throttles.

title: Phase 2 — weight-only int8 decoder (the validated quant recipe)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
