title: Tests: greedy sampler, no_repeat_ngram(35) window boundaries, EOS strip, determinism, token-exactness
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The sampler is the last gate before output; the ngram window (128/1024) and EOS strip are subtle and mode-dependent. These tests pin L4 token-exactness and the determinism gate.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — argmax + tie-break**: known logits -> expected token; tie -> reference tie-break (lowest index).
2. **Unit — ngram ban (single)**: synthetic token stream that would repeat a 35-gram; assert the banned continuation is set to -inf within the 128-token window and NOT banned outside it; test boundary at 127/128/129.
3. **Unit — ngram ban (multi)**: same at the 1024-token window boundary (1023/1024/1025).
4. **Unit — EOS**: stop on id 1 and strip the trailing EOS; max_length cap stops at 32768.
5. **Unit — all-banned fallback**: if every continuation is banned, behavior matches the recorded reference fallback.
6. **Unit — determinism**: same logits stream twice -> identical tokens.
7. **Integration (model-gated)**: full decode token sequence EXACT vs oracle for a single-image and a multi-image golden doc over the deterministic prefix; determinism gate (same input twice -> byte-identical).

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per step: `{step, mode, argmax, banned_count, eos, token_match}`; aggregate `{token_exact_prefix_len, first_divergence_pos, deterministic}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration token-exact over the deterministic prefix for both modes; determinism gate green (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-sampler**.

comments.author: ScarletCoast
comments.text: Breadcrumb from bd-3q2g: added the standalone unit coverage for no_repeat_ngram_size=35 window boundaries in src/native_engine/sampler.rs (single 127/128/129, multi 1023/1024/1025), all-banned fallback, and boundary determinism. I did not claim/close this blocked feature-test bead; remaining acceptance here is the broader EOS-strip/max_length/logging/oracle integration coverage.

title: Postprocess: EOS strip, <|ref|>/<|det|> tag regex, bbox /999 rescale, markdown/<page> assembly
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The decoded token sequence is detokenized to text, then parsed into the final structured OCR output: Markdown with HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, normalized 0-999 bounding boxes, reading order, and `<page>` separators for multi-page (plan §2.4). This is the L5 end-to-end output that the user/agent consumes and the conformance corpus compares (`focr ocr --json`). Lives in `src/native_engine/postprocess.rs`.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement in `postprocess.rs`:
1. **Detokenize** the token sequence (via the tokenizer, vision/E-P1-tokenizer half) to text; the trailing EOS is already stripped by `P1-sampler` (belt-and-suspenders strip here too).
2. **Layout tag parsing**: parse `<|ref|>...<|/ref|><|det|>...<|/det|>` via regex (plan §2.4, §4.1 postprocess.rs) — `<|ref|>` holds the recognized text/label, `<|det|>` holds the bbox coords. Confirm the exact tag grammar from the spec.
3. **bbox rescale**: coords are normalized 0-999; rescale `coord/999 × W` (x) and `coord/999 × H` (y) to pixel space for the original image dimensions (plan §2.4: 'bbox coords /999 -> ×W/H'). Confirm the coord order (x1,y1,x2,y2) and rounding from spec.
4. **Markdown assembly**: assemble the Markdown (HTML tables, LaTeX preserved verbatim, reading order from the token stream).
5. **Multi-page**: split/join on `<page>` separators for multi-page docs.
6. **Prompt-mode taxonomy (OQ-8)**: whether bboxes are emitted in ALL modes (free OCR vs layout vs grounding vs markdown) is OQ-8 — honor the recorded answer (some modes may emit text-only, no `<|det|>`).

Return a structured `OcrResult` (markdown text + parsed bbox list + per-page split) so `--json` and the markdown human output both derive from it.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Derive both --json and markdown from one structured result** (§7.3): machine and human output never diverge.
- **bbox /999 rescale** is the documented coord contract; a wrong divisor or axis swap silently misplaces every box.
- **Prompt-mode-dependent bbox emission** (OQ-8): do not assume bboxes in every mode.
- **LaTeX/HTML preserved verbatim**: do not 'normalize' formula/table markup — exact-match is the L5 gate.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Regex must handle the DeepSeek glyph specials and malformed/truncated tags gracefully (a truncated `<|det|>` at max_length must not panic).
- bbox coord order + rounding must match the reference exactly (OQ-8 / spec).
- `<page>` handling for multi-page must align with the multi-image decode (cross-page dependence, OQ-13, is an attention concern; the output is still per-page-segmented).
- Determinism: pure transform of the token sequence.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L5 end-to-end: `focr ocr --json` output exact-match vs the frozen oracle reference (`tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json`) where deterministic; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the documented budget.
- bbox coords rescale correctly (round-trip a known normalized box to pixels and back).
- Tag parsing handles malformed/truncated input without panic.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-postprocess-tests`. Unit: tag-regex on synthetic `<|ref|>/<|det|>` strings (incl. malformed/truncated); bbox /999 rescale for known W/H; markdown/<page> assembly; mode-dependent bbox emission (OQ-8). Integration: golden-corpus `--json` exact-match + CER/TEDS with structured diff logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1**, **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (tag grammar + bbox coord contract), **P1-sampler** (provides the token sequence), and **OQ-8** (prompt-mode taxonomy + whether bboxes are emitted per mode). Detokenization uses the tokenizer (E-P1 vision/tokenizer half).

title: Tests: postprocess tag parsing, bbox /999 rescale, markdown assembly, L5 golden exact-match
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Postprocess produces the user-facing output and the L5 conformance artifact; tag/bbox/markdown bugs are visible to the end user. These tests pin the structured output.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit — tag regex**: parse well-formed `<|ref|>text<|/ref|><|det|>coords<|/det|>`; malformed/truncated tags do not panic and degrade gracefully.
2. **Unit — bbox rescale**: a normalized box (e.g. `[100,200,800,900]` /999) rescales to pixels for known (W,H); axis order + rounding match the spec.
3. **Unit — markdown/<page>**: assembly preserves LaTeX/HTML verbatim; multi-page splits on `<page>`.
4. **Unit — mode-dependent emission (OQ-8)**: a mode that emits text-only produces no bboxes; a grounding mode emits bboxes.
5. **Integration (model-gated)**: golden-corpus `focr ocr --json` exact-match vs frozen oracle reference (canonicalized: strip timing, sort bbox); aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within budget.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per doc: `{doc, cer, teds, exact_match, bbox_count, first_diff}`; aggregate `{mean_cer, exact_match_rate}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Units pass; integration exact-match where deterministic and CER/TEDS within budget (skips green without weights).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-postprocess**.

title: Decoder orchestration: 12-layer loop wiring (embed -> [RMSNorm->R-SWA->residual->RMSNorm->MLP/MoE->residual]×12 -> final norm -> lm_head)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the glue that assembles the per-op kernels into the full DeepSeek-V2 MoE decoder forward (`src/native_engine/decoder.rs`), driving both prefill (build reference KV from visual+prompt) and the AR decode loop. It is the f32-parity integration point: it must reproduce the reference's exact layer structure, residual connections, and the dense(layer0)/MoE(layers1-11) split. It consumes the vision features (via the connector) and produces logits for the sampler.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the 12-layer decoder loop:
- **Input**: the fused embedding stream (token embeds via `P1-token-embed` with vision embeds masked-scattered in by the connector — vision half).
- **Per layer i (0..11)**: `h = h + RSWA_attn( RMSNorm_input(h) )` (RoPE applied to Q/K inside attention); then `h = h + MLP_i( RMSNorm_post_attn(h) )` where `MLP_0` = dense (`P1-dense-mlp0`) and `MLP_{1..11}` = MoE (`P1-moe-router` + `P1-moe-experts`). Confirm the exact residual + pre/post-norm placement (DeepseekV2: pre-norm, residual around attn and around MLP) from the spec.
- **Attention is uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers** (OQ-2, incl. layer 0; first_k_dense_replace affects ONLY the MLP). Honor the recorded OQ-2 answer.
- **Prefill vs decode**: prefill builds the reference KV block (visual+prompt) for all layers; decode runs one token at a time through the ring + reference block. Pre-allocate all per-forward buffers once and reuse across pages (§6.12, zero per-token allocation).
- **Final**: `RMSNorm_final(h)` -> `lm_head` (`P1-lmhead`) -> logits -> sampler (`P1-sampler`).
- **Concurrency**: the forward fans out across cores via the kernel's own rayon pool; the outer page/document loop is SEQUENTIAL (§6.5, AGENTS.md doctrine 5: never nest rayon under a lock, exactly one live forward).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **dense layer-0 / MoE layers-1-11 split** (first_k_dense_replace=1): the loop must branch the MLP by layer index; attention is uniform R-SWA (OQ-2).
- **Pre-norm + residual** per DeepseekV2: a wrong norm/residual placement silently shifts every layer.
- **Pre-allocate + reuse buffers** (§6.12): no per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop.
- **Sequential outer loop, internal fan-out** (doctrine 5): the deadlock-proof template; a `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (owned by E-TEST) guards regression.
- **f32 v1** (Phase 1): no quant, no hand-SIMD; correctness first.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Buffer aliasing between the residual stream and the norm scratch is a subtle bug; keep distinct buffers or prove the in-place op is safe.
- The KV-cap INVARIANT (`L·(m+128)`) and 'exactly one live forward' are gauntlet e-process invariants (§8.5) — assert them.
- Cancellation: thread a `checkpoint()` into the per-decode-step loop so Ctrl+C / per-page timeout aborts cleanly (§3.3) — cooperative cancellation, token goes INTO the closure.
- Determinism: fixed layer/reduction order -> identical hidden states and logits.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 per-layer parity: every decoder-layer hidden state vs oracle dumps, cosine >= 0.9999 in f32; L3 logits within measured tolerance; L4 tokens exact over the deterministic prefix; L5 end-to-end CER ~= reference.
- Determinism gate green; KV-cap invariant holds; no per-token allocation in the hot loop (verified).
- No `[OPEN]` blocks any wired kernel (OQ-1,2,3,4,5,13,18 resolved).

## TESTS REQUIRED
See `P1-decoder-loop-tests` (the end-to-end decoder integration test). Plus this bead is the convergence point for the L2-L5 parity ladder across the decoder; it depends on every per-op test passing first.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1**, **P1-spec-decoder-extract** (layer structure/residual placement), and ALL the per-op decoder beads it wires: **P1-token-embed**, **P1-rmsnorm**, **P1-rope**, **P1-rswa-attn**, **P1-moe-router**, **P1-moe-experts**, **P1-dense-mlp0**, **P1-lmhead**, plus **OQ-2** (uniform R-SWA across layers). Consumes the connector/masked-scatter fused embedding stream from the vision half of E-P1.

title: Tests: full decoder integration L2-L5 parity ladder + determinism + KV-cap invariant + e2e CER
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the decoder's end-to-end parity gate — the convergence test that proves the whole decoder+output half matches the bf16 reference within f32 tolerance on the golden corpus. It exercises the full L2-L5 ladder with the comprehensive structured logging the project mandates.

## WHAT TO DO
Add a `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` decoder path (model-gated; skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights, prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`):
1. **L2 per-layer**: every decoder-layer hidden state vs oracle `.npy` dumps, cosine >= 0.9999; max-abs-diff ledgered.
2. **L3 logits**: per-token logits within the MEASURED L3 tolerance; argmax matches over the deterministic prefix.
3. **L4 tokens**: decoded token sequence EXACT vs oracle over the deterministic prefix (per §8.2 nondeterminism floor — establish the oracle's own nondeterminism envelope first).
4. **L5 end-to-end**: `focr ocr --json` text + bbox vs frozen oracle reference; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the documented budget.
5. **Determinism gate**: same image+args twice -> byte-identical output.
6. **KV-cap invariant**: assert KV per layer never exceeds `L·(m+128)` over the run.
7. **No-realloc**: assert zero per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop (instrument the allocator or a counter).

Run over the per-regime corpus {sparse page, dense page, 10-page, 40-page} so cross-page (OQ-13) behavior is exercised, and over both single- and multi-image modes (ngram window 128/1024).

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per layer/token/doc: `{doc, regime, mode, layer, token_pos, cosine, max_abs_diff, argmax_match, token_match, kv_len}`; aggregate `{min_layer_cosine, l3_tol, token_exact_prefix_len, first_divergence_pos, mean_cer, teds, deterministic, max_kv_len}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 min cosine >= 0.9999; L3 within measured tolerance + argmax match; L4 token-exact over the deterministic prefix; L5 CER/TEDS within budget; determinism green; KV-cap invariant holds; no per-token allocation. (Skips green without weights.)

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P1-decoder-loop** (the integrated forward), and on **P1-sampler** + **P1-postprocess** (needed for the L4/L5 end-to-end output). This is the decoder half's exit-gate test for the E-P1 epic.

title: Multi-page document orchestration (infer_multi equivalent): N-image assembly, cross-page reference KV, <page> separators, per-page streaming
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is THE headline 'Unlimited' feature and it currently has NO implementation bead. Plan §2.1: *"that, not arbitrary input resolution, is what 'Unlimited' means: constant-KV long-output decoding of dozens of pages in one 32K pass."* §7.2 lists the `focr ocr` multi-page intent (reference prompt `'<image>Multi page parsing.'`) and §4.2 mandates streaming per-page output. The reference exposes `infer_multi(...)` which **concatenates pages at `<image>` with a dummy crop tensor to force the no-crop (Base) branch** (§2.4). `P1-decoder-loop` builds exactly ONE single-image forward; `P5-streaming-polish` only polishes the mpsc/std::thread transport; nothing assembles N page-images into one cross-page decode pass. Without this bead the project cannot parse a multi-page document at all — only single images.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement multi-page document orchestration in `src/native_engine/` (and the `OcrEngine` facade), replicating the reference `infer_multi(...)` semantics extracted in the truth pack (EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md):
1. **Input assembly**: accept an ordered list of page images; preprocess each in **Base mode** (1024×1024, `crop_mode=false` — multi-page forces no-crop, §2.4) into its 256 image-feature + structural token block; concatenate all pages' vision+prompt prefixes into ONE prompt at the `<image>` placeholders (the `infer_multi` concat-at-`<image>` + dummy-crop-tensor branch). Honor the exact per-page token-class census (OQ-18) so the assembled reference length `m` is exact.
2. **Cross-page reference KV (OQ-13, load-bearing)**: in a single 32K pass, **all** pages' visual+prompt prefixes form ONE frozen reference block — page N can attend to pages 1..N-1 (§8.3). Build the prefill reference KV across the full multi-page prefix (NOT per-page-independent), then run the AR decode loop once over the whole document. Honor the recorded OQ-13 answer for the exact reference-block span; the buffer is the worst-case `m` (≈32768−128) pre-allocated per layer (§6.4).
3. **ngram_window per mode (OQ-18)**: the `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` sliding window is **1024 for multi-image** (vs 128 single-image) — wire the multi-image window into the sampler (`P1-sampler` exposes the window parameter; this bead selects 1024).
4. **`<page>` separators (OQ-8)**: emit `<page>` separators between pages in the assembled markdown output per the reference's multi-page output contract.
5. **Per-page streaming**: stream each page's decoded text/bbox to the robot/NDJSON consumer as it completes (the `page` event, §7.3), via the bounded `sync_channel` + `std::thread` worker (the transport polished later by P5-streaming-polish). The document loop that FEEDS pages stays SEQUENTIAL; the single multi-page forward fans out internally (§6.5 — never concurrent forwards).
6. **32K cap handling**: if the assembled multi-page prefix + generation would exceed `max_position_embeddings=32768`, fail with an actionable error (or document the reference's truncation behavior, confirmed from source) — do NOT silently corrupt.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Cross-page DEPENDENT, not a concatenation of independent single-page parses** (§8.3, OQ-13): the metamorphic property is the OPPOSITE of 'multi-page = sum of single-page' — changing page order/earlier content MAY change later-page output. The orchestrator must therefore build one shared reference block, never N independent forwards.
- **Base mode forced for multi-page** (the dummy-crop-tensor branch): Gundam tiling is single-page-only.
- **Worst-case `m` pre-allocated, reused across documents** (§6.12): zero per-page allocation in the hot loop.
- **Sequential page-feed, single internal fan-out** (§6.5, doctrine 5): no page-level `par_iter`; exactly one live forward.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **OQ-13 is a hard gate**: if the reference block does NOT span all pages, the design changes fundamentally — do not finalize the reference-KV span until OQ-13 is answered from `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` + `infer_multi` source.
- Page-ordering and the exact `<image>` concat order are load-bearing (wrong order silently corrupts cross-page attention).
- The dummy-crop tensor that forces the no-crop branch must match the reference exactly.
- ngram_window=1024 multi vs 128 single is a first-class generation semantic, not a CLI flag — a wrong window changes decoded tokens.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Multi-page parse of a frozen 2-page / 10-page / 40-page golden document reproduces the oracle's `infer_multi` output: decoded text + `<page>` separators exact where deterministic; aggregate CER within budget.
- Cross-page dependence holds (page N's output reflects pages 1..N-1 per OQ-13).
- KV-cap invariant: assembled reference `m` + 128-ring never exceeds `L·(m+128)` and never exceeds the 32K context.
- Per-page streaming emits one `page` event per page in order; deterministic under greedy.
- Zero per-page allocation in the hot loop.

## TESTS REQUIRED
See P1-r1-multipage-tests for the dedicated multi-page integration + metamorphic gate. Unit-level here: input-assembly token-class census exactness per page; `<image>` concat order; dummy-crop branch selection; ngram_window=1024 selection; `<page>` separator emission. All NDJSON-logged: `{doc, n_pages, page_idx, assembled_m, ngram_window, kv_len, page_tokens, streamed_order}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Blocked by E-PM1 (oracle `infer_multi` fixtures + the resolved OQ-13/OQ-18/OQ-8 answers), OQ-13 (cross-page span — the load-bearing question), OQ-18 (per-mode token census + ngram window), OQ-8 (`<page>`/multi-page output contract), P1-decoder-loop (the single-page forward this generalizes), P1-connector-fusion (per-page assembly), P1-sampler (the windowed ngram processor), P1-postprocess (markdown/`<page>` assembly).

title: Tests: multi-page orchestration — cross-page dependence (OQ-13), <page> assembly, ngram-window-1024, per-page streaming order, L5 multi-page CER
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Multi-page is the model's headline feature and its correctness is subtle (cross-page dependence under R-SWA, OQ-13). It needs a dedicated gate beyond the single-image decoder test (`P1-decoder-loop-tests`). A wrong reference-block span or page-concat order produces plausible-but-wrong output that single-page tests will not catch.

## WHAT TO DO
Add a model-gated `tests/native_engine_multipage.rs` (skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`):
1. **L5 multi-page parity**: 2-page / 10-page / 40-page golden documents → `focr ocr --json` (multi-page mode) text + `<page>` separators + bbox vs the frozen oracle `infer_multi` reference; aggregate CER/TEDS within the documented budget; graceful long-horizon degradation expected (edit distance grows with page count, §2.5).
2. **Cross-page dependence (OQ-13)**: assert the CORRECT metamorphic property per §8.3 — `multi_page_parse(pages) != concat(single_page_parse(p) for p in pages)` where the reference block spans all pages; AND `parse(reorder(pages))` MAY differ from `parse(pages)` (page order matters). Do NOT assert the false 'concat = sum' property.
3. **`<page>` separator structure**: exactly N−1 (or N) separators for N pages, in order.
4. **ngram_window=1024 multi vs 128 single**: assert the multi-image path uses the 1024 window (a repeated 35-gram across a >128-token but <1024-token span is blocked in multi but not single, per the reference).
5. **Per-page streaming order**: assert `page` events arrive in document order, one per page, under the bounded channel.
6. **KV-cap invariant**: assembled `m` + 128-ring ≤ `L·(m+128)` and ≤ 32K over the full run.
7. **Determinism**: same multi-page input twice → byte-identical output under greedy.

## STRUCTURED LOGGING
NDJSON per page/doc: `{doc, n_pages, page_idx, assembled_m, ngram_window, kv_len, page_cer, streamed_order, cross_page_dependence_observed}`; aggregate `{doc_cer, teds, n_page_separators, deterministic, max_kv_len, oq13_property_held}`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Multi-page CER within budget on 2/10/40-page corpora; `<page>` structure exact; ngram-window-1024 behavior verified; streaming in order; cross-page-dependence property (OQ-13) held; KV-cap invariant holds; deterministic. Skips green without weights.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Blocked by P1-r1-multipage-orchestration (the implementation under test), VERIFY-metamorphic-suite (shares the cross-page-dependence metamorphic harness — this test instantiates the multi-page case), TEST-log-emitter (the NDJSON logging module).

title: Phase-1 single-image end-to-end pipeline assembly: wire preprocess+tokenize → vision → connector → decoder → sampler → postprocess into OcrEngine::recognize / focr ocr
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 1 has per-half integration (`P1-decoder-loop` wires the decoder; `P1-vision-parity-harness` is a TEST of the vision half), but **no bead implements the actual `OcrEngine::recognize()` body** that chains the full pipeline into the real `focr ocr <image>` command. The plan §4.2 defines an explicit pipeline: `Decode/Load image → Preprocess → Tokenize prompt → Vision encode → Connector → Prefill → Decode loop → Postprocess → Emit`, each a `PipelineStage` with a per-stage env-overridable budget (`FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS`) and a `checkpoint()` cancellation boundary (§4.2, §6.5). `P0-engine-runtime` wires the runtime/budget WRAPPER with a no-op `op`; `P0-stubs-convert-ocr-doctor` keeps `focr ocr` returning NotImplemented. This bead replaces the NotImplemented with the real single-image forward — the integration glue that closes E-P1 and is the direct prerequisite for the L5/end-to-end gate.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the real single-image `focr ocr` pipeline in `src/orchestrator.rs` + `OcrEngine::recognize()` (and `native_engine/mod.rs` `OcrModel`):
1. **PipelineStage enum + per-stage budget + checkpoint** (§4.2): drive the stages `LoadImage → Preprocess(resize/pad/normalize/tile) → Tokenize → VisionEncode(SAM→bridge→CLIP→concat→project) → Connector(structural tokens + masked-scatter) → Prefill(build reference KV: visual+prompt) → Decode(R-SWA + MoE AR loop) → Postprocess(EOS/ngram/tag/bbox/markdown) → Emit`. Each stage runs via `spawn_blocking` under `asupersync::time::timeout(wall_now(), budget, ...)` with `FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS` override and a `checkpoint()` so Ctrl+C / per-stage timeout aborts at the next boundary (§3.3, §4.2).
2. **Wire the halves**: feed the connector-assembled `inputs_embeds` (vision half: `P1-vision-parity-harness` chain output) into the decoder (`P1-decoder-loop`) → sampler → postprocess → return the markdown/JSON `OcrResult`.
3. **Sync facade**: `OcrEngine::recognize(&self, image_path, args) -> OcrResult` stays SYNCHRONOUS (G6) — internally `runtime.handle().spawn` + `block_on`. The single `OcrModel` lives behind the `Arc`+`Weak` cache; the outer call is sequential, the forward fans out internally (§6.5).
4. **Stage timing → robot events**: each stage emits a `stage` NDJSON event (name, seq, elapsed, budget) per §7.3.
5. Map stage failures to the stable exit codes (input decode → 4, budget/timeout → 5, cancelled → 6) via `error.rs`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **PipelineStage is the spine** the budgets/cancellation/robot-events all hang on — building it once here means every later phase (int8/int4/SIMD) swaps kernels under the same orchestration without re-plumbing.
- **Sync facade over owned runtime** (§3.3, G6): no `#[asupersync::main]`; the runtime never spans the process.
- **Sequential outer, internal fan-out** (§6.5): the deadlock-proof template.
- **f32 v1**: this assembles the f32 kernels; quant phases reuse the same assembly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Buffer ownership across stages must avoid copies where the next stage can consume in place (§6.12 buffer reuse).
- Cancellation token must go INTO the `spawn_blocking` closure (cooperative; `spawn_blocking` keeps running on drop).
- The Base-vs-Gundam tile decision (single image) routes here from `--crop-mode`; the multi-page document path is a SEPARATE orchestrator (P1-r1-multipage-orchestration).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr ocr <golden image> --json` runs the full f32 forward end-to-end and reproduces the oracle reference (the L5 gate, VERIFY-ladder-l5, runs against this).
- Per-stage `stage` events emitted with elapsed/budget; per-stage budget override + cancellation work (a timeout returns exit 5, Ctrl+C returns exit 6).
- Deterministic under greedy (same image+args → byte-identical).
- No NotImplemented from the engine on the happy path.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Integration: `focr ocr` happy path end-to-end (covered by VERIFY-ladder-l5 + TEST-e2e-cli-script); per-stage budget override + cancellation boundary unit tests (a stage that exceeds its budget aborts at exit 5; a token injected into the cancellation closure aborts at exit 6). NDJSON-logged: `{stage, seq, elapsed_ms, budget_ms, cancelled, exit_code}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Blocked by P0-engine-runtime (the runtime/budget wrapper this fills in), P1-vision-parity-harness (the proven vision-half chain it consumes), P1-decoder-loop (the decoder forward), P1-postprocess (the Emit stage), P1-preprocess-tiling (Base/Gundam single-image routing).

title: SAM MLPBlock GELU activation kernel (distinct from CLIP quick_gelu and LLM SiLU)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §2.7 names THREE distinct activations that MUST be kept separate: **SiLU** (LLM MLP/experts, owned by P1-silu), **quick_gelu** (CLIP FFN, owned by P1-clip-tower), and **GELU** (the SAM-ViT-B `MLPBlock` FFN). The SAM GELU is currently only mentioned inline in P1-sam-window-global-attn ('MLPBlock (GELU)') with NO dedicated kernel, NO variant pin, and NO test that locks SAM-GELU ≠ CLIP-quick_gelu ≠ SiLU. This is a classic silent-drift trap: PyTorch `nn.GELU()` defaults to the EXACT erf form, but many ViT/SAM lineages use the tanh ('gelu_new'/'gelu_pytorch_tanh') approximation — picking the wrong one fuzzes every SAM block output and surfaces only as a fuzzy L2 cosine, never a crash. frankentorch supplies a `gelu` op (plan §4.3: 'GELU/SiLU EXISTS, reuse'); this bead wires it for SAM and PINS the exact variant from source.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement / wire the SAM `MLPBlock` activation in `src/native_engine/vision_sam.rs`:
- Each of the 12 SAM transformer blocks has an MLPBlock FFN: `Linear(768→mlp_dim) → GELU → Linear(mlp_dim→768)`. Read the exact `mlp_ratio`/`mlp_dim` (SAM-ViT-B default mlp_ratio=4 → 3072) AND the exact GELU variant from the pinned `deepencoder.py` SAM block — record whether it is `nn.GELU()` (erf-exact: `0.5*x*(1+erf(x/sqrt(2)))`) or the tanh approximation `0.5*x*(1+tanh(sqrt(2/pi)*(x+0.044715*x^3)))`. Use the recorded variant; do NOT assume.
- Reuse frankentorch's `gelu` tensor op for the chosen variant; if the pinned variant is the erf-exact form and frankentorch ships only tanh-approx (or vice-versa), implement the matching small kernel (faithful scalar/autovectorized, NE-INH-1: do NOT hand-SIMD).
Transcribed facts: SAM-ViT-B width 768, 12 layers; MLPBlock = Linear→GELU→Linear; SAM uses standard GELU (NOT quick_gelu, NOT SiLU) per plan §2.7 activation table.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Three activation families stay separate** (§2.7): a single shared 'gelu' helper that silently serves CLIP (quick_gelu, x*sigmoid(1.702x)) would corrupt SAM. Keep vision-family (SAM-GELU, CLIP-quick_gelu) and text-family (SiLU) kernels distinct and individually tested.
- **Pin the erf-vs-tanh variant from source FIRST** — the two differ by up to ~1e-3 per element, enough to drift L2 below 0.9999 over 12 blocks. No kernel ships against an unread activation.
- **f32, vision high-precision** (validated recipe; ViT post-GELU activations have severe outliers).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- erf-exact vs tanh-approx is the silent bug; the unit test must lock the variant against the oracle's SAM-FFN intermediate.
- mlp_dim must be read, not assumed (mlp_ratio=4 is the SAM default but confirm).
- This activation feeds the residual add inside each SAM block — wrong activation compounds across all 12 blocks.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1: SAM-GELU output cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs the oracle's SAM MLPBlock FFN intermediate (where the oracle dumps it), max-abs-diff ledgered.
- The chosen GELU variant matches the recorded `deepencoder.py` answer; a unit test proves SAM-GELU(x) ≠ quick_gelu(x) ≠ silu(x) on a shared input (so a mis-wire to the wrong family fails loudly).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: SAM-GELU vs the recorded reference formula (erf-exact OR tanh-approx) over a value sweep {-6..6} incl. 0 (gelu(0)=0), large +x (~x), large -x (~0); assert it differs from quick_gelu and silu on the same input (family-separation lock); determinism.
- Integration (model-gated): per-SAM-block FFN intermediate cosine vs oracle dump; NDJSON `{block, variant, cosine, max_abs_diff}`; aggregate min cosine. Folds into P1-sam-tests as an additional per-block FFN checkpoint.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on E-PM1 (the pinned `deepencoder.py` SAM block + the recorded GELU variant + oracle SAM-FFN fixture) and P1-sam-patch-embed (provides the patch grid the SAM blocks consume). Consumed by P1-sam-window-global-attn (the MLPBlock runs inside each SAM block) and tested via P1-sam-tests.

title: Base vs Gundam tiling: dynamic_preprocess / find_closest_aspect_ratio
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Unlimited-OCR retains TWO resolution modes (DeepEncoder natively had 5). Tiling decides how many image tensors and how many tokens each page produces — which drives ALL downstream KV/buffer sizing and the R-SWA reference block `m`. Wrong tiling = wrong token count = wrong everything. Part of the L0 exact gate (tile geometry must match).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/preprocess/tile.rs`:
- **Base mode**: 1024×1024, `crop_mode=false` — multi-page/PDF; **256 visual (image-feature) tokens/page** (16×16 compressed grid).
- **Gundam mode**: `base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `crop_mode=true` — dynamic-resolution tiling for a single page; a global padded 1024 view (`global_view_pos='head'`) PLUS 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()` / `find_closest_aspect_ratio()` over `i*j ∈ [min_num, max_num]`; each 640 crop ≈ **100 tokens**. `tile_tag='2D'`, `candidate_resolutions=[[1024,1024]]`.
Transcribed `infer()` defaults: `base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True`.
The token CLASS census (OQ-18): a 1024 view yields **256 image-feature tokens (16×16) PLUS structural tokens** → placeholder structure `(16 + 1)·16 + 1 = 273` (16 image tokens + 1 image_newline per row × 16 rows + 1 view_seperator).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Token count is deterministic but it is NOT just 256 — count every token CLASS.** This bead must emit, per mode/aspect-ratio, the exact (image-feature, image_newline, view_seperator) counts consistent with the OQ-18 machine-readable census, so buffer sizing downstream is exact.
- Resolve OQ-7/OQ-18: `dynamic_preprocess` `min_num`/`max_num` defaults (reported 2..32) and exact Gundam multi-tile token totals per aspect ratio MUST come from `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`, not be guessed.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- `find_closest_aspect_ratio` tie-breaking and the candidate (i,j) enumeration order must match the reference exactly.
- The global-view-then-crops ordering (`global_view_pos='head'`) is load-bearing for the connector assembly downstream.
- A wrong min_num/max_num bound changes the number of crops → changes token totals → breaks every buffer size.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- For each mode and a battery of aspect ratios, the produced tile set (count, sizes, order) and the per-mode token-class census match the OQ-18 census exactly.
- Base mode yields exactly 256 image-feature + 16 image_newline + 1 view_seperator = 273 placeholder slots at 1024.
- Tile geometry is bit-exact vs the oracle (part of L0).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: Base mode square; Gundam mode for several aspect ratios; min/max crop bound edges; verify tile count + sizes + order.
- See P1-preprocess-tests for the differential L0 tiling-geometry gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-18 (full per-mode token census + ngram window) and OQ-7 (Gundam token count per aspect ratio + dynamic_preprocess bounds), E-PM1 (pinned `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`), and P1-preprocess-decode-normalize (tiling consumes decode/resize/pad/normalize).

Also made CropGrid::blocks and placeholder_token_count saturating so malformed in-memory public structs cannot panic on debug overflow. Added regressions preprocess_rejects_invalid_mode_sizes and placeholder_count_saturates_for_malformed_grid. title: Preprocess + tiling L0 parity test bead (exact tensor + tile geometry)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
L0 preprocessing must be EXACT — any pixel or geometry mismatch silently changes OCR output. This test bead is the L0 gate for the whole ingest front end.

## WHAT TO DO
Build a differential L0 harness (`tests/preprocess_parity.rs`) comparing our preprocessed/tiled tensors against the oracle's preprocessed tensors and tile geometry (from E-PM1 fixtures):
- Exact normalized-tensor comparison (gray-127 pad, [-1,1] normalize, RGB CHW).
- Exact tile set comparison (Base + several Gundam aspect ratios): tile count, sizes, order.
- Token-class census assertion per mode (image-feature/image_newline/view_seperator) vs the OQ-18 census.
Emit structured JSON-line logs: per case {mode, in_w, in_h, n_tiles, tensor_max_abs_diff, geometry_match, census_match, pass}.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Metamorphic checks alongside differential: identity-resize invariance (an already-correct-size image is unchanged); padding a page with whitespace must not change recognized geometry classes.
- Fixtures carry PROVENANCE.md (processor version, command).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- This test runs without the 6.67 GB weights (only needs the processor fixtures) — keep it always-on.
- Watch the resize-filter exactness: a 1-ULP-per-pixel resize drift fails L0 and must be diagnosed, not tolerated.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Tensor exact (or within the oracle's documented preprocessing nondeterminism floor, which for deterministic resize is zero).
- Tile geometry + token-class census exact for all cases.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead (differential + metamorphic L0 gate).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-preprocess-decode-normalize + P1-preprocess-tiling (under test) and E-PM1 (oracle preprocessed-tensor + geometry fixtures, OQ-18 census).

title: f32 bicubic position-embedding interpolation (for tiled / non-1024 inputs)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
SAM-ViT-B has a learnable absolute `pos_embed (1,64,64,768)`. For non-1024 / tiled (Gundam) inputs the grid size differs from 64×64, so the pos-embed must be **bicubic-interpolated** to the target grid before being added to patch embeddings. This is a known GAP: frankentorch ships only an f64 autograd `interpolate` in ft-api — we BUILD an f32 bicubic.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement an f32 bicubic 2D interpolation kernel (in `vision_sam.rs` or a small interp helper) that maps `pos_embed` from its native 64×64×768 grid to the target H×W×768 grid for the current tile, matching the reference's interpolation (almost certainly `F.interpolate(..., mode='bicubic', align_corners=False)` — CONFIRM exact mode/align_corners/antialias from `deepencoder.py`).
Transcribed facts: `pos_embed` shape `(1,64,64,768)`, bicubic-interpolated for non-1024 / tiled inputs.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **f32, not f64** — we never materialize f64 in the forward; match the reference's f32 (or downcast bf16→f32) numerics.
- **Match align_corners and the bicubic kernel coefficient (a=-0.75 is PyTorch's default)** exactly — these change every interpolated value.
- The windowed-block pos-embed scheme is part of OQ-15; coordinate this kernel with the SAM window-attention bead so the per-window pos-embed slicing matches the reference.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A wrong align_corners or cubic coefficient produces a fuzzy L2 cosine that's hard to localize — pin it from source.
- For Base 1024 mode the grid is exactly 64×64 → interpolation is identity; ensure the identity path is exact (no spurious resampling).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs the oracle's interpolated pos-embed for the target grids used by Base and the Gundam tile sizes.
- Identity case (64×64) is exactly bit-equal to the stored pos_embed.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: identity (64×64) exactness; upsample to a larger grid; downsample to a smaller grid; compare against a known-good reference grid.
- See P1-bicubic-posembed-tests for the differential L1 gate vs the oracle.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on E-PM1 (exact interpolation mode/align_corners from `deepencoder.py` + oracle interpolated-pos-embed fixture) and P1-preprocess-tiling (the target grid sizes come from the tiling decision).

title: Bicubic pos-embed interpolation L1 parity test bead
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The bicubic pos-embed interp is a silent-corruption risk (wrong align_corners/coefficient → fuzzy features). This bead proves it matches the oracle.

## WHAT TO DO
Differential L1 test (`tests/bicubic_posembed_parity.rs`) comparing our interpolated pos-embed against the oracle's, for the identity grid and each non-1024 / Gundam target grid. Structured JSON-line logging: per grid {src_hw, dst_hw, cosine, max_abs_diff, pass}.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Include the identity (64×64) exactness case explicitly — it catches a spurious resample bug.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Cosine ≥ 0.9999 for all non-identity grids; bit-exact for the identity grid.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-bicubic-posembed (under test) and E-PM1 (oracle interpolated-pos-embed fixtures).

title: SAM-ViT-B patch-embed Conv2d (k16 s16, 1024→64×64=4096 tokens, width 768)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The SAM patch-embed is the first vision kernel: a non-overlapping (k=stride) Conv2d that tiles a 1024×1024 image into a 64×64 grid of 768-dim tokens (4096 tokens). Non-overlapping conv means im2col is cheap and exact → a single big GEMM. frankentorch supplies `conv2d_forward_f32` / `conv2d_im2col_f32` — REUSE, hardcoding the fixed shape.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the SAM patch-embed in `src/native_engine/vision_sam.rs`:
- `Conv2d` k=16 s=16: input 1024×1024×3 → output 64×64×768 = **4096 tokens, width 768**.
- Weights: `model.sam_model.patch_embed.proj.{weight,bias}` (internal `vision.sam.patch_embed`).
- Add the (bicubic-interpolated where needed) learnable absolute `pos_embed (1,64,64,768)` to the patch grid.
Transcribed facts: SAM-ViT-B width 768, 12 layers, 12 heads; PatchEmbed Conv2d k=16 s=16; 1024×1024 → 64×64 = 4096 tokens, width 768.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Keep f32 (vision stays high-precision in all phases)** — this is the validated recipe; ViT post-LN/post-GELU activations have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant.
- Non-overlapping → exact im2col → one big GEMM (ideal future SMMLA/VNNI target, but f32 now).
- pos_embed addition order (add after conv, before blocks) must match the reference.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The bias add and the channel/spatial layout (NCHW vs NHWC, the SAM tower uses NHWC token layout 64×64×768) must match the reference exactly.
- Confirm whether pos_embed is added before or after any pre-block norm.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs the oracle post-patch-embed activation (the 64×64×768 grid + pos_embed).
- Shapes exact: 4096 tokens × 768.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: conv output shape; bias correctness; pos_embed addition; identity-pos-embed (1024) path.
- See P1-sam-tests for the differential L1/L2 gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on E-PM1 (oracle post-patch-embed fixture + weight shapes), P1-preprocess-decode-normalize (provides the normalized input tensor), P1-bicubic-posembed (pos_embed interpolation for non-1024 grids).

title: SAM window attention (window=14, OQ-15) + global attention at blocks 2/5/8/11
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
SAM-ViT-B's 12 transformer blocks use **window attention for most blocks** (processing the dense uncompressed tokens) and **global attention only at blocks [2,5,8,11]** (config-confirmed). Window attention keeps activations low for high-res. This is the centerpiece of vision parity risk: a wrong window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme silently corrupts features (shows only as a fuzzy L2 cosine). frankentorch's `sdpa_forward_f32` is REUSED for the SDPA core; we BUILD the windowed partition/mask on top.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement in `vision_sam.rs`:
- 12 SAM transformer blocks, width 768, 12 heads. Each block: (windowed or global) self-attention + MLPBlock (GELU).
- **Window attention** (window=14 — [OPEN OQ-15], confirm from `deepencoder.py`): partition the 64×64 token grid into 14×14 windows (with padding to a multiple of 14 as the reference does), run local SDPA per window, un-partition. Apply the windowed-block position-embedding scheme per OQ-15.
- **Global attention** at blocks {2,5,8,11}: full SDPA over all 4096 tokens (use `sdpa_forward_f32`).
- Q/K/V/O projections, the SAM relative/absolute pos-embed used inside attention per OQ-15.
Transcribed facts: `global_attn_indexes [2,5,8,11]`; window=14 is OQ-15 (inherited from SAM/DeepSeek-OCR lineage, NOT config-verified — only global_attn_indexes is config-confirmed).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Resolve OQ-15 FIRST**: window size AND the windowed-block pos-embed scheme from `deepencoder.py` line-by-line. No kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN].
- Window partition padding and the un-partition reconstruction must match SAM exactly (the pad/crop of the final partial window is a classic source of drift).
- f32 SDPA (vision stays high precision).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A wrong window=14 (vs e.g. 16) or wrong global-block set silently fuzzes L2 — pin from source.
- Whether SAM uses relative position bias inside attention (decomposed rel-pos, common in SAM) is part of OQ-15 — must be read, not assumed.
- Padding the 64×64 grid to a multiple of the window then cropping back: the crop must restore exactly 64×64.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 per block output (windowed and global) vs oracle.
- L2 cosine ≥ 0.9999 for the full SAM-tower output before the neck.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: window partition/un-partition round-trip (recovers the grid); a single windowed block vs oracle; a single global block vs oracle; the global-block index set.
- See P1-sam-tests for the L1/L2 differential gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on OQ-15 (SAM window size + windowed-block pos-embed scheme + any rel-pos), E-PM1 (oracle per-block fixtures), P1-sam-patch-embed (provides the patch-embed grid input).

title: SAM neck convs + 16× token compression (64×64×768 → 16×16×1024 → 256 tokens)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The SAM neck reduces the 64×64×768 token grid to a compressed 16×16×1024 set via four convs (two of them stride-2), achieving the headline **16× token compression at the SAM→CLIP bridge** (a 1024×1024 page → 256 tokens). This is what makes DeepEncoder efficient. Reuses `conv2d_forward_f32` + a LayerNorm2d wrapper.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the SAM neck in `vision_sam.rs`:
- `Conv2d(768→256, k1) + LayerNorm2d` → `Conv2d(256→256, k3, p1) + LayerNorm2d` → `Conv2d(→512, s2)` → `Conv2d(→1024, s2)`, output **16×16×1024**.
- The two stride-2 convs take 64×64 → 16×16 = **256 tokens** (the 16× compression).
- LayerNorm2d is a thin wrapper over `layer_norm_forward_f32` (BUILD the 2D variant: normalize over the channel dim with NCHW layout).
Transcribed facts: neck = `Conv2d(768→256,k1)+LayerNorm2d` → `Conv2d(256→256,k3,p1)+LN2d` → `Conv2d(→512,s2)` → `Conv2d(→1024,s2)`, output 16×16×1024; `downsample_channels [512,1024]`; 'DeepEncoder can compress a 1024×1024 PDF-image to just 256 tokens'.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- LayerNorm2d normalizes per-channel over spatial (the SAM `LayerNorm2d` from the SAM lineage) — confirm the exact axis from source; getting the norm axis wrong fuzzes everything after.
- Keep f32.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The stride-2 conv padding and the exact downsample arithmetic (64→32→16) must match; an off-by-one in padding changes the 16×16 grid.
- LayerNorm2d eps and axis are load-bearing.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L2 cosine ≥ 0.9999 vs oracle post-SAM/post-compression output (16×16×1024 = 256 tokens).
- Output shape exactly 256 tokens × 1024.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: each conv output shape; LayerNorm2d axis/eps correctness; the full neck end-to-end shape.
- See P1-sam-tests for the L2 differential gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on E-PM1 (oracle post-compression fixture + neck weight shapes/LN axis) and P1-sam-window-global-attn (provides the 64×64×768 SAM-tower output the neck consumes).

title: SAM-ViT-B L1/L2 parity test bead (per-block + post-compression)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The SAM tower is the highest silent-corruption risk in vision (window size, pos-embed scheme, neck norm axis all fuzz L2). This bead is the SAM parity gate.

## WHAT TO DO
Differential harness (`tests/sam_parity.rs`) comparing each SAM stage vs oracle fixtures:
- post-patch-embed (64×64×768 + pos_embed),
- per-block output for all 12 blocks (windowed + the 4 global),
- post-neck/compression (16×16×1024 = 256 tokens).
Structured JSON-line logging: per stage {stage, shape, cosine, max_abs_diff, pass}. Model-gated (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at /nonexistent).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Per-block granularity localizes a window/pos-embed bug to the exact block.
- Fixtures carry PROVENANCE (oracle torch/transformers versions, command).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 ≥ 0.9999 every block; L2 ≥ 0.9999 post-compression; max-abs-diff ledgered.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- This IS the test bead.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P1-sam-patch-embed, P1-sam-window-global-attn, P1-sam-neck-compression (under test) and E-PM1 (per-stage oracle fixtures).

title: CLIP-L/14 vision tower (24 layers SDPA + quick_gelu FFN, class+abs pos embeds)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The CLIP-L/14 stage is the second tower of DeepEncoder, running over the compressed token set. It contributes the CLIP half of the 2048-dim fused feature. frankentorch supplies `sdpa_forward_f32`; we BUILD quick_gelu.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs`:
- **patch_size 14, 24 layers, width 1024, 16 heads**, with `class_embedding` + absolute position embeddings.
- Each block: **LayerNorm → SDPA attention → residual → LayerNorm → FFN with quick_gelu**.
- **quick_gelu**: `x * sigmoid(1.702 * x)` (BUILD; small kernel).
- Weights: `model.vision_model.embeddings.{patch_embedding,class_embedding,position}` (internal `vision.clip.embed.*`), `model.vision_model.transformer.layers.{0..23}.*` (internal `vision.clip.layer[i].*`).
Transcribed facts (config `clip-l-14-224`): patch_size 14, 24 layers, width 1024, 16 heads, has class_embedding + abs pos embeddings; block order LN→SDPA→residual→LN→FFN(quick_gelu).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **quick_gelu, NOT standard gelu** for CLIP FFN — getting this wrong drifts every CLIP layer. (SAM MLP uses standard GELU; keep vision-family activations separate.)
- f32 (vision high-precision).
- The class_embedding is prepended to the token sequence; abs pos embeds added; confirm the exact pre-LN/post-LN block structure from source.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- quick_gelu coefficient 1.702 is exact; do not approximate.
- Whether the CLIP patch-embedding is applied to the SAM-compressed tokens directly or to a re-patched input is part of the bridge — coordinate with the vision-bridge bead and OQ-6.
- LayerNorm eps and the residual ordering matter.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 per CLIP layer vs oracle.
- L2 cosine ≥ 0.9999 for the full CLIP-tower output.
- quick_gelu unit-exact vs reference formula.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: quick_gelu vs `x*sigmoid(1.702x)`; one CLIP block vs oracle; class_embedding prepend + pos-embed; 24-layer stack shape.
- See P1-clip-tests for the L1/L2 differential gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on E-PM1 (oracle per-CLIP-layer fixtures + weight shapes + confirmation of block structure) and P1-sam-neck-compression (the bridge feeds CLIP the compressed tokens; exact path is OQ-6).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM — sub-op decomposition + 1D LayerNorm + patch-embed/OQ-6 path (review enrichment)
This bead bundles several distinct sub-ops that each carry their own parity risk and must be individually pinned and tested (mirror the SAM tower's per-op granularity):
- **CLIP patch-embed Conv2d k14 s14** (plan §2.7 item 1 lists 'CLIP patch-embed (k14s14)' as a required Conv2d). Whether this conv runs at all in OUR path is GATED BY OQ-6: if CLIP re-patches a raw/resized input it needs the k14s14 conv (reuse `conv2d_forward_f32`); if CLIP instead runs over the SAM-compressed tokens, the patch-embed is bypassed and the tokens enter the CLIP stack directly. Read `deepencoder.py` (the `low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu` / `hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu` variants) line-by-line to determine which — and record it — BEFORE wiring the embedding path. Do not assume.
- **class_embedding prepend + absolute position embeddings**: the learnable `class_embedding` is prepended to the token sequence and abs pos-embeds are added; confirm the exact add order and whether the CLS token participates in the projector fusion or is dropped (coordinate with OQ-6 / P1-vision-bridge-projector).
- **1D LayerNorm (vision-family, NOT RMSNorm)**: each CLIP block applies a standard 1D LayerNorm (mean-subtract + variance-normalize + affine), distinct from the LLM RMSNorm (P1-rmsnorm) and the SAM neck's LayerNorm2d (P1-sam-neck-compression). Reuse frankentorch's `layer_norm_forward_f32`. PIN the LayerNorm `eps` and the pre-LN-vs-post-LN block ordering (the bead currently states LN→SDPA→residual→LN→FFN — confirm this is pre-norm and that the residual wraps SDPA and FFN exactly as the reference) from the pinned source; a wrong eps or residual placement silently shifts every CLIP layer.
- **Test additions (fold into P1-clip-tests)**: a 1D-LayerNorm unit (vs hand/f64 with the real eps, and a family-separation check that CLIP-LN ≠ RMSNorm ≠ LayerNorm2d on a shared input); a patch-embed conv shape/value unit IF OQ-6 says the conv is in-path; a class_embedding+pos-embed prepend unit. Keep all per-CLIP-layer cosine checkpoints already specified.

Harden these paths to return FocrError instead of panicking, add focused regression tests for malformed Mat/overflow inputs, and keep behavior identical for valid fixtures.

Added checked rows*cols validation for public Mat inputs, checked transpose/class-token/attention shape products, and regression tests for malformed flat buffers and overflow cases. OcrModel::last_hidden_row now checks rows*cols overflow and data.len() before borrowing the final row, returning FocrError instead of panicking. Added last_hidden_row_rejects_malformed_decoder_output_without_panic. title: TestLog NDJSON emitter — the reusable Rust module all test scaffolds use to log inputs/shapes/timings/parity-deltas
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The logging CONVENTION (TEST-log-conventions) is useless without a single ergonomic emitter that all test code calls. Hand-rolling `serde_json` per test would guarantee drift from the schema. This bead builds the one `TestLog` emitter that produces schema-valid NDJSON lines with minimal ceremony, so the "great detailed logging" mandate is the path of least resistance, not an extra chore.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/common/test_log.rs` (a crate-internal test-support module):
1. **`TestLog::new(test_name)`** — opens a per-test NDJSON sink (stdout in CI; an optional `TEST_LOG_DIR/<test>.ndjson` when set for artifact capture).
2. **Builder-style stage logging**: `log.stage("prefill").inputs(...).shapes(&[[T,1280]]).dtype(Dtype::F32).simd_tier(tier).elapsed_us(us).emit()` — produces one schema-valid line with `event="stage"` and `schema_version=TEST_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
3. **Parity logging**: `log.parity(Gate::L3, Metric::MaxAbsDiff).value(v).tolerance(t).oracle(fixture, sha).envelope(env).pass(b).emit()`.
4. **Result/skip logging**: `log.skip_no_model()`, `log.result(Result::Pass)`, `log.xfail("DISC-007")`.
5. **Native-path proof helper**: `log.native_path_ran(true).fallback_target("/nonexistent").emit()`.
6. **Auto-scrub-friendly `ts`**: monotonic-relative-to-test-start so logs are golden-comparable after scrubbing.
7. **Zero hot-loop cost**: emit only at stage/parity/result boundaries; buffer if needed; never allocate per decode-token under test.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **One emitter, schema-locked**: the emitter cannot produce an invalid line by construction (typed enums for `event`/`stage`/`result`/`metric`/`gate`/`dtype`/`simd_tier`), so the schema validator is a backstop, not the primary defense.
- **Builder ergonomics** so authors actually log richly — the mandate is DETAILED logging; friction kills detail.
- **Optional file sink** (`TEST_LOG_DIR`) so CI can archive per-test NDJSON as artifacts for the gauntlet's evidence graph (§8.4 `artifacts/perf/<bead>/`).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must be test-only (`#[cfg(test)]` / `tests/common/`) — never compiled into the shipping `focr` binary (preserves G3 no-bloat).
- Interleaving with `cargo test`'s captured stdout: when run with `--nocapture` lines stream; when captured they go to the file sink — handle both.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every emit() produces a line that passes the TEST-log-conventions validator.
- Typed enums make an invalid `event`/`stage`/`result` a compile error.
- `TEST_LOG_DIR` capture writes per-test `.ndjson` files; absent, it streams to stdout.
- Not linked into `focr` (verified by a no-symbol check or `#[cfg(test)]` confinement).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: each builder path emits a validator-passing line.
- Unit: file-sink mode writes the expected file; stdout mode streams.
- Unit: scrubbing `ts` yields a stable, golden-comparable line.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-log-conventions**: implements that schema; the validator + enums come from there.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — tests/common aggregation, panic-safe flush, supersede the P0 seed
- **`tests/common/mod.rs` aggregation + the cross-test-binary `dead_code` gotcha.** Each Cargo INTEGRATION test file compiles as its OWN crate, so `tests/common/test_log.rs` is only visible via a `mod common;` in each test file, and any helper a given test binary doesn't use triggers `dead_code` warnings that `-D warnings` turns into errors. Standardize on `tests/common/mod.rs` re-exporting the support modules and annotate the support crate with `#![allow(dead_code)]` (or `#[allow(dead_code)]` per item) so the shared emitter compiles cleanly in every test binary. Document this so module authors don't each rediscover it.
- **Panic-safe flush (Drop impl).** The emitter MUST flush its buffer on `Drop` and install a flush in the panic path (or use line-buffered unbuffered emit at stage boundaries) so a panicking test still leaves its NDJSON up to the crash — the failing test is the one whose log matters most. Make the `TEST_LOG_DIR` file sink fsync/flush on drop.
- **Supersede, don't duplicate, the Phase-0 tiny logger.** `P0-test-harness` (bd-223.8) seeds a 'tiny test-only logger (`{test, phase, stage, elapsed_ms, gated, outcome}`)'. This emitter is the FORMALIZATION of that seed — it MUST replace it (same sink, schema-locked) so the project never carries two diverging test loggers. Note the migration explicitly in the bead's WHAT-TO-DO.
- **`run_seq`/`trace_id` support.** Implement the correlation-id fields added in TEST-log-conventions' review-2 addendum (a `TestLog` carries a per-invocation `run_seq`; a `forward()` scope stamps `trace_id` on all its stage lines) so multi-invocation tests (determinism, deadlock soak) produce joinable logs.

Harden the .focrq reader to reject malformed header overrides while retaining the fixed-prefix fallback for an empty header field. Add focused regression tests.

The .focrq reader now validates any non-empty header source_sha256 override as exactly 64 lowercase hex characters before accepting it, while preserving the empty-header fallback to the fixed preamble bytes. Added regression coverage for a valid header override taking precedence over the prefix and for malformed uppercase/short/non-hex header source_sha256 rejection with FormatMismatch. Verification passed: rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/native_engine/weights.rs; git diff --check -- src/native_engine/weights.rs; CARGO_HOME=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken_ocr_cargo_home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken_ocr_codex_postprocess_target TMPDIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/tmp cargo test --lib native_engine::weights -- --nocapture (35 passed); ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/weights.rs (0 critical).

title: Dense numeric/table/code exact-token-sensitive accuracy corpus + CER curve per bit-width
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §10 Phase 4 mandates a 'dense-text accuracy curve per bit-width (tables/code/numbers/sub-superscripts — exact-token-sensitive corpus)', and §6.3/§11 are emphatic: **OCR of dense numbers/tables/code is exact-token-sensitive and perplexity UNDER-predicts this** — the accuracy cliff is below 4-bit and only a CER curve on exact-token content reveals it. This corpus is the measurement substrate for the entire Phase-4 quality story: AF-1's proof obligation, the expected-loss-guided per-layer quant, and the AF-2 tail-risk gate all consume it.

## WHAT TO DO
- **Assemble the corpus:** document images dense in (a) numbers / decimals / currency / large tables of figures, (b) source code (where a single wrong token breaks meaning), (c) LaTeX math with sub/superscripts and fractions, (d) mixed dense layout. Freeze the images + the bf16-reference decoded text/bbox as golden fixtures (from the CUDA correctness oracle, §8.1 — never CPU-HF-dependent).
- **CER harness:** compute Character Error Rate (and TEDS for tables, Formula-CDM for math, §1.1 G1) of each candidate bit-width config vs the bf16 reference, **per document and aggregate**, with the **AF-2 tail metrics** (mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999, §9.7).
- **CER curve per bit-width:** sweep {bf16, int8, int4-g32, int4-g16, AF-1-allocated} and plot/emit CER (+ CVaR + EVT) per config — the curve that locates the cliff and proves int4 stays above it (Q4_K_M-class).
- **Calibration batch:** designate a held-out subset as the AF-1 calibration batch (for D_t(b) cosine-drop curves) DISJOINT from the CER-evaluation set (no train/test leakage). Record provenance.
- **Structured logging:** NDJSON per-doc `{doc_id, content_class, bit_config, cer, teds, formula_cdm, first_divergence_token}` so a regression localizes to a document and content class.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Content-class stratification** (numbers/tables/code/math) because the cliff hits these classes FIRST and hardest; an aggregate CER hides a catastrophic table regression.
- **Tail metrics, not just mean** (AF-2): the gate is CVaR/EVT, not the mean (§9.7) — most pages fine, the tail is what kills.
- **Calibration/eval split** to keep the AF-1 proof obligation honest (allocated CER measured on held-out docs).
- **Golden fixtures from the CUDA correctness oracle**, frozen once (§8.1) — parity never depends on a CPU HF run.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Establish the oracle's bf16 nondeterminism floor FIRST** (§8.2): run the oracle twice / two thread counts, record the per-token divergence envelope; 'exact-match' and CER thresholds are defined RELATIVE to that floor. A CER delta inside the oracle's noise is not a regression.
- The corpus must be large/diverse enough for stable CVaR_0.1 and an EVT-p999 fit (a handful of docs can't estimate a 99.9th percentile) — size for the tail.
- Fixture provenance mandatory (PROVENANCE.md: transformers==4.57.1, torch==2.10.0, git ref, exact command).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A frozen, content-class-stratified golden corpus + reference outputs committed under `tests/fixtures/`.
- A CER harness emitting (mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999, TEDS, Formula-CDM) per bit-width config.
- The CER-per-bit-width curve locates the cliff and demonstrates int4 (Q4_K_M-class) stays above it on dense content.
- Calibration/eval split documented; oracle nondeterminism floor recorded; provenance committed.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Model-gated: the harness reproduces the reference CER on bf16 (≈0 within the oracle floor); int8 CER within noise; int4 CER within the ledgered budget. Unit: CER/TEDS/CVaR/EVT computations on synthetic inputs with known answers. See `P4-dense-corpus-tests`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `E-PM1`: needs the pinned oracle, the truth-pack fixtures, and the established nondeterminism floor.
- `P4-convert-int4`: needs the int4 `.focrq` to produce the int4 points on the curve.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — curve must carry BOTH int4 group sizes + the out-of-recipe footprint levers
- **Sweep BOTH group sizes.** The CER-per-bit-width curve must include distinct points for int4-g16 AND int4-g32 (not a single 'int4'), because AF-1's action set and the footprint/scales trade differ between them; the curve is what validates AF-1's cosine-drop proxy against true CER for each.
- **Include the out-of-recipe footprint levers on the curve.** §12 says hitting <2.9 GB may require int8 `lm_head` and possibly int8 embeddings — choices OUTSIDE the validated recipe. The corpus must measure the CER cost of each (int8-lm_head, int8-embeddings) so P4-footprint-arithmetic can show the byte-savings-vs-CER trade with a MEASURED CER, gated by the AF-2 tail gate + kill-switch + DISCREPANCIES, not an assumption.
- **Sub/superscript + fraction stratum explicitly.** §10 Phase 4 names 'sub-superscripts'; ensure the math stratum carries sub/superscript and fraction-dense pages distinct from plain LaTeX, since these are among the first to fall off the cliff.

title: Clean STALE doc-comment drift claiming 'NotImplemented until the .focrq reader lands' (vision_sam/vision_clip/mod.rs)

The .focrq reader landed and the vision/decoder kernels now consume Weights accessors, but stale doc comments still claim the forward 'surfaces NotImplemented until the .focrq reader lands': vision_sam.rs:14-18/256, vision_clip.rs:176-185, mod.rs:541-547. False now and actively misleading to any agent tracing the call graph. title: R-SWA cache length arithmetic rejects overflow before allocation/indexing
description: Fresh-eyes hardening for src/native_engine/rswa.rs: RingCache::new and record_prefill compute ref_capacity*HEAD_DIM, seq*HEAD_DIM, and NUM_HEADS*seq*HEAD_DIM with unchecked usize multiplication. Debug catches some cases, but release can wrap before allocation or validation and later panic/index incorrectly. Add checked arithmetic helpers so impossible cache shapes fail early with a clear panic for constructor overflow or FocrError for input metadata overflow, plus tests that exercise the overflow paths without allocating.
close_reason: Implemented R-SWA cache length overflow hardening. RingCache::new now checks cache row*HEAD_DIM arithmetic before allocation, and record_prefill now validates seq*HEAD_DIM plus NUM_HEADS*seq*HEAD_DIM through a shared checked layout helper before comparing K/V lengths or slicing. Added regression tests for constructor row overflow, stride overflow, and total head-major length overflow without allocating huge buffers. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fmt --check; cargo +nightly test native_engine::rswa --lib; ubs --format=json --ci --only=rust src/native_engine/rswa.rs (0 critical); git diff --check -- src/native_engine/rswa.rs; cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test.

title: Profile-guided contiguous-transpose and buffer-reuse experiments for native OCR hot paths
description: Carry the frankensearch clean-sweep lesson into franken_ocr in an organized, evidence-led way. After the head-to-head gauntlet harness is usable, run the extreme-software-optimization loop on native OCR hot paths: baseline first, profile top-5 frames, score levers (Impact*Confidence/Effort >= 2.0), prove parity/golden behavior before and after, then implement one lever per commit. Candidate levers to evaluate include contiguous-store transpose/repack layouts, reusable q/k/v/projector buffers, pretransposed static weights where loader format permits, and attention repack removal. Every accepted speed claim must write PERF_LEDGER with reference/focr ratio, precision/thread/allocator controls, and roofline distance; every neutral/regressing lever must write docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md. Coordinate via Agent Mail with the gauntlet owner before touching benches or hot-path files.

title: int4 GEMM accepts noncanonical QInt4 group sizes
description: simd::int4::igemm_s4s8 documents QInt4 group size as 16 or 32, and the .focrq reader/writer/quantizer enforce that canonical set, but the kernel currently accepts any nonzero group that divides k. That leaves a runtime path that can normalize malformed QInt4 metadata and drift from the format contract. Harden igemm_s4s8 to reject non-{16,32} group sizes and add a regression test.
close_reason: Hardened simd::int4::igemm_s4s8 to reject noncanonical groups outside {16,32}, then retain the exact-divides-k check for canonical groups. Added a regression where group=8 divides k=32 but panics, and updated the overflow fixture to use canonical group=16. Verified with simd::int4 lib tests, full cargo fmt/check/clippy/test, focused UBS on src/simd/int4.rs, rustfmt/diff checks, and grep for loose group checks.

title: Hardening: CLIP self-attention shape contracts must return FocrError before QKV indexing
description:
## Problem
Fresh-eyes review after `bd-ethz` found the adjacent CLIP tower has a narrower version of the same failure class. `vision_clip::linear` already validates its own input/weight/bias shapes, but `self_attention` still relies on `debug_assert_eq!(qkv.cols, 3 * dim)` and `debug_assert_eq!(ctx.len(), heads * seq * hd)` while also assuming `hidden_size` is cleanly divisible by `num_heads` and that `out_proj` returns `[seq, dim]`.

Valid model weights should satisfy these invariants, but malformed/future `.focrq` accessors should produce a typed `FocrError::Other`, not a production panic or row-index out-of-bounds while splitting Q/K/V.

## Acceptance criteria
- `self_attention` validates non-zero heads, hidden/head divisibility, input width, qkv output shape, SDPA context length, and output projection shape before using the buffers.
- Focused tests cover malformed head configuration and malformed qkv/projection shapes.
- Existing CLIP/SAM/native vision tests remain green.

## Coordination
WhiteCave owns this narrow `src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs` hardening slice. Avoid tokenizer (`bd-re8.8`) and gauntlet lanes.
close_reason: Implemented CLIP self-attention shape-contract hardening in src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs. self_attention now rejects zero heads, non-divisible hidden/head configurations, input-width mismatches, malformed QKV projection output, malformed SDPA context length, and malformed output-projection shape with FocrError::Other before indexing/splitting buffers. Added focused tests for bad head configuration and malformed qkv/out projection shapes. Verification: rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs; cargo +nightly fmt --check; git diff --check -- .beads/issues.jsonl src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs; br dep cycles --json; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config 'patch.crates-io.asupersync.path="/dp/asupersync"' test vision_clip::tests --lib -- --nocapture; same config check --all-targets; same config clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; same config test; python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py; timeout 240s ubs --diff --jobs=1 --quiet --ci . (0 findings).

title: Perf: optimize f32 decode_attention (R-SWA) — the real decode attn-phase cost
description: Decode attn phase = 37% of decode (5436ms/15.15s on page_0023, 821 tok). MEASURED 2026-06-26 (bd-attn-serial): the int8 q/k/v/o projections are NOT the cost (serializing them regressed +19%; they parallelize well). The cost is rswa::decode_attention — the f32 R-SWA over ~277 reference + 128 ring keys x 10 heads x 128 head_dim x 12 layers, scalar-dot per key. Levers: (a) batched-GEMM formulation (QK^T and probs@V as GEMM over the key block instead of per-key dot loops, like the SAM gemm-attention WIN bd-3n16); (b) int8 KV cache -> int8 QK^T/PV (halves KV bandwidth + SDOT dots, but KV-int8 is BEYOND-VALIDATED per doctrine #2 -> gate behind measured-CER kill-switch, DISCREPANCIES.md). Start with (a) which is lossless. Bit-exact/CER gate required.

title: Stale format and SIMD dispatch prose contradicts the implemented contracts
description: Fresh-eyes audit found documentation/comment drift in live changed files. docs/focrq-format.md describes tensor directory tier as a string enum/null with examples like "tier": "BF16", but the committed reader/writer deserialize and emit a numeric u8 tier for quantized tensors only and omit tier for high-precision tensors. Separately, src/simd/dispatch.rs still says the ARM backend picks SMMLA > SDOT > scalar internally even though the implemented aarch64/macOS policy is SDOT > SMMLA > scalar on Apple Silicon, with SMMLA > SDOT only on other aarch64. Patch only the stale prose/comments; do not touch active tokenizer, gauntlet, CI, or hot-path behavior.
close_reason: Fixed stale .focrq format and SIMD dispatch prose to match committed v1 reader/writer and Apple-Silicon SDOT-first dispatch; updated the focrq spec checker accordingly. Verified with python3 scripts/check_focrq_format.py, py_compile, cargo +nightly fmt --check, cargo +nightly test simd::dispatch --lib, git diff --check, ubs --only=rust src/simd/dispatch.rs, and ubs --only=python scripts/check_focrq_format.py.

title: Perf(structural): lm_head over LAST prefill row only (CLS-only analog) — mod.rs slices hidden last row before the vocab=129280 head GEMM; bit-identical (per-row independent), proved by decoder::lm_head_last_row_is_full_last_row
