title: Epic: Alien-artifact math families (AF-1..AF-5) compiled to runtime artifacts with proofs + deterministic fallbacks
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY

This epic operationalizes plan section 9.7 (AF-1..AF-5) and 9.5 (the radical-ideas register that AF-1/AF-3 formalize). These are the genuinely high-leverage, non-obvious levers that make Unlimited-OCR FLY on CPU *without* drifting OCR output. They are NOT name-dropped math: per `/alien-artifact-coding`, the complexity budget for a >100K-LOC perf-critical project is **3-5 families, natural-fit only, each compiled to a concrete runtime artifact with a proof obligation + a deterministic conservative fallback**. Per `/alien-graveyard`, each lever carries a recommendation contract: **EV = Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction) >= 2.0**, an S/A/B/C tier, and a fallback trigger.

The over-arching goal these serve: **maximal quantization (int4 on the expert bulk + int8 on the sensitive minority) pushed exactly as far as a MEASURED worst-case CER bound allows**, plus correct many-core pool sizing so we don't oversubscribe memory-bound decode. The whole project's thesis is mixed int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels for THIS one model; AF-1/AF-2/AF-4 govern *where* to spend bits and *how to gate the risk*, AF-5 governs *how many threads* each op-class gets, and AF-3 is the speculative-decode upside.

## THE FIVE FAMILIES (one spike task each)

- **AF-1 [Tier A, EV high]** Rate-distortion + convex duality (water-filling) for optimal per-tensor quant allocation. The int4/int8/bf16 split optimizer. **Feeds E-P4 (int4).** Replaces uniform bit-width; principled form of the section-9.5 'expected-loss-guided per-layer quant.'
- **AF-2 [Tier A, EV high]** Distributionally-robust / tail-risk (CVaR + EVT) for worst-case CER. **The release-gate metric. Feeds E-VERIFY.** OCR fails in the TAIL; mean-CER and perplexity under-predict exact-token failure on dense tables/sub-scripts/code.
- **AF-3 [Tier B, EV medium-high]** Conformal / sequential testing (SPRT, e-values) for provably-safe early-exit + speculative decode with a finite-sample token-flip bound. Upside lever behind its guarantee. Feeds the E-P3 decode path.
- **AF-4 [Tier B, EV medium]** Submodular maximization (monotone, knapsack-constrained, greedy (1-1/e) guarantee) for the high-precision tensor set. **Feeds E-P4.** Certifiable refinement of the hand-picked 'keep vision/router/norms' heuristic.
- **AF-5 [Tier A, EV high, low effort]** Queueing theory (Universal Scalability Law) for many-core pool sizing per (arch, op-class). **Feeds E-P3 NUMA / many-core scaling (section 6.9).**

## DIMINISHING-RETURNS DISCIPLINE (governs the whole epic)
Stop escalating math when the dominant metric is within ~10-20% of optimal, the bottleneck becomes operational, or verification cost exceeds the gain. **AF-1/AF-2/AF-5 are the load-bearing trio** (quant quality + many-core); **AF-3/AF-4 are upside levers behind their guarantees**.

## NON-NEGOTIABLE CONTRACT FOR EVERY FAMILY (AGENTS.md 'Alien-Artifact Engineering Contract')
Every adaptive/runtime decision in this epic MUST ship:
1. an explicit state space, explicit actions, a loss matrix;
2. posterior/confidence terms and a calibration metric;
3. a **deterministic fallback trigger** (wired FIRST, before the adaptive path);
4. an evidence-ledger artifact;
5. a **galaxy-brain transparency card** (equation - substituted values - plain-English intuition - validity assumptions - what would flip the decision);
6. an **assumptions ledger**.
**No adaptive controller ships without a conservative deterministic fallback.** Each family is a `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`-gated hypothesis until its proof obligation is met on real fixtures; if the proof fails it is REVERTED (no source landed) and ledgered, exactly like any perf lever (G1 > G2 always).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (epic-level)
- All 5 spike tasks resolved (each KEEP-with-proof or REVERT-with-ledger-entry); none left ambiguous.
- Every KEPT family has: its runtime artifact materialized, its proof obligation passing on the real golden corpus, its deterministic fallback wired and demonstrably engaged when the proof/calibration fails, its galaxy-brain card committed under `docs/`, and its assumptions ledger committed.
- `focr robot backends` reflects AF-5's pool-sizing table; the `.focrq` header carries AF-1's bit_allocation_table and AF-4's high-precision set; the E-VERIFY release scorecard gates on AF-2's CVaR/EVT bound (not the mean).
- No family lowered any per-category parity lower-bound (the section-8.5 conformal ratchet).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
The epic as a whole depends on E-PM1 (the Phase -1 truth pack: pinned config, the token/shape/buffer census, the resolved OQs, the oracle strategy) and on E-P1 (the f32 parity oracle that lets us MEASURE distortion/CER). AF-1/AF-4 also need E-P2 (int8 baseline) to measure per-tensor distortion curves; AF-2 needs the quant pipeline + golden corpus; AF-3/AF-5 need E-P3 decode kernels. Per-family deps are on each task.

title: AF-1 SPIKE: rate-distortion / water-filling per-tensor bit-allocation optimizer (int4/int8/bf16 split)
description:
## RECOMMENDATION CONTRACT (verbatim from plan section 9.7, AF-1)
- **Family:** Rate-distortion + convex duality (water-filling) for optimal per-tensor quant allocation.
- **Tier:** A. **EV:** high. (EV = Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction) >= 2.0 — high impact: governs the headline int4/int8 split, the whole project's perf thesis; high confidence: classic RD theory; reusable across every future quant decision.)
- **Artifact:** an offline `bit_allocation_table` baked into the `.focrq` header, computed by `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>`.
- **Proof obligation:** the allocated config's end-to-end CER <= the uniform-bit config's at EQUAL footprint, on the dense-numeric corpus.
- **Deterministic fallback:** uniform Q4_K_M-class allocation (section 5) — i.e. the static recipe E-P4 ships without AF-1.

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The choice of per-tensor bit-width from the menu **{BF16/F32, int8, int4-g32, int4-g16}** under a total-footprint budget *is* a rate-distortion problem. We are NOT going to quantize uniformly: int4 group-quantized weights (g=16-32) halve the bytes streamed per token vs int8 (decode is memory-bandwidth-bound -> up to ~2x decode throughput on the expert FFN bulk), but the accuracy cliff is *below* 4-bit and OCR of dense numbers/tables/code is exact-token-sensitive (perplexity under-predicts this). So bits must be spent where they buy the most accuracy and starved where they buy little. This is the principled form of the section-9.5 radical idea 'decision-theoretic per-tensor quant'. It directly feeds E-P4: E-P4's int4 conversion consumes the table AF-1 produces.

## WHAT TO DO (the concrete deliverable, with verified facts transcribed)
1. **State / action / loss (the alien contract):** State = the per-tensor bit choice `b_t` for each quantizable tensor `t`. Action = pick `b_t` from {bf16, int8, int4-g32, int4-g16}. Loss = end-to-end distortion `D_t(b_t)` (measured, see below) under footprint constraint.
2. **Formulate the optimization:** minimize `sum_t D_t(b_t)` subject to `sum_t bits_t(b_t) <= B`. The Lagrangian `sum_t [ D_t(b_t) + lambda * bits_t(b_t) ]` **separates per-tensor**: at a fixed lambda, each tensor independently picks the `b_t` minimizing `D_t(b_t) + lambda*bits_t(b_t)`. Sweeping lambda traces the **optimal bit-allocation frontier (water-filling)** — spend bits where the marginal distortion-per-bit `dD_t/dbits` is steepest, starve the flat ones.
3. **Measure the per-tensor distortion curves `D_t(b)`:** for each quantizable tensor and each candidate bit-width, measure the **layer-output cosine drop on a calibration batch** (cosine distance between the f32-reference layer output and the quantized-tensor layer output). This is the distortion signal. The calibration batch is a representative slice of the golden corpus (dense page + table + formula + multi-page sample).
4. **The quantizable tensor set (from the census):** decoder expert GEMMs (experts `mlp.experts.{0..63}.{gate,up,down}_proj`, 11 MoE layers x 64 x 3), shared experts (11 x 2 x 3), dense layer-0 `mlp.{gate,up,down}_proj` (intermediate 6848), attention `q/k/v/o`, and `lm_head` (1280->129280). Vision tower, projector, `embed_tokens`, the MoE router gate, and ALL norms are NOT in the menu — they stay BF16/F32 (the validated recipe; quantizing them wrecks OCR). The plan calls out the steepest-marginal tensors to spend on: the wide `intermediate=6848` dense `down_proj` and the attention `v_proj`.
5. **Bake the result:** emit `bit_allocation_table` (tensor-name -> chosen bit-width) into the `.focrq` header; `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>` runs the sweep and writes it.
6. **Galaxy-brain transparency card** under `docs/` (equation: the Lagrangian above; substituted values: the actual lambda chosen, per-tensor D curves, the resulting bit map; plain-English: 'spend bits where they buy the most accuracy'; validity assumptions: per-tensor distortion is additive/separable, layer-output cosine is a faithful proxy for end-to-end CER; what would flip the decision: if cosine-proxy and measured CER disagree, or if cross-tensor interactions dominate).
7. **Assumptions ledger** under `docs/`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Separable Lagrangian** is the win: it turns a combinatorial knapsack over thousands of tensors into N independent per-tensor minimizations at each lambda. Cheap, exact at the convex hull.
- **Cosine-drop as the distortion metric** (not perplexity): perplexity under-predicts exact-token OCR failure (the project's repeated lesson). Cosine of the layer output is a cheap, per-tensor, locally-measurable proxy; AF-2's CER tail bound is the FINAL arbiter.
- **down_proj and v_proj are kept one tier higher** as a prior (section 6.3): AF-1 should *recover* this (steep marginal distortion) rather than contradict it; if it contradicts it, that's a flag to investigate, not auto-accept.
- **This is OUR risk to retire by measurement.** int8 on attention q/k/v/o and on lm_head go BEYOND the NVFP4/GGUF-validated set (OQ-14); the allocator may choose them but every such choice is ledgered with measured CER impact and sits behind the existing kill-switches.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Separability assumption can break:** quantizing tensor A may change the optimal bit-width of tensor B (activation-distribution coupling). Mitigation: validate the allocated config END-TO-END (the proof obligation), not just per-tensor; if end-to-end CER exceeds uniform at equal footprint, the separability assumption failed -> fall back to uniform and ledger it.
- **Cosine-proxy fidelity:** if a tensor with a small cosine drop nonetheless causes large CER on dense numerics (tail effect), AF-1 alone will mis-allocate. **AF-1 must be composed with AF-2's tail bound** — AF-1 chooses, AF-2 vetoes any choice that violates the CVaR/EVT bound (the fallback in AF-2 is 'keep the tail-offending tensor one tier higher').
- **Determinism:** the sweep must be deterministic (fixed calibration batch, fixed seed) so the `bit_allocation_table` is reproducible; a round-trip test asserts the same input config -> byte-identical table.
- **Kill-switch:** `focr convert` without `--optimize-bits` MUST produce the deterministic uniform allocation; the optimizer is opt-in. The runtime never depends on the optimizer having run.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>` produces a deterministic `bit_allocation_table` for a chosen footprint budget.
- **Proof obligation MET:** at equal footprint, end-to-end CER(allocated) <= CER(uniform Q4_K_M) on the dense-numeric corpus, with the comparison logged (per-document CER, mean, and the AF-2 CVaR_0.1 for both configs).
- The allocated config respects the validated recipe (vision/projector/embeddings/router/norms stay BF16/F32; never demoted).
- Galaxy-brain card + assumptions ledger committed.
- If the proof FAILS: REVERT (no `.focrq` ships the table), ledger the failure in `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md` with the measured numbers and the explicit retry condition, and E-P4 ships the uniform fallback.

## TESTS REQUIRED (comprehensive, with great structured logging)
- **Unit:** the per-tensor Lagrangian minimizer (given synthetic D curves, returns the known water-filling optimum); lambda-sweep monotonicity (footprint decreases as lambda increases); table round-trip determinism.
- **Integration / E2E:** end-to-end CER comparison allocated-vs-uniform at equal footprint on the full golden corpus, emitting structured NDJSON/JSON logs of: per-tensor chosen bit-width, per-tensor measured cosine drop, footprint achieved, per-document CER for both configs, mean CER, CVaR_0.1 (via AF-2), and the keep/revert decision with evidence IDs. Logs go under `tests/artifacts/` and the decision is reproducible from them.
- A dedicated test bead (`ALIEN-af1-tests`) owns the conformance + proof-obligation harness and depends on this task.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1** (truth pack): need the pinned config + the machine-readable token/shape/buffer census to enumerate the exact quantizable tensor set and shapes (e.g. dense down_proj K=6848, expert moe_intermediate=896), and the resolved OQ-14 (which modules are validated-by-prior-art vs OUR risk).
- **E-P1** (f32 parity): the f32 reference is the oracle against which distortion (cosine drop) and CER are measured.
- **E-P2** (int8): the int8 quantizer/`.focrq` writer and per-output-channel scheme are the substrate; AF-1 chooses among int8/int4/bf16 tiers that E-P2/E-P4 implement.
- **E-P4** (int4): AF-1 FEEDS E-P4 — E-P4 consumes the bit_allocation_table; AF-1's whole purpose is to drive E-P4's per-tensor split. (Dep is bidirectional in spirit; AF-1 needs E-P4's int4 GEMM + group-quant machinery to exist to measure int4 distortion, and E-P4 takes AF-1's table as input. Sequence: E-P4 ships uniform first, AF-1 refines it.)
- Composes with **ALIEN-af2** (tail bound vetoes unsafe allocations) and **ALIEN-af4** (submodular high-precision set is a complementary view of the same budget).

title: AF-1 tests: bit-allocation optimizer unit + proof-obligation E2E harness with structured logging
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-1 is a NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE-gated hypothesis: it only KEEPS if its proof obligation (allocated CER <= uniform CER at equal footprint, dense-numeric corpus) is met on real fixtures. This bead owns the test harness that proves it, with the comprehensive structured logging the project mandates so the decision is reproducible from artifacts.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit tests** for the optimizer math (separate from the E2E proof):
   - Lagrangian per-tensor minimizer returns the known optimum on synthetic monotone-convex D curves.
   - Water-filling frontier: sweeping lambda is monotone in footprint; the chosen allocation at budget B is on the convex hull.
   - `bit_allocation_table` round-trip determinism: same calibration batch + seed -> byte-identical table; `.focrq` header write/read round-trips.
   - Recipe-invariant guard: vision/projector/embeddings/router/norm tensors are NEVER assigned a quantized tier (assert they are absent from the menu).
2. **Proof-obligation E2E harness** (the gate):
   - Convert the model twice at equal footprint: (a) `--optimize-bits` allocated, (b) uniform Q4_K_M fallback.
   - Run both over the full golden corpus through the parity ladder; compute per-document CER, mean CER, and CVaR_0.1 (calling AF-2's tail-risk monitor).
   - Assert CER(allocated) <= CER(uniform) AND CVaR_0.1(allocated) within the ledgered budget.
3. **Structured logging:** emit JSON-line artifacts under `tests/artifacts/af1/` recording per-tensor bit-width, per-tensor cosine drop, footprint, per-document CER for both configs, the keep/revert decision, model-source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, and the exact command/env (the NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE schema).
4. **Model-gated:** skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Unit tests pass on all arches (deterministic, no weights needed).
- E2E proof harness runs green when weights present, skips-green when absent.
- The keep/revert decision is fully reconstructable from the logged artifacts alone.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **ALIEN-af1-rate-distortion-bit-allocation**: the thing under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: reuses the parity-ladder / CER measurement machinery and the golden corpus; the proof-obligation result feeds the E-VERIFY release scorecard.

title: AF-2 SPIKE: tail-risk CVaR + EVT worst-case CER bound (the release-gate metric)
description:
## RECOMMENDATION CONTRACT (verbatim from plan section 9.7, AF-2)
- **Family:** Distributionally-robust / tail-risk (CVaR + EVT) for worst-case CER.
- **Tier:** A. **EV:** high. (Impact: it is THE release-gate metric that keeps maximal quantization honest; Confidence: CVaR/EVT are standard, well-founded; Reuse: every quant config and every future model uses the same gate.)
- **Artifact:** a `tail_risk_monitor` over the golden corpus emitting `(mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999)`; the E-VERIFY release scorecard gates on the **CVaR/tail bound, NOT the mean**.
- **Proof obligation:** int4 `CVaR_0.1` within the ledgered budget of f32.
- **Deterministic fallback:** keep the tail-offending tensor one precision tier higher (the llama.cpp `_M` discipline, **derived rather than guessed**).

## BACKGROUND / WHY
OCR fails in the **tail**: most pages are fine, but a quant choice that wrecks dense tables / sub-scripts / code / numbers is unacceptable even if mean-CER looks great. **Perplexity and mean-CER under-predict exact-token failure** — this is the single most repeated lesson in the plan (sections 6.3, 9.5, AGENTS.md doctrine #2). So the quant objective AND the release gate must optimize/bound the **worst alpha-fraction** of documents, not the average. AF-2 is the conscience that lets AF-1/AF-4 push quantization as far as it can go without silently corrupting the hard pages. It feeds E-VERIFY (the release scorecard gates on it) and is the veto authority over AF-1's allocations.

## WHAT TO DO (concrete deliverable, math transcribed)
1. **State / loss (the alien contract):** State = the per-document CER distribution produced by a given quant config over the golden corpus. Loss = the **mean of the worst alpha-fraction of per-document CER**, i.e. **CVaR_alpha** (Conditional Value at Risk / expected shortfall). Default alpha = 0.1 (CVaR_0.1 = mean CER of the worst 10% of documents).
2. **Compute CVaR_alpha:** sort per-document CER descending, average the top alpha-fraction. Emit it per quant config.
3. **Fit the EVT tail:** fit a **generalized-Pareto distribution (GPD)** to the upper tail of the per-document CER distribution (peaks-over-threshold), and estimate **EVT_p999** = the modeled 99.9th-percentile document CER. This extrapolates beyond the observed corpus to bound the worst document we have NOT yet seen.
4. **The artifact `tail_risk_monitor`:** runs over the golden corpus for any quant config, emits `(mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999)` as a structured record. AF-1 and AF-4 call it to veto allocations; E-VERIFY calls it as the release gate.
5. **Wire the deterministic fallback FIRST:** when the tail bound is violated, the fallback is to **bump the tail-offending tensor(s) one precision tier higher** and re-measure — this is the llama.cpp `_M` discipline, but DERIVED from the EVT attribution (which tensor's demotion grew the tail), not hand-guessed. The fallback must exist and be exercised before the adaptive bound is trusted.
6. **Galaxy-brain card** (equation: CVaR_alpha = E[CER | CER >= VaR_alpha], plus the GPD tail; substituted values: the actual mean/CVaR/p999 for f32 vs int8 vs int4; plain-English: 'we gate on the worst 10% of pages, not the average, because OCR fails in the tail'; validity assumptions: the golden corpus is representative of the dense-numeric tail, the GPD fit is adequate (check the QQ-plot / threshold stability); what would flip: a production page worse than EVT_p999, or a GPD misfit).
7. **Assumptions ledger.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Gate on CVaR/EVT, not the mean** — the central design decision. A maximally-quantized config with great mean CER but a wrecked tail must FAIL the release gate. This is what makes 'push quantization to the limit' safe.
- **EVT for the unseen worst case** — the golden corpus is finite; EVT lets us bound the 99.9th-percentile document we have not sampled, which is exactly the distributionally-robust property the model needs (it is deployed on arbitrary documents).
- **Fallback is derived, not guessed** — the EVT/CVaR attribution tells us WHICH tensor's low-bit choice grew the tail, so the one-tier-up bump is targeted, recovering the llama.cpp `_M` recipe from measurement rather than folklore.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Corpus representativeness:** if the golden corpus lacks the genuinely hardest content (e.g. nested chemistry sub/superscripts, dense financial tables), CVaR/EVT will under-estimate real tail risk. Mitigation: the golden corpus MUST include an exact-token-sensitive dense-numeric/table/code/sub-superscript slice; ledger the corpus composition.
- **EVT fit quality:** a bad GPD fit gives a meaningless p999. Mitigation: report fit diagnostics (threshold-stability plot, shape-parameter CI); if the fit is poor, fall back to the empirical CVaR bound only and ledger the limitation.
- **Interaction with the section-8.5 conformal ratchet:** AF-2's bound is a per-config metric; the release decision still uses the conformal LOWER bound per category (section 8.5). AF-2 supplies the CER tail metric that feeds that ratchet; it does not replace it.
- **Determinism:** CVaR/EVT must be computed from a deterministic per-document CER vector (greedy temperature=0 decode); a determinism gate asserts byte-identical CER vectors across runs.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `tail_risk_monitor` emits `(mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999)` deterministically for any quant config over the golden corpus.
- **Proof obligation MET:** int4 CVaR_0.1 within the ledgered budget of f32 (and the budget itself is recorded in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` / the release scorecard, not invented per-run).
- The E-VERIFY release scorecard gates on the CVaR/EVT bound (wired), not the mean.
- The deterministic fallback (one-tier-up bump on the EVT-attributed tensor) is implemented and demonstrably re-passes the bound on a constructed tail-offending config.
- Galaxy-brain card + assumptions ledger committed.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- **Unit:** CVaR_alpha computation on a known CER vector (matches the analytic expected-shortfall); GPD fit on a synthetic Pareto sample recovers the known shape parameter; EVT_p999 monotonicity (heavier tail -> larger p999).
- **Integration / E2E:** run `tail_risk_monitor` on f32, int8, and int4 configs over the golden corpus; assert the int4 proof obligation; construct a deliberately-tail-offending int4 config (demote a sensitive tensor), assert the gate FAILS, then apply the fallback bump and assert it PASSES — proving the gate has teeth and the fallback works.
- **Structured logging:** per-config `(mean_CER, CVaR_0.1, EVT_p999)`, per-document CER vector, GPD fit diagnostics, the gate decision, and full provenance, under `tests/artifacts/af2/`.
- Dedicated test bead `ALIEN-af2-tests` depends on this task.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: census + oracle strategy + the documented CER tolerance budget.
- **E-P1**: f32 CER is the reference distribution.
- **E-P2**: produces the int8/int4 configs whose tails AF-2 bounds.
- **E-VERIFY**: AF-2 IS the release-gate metric; E-VERIFY's scorecard consumes `tail_risk_monitor` output and AF-2 reuses E-VERIFY's CER/parity-ladder machinery and golden corpus. AF-2 also serves as the VETO for ALIEN-af1 and ALIEN-af4 allocations.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — AF-2 is the release-gate metric; wire it so it cannot be a dangling promise
The release gate REALLY using CVaR/EVT (not the mean) requires three consumers to read `tail_risk_monitor` output: (1) VERIFY-ladder-l5's AC 'No catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content (checked via the tail-risk monitor)'; (2) VERIFY-three-pillar-cert / the conformal ratchet (AF-2 supplies the CER tail metric that feeds the per-category lower bound — it does NOT replace the ratchet); (3) the P4 production gate P4-tail-risk-gate. AF-2 (the SPIKE here) and P4-tail-risk-gate (the production wiring) are the same metric at two altitudes — P4-tail-risk-gate already depends on E-ALIEN; ensure it depends on THIS bead specifically so the production gate consumes the proven monitor, and ensure L5/three-pillar read the monitor's output rather than re-deriving a mean. The dependency from AF-2 onto E-VERIFY (machinery reuse) plus AF-2 being consumed BY L5/three-pillar is a producer/consumer split at different beads (no cycle), but it must be explicitly wired or the 'gate on the tail, not the mean' guarantee silently degrades to a mean check.

title: AF-2 tests: CVaR/EVT unit + release-gate-with-teeth E2E (tail-offending config fails, fallback re-passes)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-2 is the release gate; a gate that can never fail is worthless. This bead proves the gate has TEETH (it rejects a genuinely tail-offending quant config) and that the deterministic fallback (one-tier-up bump) recovers it, with full structured logging.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit tests:** CVaR_alpha matches analytic expected-shortfall on a known CER vector; GPD fit recovers a known shape parameter on synthetic Pareto data; EVT_p999 is monotone in tail heaviness; deterministic CVaR/EVT given a fixed CER vector.
2. **Gate-has-teeth E2E:** construct a deliberately tail-offending int4 config (demote a sensitive tensor, e.g. dense down_proj or v_proj, to int4-g16) -> assert `tail_risk_monitor` makes the release gate FAIL (CVaR_0.1 / EVT_p999 over budget).
3. **Fallback E2E:** apply the EVT-attributed one-tier-up bump -> assert the gate now PASSES and the bumped tensor is exactly the EVT-attributed one (proves the fallback is targeted, not blanket).
4. **Proof-obligation E2E:** the production int4 config's CVaR_0.1 is within the ledgered budget of f32.
5. **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af2/`: per-config tail metrics, GPD diagnostics, the gate decision, the fallback attribution, provenance.
6. **Model-gated** skip-with-SUCCESS without weights.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Unit tests deterministic and arch-independent.
- The tail-offending config provably FAILS and the fallback provably RECOVERS it.
- All decisions reconstructable from logged artifacts.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **ALIEN-af2-tail-risk-cvar-evt-cer-bound**: the monitor + fallback under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: the scorecard + golden corpus the gate plugs into.

title: AF-3 SPIKE: conformal/SPRT/e-value provably-safe early-exit + speculative decode (token-flip bound)
description:
## RECOMMENDATION CONTRACT (verbatim from plan section 9.7, AF-3)
- **Family:** Conformal / sequential testing (SPRT, e-values) for provably-safe early-exit & speculative decode.
- **Tier:** B. **EV:** medium-high. (Impact: real decode-throughput upside on easy printed-text runs; Confidence: medium — exact-token OCR is high-risk; Effort/Friction: higher, hence Tier B upside lever.)
- **Artifact:** a `speculative_guard` e-process per decode step + a calibration fixture.
- **Proof obligation:** measured token-disagreement rate <= alpha on the golden corpus; **no CER regression**.
- **Deterministic fallback:** **alpha -> 0 disables speculation (always full forward).**

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Speculative / early-exit decode is high-EV (skip the full forward on easy printed-text runs) but high-RISK for exact-token OCR — a single wrong token corrupts the output and silently fails parity. The plan's stance (section 9.5): make it *provably* safe via an **anytime-valid sequential test** that bounds the probability the cheap draft disagrees with the full model at risk level alpha, turning 'risky' into a **finite-sample token-flip guarantee** (Ville's inequality — the SAME machinery the section-8.5 gauntlet uses for invariant e-processes). This is the genuine upside lever for decode latency once parity is locked.

## WHAT TO DO (concrete deliverable, math transcribed)
1. **State / action / loss (the alien contract):** State = the decode step's draft-vs-full evidence so far (the e-process value). Action = accept the cheap draft token OR fall back to the full forward. Loss matrix = {accept-correct: cheap win; accept-wrong: CER-corrupting error (heavily penalized for exact-token OCR); reject: pay the full forward (safe)}.
2. **The cheap draft:** a reduced forward — e.g. **shared-experts-only** (the 2 always-on shared experts, skipping the top-6 routed gather) or a **reduced top-k** routed set — proposes the next token. The full forward verifies.
3. **The sequential test / e-process:** maintain an **e-value / e-process** on the **draft-vs-full logit margin**; accept the draft token only while the e-process stays **below 1/alpha**; otherwise fall back to the full forward. By Ville's inequality, the probability the e-process ever exceeds 1/alpha under the null (draft agrees with full) is <= alpha, giving an **anytime-valid, finite-sample token-flip bound** at risk level alpha — no Bonferroni penalty over the unbounded decode stream. (An SPRT formulation is the bounded-horizon analog; the e-value/e-process form is the anytime-valid one and is preferred for the open-ended decode loop.)
4. **Artifact `speculative_guard`:** the per-decode-step e-process + a **calibration fixture** that sets the e-process parameters (p0, lambda) to the chosen alpha on a held-out slice of the golden corpus.
5. **Wire the deterministic fallback FIRST:** **alpha -> 0 disables speculation entirely** (every token does the full forward). This is the default; AF-3 is opt-in behind an env switch (consistent with the project's OnceLock-gated numerics levers, e.g. `FOCR_SPECULATIVE_DECODE`).
6. **Galaxy-brain card** (equation: the e-process recurrence + Ville `P(sup_t E_t >= 1/alpha) <= alpha`; substituted values: the calibrated p0/lambda, the chosen alpha, measured acceptance rate, measured speedup; plain-English: 'accept the cheap guess only while the evidence says it almost surely matches the full model'; validity assumptions: the draft-vs-full margin is exchangeable/calibrated on the corpus, the null is correctly specified; what would flip: a measured token-disagreement rate > alpha, or any CER regression -> revert).
7. **Assumptions ledger.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Anytime-valid e-process over a fixed-N test:** decode is an open-ended stream of unknown length; the e-process gives a valid bound at EVERY step without paying a multiple-testing penalty — exactly the property the section-8.5 gauntlet already relies on for its invariant monitors, so the machinery is reused.
- **Default OFF (alpha->0):** correctness outranks speed (G1 > G2). Speculation is pure upside; it must never be the reason an OCR token flips. Off by default, on only behind a measured A/B + a passing token-disagreement gate.
- **Shared-experts-only as the draft:** the 2 shared experts are always loaded and cheap; using them as the draft avoids extra weight streaming (the decode bottleneck is memory bandwidth, section 6.3).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Exact-token sensitivity:** OCR has zero tolerance for silent token flips on dense numerics; the e-process bound is the ONLY thing making this acceptable. If the measured disagreement rate exceeds alpha on ANY corpus slice, REVERT and ledger (the do-not-retry condition in NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE).
- **Calibration drift:** the e-process parameters are calibrated on the golden corpus; out-of-distribution documents may break the bound. Mitigation: the bound is anytime-valid under the null, but the null (draft~=full) is empirical — report the measured acceptance/disagreement rate per corpus slice and keep alpha conservative.
- **Determinism:** speculative decode must remain deterministic under greedy temperature=0 — given the same image, the same tokens regardless of whether speculation accepted or fell back (the fallback always recomputes the full forward, so the EMITTED token is always the full-model token; speculation only ever skips work when it would have agreed). A determinism gate asserts identical output with speculation ON vs OFF.
- **No nested rayon / single live forward:** the draft and full forward share the one rayon pool; do not spawn a concurrent draft (oversubscription / deadlock rule, AGENTS.md doctrine #5).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `speculative_guard` + calibration fixture implemented; `FOCR_SPECULATIVE_DECODE` defaults OFF (alpha->0 full forward).
- **Proof obligation MET:** measured token-disagreement rate <= alpha on the golden corpus AND zero CER regression vs full-forward decode AND byte-identical output (speculation ON == OFF under greedy).
- Measured decode speedup recorded honestly in `docs/PERF_LEDGER.md` (acceptance rate, speedup, alpha).
- Galaxy-brain card + assumptions ledger committed.
- If the disagreement bound or determinism fails: REVERT (no source landed), ledger in `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- **Unit:** e-process update + Ville threshold (synthetic null: the e-process exceeds 1/alpha at most an alpha-fraction of the time; synthetic alternative: it fires reliably); calibration sets p0/lambda to hit the target alpha.
- **Integration / E2E:** decode the golden corpus with speculation ON; assert byte-identical output to speculation OFF (greedy determinism); assert measured token-disagreement <= alpha per corpus slice; assert no CER regression; record the speedup.
- **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af3/`: per-step accept/reject, e-process trajectory, acceptance rate, disagreement rate, speedup, provenance.
- Dedicated test bead `ALIEN-af3-tests` depends on this task.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: the resolved R-SWA / MoE semantics (OQ-1..3) and the sampler/ngram-window census (OQ-18) — the draft must respect the same decode contract as the full forward.
- **E-P1**: the full-forward decode is the verifier and the parity oracle; no speculation before f32 parity exists.
- **E-P3**: AF-3 lives on the optimized decode path (the SIMD-kernel decode loop); the shared-experts-only draft reuses E-P3's MoE dispatch + decode GEMV. AF-3 is a Phase-3+ upside lever, not a v1 default.

title: AF-3 tests: e-process unit (Ville bound) + speculative-decode determinism & token-flip E2E
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-3's whole safety case is the e-process token-flip bound and greedy determinism. This bead proves both with structured logging, so speculative decode can only KEEP if it is provably output-identical to full decode and stays under its disagreement budget.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit:** e-process update is correct; under a synthetic null the e-process exceeds 1/alpha at most an alpha-fraction of streams (Ville); under a synthetic alternative it fires; the calibration routine hits the target alpha on the calibration fixture.
2. **Determinism E2E:** decode the golden corpus with `FOCR_SPECULATIVE_DECODE` ON and OFF under greedy temperature=0; assert BYTE-IDENTICAL output (the emitted token is always the full-model token; speculation only skips work it would have agreed on).
3. **Token-flip / no-regression E2E:** assert measured token-disagreement rate <= alpha on every corpus slice (printed text, tables, dense numerics) and zero CER regression vs full decode.
4. **Perf:** record acceptance rate and decode speedup honestly (PERF_LEDGER), with the §9.3 fairness controls.
5. **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af3/`: per-step accept/reject, e-process trajectory, acceptance + disagreement rates, speedup, provenance, keep/revert decision.
6. **Model-gated** skip-with-SUCCESS without weights.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Unit tests deterministic; the Ville bound holds under the synthetic null.
- ON==OFF byte-identical output proven; disagreement <= alpha; no CER regression.
- Keep/revert decision reconstructable from artifacts.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **ALIEN-af3-conformal-sequential-early-exit-speculative-decode**: the guard + draft under test.

title: AF-4 SPIKE: submodular maximization for the high-precision tensor set (greedy (1-1/e) under knapsack)
description:
## RECOMMENDATION CONTRACT (verbatim from plan section 9.7, AF-4)
- **Family:** Submodular maximization for the high-precision tensor set.
- **Tier:** B. **EV:** medium. (Impact: certifiable refinement of the keep-high-precision heuristic; Confidence: high — submodularity of accuracy-recovered is well-motivated; complementary to AF-1.)
- **Artifact:** `focr convert --select-high-precision-set --budget` emitting the chosen set + the marginal-gain ledger.
- **Proof obligation:** greedy set's CER <= heuristic set's at EQUAL footprint.
- **Deterministic fallback:** the validated NVFP4/GGUF heuristic set (section 2.6) — i.e. keep vision tower, projector, embeddings, MoE router gate, and ALL norms high-precision.

## BACKGROUND / WHY
'Which tensors stay high-precision under a footprint budget' is the dual of AF-1's bit-allocation. The plan frames it as **monotone submodular maximization under a knapsack constraint**: accuracy-recovered is submodular in the kept high-precision set (diminishing returns — each additional high-precision tensor recovers less marginal accuracy). **Greedy selection gives a (1 - 1/e) approximation guarantee** — near-optimal, cheap, and *certifiable*, vs the hand-picked 'keep vision/router/norms' heuristic (which greedy should RECOVER and may REFINE). It feeds E-P4 (the int4 split) as a principled, guaranteed alternative/complement to AF-1.

## WHAT TO DO (concrete deliverable, math transcribed)
1. **State / action / loss (the alien contract):** State = the set S of tensors kept high-precision (BF16/F32). Action = greedily add the tensor with the largest marginal accuracy-gain-per-byte until the footprint budget is exhausted. Objective = accuracy recovered f(S), maximized subject to `sum_{t in S} bytes_t <= B` (knapsack).
2. **Marginal gain = the submodular signal:** for a candidate tensor, the marginal gain is the **CER improvement (or layer-output-cosine recovery) when that tensor is promoted from its quantized tier to high precision**, divided by its byte cost (the cost-benefit greedy variant for knapsack constraints).
3. **Greedy with the (1-1/e) guarantee:** run cost-effective greedy (Khuller-Moss-Naor style for knapsack, or the lazy-greedy/CELF acceleration exploiting submodularity to avoid re-evaluating every tensor each round). Emit the chosen set + the **marginal-gain ledger** (each tensor, its marginal gain, the round it was added, the running footprint).
4. **Artifact:** `focr convert --select-high-precision-set --budget` writes the high-precision set into the `.focrq` header (complementary to AF-1's bit_allocation_table) + the marginal-gain ledger to `docs/`.
5. **Wire the deterministic fallback FIRST:** the validated NVFP4/GGUF heuristic set (vision/projector/embeddings/router-gate/norms high-precision; section 2.6, OQ-14). The runtime never depends on greedy having run; without `--select-high-precision-set`, the static recipe ships.
6. **Galaxy-brain card** (equation: max f(S) s.t. knapsack; greedy >= (1-1/e)*OPT for monotone submodular; substituted values: the chosen set, per-tensor marginal gains, the footprint; plain-English: 'add the most accuracy-per-byte tensor next, with a proven near-optimality guarantee'; validity assumptions: f is monotone submodular in the kept set — verify empirically that marginal gains are non-increasing; what would flip: non-submodular interactions (a tensor whose gain INCREASES given others kept) -> the guarantee voids, fall back to heuristic).
7. **Assumptions ledger.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Greedy with a guarantee beats hand-picking:** the heuristic 'keep vision/router/norms' is folklore from prior-art quants; greedy should recover it (validating the folklore) and may refine it at the margin under a tight budget, WITH a (1-1/e) certificate.
- **Lazy/CELF greedy:** submodularity lets us skip re-evaluating tensors whose stale marginal gain already lost — cheap even over the ~2200-tensor menu.
- **Complement to AF-1, vetoed by AF-2:** AF-4 chooses the high-precision SET; AF-1 chooses bit-widths within the quantized remainder; AF-2's tail bound vetoes any set that wrecks the tail. The three compose; AF-4 and AF-1 are two views of the same footprint budget and must agree on the validated invariants (router/norms/vision never demoted).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Submodularity is an ASSUMPTION, not a theorem here:** accuracy-recovered may not be perfectly submodular (cross-tensor coupling). Mitigation: empirically check marginal gains are non-increasing along the greedy path; if a later addition has a larger marginal gain than an earlier one, flag the violation and treat the guarantee as advisory; the PROOF OBLIGATION (greedy CER <= heuristic CER at equal footprint) is the real gate regardless of whether submodularity holds exactly.
- **Must respect the hard invariants:** norms, router gate, and the vision tower are NEVER demoted regardless of marginal gain — these are validated-recipe locks, not negotiable budget items. Encode them as forced-in members of S.
- **Determinism:** greedy ties broken deterministically (by tensor name) so the chosen set is reproducible; round-trip test.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr convert --select-high-precision-set --budget` emits a deterministic high-precision set + marginal-gain ledger.
- **Proof obligation MET:** greedy set's end-to-end CER <= heuristic set's CER at equal footprint on the dense-numeric corpus (and within AF-2's CVaR/EVT bound).
- The forced-in invariants (norms/router/vision) are honored.
- Galaxy-brain card + assumptions ledger committed.
- If the proof fails or submodularity is grossly violated: REVERT to the heuristic set, ledger in `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- **Unit:** cost-effective greedy returns the (1-1/e)-optimal set on a synthetic monotone-submodular knapsack instance; lazy/CELF greedy matches naive greedy; forced-in members are always kept; deterministic tie-breaking.
- **Integration / E2E:** greedy-vs-heuristic CER at equal footprint on the golden corpus, with the AF-2 tail metrics for both; empirical submodularity check (marginal gains non-increasing) logged.
- **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af4/`: the chosen set, per-tensor marginal gains + round + footprint, greedy-vs-heuristic CER + CVaR, provenance, keep/revert decision.
- Dedicated test bead `ALIEN-af4-tests` depends on this task.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: census (tensor set + byte costs) + OQ-14 (the validated heuristic set to beat / fall back to).
- **E-P1**: f32 CER oracle for marginal-gain and proof measurement.
- **E-P2**: the quantizer that produces the quantized remainder once the high-precision set is fixed.
- **E-P4**: AF-4 FEEDS E-P4 (the int4 split); it is complementary to ALIEN-af1 (two views of one budget) and vetoed by ALIEN-af2 (tail bound).

title: AF-4 tests: greedy-(1-1/e) unit + greedy-vs-heuristic high-precision-set proof E2E with structured logging
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-4 only KEEPS if greedy beats (or matches) the validated heuristic set at equal footprint AND honors the recipe invariants. This bead owns the unit proof of the (1-1/e) greedy and the E2E proof obligation, with structured logging.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit:** cost-effective/lazy greedy returns the (1-1/e) set on a synthetic submodular-knapsack instance; lazy/CELF == naive greedy; forced-in invariants (norms/router-gate/vision) always present; deterministic tie-break by tensor name; marginal-gain ledger correctness.
2. **Proof-obligation E2E:** convert at equal footprint with the greedy set vs the NVFP4/GGUF heuristic set; run both through the parity ladder; assert CER(greedy) <= CER(heuristic) and CVaR_0.1(greedy) within budget (calls AF-2).
3. **Submodularity diagnostic:** log whether marginal gains are non-increasing along the greedy path; flag violations.
4. **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af4/`: chosen set, per-tensor marginal gains/round/footprint, greedy-vs-heuristic CER + tail metrics, provenance, keep/revert decision.
5. **Model-gated** skip-with-SUCCESS without weights.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Unit tests deterministic and arch-independent.
- Proof obligation passes when weights present; skips-green when absent.
- Decision reconstructable from artifacts.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **ALIEN-af4-submodular-high-precision-set**: the selector under test.
- **E-VERIFY**: parity-ladder / CER machinery + golden corpus; result feeds the release scorecard.

title: AF-5 SPIKE: Universal Scalability Law many-core pool sizing per (arch, op-class) (feeds E-P3 NUMA)
description:
## RECOMMENDATION CONTRACT (verbatim from plan section 9.7, AF-5; detail in section 6.9)
- **Family:** Queueing theory (Universal Scalability Law) for many-core pool sizing.
- **Tier:** A. **EV:** high, **low effort**. (Impact: prevents the measured anti-win of over-threading memory-bound decode; Confidence: high — USL is a standard, well-fit model; Effort: low — a thread sweep + a 2-parameter fit.)
- **Artifact:** a `pool_sizing` table per **(arch, op-class)** baked from the sweep; **`focr robot backends` reports it**.
- **Proof obligation:** measured throughput at the chosen N >= throughput at `num_cpus`.
- **Deterministic fallback:** **physical-core count.**

## BACKGROUND / WHY
'Make it fly on 64-core Threadrippers' requires an HONEST scaling model, not a blind `par_iter` over 64 threads. The measured reality (section 6.9): **prefill scales with cores; decode does NOT — it is memory-bandwidth-bound.** A single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token; beyond the cores needed to saturate DRAM bandwidth, more threads add NOTHING and HURT via contention. On a 64-core Threadripper this is the difference between decode scaling to ~8-16 effective cores and pointlessly oversubscribing 64 (beta-dominated slowdown). **Over-threading decode is a measured anti-win to ledger.** AF-5 fits the USL and caps each pool at its peak. It directly feeds E-P3's NUMA / many-core scaling work (section 6.9) and is part of the load-bearing trio (AF-1/AF-2/AF-5).

## WHAT TO DO (concrete deliverable, math transcribed)
1. **The model:** **USL** `C(N) = N / (1 + alpha*(N-1) + beta*N*(N-1))` where alpha = **contention** (serialization) and beta = **cross-core coherency/crosstalk**. C(N) is speedup at N threads.
2. **Fit per op-class SEPARATELY:** run a thread sweep at N = 1,2,4,8,16,32,64 for (a) the **decode GEMV** (memory-bound) and (b) the **prefill GEMM** (compute-bound) — they have very different alpha/beta. Fit alpha, beta by least-squares to each sweep.
3. **Find the USL peak:** the N maximizing C(N) (closed form: `N* = sqrt((1-alpha)/beta)`). **Cap each pool at its USL peak**, not at `num_cpus`. Decode is expected to peak far below 64 on a Threadripper (beta-dominated); prefill peaks higher.
4. **Artifact `pool_sizing` table:** per (arch, op-class) the fitted alpha/beta and the chosen N*. Baked from the sweep; **`focr robot backends` reports it** (alongside the detected SIMD tier and core count).
5. **NUMA composition (section 6.9):** the pool cap composes with NUMA — pin each page-worker + its rayon sub-pool to one node; the capacity certificate (section 3.3 / 8.5) must prove `workers * per_worker_threads <= physical-core budget` and **no nested global-pool oversubscription** (the deadlock/oversubscription rule, AGENTS.md doctrine #5). Expose `FOCR_NUMA={replicate|interleave|local}`, default `local` with first-touch.
6. **Wire the deterministic fallback FIRST:** **physical-core count** is the pool size when no fitted table is available (no env, unknown arch). The runtime never requires the sweep to have run.
7. **Galaxy-brain card** (equation: USL + N* = sqrt((1-alpha)/beta); substituted values: fitted alpha/beta and N* per (arch, op-class), the measured speedup curve; plain-English: 'decode is bandwidth-bound, so past ~8-16 cores more threads just contend — cap there'; validity assumptions: the sweep arch == the deploy arch, the workload mix matches the corpus; what would flip: a different memory-bandwidth ceiling or a kernel that becomes compute-bound -> re-sweep).
8. **Assumptions ledger.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Per-op-class fit, not one global pool:** prefill and decode have opposite scaling characters; a single pool size is wrong for one of them. This is the whole point — cap decode low, let prefill scale.
- **USL over Amdahl:** USL's beta term captures the *retrograde* slowdown (throughput DROPS past the peak) that memory-bound decode actually exhibits on many-core NUMA boxes; Amdahl only saturates, it never regresses, so it would mis-predict.
- **robot backends transparency:** the chosen N* is reported so operators/agents can see and override it (`FOCR_THREADS`), and so a regression is visible.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Arch-specificity:** the fit is per (arch, op-class) — an M4 P/E sweep, a Threadripper multi-CCD sweep, and an EPYC dual-socket sweep give different alpha/beta. Ship a table per arch; the fallback (physical cores) covers un-swept arches.
- **Apple Silicon P/E asymmetry (section 6.9):** pin the heavy GEMM pool to P-cores (QoS USER_INTERACTIVE), orchestration on E-cores; the sweep should be over P-cores, not the heterogeneous total.
- **No oversubscription / single live forward:** one global rayon pool sized to the USL peak; the asupersync blocking pool stays tiny; exactly one live forward fans out at a time (deadlock rule). AF-5 sets the pool SIZE; it must not introduce a second pool or nest under a lock.
- **Fit quality:** report R^2 / residuals of the USL fit; a poor fit (non-USL behavior) -> fall back to physical cores and ledger.
- **Determinism of the artifact:** the sweep is timing-based (noisy); use best-of-N with warmup discard and report cv_pct (>5% = noise). The CHOSEN N* must be stable across re-sweeps, or the table is untrustworthy.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `pool_sizing` table per (arch, op-class) with fitted alpha/beta + chosen N*; `focr robot backends` reports it.
- **Proof obligation MET:** measured decode and prefill throughput at the chosen N >= throughput at `num_cpus` (i.e. capping is a win or neutral, never a loss) on the primary arches, with §9.3 fairness controls and cv_pct reported.
- The capacity certificate proves `workers * per_worker_threads <= physical-core budget` and no nested-pool oversubscription under the `many_pages` soak (composes with the deadlock watchdog).
- Deterministic fallback (physical-core count) wired and exercised on an un-swept arch.
- Galaxy-brain card + assumptions ledger committed.
- If capping does not help (or hurts): REVERT to physical cores, ledger the result.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- **Unit:** USL least-squares fit recovers known alpha/beta on synthetic `C(N)` data; N* = sqrt((1-alpha)/beta) closed form matches the argmax of the sampled curve; the fallback returns physical-core count when no table is present.
- **Integration / E2E:** thread-sweep the decode GEMV and prefill GEMM on the available arch, fit USL, choose N*, then assert throughput at N* >= throughput at num_cpus (the proof obligation); assert `robot backends` reports the table; run the `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog with the capped pools to prove no oversubscription/deadlock.
- **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af5/`: the per-N throughput sweep, fitted alpha/beta, R^2, chosen N*, throughput at N* vs num_cpus, cv_pct, arch + CPU feature string, the capacity-certificate result, keep/revert decision.
- Dedicated test bead `ALIEN-af5-tests` depends on this task.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: the census fixes the decode/prefill op shapes (active-param count, GEMM dims) the sweep exercises.
- **E-P3**: AF-5 FEEDS E-P3's NUMA / many-core scaling (section 6.9). It needs E-P3's SIMD decode GEMV + prefill GEMM kernels to exist to sweep them, and the `pool_sizing` table is consumed by E-P3's pool setup + `robot backends`. It composes with the E-P3 NUMA pinning and the capacity certificate. AF-5 is the lowest-effort, highest-certainty of the three Tier-A families.

title: AF-5 tests: USL-fit unit + per-op-class pool-cap proof E2E + capacity-certificate / no-oversubscription
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-5's claim is that capping pools at the USL peak beats `num_cpus` for memory-bound decode and never hurts prefill, with no oversubscription/deadlock. This bead proves the math, the throughput win, and the capacity certificate, with structured logging.

## WHAT TO DO
1. **Unit:** USL least-squares recovers known alpha/beta on synthetic data; N* = sqrt((1-alpha)/beta) matches the curve argmax; fallback returns physical-core count with no table; deterministic table round-trip.
2. **Proof-obligation E2E:** sweep decode GEMV + prefill GEMM at N=1,2,4,8,16,32,64 on the available arch; fit USL; choose N*; assert throughput(N*) >= throughput(num_cpus) for BOTH op-classes (capping is a win or neutral, never a loss), with §9.3 fairness (best-of-N, warmup discard, cv_pct reported, >5% flagged as noise).
3. **robot backends E2E:** assert `focr robot backends` reports the `pool_sizing` table (alpha/beta/N* per op-class) plus the detected SIMD tier and core count, as valid versioned JSON.
4. **Capacity-certificate / oversubscription E2E:** run `many_pages_without_deadlock` (pages >> pool) with the capped pools; assert no hang, and that `workers * per_worker_threads <= physical-core budget` with no nested global-pool fan-out (composes with the deadlock watchdog).
5. **Structured logging** under `tests/artifacts/af5/`: per-N throughput, fitted alpha/beta + R^2, N*, throughput(N*) vs num_cpus, cv_pct, arch + CPU feature string, capacity-certificate result, keep/revert decision.
6. **Model-gated** skip-with-SUCCESS without weights for the full-forward sweep; the USL-fit unit + synthetic-kernel sweep run without weights.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- USL-fit unit deterministic; throughput proof obligation holds on the primary arch.
- `robot backends` reports the table; `many_pages` watchdog stays unhung with capped pools.
- Decision reconstructable from artifacts.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **ALIEN-af5-usl-pool-sizing**: the fitter + pool-cap under test.
- **E-P3**: the SIMD decode GEMV / prefill GEMM kernels being swept, the pool setup, and `robot backends`.

title: MoE linear helper: checked shape arithmetic and contiguous transpose stores
description:
Fresh-eyes audit found src/native_engine/moe.rs::linear_no_bias still computing out*in with unchecked usize multiplication in both the weight-length guard and transpose allocation. That can panic or wrap before returning a clean FocrError on impossible dimensions, unlike the hardened decoder/connector helpers. The same helper also writes the transposed weight with strided stores; while the real prepacked .focrq fix remains bd-2e4b and is gated on weight accessors, this local helper can use contiguous destination rows now with bit-identical output and no perf claim.

Scope:
- Add a checked_shape_mul helper or equivalent in moe.rs.
- Use one computed expected_weight_len for guard and allocation.
- Reorder the transpose loop so each wt row is written contiguously.
- Add focused tests for overflow-as-error and transpose equivalence.
- Do not update docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md unless an attempted optimization is rejected; this accepted locality cleanup will be documented in the bead with verification evidence rather than claimed as a measured win.
close_reason: MoE linear_no_bias now rejects out*in overflow cleanly, writes transpose destination rows contiguously, and has focused tests plus full gate evidence.

comments.author: WhiteCave
comments.text:
Implemented MoE linear helper hardening/locality cleanup. src/native_engine/moe.rs now computes out*in once via checked_shape_mul, returns a clean FocrError on usize overflow, reuses the checked expected length for allocation, and writes the transposed [in,out] buffer with contiguous destination rows. Added focused tests for multi-row/non-square PyTorch-linear semantics and overflow-as-error. Also converted the MoE module tests from unwrap/unwrap_err happy paths to FocrResult/matches so UBS reports no unwrap/expect inventory in this file. No NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry was added because no optimization was rejected and no numeric performance claim was made; bd-2e4b remains the gated .focrq prepack perf lane. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fmt --check; git diff --check; python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py; br dep cycles --json => count 0; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config 'patch.crates-io.asupersync.path="/dp/asupersync"' test native_engine::moe --lib; same check --all-targets; same clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; same full cargo test (461 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored in lib; all integration suites passed); required UBS file-list form 'ubs .beads/issues.jsonl
src/native_engine/moe.rs' is unsupported by installed UBS v5.0.3 and exits usage 2; supported final scan 'ubs --only=rust --diff --ci' exited 0 with Critical 0, Warning 36, Info 14. Known nonblocking output during Cargo gates: tokenizers AppleDouble auto-clean race and sibling frankensqlite unused core_intrinsics warnings.

title: Vision CLIP linear helper: contiguous transpose stores and focused non-square proof
