title: Phase 3 — Multi-arch SIMD kernels (the make-it-FLY epic)
description:
# E-P3 — Phase 3: multi-arch SIMD kernels ("make it FLY")

## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is a pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for ONE model (Baidu Unlimited-OCR, a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). Phases -1/0/1/2 establish the truth pack, scaffold, an fp32 reference-parity forward, and a weight-only int8 decoder. **Phase 3 is where the int8 GEMMs run on native intrinsics across arches and the DECODE path is demonstrably faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference** — the defensible, gating part of G2 (end-to-end-faster is a later/optional stretch). Goal Phase 3 serves: max CPU speed for THIS one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels, correctness before speed (G1 > G2), portable single binary, agent-ergonomic.

## THE MEASURED WEDGE (plan §3.2)
The sibling reranker campaign (frankensearch on frankentorch, M4) is the live measured guide. **The bar is ONNX-int8/MLAS: 7.6 / 14.5 / 41.4 ms per doc at seq 128/256/512.** Ratchet: scalar 23/53/146 → SDOT (bit-identical, 16 MAC/instr) 14.8/34/64 → register-blocked SDOT (4×4 tile) 12.4/28/56 — now ~1.4–1.9× behind ONNX. Lessons that bind this epic (NE-INH-1..5): (1) The gap is kernels-below-peak, NOT framework overhead — a naive fused scalar-f32 forward regressed 3–10×. (2) Un-blocked SMMLA is a TRAP: a tiled SMMLA with 2× MAC density but no register blocking was SLOWER than SDOT (19/41/77) because it is load-bound (≈2 loads:1 SMMLA); the win requires register/cache blocking (compute:load ≥ 2:1) + offline pre-packing. (3) int8 attention is a named lever ONNX uses; f32 attention leaves ~20–33% of matmuls un-accelerated. (4) AMX-f32 does NOT beat ONNX-int8 (bandwidth-bound); AMX must be int8 and helps compute-bound prefill, not decode; Apple's AMX is not directly programmable (NEON SMMLA/SDOT is the no-FFI Mac path; Accelerate/BNNS is FFI/opt-in).

## SCOPE (plan §10 Phase 3)
Build the per-arch SIMD dispatch catalog (§6.6): tiled SMMLA/AMX prefill GEMM + SDOT/VNNI decode GEMV, runtime feature dispatch (robot backends reflects it); offline arch-specific weight pre-packing (--arch); MoE token-grouping (§6.7); online-softmax R-SWA (§6.8); int8 attention behind FOCR_INT8_ATTN/CER gate; fusion (§6.10); vectorized transcendentals (§6.11); NUMA + USL many-core pool sizing (§6.9/AF-5); memory/allocator (§6.12); PGO + BOLT (§6.13); profile-first → §9.2 optimization loop on per-regime ranked hot ops; gauntlet head-to-head vs reference (§8.5, §9.3); roofline/compute-floor analysis (§9.1).

## ACCEPTANCE (exit gates, plan §10 Phase 3 + §12 G2)
- **Bit-identical to scalar** (integer accumulation is exact/associative) for every SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI/AMX path; AVX2 carries its own overflow proof or a ledgered DISCREPANCIES entry.
- **Gauntlet per-stage ratios recorded with §9.3 fairness controls, and decode-per-token is faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference** on primary arches (vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 acceptable, recorded honestly).
- Every landed lever has a NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md / PERF_LEDGER.md entry.
- Parity (L0–L5) still green on every perf commit.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS
G1 > G2 always — a faster kernel that drifts OCR output is reverted, no source landed, ledgered. Deadlock rule: single OcrModel behind cache, sequential outer page loop, forward fans out internally via the kernel rayon pool; NEVER nest rayon under a held lock, NEVER nest a 2nd asupersync runtime; many_pages_without_deadlock watchdog. i32-overflow is a proof obligation (worst case dense layer-0 down_proj K=6848). #![forbid(unsafe_code)] except named SIMD islands, each with a bit-identical scalar fallback that cross-compiles to every target.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on E-P2 (the int8 weight-only decoder + .focrq format + per-row dynamic activation quant are the substrate every Phase-3 kernel accelerates) and E-VERIFY (the parity ladder L0–L5, the bit-identical-to-scalar gate harness, the gauntlet fairness controls, and the CER gates are the acceptance machinery; no kernel lands without them).

title: Runtime ISA dispatch: one int8_gemm/int8_gemv entrypoint, IsaTier in OnceLock
description:
# P3-isa-dispatch — runtime ISA dispatch core

## BACKGROUND / WHY
We ship ONE portable binary per arch and select SIMD at RUNTIME (so `target-cpu=native` is wrong — it would break the cross-compile floor; we use `target-feature` per target and gate the rest with `is_*_feature_detected!`, plan §6.13). Every Phase-3 kernel hangs off a single dispatch decision so backends are uniform, testable, and reflected to agents.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement one `int8_gemm` (prefill, compute-bound) and one `int8_gemv` (decode, BW-bound) entrypoint, runtime-dispatched ONCE per process and cached in a `OnceLock<IsaTier>` (plan §6.6). Define the `IsaTier` enum exactly per the catalog:
- **A1** aarch64 `i8mm` → SMMLA `vmmlaq_s32` prefill, SDOT decode.
- **A2** aarch64 `dotprod` → SDOT `vdotq_s32` (16 MAC/instr) blocked, SDOT decode.
- **X1** x86 `avx512vnni+avx512bw` → VPDPBUSD `_mm512_dpbusd_epi32` (U8S8, 64 MAC/instr).
- **X2** x86 `avxvnni` → `_mm256_dpbusd_epi32` (256-bit).
- **X3** x86 SPR+ `amx-int8` → `_tile_dpbssd` 16×16×64 (prefill only; decode uses X1).
- **X4** x86 `avx2` → `_mm256_maddubs_epi16`→`_mm256_madd_epi16` (saturating — carries its own overflow proof, see P3-avx2-overflow-proof).
- **S** scalar i32 MAC — always built (cross-compile floor).
Dispatch order: **X3(AMX) > X1(VNNI-512) > X2(VNNI-256) > X4(AVX2)** on x86; **A1(SMMLA) > A2(SDOT)** on ARM; **S** everywhere as the build-always fallback. Gate detection on `is_aarch64_feature_detected!("i8mm")`/`("dotprod")` and `is_x86_feature_detected!(...)`. Expose the selected tier (and a forced-tier override env `FOCR_ISA_TIER` for testing/oracle comparisons) so `focr robot backends` can print it (see P3-robot-backends).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- One dispatch, cached: avoids re-detecting per call; the hot loop never branches on ISA. (§6.6: "runtime-dispatched once per process, cached in a OnceLock<IsaTier>".)
- Bit-identical across paths: i32 accumulation is exact/associative, so SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI/AMX/scalar all return the same i32 — tolerance is needed only for int8-vs-f32 drift, NOT for the SIMD path (NE shipped lesson).
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` everywhere except named SIMD island modules behind `#[allow(unsafe_code, unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn)]`, each load with a `// SAFETY:` note; the scalar fallback cross-compiles to every target so the binary builds even where intrinsics are absent.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The forced-tier override must be able to select S (scalar) so the bit-identical gate can compare every tier against scalar on the same host.
- Detection must be robust on hosts where AVX-512 is present but the OS doesn't enable it (use the std feature detection, not raw CPUID).
- nightly toolchain required for stdarch i8mm/dotprod intrinsics + portable_simd (plan toolchain). Confine nightly portable_simd to kernel islands.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A single `int8_gemm`/`int8_gemv` API compiles on all 5 targets (linux x86-64/arm64, darwin x86-64/arm64, windows-msvc x86-64).
- `FOCR_ISA_TIER` can force any tier (incl. S) at runtime.
- On every host, the selected tier matches the highest available in the documented order.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: tier-selection table test (mock/override feature flags) asserts the documented order; an `IsaTier::Scalar` round-trips a tiny known GEMM to a hand-computed i32 result. See P3-isa-dispatch-tests for the cross-tier bit-identical harness. Structured logging: every tier-selection emits a one-line NDJSON `{event:"isa_selected", tier, features:[...]}`.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on E-P2 (consumes the int8 weight/activation layout + scales the int8 decoder produced) and E-PM1 (the truth pack pins the exact shapes 1280/896/6848/128 and the i32-overflow census the dispatch's kernels rely on).

title: Cross-tier bit-identical harness for int8_gemm/int8_gemv
description:
# P3-isa-dispatch-tests — every tier == scalar, bit for bit

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The central correctness invariant of the whole epic: i32 accumulation is exact/associative, so EVERY native int8 path must return the SAME i32 as the scalar reference. This harness is the gate that every other Phase-3 kernel test reuses.

## WHAT TO DO
Build a test harness that, for each tier available on the host AND for `FOCR_ISA_TIER`-forced lower tiers, runs `int8_gemm` and `int8_gemv` over a battery of shapes — at least {K=1280 (q/k/v/o, gate, up, lm_head), K=896 (expert down_proj), K=6848 (dense layer-0 down_proj, the worst case)} and asserts bit-identical i32 (and bit-identical final dequantized f32 given fixed scales) vs the scalar path. Include adversarial saturated operands (all ±127 / U8S8 0..255). EXCLUDE the AVX2 path from the bit-identical assertion (it has separate overflow semantics — see P3-avx2-overflow-proof) but include it in a separate "within-documented-tolerance" check.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Forced-tier override (P3-isa-dispatch) lets a single CI host exercise scalar + whatever native tiers it has, comparing all to scalar.
- Worst-case K=6848 is mandatory in the battery because the i32-overflow proof obligation lives there (plan §5.4, do NOT inherit frankensearch's k≤1536 bound).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- For every non-AVX2 tier on the host: `assert_eq!(tier_i32, scalar_i32)` for all shapes incl. saturated operands.
- For AVX2: documented-tolerance check passes OR a DISCREPANCIES entry exists.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Emits structured NDJSON per case `{event:"bitident", tier, k, n, m, ok, max_abs_i32_diff}` so a CI log shows the full matrix.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (it tests that entrypoint and the IsaTier override).

title: Scalar int8 GEMM/GEMV fallback (cross-compile floor, bit-exact oracle)
description:
# P3-scalar-floor — the build-always, bit-exact reference path

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Every SIMD kernel must have a bit-identical scalar fallback that CROSS-COMPILES TO EVERY TARGET (G4, plan §6.6 tier S). This scalar path is also the correctness ORACLE every other tier is checked against, and the path selected at runtime on hosts without any accelerated tier.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the S tier: scalar i32 MAC GEMM + GEMV, row-major, per-row scale, i32 accumulate, scales post-accumulation, +bias, activation. NO unsafe, NO intrinsics — compiles on all 5 targets. This is the definition of the int8 i32 semantics that every native tier must match exactly.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Tight scalar loop with i32 accumulation; let LLVM autovectorize (the §6.2 doctrine: do NOT hand-wide-SIMD the scalar loop — that measured 5× slower; the win is native matmul intrinsics in the OTHER tiers).
- Must be the literal oracle for the bit-identical gate (P3-isa-dispatch-tests).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Compiles on all 5 targets; produces the i32 semantics all native tiers match; selected when no native tier is available; i32-overflow-safe at K=6848 (i64 cross-check in P3-i32-overflow-proof).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: hand-computed tiny GEMM equals the scalar output; the worst-case-K i64 cross-check (P3-i32-overflow-proof). depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (the S tier entrypoint).

title: i32-accumulation overflow proof at worst-case K=6848 on every arch
description:
# P3-i32-overflow-proof — the proof obligation, not an assumption

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Doctrine rule 6: int8 i32-accumulation overflow is a PROOF OBLIGATION. The real worst case for THIS model is the dense layer-0 `down_proj` at **K = 6848** (U8S8 ≤ ~221.7M; S8S8 ≤ 110.4M); it fits i32 (max 2,147,483,647) with ≥9× headroom but must be proven by a unit test at worst-case K on EVERY arch. **Do NOT inherit frankensearch's k≤1536 bound** (plan §5.4).

## WHAT TO DO
A unit test that multiplies worst-case SATURATED operands at K=6848 on every kernel/arch path (scalar, SMMLA, SDOT, VNNI-512, AVX-VNNI, AMX, AVX2-with-split) and asserts the i32 result equals an i64 reference (and that NO path silently saturates). Per the census from §5.4: attention q/k/v/o, expert/dense gate/up, lm_head all K=1280 (S8S8 ≤20.6M, U8S8 ≤41.4M); expert down_proj K=896 (S8S8 ≤14.5M, U8S8 ≤29.0M); dense layer-0 down_proj K=6848 (S8S8 ≤110.4M, U8S8 ≤221.7M). If any future fused/grouped/accumulating path approaches i32::MAX, switch THAT path to i64/blocked accumulation.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Every path's i32 result == i64 reference at K=6848 with saturated operands; no silent saturation; documented headroom recorded.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Structured NDJSON `{event:"i32_overflow", path, k, s8s8_max, u8s8_max, matches_i64}`. depends_on P3-scalar-floor + P3-smmla-prefill-gemm + P3-sdot-decode-gemv + P3-vnni512-gemm (the paths proven; AVX2/AMX added as they land) and E-PM1 (the K-census that fixes the worst case).

title: MoE prefill token-grouping: counting-sort → dense per-expert GEMMs
description:
# P3-moe-token-grouping — the biggest STRUCTURAL prefill MoE lever

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The MoE FFN is the bulk of the 3B params and the dominant decode cost. Naively each token gathers its 6-of-64 routed experts and does six separate small GEMVs — memory-bound, poor register reuse. Token-grouping converts those scattered GEMVs into dense GEMMs with full register-tile reuse (the "DeepSeek MoE on Xeon AMX/VNNI" grouped-GEMM pattern, plan §6.7).

## WHAT TO DO
For **prefill** (many tokens at once — prompt + all vision tokens): after the router assigns each of the T prefill tokens its top-6 experts, **sort/group tokens by expert id via a counting-sort over 64 buckets, O(T)**, then run **ONE dense int8 GEMM per active expert** over its grouped token-block (`tokens_e × 1280 → tokens_e × 896`, SwiGLU, → 1280), and scatter results back. This turns 64 scattered GEMVs into ≤64 dense SMMLA/AMX/VNNI GEMMs (using P3-smmla-prefill-gemm / P3-vnni512-gemm / P3-amx-prefill-gemm). Config facts (truth pack): 64 routed experts, top-6, 2 shared experts, hidden 1280, moe_intermediate 896, each expert a 1280→896 SiLU-gated MLP (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Counting-sort over 64 buckets is O(T), trivially correct, deterministic — no comparison sort.
- Bit-exact: grouping is just a permutation; the scatter restores token order, so the result is identical to per-token GEMVs (gate by the isomorphism proof).
- The grouped GEMM feeds the register-blocked prefill kernels so each expert's weight panel is reused across all its grouped tokens (the compute:load ≥ 2:1 win).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The router stays f32, never quantized (quantizing the gate drifts expert selection → cliff, §6.7) — the grouping consumes the f32 top-6 result.
- Decode (one token) cannot batch tokens — that is a SEPARATE strategy (P3-moe-decode): pre-packed cache-contiguous per (layer,expert) + software prefetch.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-identical decoded output vs the per-token-GEMV path; counting-sort verified O(T); prefill MoE measurably faster than scattered GEMVs.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: counting-sort correctness + scatter restores order; grouped-GEMM result == per-token GEMV result (bit-identical). Perf bead P3-moe-grouping-perf. Structured NDJSON `{event:"moe_group", T, active_experts, ms, ratio_vs_scattered}`. depends_on P3-smmla-prefill-gemm + P3-vnni512-gemm (the dense per-expert GEMMs it dispatches) and E-PM1 (expert count/dims/top-k census).

title: Perf: grouped per-expert GEMMs vs scattered GEMVs (prefill MoE)
description:
# P3-moe-grouping-perf

Criterion bench of the counting-sort grouped per-expert dense GEMMs vs naive scattered per-token GEMVs on realistic prefill token counts (256/4096 vision tokens + prompt). Best-of-N, ledger keep/revert. Acceptance: grouped path materially faster, no accuracy change. depends_on P3-moe-token-grouping.

title: MoE decode: cache-contiguous per-(layer,expert) packing + software prefetch
description:
# P3-moe-decode — minimize bytes streamed per token

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Decode (one token) cannot batch tokens, so the only lever is minimizing bytes streamed — decode is memory-bandwidth-bound and the MoE FFN is the bulk of params (§6.7).

## WHAT TO DO
Pre-pack experts **cache-contiguous per (layer, expert)** so the 8 active experts (6 routed + 2 shared) load as contiguous int4/int8 blocks; **software-prefetch the next expert's weights while computing the current**; the router (f32) selects which blocks to touch. Each expert: 1280→896 SiLU-gated MLP (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj) via P3-sdot-decode-gemv (ARM) / P3-vnni512-gemm GEMV (x86). Fused SwiGLU (P3-fusion-swiglu) shares the input activation quant. int4 here (Phase 4) halves the streamed bytes — the direct decode-throughput lever, but Phase 3 ships int8.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Contiguous per-(layer,expert) layout + prefetch is the whole decode-MoE win on BW-bound hardware.
- Router stays f32 (never quantized).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-identical decoded output; decode-MoE faster than the non-prefetched contiguous baseline; ledgered.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: 8-active-expert gather correctness. Perf: prefetch on/off A/B. depends_on P3-sdot-decode-gemv + P3-vnni512-gemm (the GEMVs), P3-weight-prepack (the per-(layer,expert) contiguous packing), E-PM1 (expert layout census).

title: Online (FlashAttention-style) softmax R-SWA kernel over the reference block
description:
# P3-online-softmax-rswa — cache-efficient R-SWA at 40-page scale

## BACKGROUND / WHY
R-SWA score set per decode step is `Q · [reference-K (length m, up to ~32K) ∪ ring-K (128)]`. Materializing the full 1×m score row per head per layer is wasteful and cache-hostile at m≈32K × 10 heads × 12 layers. Online softmax (the FlashAttention recurrence) streams the reference-K/V block in cache-sized tiles, maintaining running (m_max, l_sum, acc) per head — never materializing the full score vector, never a second pass. This is the kernel that makes the constant-MEMORY / compute-grows-with-pages R-SWA (§6.4) actually cache-efficient at scale (§6.8).

## HONEST CAVEAT (doctrine rule 7)
R-SWA gives **constant generated-token KV (a 128-ring), NOT constant per-token COMPUTE**. The reference block m grows ~256 tokens per page (base mode: 256 image-feature tokens + structural/prompt — 273 slots at 1024 before prompt) and is attended EVERY decode step, so per-token attention is O(m+128) and its compute scales linearly with page count up to the 32K cap. Do NOT market decode as constant-time per token across document sizes.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the online-softmax R-SWA decode kernel (`rswa.rs`): stream the reference-K/V block in cache-sized tiles, maintain running (m_max, l_sum, acc) per head, single pass; handle the 128-wide ring window as a tail tile. Pre-allocate, per layer, ONE fixed buffer sized for the WORST-CASE m (≈32768−128): reference KV block (length m, visual+prompt, read-only for the whole generation, never grows/evicts) + a 128-wide ring buffer for generated-token KV. Decode step t: compute Q for the new token; scores = (Q·reference-K) ∪ (Q·ring-K); softmax over the union (online); weighted sum of (reference-V ∪ ring-V); write new K,V into the ring at slot = prefill_len + (t % 128) after warm-up. Plain MHA (no MLA): q/k/v/o_proj separate dense GEMMs, 10 heads, head_dim 128. The online-softmax STRUCTURE is independent of the OQ answers, but the exact MASK is gated by OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-3/OQ-13.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Online softmax: bounded working set (one K/V tile + accumulator), single pass, composes with an int8 Q·Kᵀ GEMM (the reference block is large enough to be a real GEMM, not a GEMV).
- Preallocated worst-case buffers: no realloc in the hot loop, trivially memory-safe (§6.12 buffer reuse).
- f32 attention in v1 for parity; int8 attention is a separate gated lever (P3-int8-attn).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **[OPEN] OQ-1/OQ-2/OQ-3/OQ-13 gate the exact mask** — no kernel ships against an unresolved OQ. The kernel must consume the mask semantics those OQs pin (reference set m visual-only vs visual+prompt; whether all 12 layers are R-SWA incl. layer 0; warm-up vs ring-overwrite during the first 128 steps; multi-page cross-page span).
- Multi-page decode is cross-page dependent (OQ-13) — do NOT assume multi-page = sum of single-page parses.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-exact (same arithmetic) vs the Phase-1 two-pass f32 softmax within f32 tolerance; per-layer cosine ≥ 0.9999; decoded tokens exact where reference deterministic; bounded working set demonstrated; no realloc in the hot loop; mask matches the pinned OQ answers.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: online-softmax recurrence == two-pass softmax on random scores (within f32 ulp budget). Integration: R-SWA decode output == Phase-1 f32 R-SWA output on the golden corpus. Perf bead P3-rswa-perf (cache-residency at small m, streamed at large m; scaling sweep over page count). Structured NDJSON per decode step `{event:"rswa", layer, m, ring_fill, ms}`. depends_on E-P2 (the int8 substrate + Q·Kᵀ GEMM), E-PM1 (the R-SWA OQ answers + buffer-sizing census OQ-18), E-VERIFY (the per-layer cosine + decoded-token gates).

title: Perf: online-softmax R-SWA cache-residency + page-count scaling sweep
description:
# P3-rswa-perf

Bench online-softmax R-SWA vs the two-pass f32 baseline across m ∈ {small (1 page ~273), 10-page, 40-page (toward 32K)} to show (a) cache-residency at small m, (b) graceful streaming at large m, (c) the honest O(m+128) compute growth with page count. Best-of-N. Ledger. Acceptance: online softmax is faster than two-pass at scale and bounded-working-set holds; the per-page compute growth is recorded honestly (NOT marketed as constant-time). depends_on P3-online-softmax-rswa.

title: int8 attention (Q·Kᵀ, scores·V) behind FOCR_INT8_ATTN + CER gate
description:
# P3-int8-attn — the lever ONNX uses that f32 attention leaves on the table

## BACKGROUND / WHY
ONNX/MLAS runs the `Q·Kᵀ` and `scores·V` bmm in int8; an f32 attention leaves ~20–33% of the matmuls un-accelerated, and that is a MEASURED part of the reranker's remaining gap (§3.2 lesson 3, §6.8). This is a named Phase-3 perf lever — NOT a v1 default.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement int8 `Q·Kᵀ` and `scores·V` for: (a) **vision attention** (prefill, compute-bound, many tokens) on SMMLA/VNNI — a real, lower-risk lever; (b) **R-SWA decode attention** — more delicate because the scores feed softmax/exp (error-amplifying), so it goes behind a `FOCR_INT8_ATTN` kill-switch with a MEASURED-CER gate (AF-2 tail-risk: bound CVaR_0.1, not just mean). v1 keeps attention f32 for parity; int8 attention defaults ON only after a measured A/B + passing CER gate, and is ledgered. Note: int8 on attention q/k/v/o goes BEYOND the validated weight-only set (doctrine rule 2 / OQ-14) — never assume lossless.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Two-tier: vision int8 attention is lower risk (compute-bound, many tokens); R-SWA decode int8 attention is gated harder because softmax amplifies score error.
- The CER gate uses CVaR_α (mean of worst α-fraction of per-doc CER) + EVT tail, not mean CER, because OCR fails in the tail (dense tables/sub-scripts/code), and perplexity under-predicts exact-token failure (AF-2).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Kill-switch FOCR_INT8_ATTN must default to OFF (f32) until the gate passes; a regression reverts the default, ledgered in DISCREPANCIES.md with the measured CER delta + review date.
- OQ-14 determines whether NVFP4 even quantizes q/k/v/o — reconcile before claiming "validated."

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Vision int8 attention bit-identical-where-integer + within CER budget; R-SWA int8 attention only enabled if CVaR_0.1 within the ledgered budget of f32; kill-switch wired; perf win measured.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: int8 Q·Kᵀ bit-identical i32 to scalar. CER gate test: dense-numeric/table corpus CVaR_0.1 within budget with FOCR_INT8_ATTN on. Perf bead. Structured NDJSON. depends_on P3-smmla-prefill-gemm + P3-vnni512-gemm (the int8 bmm engines), P3-online-softmax-rswa (the attention it accelerates), E-VERIFY (the CER/CVaR gate), E-PM1 (OQ-14 validated-set reconciliation).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline 'TESTS REQUIRED' for this numerics-affecting lever is now owned by a dedicated, dependency-tracked bead **P3-r1-int8-attn-tests** (bit-identity i32 vs scalar across ISA tiers + the CVaR_0.1 / EVT-p999 tail-CER gate that decides whether FOCR_INT8_ATTN may default on). That gate MUST be green before the kill-switch defaults to ON; a regression reverts the default with a DISCREPANCIES entry. Do not flip the default on the strength of mean-CER alone — the gate is on the tail.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — the vision-int8-attention half is OQ-11-gated and needs the f32 vision attention as oracle
The '(a) vision attention (prefill)' lever in this bead is NOT free in v1: plan §6.4 keeps the **vision tower BF16/F32 in v1** ('ViT post-LayerNorm/post-GELU activations have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant') and explicitly defers int8 vision — 'revisit int8 vision (per-channel weights + per-token dynamic act + outlier-aware/register-prefix) ONLY after decoder parity is proven, as an isolated experiment' (OQ-11). Therefore: (1) the vision-int8-attention path is **gated on OQ-11** and must default OFF until OQ-11 resolves int8 viability for the vision tower; (2) it accelerates the SAM windowed/global + CLIP SDPA attention built in P1-sam-window-global-attn / P1-clip-tower (now wired as dependencies) and is compared against those f32 kernels as the oracle; (3) keep the two tiers explicitly separate — the R-SWA decode int8 attention (FOCR_INT8_ATTN + CVaR_0.1 CER gate) is the harder, error-amplifying path and is independent of the vision-int8 OQ-11 decision. Do not let an implementer ship int8 vision attention by default on the strength of 'ONNX does it' while the validated v1 recipe keeps vision f32.

title: TEST — int8 attention (Q·Kᵀ, scores·V) bit-identity + CVaR_0.1 tail-CER gate with FOCR_INT8_ATTN, structured NDJSON
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
P3-int8-attn is a NUMERICS-AFFECTING perf lever that goes BEYOND the validated weight-only int8 set (doctrine rule 2 / OQ-14): it quantizes the `Q·Kᵀ` and `scores·V` matmuls, and the scores feed softmax/exp which AMPLIFIES error and can flip a token. The bead carries only an inline 'TESTS REQUIRED' note. Because this lever defaults ON only after a measured A/B + a passing CER gate, that gate must be a durable, dependency-tracked bead — otherwise the kill-switch could be flipped on without the tail-risk proof, silently corrupting dense tables/sub-scripts/code (where OCR fails in the tail, per AF-2).

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/int8_attn_parity.rs`:
1. **Bit-identity (integer matmul)**: assert int8 `Q·Kᵀ` and `scores·V` produce bit-identical i32 to the scalar reference across every ISA tier (reuse P3-isa-dispatch-tests battery), incl. saturated operands. This is the per-op floor; it does NOT prove OCR accuracy.
2. **Vision-attention path (lower risk)**: prefill, compute-bound — assert within the documented int8 tolerance vs f32 attention (cosine ≥ documented int8 bar, max_abs_diff ledgered).
3. **R-SWA decode-attention CER gate (the teeth)**: with FOCR_INT8_ATTN=1, run the dense-numeric/table/code exact-token-sensitive corpus (P4-dense-corpus once available; a seed corpus otherwise) and assert **CVaR_0.1 (mean of the worst 10% of per-document CER) within the ledgered budget of f32** AND the EVT-p999 tail bound holds — NOT just mean CER. With the switch OFF, attention stays f32 (assert the default is OFF until the gate passes).
4. **Kill-switch wiring**: assert FOCR_INT8_ATTN default OFF; flipping it changes the dispatched path (visible in robot backends / simd_tier logs); a regression reverts the default with a DISCREPANCIES entry + review date.

## DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (mandatory)
Via TestLog: per-op `stage` line `{event:'stage', stage:'decode'|'prefill', op:'qk'|'scores_v', shapes, dtype:'i8', simd_tier, elapsed_us}`; per-op `parity` line `{event:'parity', gate:'L1', metric:'max_abs_i32_diff'|'cosine', value, tolerance, pass}`; the CER gate emits `{event:'parity', gate:'L5', metric:'cvar_0.1'|'evt_p999'|'mean_cer', value, budget, int8_attn:true, pass}` per document + a summary. schema_version present; validated against TEST-log-conventions.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Two-tier test mirrors the two-tier lever: vision int8 attention is lower risk (compute-bound, many tokens), R-SWA decode int8 attention is gated harder because softmax amplifies score error.
- The gate uses CVaR_α + EVT, not mean CER, because perplexity/mean under-predicts exact-token failure (AF-2); a mean-only gate would green-light a quant that wrecks the tail.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- OQ-14 must be reconciled first (does NVFP4 even quantize q/k/v/o?) — if not, this is a franken_ocr-original divergence and MUST carry a DISCREPANCIES entry regardless of the gate result.
- Model-gated for the CER corpus (skip-with-SUCCESS without weights, prove native path ran); the bit-identity floor runs model-free on synthetic tensors (always-on).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- int8 Q·Kᵀ and scores·V bit-identical i32 to scalar on every non-AVX2 tier.
- Vision int8 attention within documented int8 tolerance of f32.
- R-SWA int8 decode attention only passes if CVaR_0.1 AND EVT-p999 within the ledgered budget of f32 on the dense-numeric corpus.
- FOCR_INT8_ATTN default OFF; flip is visible in logs; regression reverts + ledgers.
- Every case emits a schema-valid NDJSON line.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Self-test: an injected score-quant error that flips a token makes the CER gate FAIL.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P3-int8-attn (under test), P3-isa-dispatch-tests (bit-identity battery), E-VERIFY (the CVaR/EVT tail-risk gate machinery, AF-2), TEST-log-emitter (logging).

title: Fusion: dequant → int8-GEMM → bias → activation (one pass, in-register)
description:
# P3-fusion-dequant-gemm

## BACKGROUND / WHY
No framework boundary forbids fusion (§3.1, §6.10). Each fusion removes a memory round-trip of the activation; all are bit-exact (same arithmetic, fewer materializations).

## WHAT TO DO
Fuse dequant → int8-GEMM → bias → activation into ONE pass with the activation produced in registers, replacing 4 passes over the output (§6.10). The dequant is `i32_acc · scale_w[chan] · scale_a[row]`, then +bias, then activation, all before the output leaves registers. Applies to every quantized linear (expert gate/up/down, attention proj, lm_head). MUST stay at peak — never a naive scalar variant (NE-INH-5).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Bit-exact: same arithmetic, fewer materializations — gated by the isomorphism proof.
- One pass keeps the output tile resident; the activation is applied while the i32→f32 dequant result is still in registers.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-identical output vs the unfused dequant+GEMM+bias+act sequence; one fewer pass measured; parity green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: fused == unfused, bit-identical. Perf: pass-count + ms A/B. depends_on the GEMM/GEMV kernels it fuses into (P3-smmla-prefill-gemm, P3-sdot-decode-gemv, P3-vnni512-gemm).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline 'fused == unfused' obligation is now owned by the shared, dependency-tracked bead **P3-r1-fusion-parity-tests** (bit-identity vs the unfused dequant→GEMM→bias→act sequence across every ISA tier incl. saturated operands, AVX2 excluded from bit-identity). The fusion may not land without that gate green.

title: Fusion: RMSNorm → RoPE (one pass over the hidden)
description:
# P3-fusion-rmsnorm-rope

## BACKGROUND / WHY
RMSNorm+RoPE fused replaces 2 passes over the hidden with 1 (RoPE reads the normed Q/K live, §6.10). Also fuse residual-add → next RMSNorm (2 passes → 1).

## WHAT TO DO
Fuse RMSNorm → RoPE so RoPE consumes the normed Q/K without a second pass over the hidden. Norms stay HIGH PRECISION (BF16/F32) — ALL norms and the router gate are never quantized (doctrine rule 2). RoPE uses the pinned rope_theta (OQ-5; DeepseekV2 default 10000 assumed — confirm whether YARN/NTK is active at 32K) and head_dim (OQ-4, likely 128). Autovectorize the elementwise glue (the §6.2 doctrine — do NOT hand-wide-SIMD norm/softmax), f64 accumulation where precision matters. Also implement residual-add → next-RMSNorm fusion.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- [OPEN] OQ-4 (head dim) / OQ-5 (rope_theta, YARN/NTK) gate the RoPE kernel — no kernel ships against an unresolved OQ.
- A wrong norm (quantized) silently corrupts everything — keep norms high-precision.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-exact vs unfused RMSNorm-then-RoPE within f32 tolerance; per-layer cosine ≥ 0.9999; one fewer pass.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: fused == unfused. Perf: pass-count A/B. depends_on E-P2 (the f32/bf16 norm+RoPE substrate) and E-PM1 (OQ-4/OQ-5 answers).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline 'fused == unfused' obligation is now owned by **P3-r1-fusion-parity-tests** (cosine ≥ 0.9999 + max_abs_diff ledgered vs the unfused RMSNorm-then-RoPE sequence, RoPE convention locked to OQ-4/OQ-5). The fusion may not land without that gate green.

title: Fusion: gate_proj ‖ up_proj → SiLU·mul (SwiGLU), one input dequant
description:
# P3-fusion-swiglu

## BACKGROUND / WHY
gate_proj ‖ up_proj → SiLU·mul fused replaces 3 GEMMs + 2 passes with 1 input-dequant + fused elementwise (§6.7, §6.10). Each expert is a 1280→896 SiLU-gated MLP.

## WHAT TO DO
Compute gate_proj and up_proj in ONE fused pass sharing the activation quant of the input row (one dequant of the input, not three), then `silu(gate) * up`, then down_proj. Applies to every expert (6 routed + 2 shared) and the dense layer-0 MLP. SiLU's sigmoid is a transcendental — use the vectorized exp (P3-vec-exp) for it, numerics-gated.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-exact vs unfused gate/up/down within tolerance; one input-dequant instead of three measured; parity green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: fused == unfused. Perf: input-dequant count + ms A/B. depends_on the expert GEMM/GEMV kernels (P3-smmla-prefill-gemm, P3-sdot-decode-gemv, P3-vnni512-gemm).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline 'fused == unfused' obligation is now owned by **P3-r1-fusion-parity-tests**, which also covers the FOCR_VEC_EXP off/on interaction (the SwiGLU SiLU uses the vectorized poly-exp). The fusion may not land without that gate green.

title: Fusion: router → top-6 select → expert-gather (counting-sort + scatter)
description:
# P3-fusion-router-gather

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Fuse router → top-6 select → expert-gather, replacing sort+gather with counting-sort + scatter (§6.7, §6.10).

## WHAT TO DO
Router: 1280→64 **f32 GEMV (NEVER quantized)** → softmax → **top-6 via partial selection (select_nth, NOT a full sort)** → norm_topk_prob. Then gather the 6 active routed experts (+2 shared) using the counting-sort/scatter from P3-moe-token-grouping. The lm_head greedy fusion (lm_head GEMV → argmax-only, no full 129280 softmax) lives in P3-decode-sampler.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Router f32, never quantized — quantizing the gate drifts expert selection → accuracy cliff (NVFP4 keeps it f32, §6.7).
- select_nth (partial selection) is O(64) not O(64 log 64) and deterministic.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Top-6 selection bit-identical to the reference greedy top-k; norm_topk_prob exact; parity green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: top-6 select_nth == reference top-k on random gate logits incl. ties (deterministic tie-break matching the reference). depends_on P3-moe-token-grouping (the gather/scatter) and E-PM1 (router dims + norm_topk_prob semantics).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline top-6-select correctness obligation is now owned by **P3-r1-fusion-parity-tests** (fused top-6 select_nth + counting-sort scatter bit-identical to reference greedy top-k incl. ties; norm_topk_prob exact; router GEMV proven still f32). The fusion may not land without that gate green.

title: focr robot backends reflects selected ISA tier + USL pool sizing
description:
# P3-robot-backends — agent-visible backend reflection

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Agent ergonomics (G5) require the runtime to self-describe which kernels are live. `focr robot backends` is how an agent (or a benchmark harness) confirms the right path was selected — critical for honest gauntlet rows (a perf claim must record the tier).

## WHAT TO DO
Extend `focr robot backends` to emit versioned NDJSON reporting: the selected `IsaTier` (A1/A2/X1/X2/X3/X4/S), the detected CPU feature string, the per-(arch,op-class) USL pool sizing table baked from AF-5 (decode-GEMV pool N, prefill-GEMM pool N — see P3-numa-usl), the NUMA mode (FOCR_NUMA), the allocator posture (system vs mimalloc feature), and whether int8 attention (FOCR_INT8_ATTN) / int8 lm_head are active. Must be machine-parseable, line-oriented, stable schema, no human decoration in robot mode.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The gauntlet (P3-gauntlet-bench) annotates every row with this info; an unannotated ratio across different tiers/allocators/precisions is meaningless (§9.3 precision/allocator/thread fairness).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr robot backends` emits valid versioned JSON validated against the frozen robot schema fixture.
- Reflects FOCR_ISA_TIER override and FOCR_NUMA correctly.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Contract test: emitted events validate against the frozen JSON schema. Unit: forcing each tier via FOCR_ISA_TIER yields the matching `tier` field.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (the tier it reflects) and P3-numa-usl (the pool-sizing table it prints).

Updated the golden test to assert the live SIMD block structurally before scrubbing host-specific tier values, refreshed `tests/fixtures/golden/robot_backends.golden`, and documented the scrub in `tests/fixtures/golden/PROVENANCE.md`. This does NOT close bd-2mo.2: USL pool sizing, NUMA, allocator posture, int8-attention/lm-head activity, and the older bead text mismatch around `FOCR_ISA_TIER` vs the implemented `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` still need a deliberate follow-up. Verification: `cargo +nightly fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py`; `br dep cycles --json`; `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config 'patch.crates-io.asupersync.path="/dp/asupersync"' check --all-targets`; same clippy with `--all-targets -- -D warnings`; same full `cargo test` (446 lib tests plus integration/parity/tokenizer/doc tests green). `ubs $(git diff --name-only)` is not supported by installed UBS (usage exit 2); reran `ubs --diff --ci`, which completed but exits nonzero on existing test-harness panic/assert inventory in `tests/cli_robot_golden.rs` (11 critical, 76 warnings) while its fmt/clippy/check/test-build sections are green.

title: Vectorized polynomial exp for softmax/SiLU/quick_gelu (numerics-gated)
description:
# P3-vec-exp — the MEASURED exception to "autovectorize the glue"

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The frankensearch lesson (don't hand-wide-SIMD scalar loops) holds for i32 MAC glue. It does NOT automatically hold for **transcendentals** (`exp` in softmax; `sigmoid` in SiLU/quick_gelu), which LLVM will NOT autovectorize (it emits scalar libm calls). A vectorized polynomial exp is a known multi-× win for softmax/activations — but it is a NUMERICS-AFFECTING lever (§6.11). THIS is the one place hand-SIMD is the right tool, and it must still clear the keep-gate.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement a vectorized polynomial exp: range-reduce + degree-(5–7) minimax poly (std::simd or the frankenjax `simd_poly_exp` pattern). Use it in the online softmax (P3-online-softmax-rswa), SiLU (SwiGLU), and quick_gelu. Gate it behind a OnceLock env switch (e.g. FOCR_VEC_EXP); default it ON ONLY after a measured A/B + a passing parity gate (online-softmax logits within the int8 tolerance). Ledger the result.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- It changes numerics (poly approx vs libm), so it is gated like a quant lever: measured A/B, parity gate, ledger — never silently on.
- Default-on only after the parity gate passes; otherwise stays off and the scalar libm path is the fallback.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The exp error feeds softmax which feeds token selection — a too-coarse poly can flip a token. The parity gate (decoded tokens exact where deterministic) is the guard.
- Confine nightly portable_simd to this kernel island.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
With FOCR_VEC_EXP on: online-softmax logits within the measured int8 tolerance; decoded tokens exact where deterministic; measured multi-× speedup on softmax/activations; ledgered (keep or revert).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: poly exp max-ulp vs libm over the operand range; softmax-with-poly == softmax-with-libm within tolerance. Parity gate A/B. Perf bench. Structured NDJSON. depends_on P3-online-softmax-rswa (its main consumer) and E-VERIFY (the parity gate that gates default-on).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## TEST BEAD (added in review)
The inline 'TESTS REQUIRED' is now owned by a dedicated bead **P3-r1-vec-exp-tests** (max-ULP-vs-libm floor + the FOCR_VEC_EXP off/on A/B consumer-parity gate asserting decoded tokens stay exact over the deterministic prefix). The lever stays OFF until that gate passes; a token flip under poly-exp = REVERT + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry. The token-exact comparison is valid only over the bf16 oracle's reproduced prefix (carry the nondeterminism envelope).

title: TEST — vectorized poly-exp ULP-vs-libm + softmax/SiLU/quick_gelu parity gate (FOCR_VEC_EXP A/B), structured NDJSON
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
P3-vec-exp is the ONE place the project deliberately hand-SIMDs (a degree-5–7 minimax poly exp for softmax/SiLU/quick_gelu — LLVM won't autovectorize libm). It is explicitly numerics-affecting: the exp error feeds softmax which feeds token selection, so a too-coarse poly can FLIP a token. The bead carries only an inline 'TESTS REQUIRED' note and gates default-on behind a parity gate — but that gate is not a tracked bead, so the lever could be flipped on without its proof. This bead is the parity gate that decides default-on, plus the ULP floor that bounds the approximation.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/vec_exp_parity.rs`:
1. **ULP floor**: over the full operand range exercised by softmax (post-max-subtraction, ≤ 0) and the SiLU/quick_gelu sigmoid range, assert `max_ulp(poly_exp, libm_exp)` ≤ a documented bound; assert monotonicity and no NaN/Inf at range edges (large -x → 0 cleanly, x=0 → 1).
2. **Consumer parity (the teeth)**: with FOCR_VEC_EXP OFF (libm) vs ON (poly): assert online-softmax logits agree within the measured int8 tolerance, and that DECODED TOKENS are exact where the reference is deterministic over its reproduced prefix (the token-flip guard). Cover softmax (R-SWA online softmax), SiLU (SwiGLU), and quick_gelu (CLIP) consumers.
3. **A/B default-on rule**: assert the lever stays OFF until this gate passes; emit the measured speedup so the keep/revert decision is logged; a token flip under poly-exp = REVERT + NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry.

## DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (mandatory)
Via TestLog: `{event:'stage', op:'poly_exp', range:[lo,hi], simd_tier, elapsed_us, vec_exp:true}`; `{event:'parity', gate:'L1', metric:'max_ulp', value, tolerance, pass}` for the ULP floor; `{event:'parity', gate:'L4', metric:'token_exact', vec_exp_on:true, divergence_position, nondeterminism_envelope, pass}` for the consumer parity; a `{event:'result', speedup_x, disposition:'keep'|'revert'}` summary. schema_version present; validated against TEST-log-conventions.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The ULP bound alone is insufficient — what matters is whether the token flips, so the consumer-parity check (decoded tokens exact) is the real gate; the ULP floor is the cheap early signal.
- Toggling FOCR_VEC_EXP off/on in one test makes the A/B the test, so 'default-on only after parity passes' is mechanically enforced.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Confine nightly portable_simd to the kernel island (don't let the test require it crate-wide).
- The token-exact comparison is only valid over the bf16 oracle's reproduced prefix (carry the nondeterminism_envelope, §8.2) — a divergence inside oracle noise is not a poly-exp bug.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- poly-exp max-ULP within the documented bound; monotone; no NaN/Inf at edges.
- softmax/SiLU/quick_gelu with poly-exp agree with libm within the int8 tolerance; decoded tokens exact over the deterministic prefix.
- Lever default OFF until the gate passes; measured speedup logged; a token flip → REVERT + ledger.
- Every case emits a schema-valid NDJSON line.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Self-test: a deliberately-coarse poly (degree-2) that flips a token makes the consumer-parity gate FAIL.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on P3-vec-exp (under test), P3-online-softmax-rswa (its main consumer), E-VERIFY (the token-exact / int8-tolerance machinery + nondeterminism envelope), TEST-log-emitter (logging).

title: NUMA + USL many-core pool sizing (AF-5): cap pools at the USL peak
description:
# P3-numa-usl — honest many-core scaling, not blind par_iter@64

## BACKGROUND / WHY
"Make it fly on 64-core Threadrippers" requires an honest scaling model. **Prefill scales with cores; decode does NOT (memory-bandwidth-bound)** — a single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token; beyond the cores needed to saturate DRAM bandwidth, more threads add nothing and hurt via contention. Over-threading decode is a MEASURED anti-win to ledger (§6.9 / AF-5).

## WHAT TO DO
1. **USL pool sizing (AF-5):** fit the Universal Scalability Law `C(N) = N/(1+α(N−1)+βN(N−1))` to a 1/2/4/8/…/64-thread sweep of the decode GEMV AND the prefill GEMM SEPARATELY; fit α (contention) and β (cross-core coherency); **cap each pool at its USL peak**, not num_cpus. Bake a `pool_sizing` table per (arch, op-class); `focr robot backends` reports it.
2. **NUMA (Threadripper/EPYC multi-CCD, dual-socket):** the weight blob is read-only and hot — first-touch or explicitly REPLICATE the .focrq weights per NUMA node, pin each page-worker (and its rayon sub-pool) to one node, allocate per-page activation buffers node-local. Cross-node weight fetches are the silent killer on EPYC. Expose `FOCR_NUMA={replicate|interleave|local}`, default `local` with first-touch. Use hwloc/affinity behind a feature; scalar/no-NUMA fallback elsewhere.
3. **Apple Silicon P/E asymmetry:** pin the heavy GEMM pool to P-cores (QoS USER_INTERACTIVE), keep orchestration on E-cores. (M4 has i8mm+dotprod+bf16, verified via sysctl.)
4. **No oversubscription:** one global rayon pool sized to physical P-cores; the asupersync blocking pool stays tiny; exactly one live forward fans out at a time.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Cap decode pool at USL peak (~8–16 effective cores, BW-bound) vs pointlessly oversubscribing 64 (β-dominated slowdown) — the difference is real on a 64-core part.
- Two parallelism axes never mixed blindly: LATENCY (within one forward: prefill GEMMs over row-blocks, MoE over experts, attention over heads) vs THROUGHPUT (across documents, only with a disjoint thread/NUMA budget per worker, no full num_cpus fan-out). A capacity certificate must prove workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical-core budget.
- Deterministic fallback (AF-5): physical-core count if the USL fit is degenerate.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- DEADLOCK RULE: never nest rayon under a held lock; never nest a 2nd asupersync runtime; single OcrModel, sequential outer page loop. The many_pages_without_deadlock watchdog (pages ≫ pool) hangs on regression.
- hwloc is FFI → behind a feature; the no-NUMA path must work without it.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (AF-5 proof obligation)
Measured throughput at the chosen N ≥ throughput at num_cpus; the USL peak is recorded per (arch, op-class) and reflected by `focr robot backends`; FOCR_NUMA modes work; many_pages_without_deadlock passes.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Thread-sweep harness producing the α/β fit + USL peak per op-class (artifact). Concurrency watchdog many_pages_without_deadlock (pages ≫ pool). NUMA A/B (replicate vs local vs interleave) on a multi-CCD host. Structured NDJSON `{event:"usl_fit", arch, op_class, alpha, beta, peak_N}`. Ledger over-threading anti-win. depends_on the GEMM/GEMV kernels (P3-sdot-decode-gemv, P3-vnni512-gemm, P3-smmla-prefill-gemm) whose pools it sizes.

title: AF-5 transparency card + fallback wiring for USL pool sizing
description:
# P3-alien-af5-pool-card

## BACKGROUND / WHY
AF-5 (USL pool sizing) is Tier A / EV high / low effort and is the load-bearing many-core lever (§9.7). The fitting + capping is implemented in P3-numa-usl; this bead ships the alien-artifact CONTRACT around it: the galaxy-brain transparency card (the USL equation `C(N)=N/(1+α(N−1)+βN(N−1))`, the substituted α/β/peak_N per (arch,op-class), plain-English intuition, validity assumptions, what would flip the decision), the assumptions ledger, and the wired-first deterministic fallback (physical-core count).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Transparency card + assumptions ledger present for each (arch, op-class); fallback to physical-core count proven to engage when the USL fit is degenerate; AF-5 proof obligation (throughput at chosen N ≥ at num_cpus) recorded.

## TESTS REQUIRED
The degenerate-fit fallback test; the card/ledger artifact check. depends_on P3-numa-usl (the implementation) and E-ALIEN (the alien-artifact contract).

title: Memory/allocator: mmap weights, buffer reuse, 64B alignment, mimalloc opt-in
description:
# P3-memory-allocator — zero per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The buffer-reuse story is the tape-truncation analog (§6.12): pre-allocate every per-forward buffer once and reuse across pages → zero per-token/per-page allocation in the hot loop. mmap avoids the 6→3 GB copy on load.

## WHAT TO DO
- **Weight blob:** `mmap` the .focrq read-only (no 6→3 GB copy on load); `madvise(WILLNEED)` the active region; consider transparent/explicit huge pages for the multi-GB blob (fewer TLB misses on the GEMM weight streams).
- **Buffer reuse:** pre-allocate every per-forward buffer once — KV ring + reference block sized for worst-case m, attention scratch, MoE token-group buffers, the f32 activation rails — and reuse across pages.
- **Alignment & SoA:** 64-byte-aligned activation and packed-weight buffers (clean SMMLA/AVX-512 loads); store packed weights + scales in struct-of-arrays so a tile load is one contiguous stream.
- **Allocator:** DEFAULT build uses the SYSTEM allocator and links NO C/FFI (preserves G3 pure-Rust / no-FFI single-binary). `mimalloc` is an OPT-IN, perf-only feature (`--features mimalloc`) — it is a C library (FFI), NEVER part of the default or the "no FFI" claim; when a head-to-head perf claim uses it, BOTH sides use the same allocator (§9.3 fairness).
- **BF16→f32 bulk dequant:** the high-precision tensors (vision/embeddings/router/norm) are BF16 on disk (checkpoint is BF16); bulk-convert BF16→f32 with SIMD/table-assisted paths; any f16 narrowing is a measured DISCREPANCIES entry, never the default (bf16-not-f16 storage doctrine).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- mimalloc behind a feature keeps the default pure-Rust/no-FFI (G3) while allowing fair perf claims.
- 64-byte alignment is load-bearing for clean AVX-512/SMMLA tile loads (no split loads).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Zero per-token/per-page heap allocation in the hot loop (verified via an allocation counter in a debug build); mmap load shows no 6→3 GB copy; default build links no C/FFI; mimalloc only under the feature.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: allocation-counter test asserts 0 allocations per decode step after warm-up. Integration: mmap load + alignment assertions. Perf: mimalloc on/off A/B (allocator-fair). depends_on E-P2 (the .focrq it mmaps) and P3-weight-prepack (the SoA-aligned packed layout).

title: PGO + BOLT on the release binary + release/release-perf profiles
description:
# P3-pgo-bolt — the free, model-agnostic last 5–10%

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Build-time optimization is free and model-agnostic — do them all (§6.13). The decode loop's branch layout (router top-6, ring-buffer wrap, ngram check) is exactly what PGO straightens; BOLT does I-cache/branch layout of the hot kernels (the last 5–10%).

## WHAT TO DO
- **Profiles (Cargo.toml):** shipping `release` profile = LTO `fat` + `codegen-units=1` + `panic=abort`. Keep a SEPARATE `release-perf` profile (frame pointers, `lto=thin`, `strip=false`, debug=line-tables-only) for profiling ONLY — never profile the size/abort build.
- **PGO:** instrument, run the golden OCR corpus (a representative dense page + a 10-page doc) to collect a profile, rebuild with it. Bake one `*.profdata` per release.
- **BOLT:** post-link binary optimization on top of PGO for I-cache/branch layout of the hot GEMM/decode loops.
- **target-feature NOT target-cpu=native:** we ship one portable binary per arch and select SIMD at RUNTIME, so `native` is wrong (it breaks the cross-compile floor). Enable the baseline feature set per target; gate the rest with `is_*_feature_detected!`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
PGO+BOLT release binary measurably faster on the decode loop than the non-PGO release; the profiling profile is distinct from the shipping profile; the binary still runs on the cross-compile floor (no native-only features baked).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Perf A/B: PGO+BOLT vs plain release on the golden corpus decode loop. Build test: the portable binary runs on a host lacking the highest tier (scalar floor still works). Ledger the gain. depends_on the decode-hot kernels (P3-sdot-decode-gemv, P3-vnni512-gemm, P3-moe-decode, P3-decode-sampler) whose branch layout it straightens.

title: lm_head GEMV → argmax-only greedy + no_repeat_ngram(35) (fused)
description:
# P3-decode-sampler — argmax-only, no full softmax over 129280

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Under greedy, the lm_head GEMV (1280→129280) feeds argmax-only — there is no need for a full softmax over the 129280 vocab (§6.4, §6.10). lm_head int8 is behind its own kill-switch (it goes BEYOND the validated weight-only set, doctrine rule 2).

## WHAT TO DO
Fuse lm_head GEMV → argmax (greedy): replace the full 129280 softmax + argmax with argmax-only (no exp over the vocab). Implement no_repeat_ngram_size=35: track the last-35 n-grams and set repeats to -inf over the 129280 vocab before argmax. lm_head int8 GEMV behind a `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` kill-switch (per-output-channel int8; default f32/bf16 until its parity gate passes). Uses the decode GEMV kernels (P3-sdot-decode-gemv / P3-vnni512-gemm).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- argmax-only is bit-exact under greedy (softmax is monotone; argmax(logits) == argmax(softmax(logits))).
- no_repeat_ngram is exact (a hard blocklist), not a numerics change.
- lm_head int8 is gated because it is outside the validated set (OQ-14 reconciliation).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Decoded tokens bit-identical to the full-softmax greedy reference; no_repeat_ngram(35) matches the reference blocklist exactly; lm_head int8 only default-on after its CER gate passes.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: argmax-only == full-softmax-argmax on random logits; ngram blocklist matches reference on a sequence with forced repeats. Perf: argmax-only vs full-softmax A/B. depends_on P3-sdot-decode-gemv + P3-vnni512-gemm (the lm_head GEMV) and E-PM1 (vocab size, ngram window OQ-18, lm_head OQ-14).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — per-mode no_repeat_ngram WINDOW is first-class, not just size=35
The ngram processor is NOT only `size=35`; the **sliding window over which 35-grams are tracked DIFFERS BY MODE — 128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image)** (plan §2.4/§6.4, OQ-18, established as first-class generation semantics in P1-sampler's `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`). This fused sampler MUST reproduce the exact per-mode window or it will diverge from the reference at L4 token-exactness on multi-image parses (the banned-continuation set changes when the window changes). Transcribe: track the last-35-token n-grams ONLY within the active `ngram_window` (128 single / 1024 multi), set repeats to -inf over the 129280 vocab before argmax, exactly as P1-sampler does. The acceptance test must include a MULTI-IMAGE sequence that forces a 35-gram repeat just outside the 128-window but inside the 1024-window, asserting it is banned in multi-image mode and allowed in single-image mode. Confirm the exact window per mode from `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor` in the spec (OQ-18) before shipping — no kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN].

title: Roofline / compute-floor analysis per stage (the falsifiable perf target)
description:
# P3-roofline — drive each stage to its roofline

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The reranker's clearest lesson (§9.1): compute the COMPUTE floor (int8/int4 GEMM FLOPs ÷ arch peak int8 throughput) and the MEMORY floor (bytes streamed ÷ DRAM bandwidth) per stage; take the max — that is what a perfect kernel hits. In the sibling campaign ONNX-int8 ran NEAR the compute floor while the Rust path sat ~2× above it, and the gap was kernels-below-peak, NOT framework overhead.

## WHAT TO DO
For each stage (MoE expert GEMMs decode, lm_head GEMV decode, R-SWA decode, vision attention/MLP prefill, patch-embed conv prefill) compute both floors and the max, on each primary arch. Make the target explicit and falsifiable: drive each stage to its roofline; treat any stage sitting **>~1.3× above its floor** as a NAMED, attackable lever (which kernel, which arch) — never an excuse. Put the roofline numbers in PERF_LEDGER.md NEXT TO every measured ratio.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Every hot stage has a recorded compute floor, memory floor, max floor, and measured ms with the ratio-above-floor; stages >1.3× above floor are filed as named levers.

## TESTS REQUIRED
A roofline-report harness (not a pass/fail test) emitting the floors + measured ratios as structured NDJSON. Feeds the §9.2 optimization loop. depends_on the core GEMM/GEMV kernels whose stages it measures (P3-smmla-prefill-gemm, P3-sdot-decode-gemv, P3-vnni512-gemm).

title: Head-to-head gauntlet bench vs the Phase -1 CPU reference (per-stage, fair)
description:
# P3-gauntlet-bench — how G2 is proved, honestly

## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the bead that proves the Phase-3 exit gate: **decode-per-token faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference** on the primary arches (the gating part of G2), with vision-prefill reported honestly (parity-or-slower in f32 acceptable). It is only meaningful with apples-to-apples fairness controls (§9.3).

## WHAT TO DO
Build `benches/gauntlet`: shell out to the Phase -1 proven CPU reference (`FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD` / `FOCR_REFERENCE_BACKEND`) PER STAGE, parse elapsed, report the HONEST reference/focr ratio (tag each row OK/warn/slower/"focr faster") — never a self-relative number. If CPU-patched HF cannot be proven equivalent to the CUDA oracle (OQ-17), the benchmark target is llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX Runtime / MLAS, LABELED as such. Mandatory fairness controls (all required):
- **Thread parity:** pin OMP_NUM_THREADS / torch set_num_threads(N) EQUAL to focr's thread budget; **NEVER benchmark torch at @64** (oversubscription inflates fake wins — measure at @8/@32).
- **Allocator fairness:** build focr with the same allocator posture used for the claim (mimalloc behind a feature), wired into the measured binary, not merely mentioned.
- **Best-of-N with warmup discard;** report the min and the precision of each side.
- **Precision annotation per row:** focr-int8 vs torch-bf16 (and torch-int8 if available) — a raw ratio across different numerics is meaningless.
- **PER-STAGE ratios:** preprocess / vision-encode / prefill / decode-per-token — so the honest story is visible (decode faster, vision-prefill maybe slower in f32 v1).
Profile across the OUTPUT-LENGTH axis: {sparse page (vision-prefill-dominated), dense page, 10-page, 40-page (decode-dominated)} — hot paths ranked per regime. Annotate every row with `focr robot backends` (ISA tier, allocator, NUMA, precision). Benchmarks SKIP GRACEFULLY (exit 0) if the model fixture is absent.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase-3 exit gate)
Gauntlet per-stage ratios recorded with the §9.3 fairness controls; **decode-per-token is faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference** on the primary arches; vision-prefill recorded honestly (parity-or-slower in f32 acceptable); every row precision/thread/allocator-annotated; results in PERF_LEDGER.md; parity still green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
The gauntlet bench itself + a benchmark-guardrail gate catching regressions vs a frozen baseline. Structured NDJSON per row `{stage, regime, focr_ms, ref_ms, ratio, tag, tier, precision, threads, allocator}`. depends_on ALL the landed Phase-3 kernels (SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI GEMM/GEMV, MoE grouping + decode, online-softmax R-SWA, USL pool sizing, decode sampler, roofline) and E-VERIFY (the reference-oracle env + fairness-control harness).

title: AF-1: rate-distortion water-filling per-tensor bit allocation (offline)
description:
# P3-alien-af1-bit-allocation — optimal per-tensor bits, not uniform

## BACKGROUND / WHY (AF-1, Tier A, EV high)
The per-tensor int4-vs-int8-vs-bf16 split is CHOSEN by a rate-distortion / water-filling allocator, never uniform (doctrine rule + §6.3, §9.7 AF-1). Choosing {BF16/F32, int8, int4-g32, int4-g16} per quantizable tensor under a total-footprint budget IS a rate-distortion problem: minimize Σ_t D_t(b_t) s.t. Σ_t bits_t ≤ B; the Lagrangian Σ_t [D_t + λ·bits_t] separates per-tensor; sweeping λ traces the optimal bit-allocation frontier — spend bits where ∂D_t/∂bits is steepest (the wide intermediate=6848 dense down_proj, the attention v_proj), starve the flat ones.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>`: compute per-tensor distortion curves D_t(b) as the layer-output COSINE DROP on a calibration batch, run the λ-sweep water-filling, emit an offline `bit_allocation_table` into the .focrq header. Ship the galaxy-brain transparency card (equation · substituted values · plain-English intuition · validity assumptions · what would flip the decision) + assumptions ledger. **Wire the deterministic fallback FIRST:** uniform Q4_K_M-class allocation (§5).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (AF-1 proof obligation)
The allocated config's end-to-end CER ≤ the uniform-bit config's at EQUAL footprint, on the dense-numeric corpus. Fallback to uniform if the optimizer is unavailable/degenerate.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: water-filling on synthetic D_t curves matches the closed-form Lagrangian optimum. Integration: allocated vs uniform CER at equal footprint on the dense-numeric corpus. depends_on E-P2 (the int8 quant substrate the bits allocate over), E-VERIFY (the CER gate), E-ALIEN (the AF-1 math family + transparency-card contract). Note: heavy lifting is owned by E-ALIEN; this bead is the Phase-3 kernel-side wiring of the allocation table into convert/.focrq.

title: int8/int4 KV-cache quantization (Gundam / multi-page memory pressure) — deprioritized lever behind a CER gate
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §6.4 names KV-cache quantization as a real (if deprioritized) lever: *"KV-cache quant — deprioritized. R-SWA keeps the cache tiny (128 positions/layer for the sliding part) and this is plain MHA, so int8/int4 KV buys little. If Gundam/multi-page long-context pressures memory, add int8 KV first (safe, ~2×); int4 KV later only if needed."* §6.3 echoes it. The reference block `m` GROWS with page count up to 32K (§6.4), so on a 40-page Gundam-or-multi-page parse the reference-KV block (length `m` ≈ thousands, ×10 heads ×12 layers ×head_dim 128, f32) is the dominant memory consumer — exactly the regime where int8 KV halves it. No bead covers this; it should exist (low priority) so the lever is tracked rather than lost.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement optional int8 (then int4) quantization of the **reference-KV block** (the large, frozen, never-evicted part — the 128-ring is tiny and stays f32):
1. **int8 KV first** (§6.4 'safe, ~2×'): quantize the reference-K and reference-V blocks per-head/per-channel symmetric int8 on prefill (the block is read-only for the whole generation, so quantize once); dequant in the online-softmax `Q·Kᵀ` / `scores·V` tiles (§6.8). Gate behind `FOCR_INT8_KV` kill-switch; default OFF.
2. **int4 KV later** (only if int8 KV is proven and memory still pressures): per-group int4 on the reference block behind `FOCR_INT4_KV`.
3. **Trigger**: only engage when the assembled reference `m` exceeds a threshold (Gundam multi-tile or multi-page) where the f32 KV block exceeds a memory budget; small single pages stay f32 (the quant overhead is not worth it, §6.4).
4. Compose with the online-softmax R-SWA kernel (§6.8) — the dequant happens inside the streaming tile loop, never materializing the full f32 block.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Reference block only, ring stays f32**: the ring is 128 positions (tiny); the reference block is the size driver. Quantizing only the big frozen part captures the win with minimal accuracy risk.
- **Quantize-once on prefill**: the reference block is read-only for the whole generation, so no per-step requant.
- **int8 before int4** (the `_M` discipline): KV feeds softmax/exp (error-amplifying), so int4 KV is the riskier tier — gate it harder.
- **CER-gated, default OFF**: a numerics-affecting lever; default ON only after a measured A/B + passing parity gate (§6.11 discipline).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- KV quant feeds the softmax — error amplifies. Measure CER, not just cosine, on the dense-numeric/table corpus before defaulting.
- Must not regress the L3/L5 parity gate; any accepted divergence → `DISCREPANCIES.md` (DISC-NNN) with the kill-switch env var.
- Interaction with multi-page cross-page reference KV (the block spans all pages): quantize the whole assembled block consistently.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- With `FOCR_INT8_KV=1` on a 40-page / large-Gundam parse: reference-KV memory ≈ halved; CER within the ledgered budget of the f32-KV run; decoded text exact where deterministic.
- Default (no env) path is byte-identical to today (f32 KV) — opt-in only.
- A `DISCREPANCIES.md` entry with measured impact + kill-switch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: int8/int4 KV quant→dequant round-trip error bound; online-softmax with quantized vs f32 KV tile equivalence within tolerance.
- E2E (model-gated): CER delta f32-KV vs int8-KV vs int4-KV on the 40-page + large-Gundam corpus; memory-high-water-mark assertion (int8 KV ≈ 0.5× f32). NDJSON: `{doc, kv_mode, ref_m, kv_bytes, cer_delta, deterministic}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Blocked by P3-online-softmax-rswa (the streaming softmax this dequants inside), P1-rswa-attn (the reference-block structure), P4-int4-gemm (the int4 unpack path reused for int4 KV).

title: TEST — P3 fusion bit-identity / parity gate (dequant-GEMM, RMSNorm-RoPE, SwiGLU, router-gather) fused == unfused with structured NDJSON
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The four §6.10 fusion beads (P3-fusion-dequant-gemm, P3-fusion-rmsnorm-rope, P3-fusion-swiglu, P3-fusion-router-gather) each CHANGE how the activation is materialized (fewer passes, register-resident intermediates) and one of them (SwiGLU) pulls in the vectorized poly-exp (P3-vec-exp). Their correctness claim is 'same arithmetic, fewer materializations → bit-exact (integer paths) / within-f32-tolerance (norm/RoPE)'. Today each fusion bead carries only an INLINE 'TESTS REQUIRED: fused == unfused' note with NO dedicated test bead and NO edge to the verification harness — so the load-bearing claim is asserted nowhere durable, and a fusion that silently drifts numerics (e.g. an off-by-one in the RoPE pairing after RMSNorm, or a SwiGLU that applies SiLU to up instead of gate) would not be caught by a gate. G1 > G2 (correctness outranks speed) is only enforceable if every numerics-touching fusion has a parity gate. This bead is that gate.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/fusion_parity.rs` (a single harness covering all four fusions), each comparing the FUSED kernel against the UNFUSED reference sequence on representative shapes:
1. **dequant→GEMM→bias→act fusion** — for every quantized linear shape {K=1280 (q/k/v/o, gate, up, lm_head), K=896 (expert down_proj), K=6848 (dense layer-0 down_proj — the worst case)}: assert `fused_output == unfused(dequant; int8_gemm; +bias; act)` BIT-IDENTICAL on the integer path (i32 accumulation is exact), incl. adversarial saturated operands (±127 / U8S8 0..255). Run across every ISA tier available on the host AND every FOCR_ISA_TIER-forced lower tier (reuse the P3-isa-dispatch-tests battery), EXCLUDING the AVX2 path from bit-identity (it saturates at i16 — within-documented-tolerance check instead, per P3-avx2-overflow-proof).
2. **RMSNorm→RoPE fusion** (+ residual-add→next-RMSNorm) — high-precision (BF16/F32), so assert `cosine ≥ 0.9999` and `max_abs_diff` ledgered vs the unfused RMSNorm-then-RoPE sequence; lock the RoPE convention (head_dim from OQ-4, rope_theta from OQ-5) so a wrong pairing is caught.
3. **SwiGLU fusion** (gate‖up→SiLU·mul→down) — assert `fused == unfused` within f32 tolerance; with FOCR_VEC_EXP off (libm) AND on (poly-exp) so the fusion's interaction with the vectorized exp is covered.
4. **router→top-6→gather fusion** — assert the fused top-6 selection (select_nth + counting-sort scatter) is BIT-IDENTICAL to the reference greedy top-k incl. ties (deterministic tie-break) and that norm_topk_prob matches exactly; the router GEMV stays f32 (never quantized) — assert it was not quantized.

## DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING (mandatory)
Use the `TestLog` emitter (TEST-log-emitter). Per case emit a `stage` line `{event:'stage', fusion:'dequant_gemm'|'rmsnorm_rope'|'swiglu'|'router_gather', shapes, dtype, simd_tier, k, n, elapsed_us, passes_fused, passes_unfused}` and a `parity` line `{event:'parity', gate:'L1'|'L2', metric:'max_abs_i32_diff'|'cosine'|'token_exact', value, tolerance, pass}`. A final summary line `{fusions_tested, cases, bitident_pass, tolerance_pass, fail}`. Every line carries schema_version=TEST_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION and validates against the TEST-log-conventions schema.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- One harness, four fusions, because they share the unfused-reference machinery and the ISA-tier sweep; a per-fusion bead would duplicate the battery.
- Integer fusions are BIT-IDENTICAL (no tolerance) because i32 MAC is exact/associative; norm/RoPE/SwiGLU are within-f32-tolerance because they are high-precision elementwise. The distinction is asserted per-fusion, never blurred.
- The poly-exp interaction is covered by toggling FOCR_VEC_EXP so a fusion that is correct with libm but drifts with poly-exp is caught here, not in production.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The unfused reference MUST be the literal pass-by-pass sequence (not a second fused variant) — otherwise both could share a bug.
- AVX2 is excluded from bit-identity (i16 saturation) — assert within-documented-tolerance + a DISCREPANCIES entry instead.
- Run model-free on synthetic tensors (no 6.67 GB weights) so this is an always-on CI gate.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- For every non-AVX2 tier: each integer fusion is bit-identical to its unfused reference on all shapes incl. saturated operands.
- RMSNorm→RoPE and SwiGLU fusions within f32 tolerance (cosine ≥ 0.9999, max_abs_diff ledgered) with FOCR_VEC_EXP both off and on.
- router→top-6 fusion bit-identical to reference top-k incl. ties; router GEMV proven still f32.
- Every case emits a schema-valid NDJSON stage+parity line; the summary line localizes any failing fusion.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Additionally a self-test that a deliberately-perturbed fusion (e.g. SiLU applied to `up` instead of `gate`) makes the gate FAIL — proving it actually compares.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on the four P3-fusion-* beads (the things under test), P3-isa-dispatch-tests (reuses the ISA-tier bit-identity battery + forced-tier override), TEST-log-emitter (the structured logging), E-VERIFY (the parity-tolerance tables + the gauntlet's parity-receipt discipline).

title: Offline arch-specific weight pre-packing (focr convert --arch) into .focrq
description:
# P3-weight-prepack — offline pre-packed register tiles (the MLAS recipe)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
MEASURED LAW (NE-INH-3): register/cache blocking + offline weight pre-packing are MANDATORY, not optional — an un-blocked SMMLA was SLOWER than SDOT (19/41/77 vs 14.8/34/64) because it re-loads the activation per weight pair (≈2 loads:1 SMMLA) and is load-bound. "The instruction is not the lever; the blocking is." The converter must therefore lay weights out in the EXACT contiguous register tiles each kernel wants so the runtime loads tiles with ZERO runtime shuffle (the llama.cpp `q4_0_4x8`/`q4_0_8x8` aarch64-repack lesson, plan §6.6).

## WHAT TO DO
Extend `focr convert` with `--arch {aarch64-smmla, x86-vnni, x86-amx, generic}` (plan §5.4) to emit per-arch packed blobs from the SAME canonical source. Per the catalog (§6.6):
- **A1 (SMMLA)**: rows interleaved 2×8 (`A_2x8`), K-panel packed; tile MR×NR = 8×8 over K-blocks of 64–256.
- **A2 (SDOT)**: row-major + per-row scale; tile 4×16.
- **X1 (VNNI-512)**: u8 activation (+128 fold) × s8 weight, 4-row interleave; tile 8×16 (zmm).
- **X2 (VNNI-256)**: as X1, ymm; tile 8×8.
- **X3 (AMX)**: AMX tile config (STTILECFG), 64-wide K; tile 16×16.
- **X4 (AVX2)**: u8×s8; tile 8×8.
Store packed weights + scales in **struct-of-arrays** so a tile load is one contiguous stream; 64-byte-aligned buffers for clean SMMLA/AVX-512 loads (§6.12). Record the chosen packing + `arch_target` in the `.focrq` header so the loader picks contiguous register tiles with zero shuffle. The loader refuses a `.focrq` whose `format_version` exceeds the binary's and warns on `arch_target` mismatch (falls back to the generic packing).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- One canonical artifact, many packings: CI verifies all packings DEQUANTIZE TO THE SAME LOGICAL WEIGHTS (§5.4). The packing is a pure permutation/interleave of already-quantized data, so it is lossless by construction.
- Fixed shapes (1280, 896, 6848, 128) tile cleanly for SMMLA 8×8 and VNNI — no remainder handling in the hot loop (§3.1); the converter exploits this.
- U8S8 convention: for VNNI/AMX the converter produces uint8 activations (zero-point +128 fold) × s8 weights since vpdpbusd wants unsigned×signed; SMMLA/SDOT are signed×signed. Converter and kernel agree on the convention PER ARCH (§6.3).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A packing bug is silent: it produces wrong logits, not a crash. The dequant-equivalence CI test is the guard.
- arch mismatch at load must degrade to generic, never UB.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr convert --arch X` emits a `.focrq` whose header records the packing; loading on the matching arch loads contiguous tiles with zero runtime shuffle.
- CI proves every `--arch` packing dequantizes to byte-identical logical int8 weights.
- Deterministic: same source → same packed bytes (assert content hash).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: pack→unpack round-trip equals the row-major int8 for each arch. Integration: `convert --arch aarch64-smmla` then run the A1 GEMM and assert bit-identical to the generic+scalar GEMM on the same weights. Structured log: converter prints realized per-section footprint and packing geometry. See P3-weight-prepack-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on E-P2 (the int8 per-output-channel quant + .focrq format it repacks) and E-PM1 (the census fixes exact shapes/dtypes and the U8S8/S8S8 conventions per tensor).

title: Pre-pack equivalence + zero-shuffle load tests across all --arch packings
description:
# P3-weight-prepack-tests

## WHAT TO DO
Prove every `--arch` packing is a lossless permutation of the canonical int8 weights and that each kernel loads its tiles with no runtime reshuffle. For {generic, aarch64-smmla, x86-vnni, x86-amx}: (1) pack then unpack and assert byte-identical to row-major int8; (2) feed the packed blob to the matching tier's GEMM and assert bit-identical i32 vs the scalar GEMM over the same logical weights; (3) assert determinism (same source → same packed bytes, content-hash equal); (4) assert the loader refuses a `.focrq` with too-new format_version and warns+falls-back on arch mismatch.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
All four checks green; structured NDJSON `{event:"prepack_equiv", arch, ok, content_hash}` per arch.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-weight-prepack (the thing it tests).

title: aarch64 SMMLA/i8mm register-blocked prefill GEMM (the key lever)
description:
# P3-smmla-prefill-gemm — THE wedge: register-blocked SMMLA prefill GEMM

## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the single highest-value NEW code in the epic — the tiled register-blocked SMMLA GEMM that frankentorch has only NAMED (verified not to exist at frankentorch a84674c8). It directly attacks the ~1.5–2.4×-vs-MLAS gap (§3.2). MEASURED LAW (NE-INH-3): un-blocked SMMLA (2× MAC density) was SLOWER than SDOT because it is **load-bound** (≈2 loads:1 SMMLA). The lever is the BLOCKING, not the instruction.

## WHAT TO DO
Build a tiled SMMLA/i8mm micro-kernel (`vmmlaq_s32`, 2×2 int8 outer-product, **32 MACs/instr**, 4× i32 lanes) for **prefill GEMM** (compute-bound: vision attention/MLP, MoE prefill grouped GEMMs, patch-embed conv). Geometry from §6.6 A1: rows interleaved 2×8 (`A_2x8`), K-panel packed, **MR×NR = 8×8 over K-blocks of 64–256**. Amortize loads + the int32 reduction across the register tile so **compute:load ≥ 2:1** (reuse each loaded activation/weight tile across many MACs). Copy the B-panel-packed, L2-cache-blocked pattern from fnp-ufunc's `matmul_accumulate_serial` (its `MATMUL_MR`/`MATMUL_NR`/`nc≈256KiB` constants and register-tile pattern), retyped to int8 i32-accumulation. Consume the offline-pre-packed weights from P3-weight-prepack (zero runtime shuffle). Gate on `is_aarch64_feature_detected!("i8mm")`. Signed×signed convention. Provide the bit-identical scalar fallback.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- 8×8 tile over K-blocks 64–256: enough accumulator tiles to hide the SMMLA latency and reach compute:load ≥ 2:1 (the documented MLAS recipe).
- L2-cache-blocked B-panels (nc≈256KiB): keep the weight panel resident across the M-loop so each weight load serves many activation rows.
- Fixed shapes (1280, 896, 6848, 128 tile cleanly for 8×8): const-generic tile sizes, no remainder handling in the hot loop (§3.1).
- i32 accumulate, scales post-accumulation: `dequant = i32_acc · scale_w[chan] · scale_a[row]` then +bias then activation (§6.3).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- i32-overflow proof obligation: signed×signed worst case is dense layer-0 down_proj K=6848 → ≤110.4M, fits i32 with ≥9× headroom — but PROVE it at worst-case K on this arch (see P3-i32-overflow-proof). If a fused/grouped/accumulating variant ever approaches i32::MAX, switch THAT path to i64/blocked accumulation.
- The kernel must stay at peak: NEVER trade it for a naive scalar variant (NE-INH-5).
- If, after blocking, SMMLA still loses to SDOT for a given shape, that is a per-shape decision to ledger — do not ship a regression as the default.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Bit-identical i32 to scalar across all shapes incl. K=6848 saturated (via P3-isa-dispatch-tests battery).
- Perf: the tiled SMMLA prefill ≥ ~2× the scalar prefill where measured, and narrows toward the ONNX/MLAS bar; compute:load ≥ 2:1 demonstrated in the micro-bench (see P3-smmla-prefill-perf).
- Parity (L0–L5) unchanged.

## TESTS REQUIRED
