Unit: bit-identical vs scalar (battery). Perf bead P3-smmla-prefill-perf: Criterion micro-bench across seq {128,256,512} + the model's real prefill shapes, reporting focr/SDOT and focr/scalar ratios and the load:compute count. Structured NDJSON `{event:"smmla_prefill", shape, tier, ms, ratio_vs_scalar, ratio_vs_sdot}`.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (the A1 tier entrypoint), P3-weight-prepack (the 2×8/K-panel pre-packed weights — without them it is load-bound and loses), and E-PM1 (the exact prefill shapes + i32-overflow census).

title: Perf: SMMLA prefill GEMM vs SDOT and scalar (compute:load proof)
description:
# P3-smmla-prefill-perf

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The entire SMMLA bet is only validated by a measured win over SDOT WITH blocking in place (NE-INH-3 says un-blocked SMMLA loses). This bead measures it honestly.

## WHAT TO DO
Criterion micro-bench of the register-blocked SMMLA prefill GEMM vs (a) register-blocked SDOT and (b) scalar, on seq {128,256,512} and the model's real prefill shapes (vision 4096/256 tokens; MoE prefill 1280→896). Report best-of-N with warmup discard, the focr/SDOT and focr/scalar ratios, and demonstrate compute:load ≥ 2:1 (count loads vs SMMLA issues). Record in PERF_LEDGER.md with the roofline compute-floor next to the measured ratio. If SMMLA does not beat SDOT for a shape after blocking, REVERT to SDOT for that shape with no source landed and a NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Tiled SMMLA prefill ≥ ~2× scalar where measured and ≥ SDOT for the shapes where it ships; compute:load ≥ 2:1 demonstrated; every result ledgered (keep or revert).

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-smmla-prefill-gemm.

title: aarch64 SDOT decode GEMV (register-blocked, reuse frankentorch dot_i8_sdot)
description:
# P3-sdot-decode-gemv — the decode workhorse on ARM

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Decode is memory-bandwidth-bound (single-token GEMV streams ~500M active params/token). SDOT (`vdotq_s32`, 16 MAC/instr) is the right decode instruction; frankentorch already ships a bit-exact `dot_i8_sdot`, so we REUSE it as the decode-GEMV baseline + correctness oracle and add register-blocking (the measured 4×4-tile SDOT got the reranker from 14.8/34/64 to 12.4/28/56 by amortizing loads).

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the decode GEMV for the A1/A2 tiers using SDOT `vdotq_s32`, register-blocked (catalog §6.6: A2 tile 4×16; reuse frankentorch's existing bit-exact `dot_i8_sdot` as the per-row dot). Targets: MoE expert GEMVs (1280↔896 ×3), lm_head GEMV (1280→129280, behind its kill-switch), attention q/k/v/o GEMVs. Consume per-arch pre-packed weights (P3-weight-prepack, A2 row-major + per-row scale). Signed×signed. Per-row dynamic int8 activation quant at forward (frankentorch scheme). i32 accumulate, scales post-accumulation, +bias, activation. Bit-identical scalar fallback.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Decode is BW-bound, so the lever is fewer bytes streamed (int4 in Phase 4) + amortized loads (register blocking), NOT more MAC throughput — that is exactly why SMMLA is for prefill and SDOT for decode (§6.6, §6.1).
- Reuse the proven `dot_i8_sdot` so the SIMD path is bit-identical to the existing production reranker path.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Software-prefetch the next expert's weights while computing the current (the MoE decode pattern, §6.7) — but that lives in P3-moe-decode; here keep the GEMV clean.
- i32-overflow proof at K=6848 still applies (shared with P3-i32-overflow-proof).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Bit-identical i32 to scalar (battery, incl. K=6848 saturated).
- Perf: register-blocked SDOT decode GEMV beats scalar and the un-blocked SDOT baseline; the decode path is on track to beat the Phase -1 CPU reference (final claim in P3-gauntlet-bench).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: bit-identical vs scalar. Perf bead P3-sdot-decode-perf: Criterion on the real decode shapes (expert 1280→896, lm_head 1280→129280), reporting ratios vs scalar and un-blocked SDOT. Structured NDJSON per case.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (A1/A2 entrypoint) and P3-weight-prepack (A2 row-major + per-row scale packing).

title: Perf: register-blocked SDOT decode GEMV vs scalar/un-blocked
description:
# P3-sdot-decode-perf

Criterion micro-bench of the register-blocked SDOT decode GEMV vs scalar and un-blocked SDOT on the real decode shapes (expert 1280→896 ×3, lm_head 1280→129280). Best-of-N, warmup discard, roofline memory-floor next to the ratio (decode is BW-bound). Ledger keep/revert. Acceptance: register-blocked SDOT beats both baselines or is reverted+ledgered.

depends_on P3-sdot-decode-gemv.

title: x86 AVX-512-VNNI GEMM/GEMV (VPDPBUSD, U8S8, +128 correction)
description:
# P3-vnni512-gemm — the x86 workhorse

## BACKGROUND / WHY
VPDPBUSD (`_mm512_dpbusd_epi32`, AVX-512-VNNI, U8S8, 64 MAC/instr) is the x86 int8 workhorse for both prefill GEMM and decode GEMV. frankentorch already ships a bit-exact `dot_i8_vnni512` with the documented +128 unsigned-correction — reuse it as the decode-GEMV baseline + oracle and BUILD the register-blocked GEMM on top.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the X1 tier: VPDPBUSD register-blocked GEMM (tile 8×16, zmm) for prefill and VPDPBUSD GEMV for decode (catalog §6.6). U8S8 convention: uint8 activation with zero-point (+128 fold) × s8 weight, 4-row interleave (matching P3-weight-prepack X1 packing). Apply the documented +128 sign-flip correction so the result equals signed×signed semantics exactly. i32 accumulate, scales post-accumulation. Reuse frankentorch `dot_i8_vnni512` as the per-row decode dot + correctness oracle. Bit-identical scalar fallback. Gate on `is_x86_feature_detected!("avx512vnni")` + `avx512bw`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- U8S8 is mandated because vpdpbusd wants unsigned activations × signed weights; the +128 fold (converter and kernel agree per arch, §6.3) makes it equivalent to the signed×signed i32 of the SMMLA/SDOT paths so ALL paths are bit-identical.
- Register blocking (compute:load ≥ 2:1) for the prefill GEMM — same MLAS recipe as SMMLA (NE-INH-3).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Bit-identical i32 to scalar (battery incl. K=6848 saturated U8S8 ≤221.7M).
- Perf: register-blocked VNNI-512 prefill beats scalar and narrows toward ONNX/MLAS.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: bit-identical vs scalar (with +128 correction verified). Perf bead P3-vnni512-perf. Structured NDJSON.

## DEPENDENCIES
depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (X1 tier) and P3-weight-prepack (X1 u8×s8 4-row-interleave packing).

title: Perf: AVX-512-VNNI prefill GEMM + decode GEMV vs scalar
description:
# P3-vnni512-perf

Criterion micro-bench of VNNI-512 prefill GEMM and decode GEMV vs scalar on the real model shapes; best-of-N, roofline floor, ledger keep/revert. Acceptance: VNNI-512 beats scalar; prefill narrows toward ONNX/MLAS. depends_on P3-vnni512-gemm.

title: x86 AVX-VNNI (256-bit) GEMM/GEMV for non-512 hosts
description:
# P3-avxvnni-gemm — X2 tier (ymm VNNI)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Many x86 hosts have AVX-VNNI but not AVX-512 (e.g. some client/Alder Lake parts). X2 (`_mm256_dpbusd_epi32`, 256-bit) is the workhorse there.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the X2 tier: `_mm256_dpbusd_epi32` register-blocked GEMM (tile 8×8, ymm) + GEMV, same U8S8 (+128 fold) convention as X1, consuming the X1-style packing in ymm. Gate on `is_x86_feature_detected!("avxvnni")`. Bit-identical scalar fallback. Structurally mirrors P3-vnni512-gemm at 256-bit width — share the tiling/correction code.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-identical i32 to scalar; beats scalar; selected on AVX-VNNI-but-not-512 hosts.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: bit-identical vs scalar. Perf row in the gauntlet. depends_on P3-isa-dispatch, P3-weight-prepack, P3-vnni512-gemm (reuses its +128 correction + tiling abstraction).

title: x86 AMX-int8 prefill GEMM (_tile_dpbssd, SPR+) behind feature
description:
# P3-amx-prefill-gemm — X3 tier, INT8 ONLY, PREFILL ONLY

## BACKGROUND / WHY
MEASURED LAW (NE-INH-4): **AMX-f32 does NOT beat ONNX-int8** (it streams 4× the bytes on memory-bound sizes). AMX must be **int8** (low bandwidth) and helps **compute-bound prefill, not memory-bound decode**. So the AMX path is int8-only, prefill-only; decode stays on X1.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the X3 tier on Sapphire-Rapids+: `_tile_dpbssd` 16×16×64 int8 tile matmul → 16×16 i32 (catalog §6.6). AMX tile config via STTILECFG, 64-wide K, tile 16×16. Consume the X3 (AMX tile config) packing from P3-weight-prepack. Use ONLY for prefill GEMM (vision/MoE-prefill/patch-embed); decode uses X1. Dispatch order puts X3 above X1 for prefill. Bit-identical scalar fallback. Gate on `is_x86_feature_detected!("amx-int8")` (+ amx-tile). Note `_tile_dpbssd` is signed×signed (s8×s8), so the packing differs from VNNI's U8S8 — record the convention.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- int8 + prefill-only, by measured law — never route decode or f32 through AMX.
- Tile config/dealloc (TILECFG/TILERELEASE) must be managed correctly around the kernel to avoid corrupting the AMX state.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If AMX-int8 prefill does not beat VNNI-512 prefill on the target's shapes, REVERT to X1 for that shape and ledger (do not ship a regression).
- Hardware availability is narrow; keep it strictly optional behind the feature gate and the dispatch order.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Bit-identical i32 to scalar; AMX-int8 prefill beats VNNI-512 prefill where measured (else reverted+ledgered).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: bit-identical vs scalar (s8×s8). Perf: micro-bench vs X1 prefill. depends_on P3-isa-dispatch (X3 tier), P3-weight-prepack (AMX tile-config packing), P3-vnni512-gemm (the X1 baseline it must beat).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — scope note vs the Apple-AMX path
This bead is **x86 AMX only** (`_tile_dpbssd`, Sapphire-Rapids+). The Apple-Silicon AMX coprocessor is a SEPARATE, deliberately-different situation (NE-INH-4 / plan §6.6 lesson 3): it is **not directly programmable** and is reached only via **Accelerate/BNNS (C/FFI), an opt-in feature** — covered by the new bead P3-r1-apple-amx-accelerate, with NEON SMMLA/SDOT as the directly-usable Mac default. Do not conflate the two: there is no `_tile_dpbssd` on Apple Silicon, and nothing in THIS bead should compile or dispatch on aarch64.

title: x86 AVX2 fallback int8 GEMM (maddubs→madd)
description:
# P3-avx2-gemm — X4 tier (the broad-compat x86 fallback)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
AVX2 is present on essentially all modern x86 without VNNI, so X4 is the broad-compatibility path above scalar.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement the X4 tier: `_mm256_maddubs_epi16` → `_mm256_madd_epi16` (u8×s8, tile 8×8, catalog §6.6). Consume the X4 u8×s8 packing. Bit-identical scalar fallback BUT NOTE: AVX2 `vpmaddubsw` SATURATES at i16 before the i32 widen, so this path is NOT automatically bit-identical to the i32-exact paths for adversarial operands — its correctness is handled by P3-avx2-overflow-proof (split-accumulate every 2 K-steps) or a documented DISCREPANCIES divergence with a measured CER delta. Gate on `is_x86_feature_detected!("avx2")`.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
With split-accumulate, AVX2 result equals i32-exact for the model's operand ranges (proven by P3-avx2-overflow-proof) OR a ledgered DISCREPANCIES entry with measured CER delta exists; beats scalar.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: split-accumulate correctness (delegated to P3-avx2-overflow-proof). Perf row. depends_on P3-isa-dispatch, P3-weight-prepack.

title: AVX2 i16-saturation overflow proof (split-accumulate every 2 K-steps)
description:
# P3-avx2-overflow-proof — the one non-i32-exact path, made safe or ledgered

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The AVX2 `vpmaddubsw` (maddubs) produces i16 intermediates that SATURATE before the `madd` widen to i32. So AVX2 is the ONE path in the catalog that is not automatically bit-identical to the i32-exact SMMLA/VNNI/SDOT/scalar paths (plan §6.6 ⚠). It must carry its OWN overflow proof.

## WHAT TO DO
Implement and prove split-accumulate: accumulate the i16 partials to i32 every 2 K-steps (plan §5.4 / §6.6) so no i16 intermediate can saturate for this model's operand ranges. Then prove bit-identical-to-scalar at WORST-CASE K=6848 (dense layer-0 down_proj; U8S8 ≤221.7M) with adversarial saturated operands (all 255 × ±127). If for some reason a divergence remains, instead record a DISCREPANCIES.md entry (reference behavior, our impl, MEASURED CER impact, kill-switch env var, review date) — never a silent numerics change.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Split every 2 K-steps because two u8(255)×s8(127) products = 2·32385 = 64770 > i16::MAX(32767), so an unsplit maddubs of a full row saturates; splitting bounds the i16 partial below the saturation point. The exact split cadence is derived from the operand ranges in the census.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
With split-accumulate the AVX2 GEMM is bit-identical i32 to scalar at K=6848 with saturated operands; OR a ledgered DISCREPANCIES entry with a measured CER delta exists. No silent divergence.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: saturated-operand bit-identical test at K=6848. Structured NDJSON `{event:"avx2_overflow", k, ok, max_abs_i32_diff}`. depends_on P3-avx2-gemm (the path it proves) and E-PM1 (the operand-range census that sets the split cadence).

title: Preprocess ImageOps.pad geometry floors fitted size and centering offsets
description: The preprocess global-view path documents itself as an ImageOps.pad equivalent for SPEC-022, but pad_to_square currently computes the fitted short edge with integer floor division and centers with (size - resized) / 2. Pillow ImageOps.contain/ImageOps.pad round the fitted edge and centering offset, so L0 geometry can drift by one pixel for ordinary aspect ratios such as 100x40 or 101x40 at base_size=64. Fix pad_to_square to mirror Pillow's positive round-to-nearest-even behavior and add tests that pin both the resized short edge and the centered pad bands.
close_reason: Fixed SPEC-022 ImageOps.pad geometry parity drift in src/preprocess/mod.rs. pad_to_square now mirrors Pillow's rounded fitted-edge and centered-offset behavior with positive round-ties-even helpers instead of flooring the short edge and (size-resized)/2 offset. Added pad_to_square_matches_pillow_rounding_geometry to pin both ordinary one-pixel drift cases: 100x40 -> 64x26 with y offset 19, and 101x40 -> 64x25 with y offset 20. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt; env CARGO_HOME=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-target TMPDIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/tmp CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo +nightly test preprocess --lib passed (21 passed); same scratch env scripts/check.sh passed Python ledger/provenance/oracle checks, cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test (565 lib tests passed / 1 ignored plus all integration/doc tests green). The wrapper's final broad ubs --diff was interrupted after it spent several minutes packaging the whole dirty checkout; scoped ubs --only=rust src/preprocess/mod.rs exited 0 with 0 critical findings.

title: Audit: make AF-5 Python fitter fall back on poor USL fit
description: Fresh-eyes audit found scripts/af5_usl_fit.py diverges from the AF-5 design doc and Rust mirror: docs/alien/AF-5-usl-pool-sizing.md promises poor-fit fallback on low R^2/non-USL curves, and src/adaptive/usl.rs implements MIN_R2=0.90 with a fallback_on_poor_fit test, but the Python offline fitter's decide() only checks fit.degenerate. A jagged low-R2 sweep can therefore emit a cap-at-usl-peak row that the Rust controller would reject, polluting the pool_sizing evidence table. Add the same poor-fit gate to the script, include a self-test for a jagged low-R2 curve, and verify Python/Rust semantics stay aligned.
close_reason: Done: aligned scripts/af5_usl_fit.py with AF-5 design/Rust controller by adding MIN_R2=0.90 poor-fit fallback, selfcheck coverage for a jagged low-R2 sweep, and guarded JSON/file input errors that return structured bad-input JSON instead of tracebacks. Verification passed: python3 -m py_compile scripts/af5_usl_fit.py; python3 scripts/af5_usl_fit.py --selfcheck (ok true, poor_fit_fallback_ok true, poor_fit_r2 -0.06); invalid JSON stdin returns exit 2 with JSON error; git diff --check -- scripts/af5_usl_fit.py; ubs --only=python scripts/af5_usl_fit.py (0 warnings); cargo test --lib adaptive::usl -- --nocapture (13 passed).

title: Perf hygiene: SAM NCHW/NHWC layout converters should write destination rows/planes contiguously
close_reason: Implemented SAM NCHW/NHWC layout converter store-order hygiene. The forward converter now fills each NHWC destination token row contiguously while preserving the same channel-plane source mapping; the inverse now fills each NCHW channel plane contiguously while reading the same NHWC row/channel mapping. Added a non-square 3-channel 2x3 roundtrip/value test so the proof covers non-square grids, not only the previous 2x2 case. No PERF_LEDGER or NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry was added because this was not measured as a perf claim or rejected trial. Verification passed: focused cargo test for native_engine vision_sam nchw_nhwc_roundtrip; cargo fmt check; git diff check; scripts/check_ledgers.py; br dep cycles; cargo check all targets with the asupersync patch; cargo clippy all targets with -D warnings; full cargo test in the same target dir; AGENTS UBS file-list form still exits usage 2 on UBS v5.0.3, and supported ubs --only=rust --diff --ci completed with Critical 0.

title: .focrq loader accepts invalid Baidu MIT license_notice text
description: Fresh-eyes follow-up from bd-1wq5. The format spec and AGENTS.md require redistributed quantized focrq artifacts to carry the Baidu Unlimited-OCR MIT notice: the header license_notice must include Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu and MIT License. The current FocrqHeader requires the JSON field to exist, but Weights::from_focrq_bytes stores it without validating the contents, so a malformed artifact with an empty or non-MIT notice can load. Patch the reader to reject invalid notice text with FocrError::FormatMismatch, add focused tests, and keep this narrower than bd-223.14 CLI, robot, and version surfaces.
close_reason: Fixed the .focrq loader to reject invalid license_notice text unless it includes both Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu and MIT License. Added a focused malformed-notice regression test and updated the hand-built .focrq helper to use the valid Baidu MIT notice. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt --check; cargo +nightly test native_engine::weights::tests --lib; cargo +nightly test quant::focrq --lib; python3 scripts/check_focrq_format.py; git diff --check on touched files; ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/weights.rs (exit 0, existing test warning noise only). scripts/check.sh passed ledger/doc scripts, cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and full cargo test; final broad ubs --diff produced no output for several minutes in the shared dirty tree and was interrupted, with targeted UBS substituted for touched files.

title: Golden-fixture harness + PROVENANCE.md (frozen reference loader, canonicalization, UPDATE_GOLDENS workflow)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Every parity gate, golden-artifact snapshot, differential test, and e2e comparison reads a FROZEN fixture produced by the Python/HF reference oracle. Without a disciplined fixture harness + provenance, fixtures rot, get silently regenerated, or are compared against the wrong model version — and the whole conformance ladder becomes meaningless. This task builds the shared, dependency-light fixture machinery the rest of E-TEST and all per-phase parity beads consume. It serves the over-arching goal directly: correctness-before-speed is only enforceable if the "correct" reference is frozen, hashed, and traceable to the exact model commit.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/fixtures/` harness with these concrete deliverables:
1. **Directory layout** (mirrors plan §4.1):
   - `tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json` — frozen `focr ocr --json` reference outputs (decoded text + bbox tags) per golden image.
   - `tests/fixtures/activations/<stage>.npy` — per-layer activation dumps from the oracle: post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector, per-decoder-layer hidden states, pre-lm_head, logits.
   - `tests/fixtures/tokenizer/<case>.json` — frozen token-id sequences for the OQ-16 tokenizer conformance corpus.
   - `tests/fixtures/golden_corpus/` — the golden image corpus spanning {sparse page, dense page, 10-page, 40-page} (the §9.1 profiling/regime corpus, shared).
2. **`tests/fixtures/PROVENANCE.md`** (MANDATORY, per §8.6): records `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, `Pillow==12.1.1`, the pinned HF repo commit + GitHub commit (from the Phase −1 truth pack), the git ref of `gen_reference_fixtures.py`, the exact command, the `model.safetensors.index.json` `total_size` (6,672,212,480 bytes / 2710 tensors), and a `claim_id → fixture-hash` index. Each fixture file carries a SHA-256 recorded here.
3. **Rust fixture loader** (`tests/common/fixtures.rs` or similar) — a dependency-free reader: load `.npy` activation tensors (use the `fnp-io` NPY pattern or a small in-repo reader), load reference JSON, expose `Fixture::load(name) -> Fixture` with the recorded SHA-256 verified on load (refuse a fixture whose hash drifted).
4. **Canonicalization** before any golden compare: strip/scrub timing, run-id, durations from JSON; normalize line endings + paths for cross-platform; sort bbox lists. Per `/testing-golden-artifacts`: exact (insta) for `--json` structure + CLI help; fuzzy (epsilon/ULP) for logits + activation tensors (the comparator itself is E-VERIFY's ULP table — this harness just plumbs the values in); scrubbed for robot NDJSON; canonicalized for cross-platform.
5. **`UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` workflow**: regenerating goldens requires the env flag + a mandatory `git diff` review; `*.actual` files gitignored; **CI never auto-updates goldens** (a CI run with `UPDATE_GOLDENS` set is rejected).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Provenance is non-negotiable** (§8.6): a fixture without recorded oracle version + command + hash is invalid. The model changed on 2026-06-24; optimizing against a stale fixture is the exact failure mode the Phase −1 truth pack exists to prevent.
- **Hash-verify on load**: catches a corrupted/edited fixture immediately, not as a confusing parity failure 5 layers later.
- **Intentional divergence = XFAIL, never SKIP** (§8.6): if our impl deliberately differs from the reference, the fixture comparison is `XFAIL` with a `DISC-NNN` pointer into `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md`, so coverage accounting still counts it.
- **bf16 oracle nondeterminism**: fixtures from the oracle carry the measured nondeterminism envelope (§8.2) so downstream tolerances derive from MEASURED oracle variance, not a guessed epsilon. This harness stores that envelope alongside the fixtures (E-VERIFY computes it).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- `.npy` fixtures for 129280-vocab logits and per-layer hidden states are large — keep the committed set to the minimal golden corpus; large multi-page activation dumps may be model-gated (regenerated locally, not committed).
- A reference JSON with embedded timestamps will diff every run if not scrubbed — scrubbing is part of the loader, applied BEFORE compare.
- The 40-page golden's reference output is cross-page DEPENDENT under R-SWA (OQ-13) — do NOT store it as a concatenation of single-page parses; it is a single multi-page oracle run.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `tests/fixtures/PROVENANCE.md` present with all required fields + a SHA-256 per fixture.
- Fixture loader verifies SHA-256 on load and refuses a drifted fixture (proven by a test that mutates a byte).
- Canonicalization deterministically scrubs timing/run-id/durations and normalizes line endings/paths (proven by a round-trip test).
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` regenerates `*.actual` and requires explicit review; CI rejects runs with the flag set.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: loader rejects a fixture whose recorded SHA-256 ≠ on-disk content.
- Unit: canonicalizer is idempotent and removes all scrub-listed fields.
- Unit: a missing PROVENANCE field fails a `provenance_complete()` check.
- See sibling bead **TEST-fixture-harness-tests** for the full structured-logging test suite.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs `tests/fixtures/` scaffold + `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py` skeleton + the `.npy`/JSON conventions established.
- depends_on **E-VERIFY**: the oracle that PRODUCES these fixtures and the ULP/tolerance comparator that consumes them are E-VERIFY's; this harness is the frozen-storage + provenance + canonicalization plumbing around them.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — committed-vs-regenerated fixture policy, git-size discipline, staleness check
- **Concrete small-vs-large fixture policy + generation manifest.** The body says large multi-page activation dumps 'may be model-gated (regenerated locally, not committed)' but gives no MECHANISM. Define it concretely: a `tests/fixtures/MANIFEST.toml` listing every fixture as `committed` (small: the OQ-16 tokenizer id sequences, the canonicalized `<doc>_reference.json` text/bbox, a handful of small post-connector activations) or `regenerated` (large: 129280-vocab logits, per-decoder-layer hidden states for multi-page) with its SHA-256, byte size, and the `gen_reference_fixtures.py` invocation that produces it. The loader treats a `regenerated` fixture as model-gated (skip-with-SUCCESS if absent). This keeps the committed set small and git-sane while the heavy `.npy` are reproducible on demand.
- **Git-size discipline.** State the rule: NO multi-MB `.npy` committed without an explicit decision; prefer storing a small canonicalized slice + the regeneration command over a full vocab-wide logits dump. (If LFS is ever adopted it's an explicit decision, not a default.)
- **Fixture-staleness check tied to the pinned model commit.** PROVENANCE.md records the HF + GitHub model commit (E-PM1 truth pack); ADD a `fixtures_match_pinned_model()` check that compares PROVENANCE's recorded model commit/hash against the project's currently-pinned model commit and FAILS LOUDLY on mismatch — the exact 2026-06-24 'model changed under us' failure mode this harness exists to prevent. A stale fixture must be a hard error, not a silent wrong-reference compare.
- **`.actual` + regenerated-fixture gitignore.** Ensure `*.actual` AND the `regenerated`-class large `.npy` are gitignored so a local oracle run never accidentally commits a 6-vocab-wide dump.

title: Fail closed on malformed focrq writer tensor layouts
description: Fresh-eyes audit of the .focrq writer found that FocrqBuilder validated quantized payload byte length but not all metadata required for artifacts to round-trip through the committed reader/accessors. Shape products and shape*dtype byte counts used unchecked arithmetic, QInt8 accepted arbitrary scale byte lengths/nonzero group metadata, and QInt4 accepted malformed group divisibility or scale byte counts before emitting an invalid artifact. Fixed staging-time validation with checked numel/byte-count math plus QInt8 and QInt4 scale/layout checks. Added regression tests for product overflow, byte-count overflow, QInt8 scale metadata, and QInt4 group/scale metadata. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test; ubs --format=json --ci --only=rust src/quant/focrq.rs (0 critical).
close_reason: Fixed in src/quant/focrq.rs. FocrqBuilder now checks shape products and dtype byte counts, validates QInt8 scale metadata, and validates QInt4 rank/group/divisibility/scale byte layout before accepting staged tensors. Added focused regression tests and verified with the full cargo gate plus per-file UBS zero critical findings.

title: Bug: native forward drops preprocessed views and original image dimensions
description: Fresh-eyes audit found OcrModel stage-helper stubs still returning Vec::new() for views and (0,0) for image_dims even though Preprocessed now carries real global/tile tensors plus original_size. This makes the forward skip the vision tower entirely and lose bbox de-normalization dimensions, then fail later at embed_prompt instead of exercising the next real stage. Fix should thread pre.global/pre.tiles and pre.original_size through the helper seam with a focused regression test.
close_reason: Fixed native OcrModel helper seam and corrected it after fresh-eyes review: image_dims now uses Preprocessed original_size, views now forwards local tile tensors first and the global thumbnail last to preserve the connector local/global ordering invariant, and global grid dimensions derive from base_size. Added unit coverage for dimensions and view ordering. Verified fmt, diff whitespace, native_engine tests, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, full cargo test, and UBS on src/native_engine/mod.rs.

title: many_pages_without_deadlock concurrency watchdog (pages ≫ pool; hangs/times out on regression)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The single most expensive bug class in the sibling projects was a **nested-rayon-under-a-held-lock deadlock**; the frankensearch deadlock saga converged after 5 commits on the architectural fix. AGENTS.md doctrine #5 and plan §3.3/§6.5 make the rule non-negotiable: **single OcrModel behind a cache, SEQUENTIAL outer page/document loop, each forward fans out across all cores internally via the kernel's own rayon pool (pinned to physical cores, ONE live forward at a time), NEVER nest rayon under a held lock, NEVER nest a second asupersync runtime inside a task.** The durable guard is a CI watchdog: `many_pages_without_deadlock` runs pages ≫ pool and HANGS (CI timeout) on regression. This bead builds that watchdog.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/many_pages_without_deadlock.rs`:
1. **Drive the engine over MANY pages** — page count ≫ the rayon pool size and ≫ the asupersync `blocking_threads` (e.g. 200+ pages over a 2-blocking-thread / N-core pool) through the real sequential page loop facade.
2. **Hard wall-clock timeout**: the test runs under `asupersync::time::timeout` (or a spawned watchdog thread) with a generous-but-bounded budget; exceeding it = a deadlock = test FAIL (in CI, the job timeout is the backstop). A deadlock manifests as a HANG, not a panic — the timeout IS the assertion.
3. **Assert no oversubscription**: log + assert that at most ONE live forward fans out at a time (the §6.5 invariant) and that `workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical-core budget` (the §6.9 capacity-certificate invariant). Emit these as structured `stage` log lines with `simd_tier` + thread counts.
4. **Model-gated**: if weights are present, run real forwards; if absent, run a faithful STUB forward that reproduces the SAME concurrency topology (kernel rayon fan-out called from the sequential loop, the engine owning one runtime) so the deadlock SHAPE is exercised in CI without the 6.67 GB model. The stub must use the real orchestrator/`OcrEngine` concurrency code, not a fake — only the math is stubbed.
5. **Regression-detector self-test**: a `#[ignore]`-by-default variant that INJECTS a nested-rayon-under-lock (or a nested second runtime) and asserts the watchdog TIMES OUT — proving the watchdog actually catches the regression (run manually, not in the green CI path).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Pages ≫ pool** is the exact condition the frankensearch saga proved exposes the deadlock — fewer pages can mask it.
- **Stub-but-real-topology** so CI (no weights) still guards the concurrency architecture; the deadlock is in the ORCHESTRATION, not the math, so stubbing the math is sound and necessary.
- **The hang IS the signal**: a deadlock cannot be caught by an assertion that never runs; the wall-clock timeout converts a hang into a deterministic CI failure.
- **Capacity-certificate invariants logged** (§3.3/§6.9): no nested-runtime, no rayon-pool oversubscription, one live forward — these are the soak-test's pass conditions.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Cancellation is cooperative**: `spawn_blocking` keeps running on drop, so the watchdog timing out does not necessarily reclaim the thread — the test process may need to be killed; in CI the job timeout handles it, but the test should still report FAIL via the watchdog thread before the job timeout where possible.
- The stub forward must NOT accidentally serialize everything (which would mask a real deadlock) — it must reproduce the real fan-out-under-the-sequential-loop topology.
- NUMA/affinity features (`FOCR_NUMA`, hwloc) are behind a feature flag; the watchdog runs the no-NUMA fallback by default and the NUMA path when the feature is enabled.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` completes within its wall-clock budget on a healthy tree (CI green).
- The injected-regression self-test TIMES OUT (proving the watchdog catches a nested-rayon-under-lock / nested-runtime regression) — demonstrated once and documented.
- Structured logs assert: ≤1 live forward at a time; `workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical cores`; no nested runtime.
- Runs model-gated (real forwards with weights; real-topology stub without).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The watchdog itself (the soak).
- The `#[ignore]` regression-detector that proves the watchdog times out under an injected violation.
- A capacity-certificate log assertion test (p95/p99 queueing evidence + no-oversubscription proof, §3.3).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs `OcrEngine` owning the one asupersync Runtime, the sequential page-loop facade, and the kernel rayon pool wiring from the scaffold.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the concurrency-topology + capacity-certificate log lines.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — cancellation cleanup + worker-panic propagation (distinct concurrency bugs)
- **Cancellation / Ctrl-C cleanup under the many-pages soak.** A deadlock is one failure class; a botched CANCELLATION is another. Add a soak variant that issues a cancel (ShutdownController / CancellationToken from P0-engine-runtime) MID-run over the pages≫pool workload and asserts: the FinalizerRegistry runs, no thread is left spinning, the engine returns **exit 6 (cancelled)** promptly within a bounded budget, and a subsequent run on the same `OcrEngine` still succeeds (the single cached `OcrModel` mutex is NOT left poisoned/locked). A cancel that hangs is as bad as a deadlock.
- **Worker-panic propagation (no poisoned mutex, no silent hang).** Inject a panic INSIDE a kernel rayon task under the sequential page loop and assert it surfaces as a typed `FocrError` (not a silent hang, not a poisoned `Mutex<OcrModel>` that wedges every subsequent page). This is the exact insidious variant of the deadlock saga: a panic while holding the model lock. Assert the engine recovers (next page works) or fails cleanly with a clear error — never hangs.
- **Log the topology invariants on BOTH the cancel and panic variants**, not just the clean soak: ≤1 live forward, `workers × per_worker_threads ≤ physical cores`, no nested runtime — so the capacity certificate covers the failure paths too.
- **Backpressure assertion.** Assert the streaming `sync_channel` stays BOUNDED under the soak (the §7.3 bounded-channel + `recv_timeout(~40ms)` drain) — an unbounded-growth regression is a memory bug the watchdog should also catch.

title: focrq writer accepts noncanonical QInt4 group sizes
description: The .focrq writer validates QInt4 group_size only as nonzero and dividing k. The format docs and quant/int4.rs define valid QInt4 group sizes as exactly 16 or 32 (tiers Int4G16 / Int4G32). As written, FocrqBuilder::add_quantized can emit artifacts with group_size=2, 4, 8, etc.; the reader mechanically accepts them, but those artifacts violate docs/focrq-format.md and the quantizer contract. Fix the writer to reject non-{16,32} QInt4 group sizes and keep tests using canonical sizes.
close_reason: Fixed FocrqBuilder validation so QInt4 writer output must use canonical group sizes 16 or 32, with canonical roundtrip and noncanonical rejection coverage. Verified with quant::focrq, quant --lib, full scripts/check.sh through cargo test, and focused UBS on src/quant/focrq.rs.

title: PDF decompression-bomb cap: clamp hostile /BitsPerComponent so cap can't saturate to u64::MAX (follow-up to bd-2zpu)
close_reason: Fixed in 89c5b21: expected_sample_cap clamps bpc to 1..=16 so a hostile /BitsPerComponent can't saturate the cap to u64::MAX (which made bounded_inflate's take(cap+1) unbounded). Added unit tests for the clamp + bounded_inflate accept/reject/fallback. Gate green.

title: PDF decompress-bomb: bound FlateDecode/LZW inflate before it allocates
description: decompressed_stream (src/pdf.rs) calls lopdf Stream::decompressed_content(), which allocates the FULL inflated output before any length check. The MAX_PIXELS guard bounds DECLARED image dims (and the cmyk/raster Vecs), but a tiny highly-compressed FlateDecode/LZW stream (zip-bomb, 1000:1) inflates to GBs during decompressed_content() regardless of declared dims — a residual OOM/DoS vector inherent to lopdf's non-streaming inflate. Mitigation options: (a) compute expected_len = width*height*comps*ceil(bpc/8) and reject before decode when the stream's declared/encoded size implies an implausible ratio; (b) upstream a size-bounded inflate in lopdf; (c) cap process RSS. Pre-existing lopdf behavior, not introduced by the native-PDF work; low severity (needs a crafted adversarial PDF). Found during the native-PDF fresh-eyes review (bd-0a7 epic).
close_reason: src/pdf.rs decompressed_stream bounds the sole-FlateDecode inflate via flate2 ZlibDecoder + Read::take(cap+1) under expected_sample_cap (4x the MAX_PIXELS-bounded sample bytes); a clean inflate overrunning the cap errors as a decompression bomb, non-zlib raw falls back to lopdf. flate2 now a direct dep. Commit ba71ff4; centralized gate (fmt/clippy -D warnings/test) green.

title: Resolve preprocess L0 resampling decision: CatmullRom vs PIL ImageOps.pad BICUBIC (bit-exactness risk)
description: REALITY-CHECK (2026-06-26, DustyMink) NO_BEAD gap. src/preprocess/mod.rs:408 uses image::imageops::FilterType::CatmullRom for the aspect-preserving resize, explicitly NOT PIL ImageOps.pad BICUBIC (the oracle path). This is a live L0 bit-exactness divergence with only the (blocked) test bead bd-1gv.3.1 downstream and no functional bead to resolve it. FIX: decide — either implement a PIL-BICUBIC-matching resampler for L0-exact preprocessing, or formally ledger the CatmullRom divergence in docs/DISCREPANCIES.md with a measured CER impact + kill-switch. Gate on the L0 EXACT compare once an oracle preprocessed-tensor fixture exists.

title: Phase -1: Source/Oracle Truth Pack — pin sources, hash everything, resolve every OPEN/OQ before any kernel
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the executable foundation of franken_ocr and **the highest-EV epic in the project**. Per the `/idea-wizard` review transcribed in plan §10 Phase -1: *"the single highest-EV move is NOT a kernel — it is an executable truth pack that stops the project from optimizing the wrong model semantics (the model changed on 2026-06-24)."* The target model `baidu/Unlimited-OCR` was released ~2026-06-22, **post knowledge-cutoff**, so every model-specific claim in the master plan is tagged [VERIFIED] (confirmed from a primary source), [REPORTED] (paper/card, not line-verified), or [OPEN] (must be code-confirmed). This epic converts all [OPEN]/OQ items into pinned, line-backed answers and freezes the exact sources so every downstream kernel is built against a known model version.

The over-arching goal franken_ocr serves: **max CPU speed for THIS one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels, correctness before speed, a portable single binary, agent-ergonomic**. None of that is possible if the model semantics are wrong. AGENTS.md hard rule: *"no kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN]. A phase exit gate cannot pass while it depends on an unresolved OQ."* This epic is what makes that rule satisfiable.

## WHAT TO DO (the Phase -1 deliverable, plan §10)
Produce, all under `docs/truth-pack/` with a machine-readable `claim_id → source-line` index:
1. **Pin the exact sources**: HF repo commit + GitHub commit. Codex-reported candidates to VERIFY against the live repos: `HF 3a7f4dbbbffcc6f9282712c5b0d7cc31b3812da5`, `GitHub 7e98affeacba24e95562fbaa234ddb89b856874a` — record the *verified* hashes, not the reported ones.
2. **Snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source**: `config.json`, `tokenizer.json`, `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`, `modeling_deepseekv2.py`, `deepencoder.py`, `conversation.py`, `model.safetensors.index.json`, `LICENSE`.
3. **Generate the machine-readable token/shape/buffer census** from the pinned config (§2.4, §8.6), CI-guarded against source drift.
4. **Extract the spec** `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` (§8.6 / §10 Phase 1) — every data structure, exact preprocessing/tiling rules, `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` semantics, projector concat order, tokenizer rules — verbatim from the pinned `modeling_*.py`/`deepencoder.py`/`conversation.py`.
5. **Resolve every [OPEN]/OQ-1..OQ-18 (§13)** with a quoted, line-backed citation from the pinned source.
6. **Decide the oracle strategy (OQ-17, CPU-vs-GPU)** — prove CPU HF runs, else GPU-correctness + CPU-perf split (§8.1) — and capture one smoke fixture.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Spec-first porting** (`/porting-to-rust`, §8.6): *extract spec → implement from spec → never translate line-by-line.* The four documents are PLAN_TO_PORT (the master plan), EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md (THE SPEC, this epic produces it), PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md. Kernels are implemented from the spec, which is what resolves the §13 [OPEN]s.
- **Pin + hash before anything**: the model is freshly released and may be re-uploaded; a moving reference silently invalidates every fixture and every measured ratio. The negative-evidence ledger schema (docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) *requires* a `model source commit + fixture hash` field on every entry — that field is sourced from this truth pack.
- **CI-guarded census**: plan §2.4 — *"A machine-readable token/shape/buffer census (§8.6, generated from the pinned config) is the source of truth and CI fails if the source/config changes."*

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Plan §11 risk *"[OPEN] model details wrong"* (MED-HIGH): hard rule no kernel ships against an unresolved [OPEN]; this epic gates Phase 1. R-SWA/RoPE/projector/SAM-window must read the actual source first.
- The token budget is **NOT just 256** (§2.4): a 1024 view yields 256 image-feature + structural tokens ⇒ `(16+1)·16 + 1 = 273` slots; the census must count every token CLASS or KV/buffer sizing is wrong.
- OQ-17 gotcha: `infer()` is CUDA-oriented; a CPU bf16 HF oracle is not guaranteed to run, which decides the meaning of G2 (perf claim).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase -1 exit gates, plan §10)
- Every OQ (OQ-1..OQ-18) answered with a pinned-source citation (file + line range + quoted text).
- All source hashes recorded (SHA-256 of every load-bearing file + verified HF/GitHub commits).
- Census generated + CI-guarded against source drift.
- Oracle strategy decided + smoke fixture captured.
- **No kernel work begins until this phase is green.**

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A CI guard test that re-hashes the pinned sources and fails on drift (the census generator's drift guard).
- A `claim_id → source-line` index that is itself validated (every OQ answer references a real, in-range line of a hashed file).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
This epic depends on nothing — it is the root of the dependency graph. Every kernel epic (E-P1, E-P2, E-P3, E-P4) and verification epic (E-VERIFY) depends on E-PM1 and on specific OQ beads it owns. The OQ beads here are the foundation every kernel depends on.
close_reason: probe

title: Pin + verify the HF repo commit and GitHub commit for baidu/Unlimited-OCR
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The target model was released ~2026-06-22 and could be re-uploaded at any time; HF model repos are mutable. Every golden fixture, every measured perf ratio, and every OQ answer is only meaningful relative to an EXACT immutable commit. Plan §10 Phase -1 makes pinning the first task. The negative-evidence ledger (docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) and DISCREPANCIES.md both require a `model source commit + fixture hash` provenance field — that value comes from here.

## WHAT TO DO
1. Resolve and VERIFY the HF repo commit for `baidu/Unlimited-OCR`. Codex reported `HF 3a7f4dbbbffcc6f9282712c5b0d7cc31b3812da5` — do NOT trust the reported value; query the live HF repo (e.g. `huggingface_hub` revision listing / `git ls-remote https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR`) and record the *verified* full-length commit SHA, the resolution date, and the method used.
2. Resolve and VERIFY the GitHub commit. Codex reported `GitHub 7e98affeacba24e95562fbaa234ddb89b856874a` — verify against the live GitHub repo (`git ls-remote`) and record the verified SHA.
3. Write both verified commits into `docs/truth-pack/PINNED_SOURCES.md` (or equivalent) with: repo URL, verified commit SHA, resolution timestamp, and a note if the verified hash DIFFERS from the Codex-reported one (which would itself be evidence the model moved).
4. Record the runtime pin from the README that the oracle must use: `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, `Pillow==12.1.1`, `pymupdf` — NOT the `4.46.3` recorded in config.json (plan §8.1, §11 reference-version-drift risk).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Verify, don't trust the reported hash**: the whole point of pinning is to detect a moved model; copying Codex's number defeats it.
- **Pin runtime to README, not config**: plan §8.1 explicitly warns config records `4.46.3` while README pins runtime `4.57.1`/`torch 2.10.0`; the oracle and all fixtures use the README runtime.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If the live HF hash differs from the Codex-reported one, the model HAS changed since the plan was written — flag loudly; all [REPORTED] facts must be re-verified.
- HF repos can have separate revisions for LFS pointers vs the resolved blob; record the commit that resolves the safetensors + config + tokenizer together.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/truth-pack/PINNED_SOURCES.md` contains verified full-length HF + GitHub commit SHAs, resolution timestamps, and the verification method.
- Runtime pin (`transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`) recorded.
- Any divergence from the Codex-reported hashes is explicitly flagged.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A check (script or CI step) that the recorded commit still resolves the expected file set; a divergence is a loud failure (this feeds PM1-hash-sources and PM1-census-generator drift guards).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on E-PM1 (parent). Blocks PM1-hash-sources (you hash files at a pinned commit) and PM1-oracle-strategy (the oracle uses the pinned runtime).
close_reason: test

title: OQ-4: Q/K head dim when use_mla=false and qk_nope/rope_head_dim=0 (likely 128 from q_proj shape)
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-4)
What is the Q/K head dimension when `use_mla=false` and `qk_nope_head_dim=0` / `qk_rope_head_dim=0`? Plan §2.3.2 notes config does not directly give it in this configuration, but it is *"almost certainly 128 from q_proj shape."* Confirm from the actual q_proj weight shape.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_deepseekv2.py` (how q/k head_dim is computed when MLA is off) AND the `q_proj` weight shape from `model.safetensors.index.json` / the tensor census: q_proj is `[out, 1280]`; out = num_attention_heads(10) × head_dim. If out=1280 ⇒ head_dim=128 (consistent with v_head_dim=128, 10·128=1280).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Attention shapes — the q/k/v/o_proj GEMM dimensions and the RoPE rotation dim (`decoder.rs`, `rswa.rs`). A wrong head_dim mis-shapes every attention matmul.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.2: `v_head_dim=128`, `num_attention_heads=10`, `num_key_value_heads=10` (no GQA), `use_mla=false` (plain MHA, no latent decompression). Plan §6.4: *"Plain MHA (no MLA decompression): q/k/v/o_proj separate dense GEMMs, 10 heads, head_dim 128."* Pinning head_dim from the actual tensor shape removes the last [OPEN] in the attention shape set.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: Q/K head_dim value (expected 128), cited to both the modeling code path and the q_proj tensor shape in the census/index.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources and PM1-census-generator (q_proj shape). Feeds the E-P1 attention kernels and the RoPE kernel (OQ-5).
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack OQ_INDEX.md (OQ-4)

title: OQ-5: rope_theta value (default 10000 assumed); is YARN/NTK active at 32K?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-5)
What is the `rope_theta` value? Plan §2.3.2 assumes the DeepseekV2 default 10000 but flags it as not surfaced. And: is YARN/NTK scaling active at the 32K-native context, or are those classes present-but-inactive?

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`configuration_deepseek_v2.py` and the config `language_config` (the hashed `config.json`). Read `rope_theta`, any `rope_scaling` dict (YARN/NTK type + factor), and whether the active `DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding` class uses scaling at `max_position_embeddings=32768`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The RoPE kernel (a BUILD item, plan §4.3: *"RoPE (theta default 10000; YARN present but likely unused). new kernel (confirm theta first)."*). A wrong theta or an unexpectedly-active YARN scaling rotates Q/K incorrectly → attention drift across all layers.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.2: *"RoPE DeepseekV2RotaryEmbedding; YARN/NTK classes present but likely inactive at 32K-native. [OPEN] rope_theta not surfaced (DeepseekV2 default 10000 assumed) — read directly before kernel work."* RoPE feeds the R-SWA attention every layer; getting theta wrong is a silent, pervasive error.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: exact `rope_theta`, the presence/absence/type of `rope_scaling`, and whether YARN/NTK is active at 32K, cited to config + configuration_deepseek_v2.py.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds the E-P1 RoPE kernel and the RMSNorm→RoPE fusion (§6.10). Coupled to OQ-4 (head_dim defines the rotation dim).
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack

title: OQ-6: Exact SAM⊕CLIP feature-fusion / concat order producing the 2048-dim projector input
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-6)
What is the exact SAM⊕CLIP feature-fusion / concat order producing the 2048-dim projector input? Plan §2.3.1 flags it [OPEN]: *"SAM-1024 ⊕ CLIP-1024 vs interleaved; deepencoder.py mentions low_high_hybrid_split_mlp_gelu / hybrid_split_feature_mlp_gelu variants — MUST be read line-by-line from deepencoder.py before implementing the projector."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`deepencoder.py` (line-level). Read how SAM features (1024-dim after neck) and CLIP-L features (1024-dim) are combined into the 2048-dim input to the single linear projector (`projector_config`: input_dim=2048, n_embed=1280, projector_type=linear). Determine: plain concat order (SAM then CLIP vs CLIP then SAM), interleaving, and which `*_hybrid_split_*` code path is actually taken.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The projector + vision bridge (`vision_bridge.rs`). A wrong concat order feeds the 2048→1280 linear the wrong channel layout → every downstream decode token is built on corrupted vision features (catastrophic, but only visible as L1/L2 vision-stage cosine drop). On the Phase-1 critical path per plan §13.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.1: SAM features + CLIP features concatenated to 2048 dims → single linear projector 2048→1280. The 16× compression bridge (64×64→16×16=256 tokens) precedes this. The concat order is the kind of detail that is invisible in the architecture diagram but corrupts everything if wrong.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the exact fusion/concat order and the specific `*_hybrid_split_*` path taken, cited to `deepencoder.py` line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

title: OQ-7: Gundam-mode vision-token count per aspect ratio; dynamic_preprocess min_num/max_num defaults
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-7)
What is the exact Gundam-mode vision-token count per aspect ratio, and what are the `dynamic_preprocess` `min_num`/`max_num` defaults? Plan §2.4 reports the bounds as 2..32 but flags them [OPEN]: *"i*j ∈ [min_num, max_num] (reported 2..32). Each 640 crop ≈ 100 tokens."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` — `dynamic_preprocess()` and `find_closest_aspect_ratio()`. Read the literal default `min_num`/`max_num`, how `i*j` tile combinations are enumerated, and the per-tile token count (each 640×640 crop ≈ 100 tokens; confirm exact). Cross-check against `candidate_resolutions=[[1024,1024]]`, `base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `global_view_pos='head'`, `tile_tag='2D'`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Preprocess tiling (`tile.rs`) and buffer sizing (the census, PM1-census-generator). Gundam multi-tile token totals feed the R-SWA reference-block `m` worst case and the prefill GEMM token counts.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.4: Gundam = dynamic-resolution tiling for a single page; global padded 1024 view + 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()`. The exact min/max tile bounds determine the maximum token count a single page can produce — which sizes the prefill buffers and the reference block.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: literal `min_num`/`max_num` defaults, the per-aspect-ratio tile enumeration, and the per-tile token count, cited to `dynamic_preprocess`/`find_closest_aspect_ratio` line range.

title: OQ-8: Full prompt-mode taxonomy and whether bboxes are emitted in all modes
description:

Determine which modes emit the `<|ref|>…<|/ref|><|det|>…<|/det|>` layout/grounding tags (bbox coords /999) vs plain text/markdown.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Postprocess (`postprocess.rs` — EOS strip, `<|ref|>`/`<|det|>` regex, bbox /999 rescale, markdown assembly) and the CLI prompt contract (which prompts `focr ocr` exposes). If a mode does NOT emit bboxes, the postprocessor must not expect them.

Plan §2.4: output is structured Markdown with HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, normalized 0-999 (×/999) bbox coords, layout tags parsed via regex. Knowing the mode taxonomy lets the CLI offer the right prompt presets and the postprocessor parse the right output shape per mode.

title: OQ-9: Exact activated-param count (500M vs 570M) — docs-only, not kernel-blocking
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-9)
What is the exact activated-parameter count per token — 500M or 570M? Plan §2.2 notes the conflict: *"paper abstract + §3.2 say 500M; Figure 2 labels '3B-MOE-A570M' → treat as ~500–570M, [OPEN] exact figure."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The paper Figure 2 (`Unlimited-OCR.pdf`) AND recompute from the safetensors tensor census: activated params = embed + per-token-active expert subset (6 routed + 2 shared of the MoE FFN) + attention + norms + lm_head. Use the census (PM1-census-generator) to recompute and reconcile with the paper's label.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Docs/claims only (NOT kernel-blocking, per plan §13). It does inform the decode roofline memory-floor estimate (plan §9.1: *"a single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token"*), so a precise figure sharpens the roofline target.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.9: decode is memory-bandwidth-bound, streaming ~500M active params/token; the USL pool-sizing (AF-5) and the roofline memory floor use this number. A precise activated count makes the decode memory-floor honest.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

title: OQ-10: Does community GGUF (PR #17400) quantize router/experts identically to NVFP4 (router-gate-in-f32)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-10)
Does the community GGUF quantization (requiring llama.cpp PR #17400, not upstream) quantize the router/experts identically to NVFP4 — specifically, does it keep the MoE router gate in f32? Plan §2.6 notes both prior-art quants keep the vision encoder unquantized; this cross-checks the router-gate treatment.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-GGUF` repo + llama.cpp PR #17400. Inspect which tensors are quantized vs kept high-precision, focusing on the router `mlp.gate.weight` (1280→64) and the expert `{gate,up,down}_proj`.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Quant-recipe cross-check (NOT blocking, per plan §13). It confirms the §6.7 decision that the router is *never* quantized: *"Router: ... Never quantized (NVFP4 keeps it; quantizing the gate drifts expert selection → cliff)."*

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.6: GGUF recommended Q4_K_M (~1.82 GiB) keeps vision/mmproj F16. If GGUF (like NVFP4) keeps the router gate f32, that is two independent prior-art confirmations of the core recipe (decoder FFN/expert weight-only quant, router/norms/vision high-precision).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A recorded answer: GGUF's router/expert quant treatment, especially whether the router gate stays f32, cross-referenced to NVFP4 (OQ-14).
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED]/[REPORTED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (the GGUF listing snapshot). Cross-checks OQ-14 (NVFP4 module set). Informs the E-P2/E-P4 quant recipe. Not blocking.

title: OQ-11: Is int8 viable for the patch-embed conv specifically (cleaner stats than mid-network ViT)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-11)
Is int8 viable for the patch-embed conv specifically — does it have cleaner activation statistics than mid-network ViT layers (which have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant)? This is an isolated later experiment (Phase 4+), not a v1 question.

## SOURCE / EXPERIMENT TO RUN
An isolated experiment (Phase 4+), NOT a source read of the model definition. Using the per-layer activation dumps from the oracle (PM1-oracle-strategy), inspect the activation distribution at the patch-embed conv output (non-overlapping k=stride, so im2col is exact) vs mid-network ViT post-LayerNorm/post-GELU outputs, and measure whether per-channel int8 + per-token dynamic activation quant on patch-embed holds CER.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
A later vision-int8 experiment ONLY (Phase 4+). Vision stays BF16/F32 in v1 (the validated recipe). Not on any v1 critical path.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.4: *"keep the vision tower BF16/F32 in v1 (the validated recipe; ViT post-LayerNorm/post-GELU activations have severe outliers that wreck low-bit activation quant). Revisit int8 vision ... only after decoder parity is proven, as an isolated experiment."* Patch-embed (§6.4: *"non-overlapping (k=stride) ... ideal SMMLA/VNNI target"*) is the most promising vision-int8 candidate because its stats are cleanest.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A recorded hypothesis + (later) measured result on whether patch-embed int8 holds CER, gated behind decoder-parity-locked.
- [OPEN] remains a deferred experiment marker (low priority; not a Phase -1 blocker, but recorded so the lever is not forgotten).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-oracle-strategy (activation dumps). Feeds a Phase-4+ vision-int8 spike (E-P4). Lowest priority; explicitly deferred.
close_reason: OQ-11 (int8 patch-embed conv) recorded in docs/truth-pack/oq/secondary.md + OQ_INDEX.md as UNMEASURED/deferred: no in-repo experiment isolates the patch-embed conv (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE has decoder-only levers; vision f32-validated at cosine 0.9996). Hypothesis + resolving A/B documented; vision stays high-precision per doctrine #2. Commit ba71ff4.

title: OQ-12: Confirm benchmark cells (TPS, v1.6 peers, 40+pg edit distances) from PDF tables
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-12)

title: OQ-13: Does R-SWA's reference block span ALL pages in a multi-page pass (page N depends on 1..N-1)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-13)
Does R-SWA's reference block span ALL pages in a multi-page pass, making page *N* attend to (and depend on) pages `1..N-1`? Plan §13 marks this as determining both the §8.3 metamorphic gate AND whether the §9.5 cross-page reference-KV "sharing" idea is even sound.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` + `infer_multi(...)` in `modeling_*.py` (`modeling_unlimitedocr.py` / `modeling_deepseekv2.py`). Plan §2.4: *"Multi-page infer_multi(...) concatenates pages at <image> with a dummy crop tensor to force the no-crop branch."* Read whether, in a single 32K pass, ALL pages' visual+prompt prefixes form ONE frozen reference block (so page N can attend to pages 1..N-1), or whether pages are independent.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
Three things: (1) the §8.3 metamorphic gate — AGENTS.md doctrine #7 and plan §8.3 forbid asserting *"multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses"*; if cross-page dependent, the defensible property is the OPPOSITE (*"changing page order / earlier-page content MAY change later-page output"*). (2) The §9.5 cross-page reference-KV sharing optimization — *"Only sound if the multi-page reference block does NOT make page N depend on 1..N-1; if it does ... naive KV-sharing would silently corrupt later pages."* (3) Multi-page decode correctness generally.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
AGENTS.md doctrine #7: *"Multi-page decode is cross-page dependent (OQ-13) — do not assume 'multi-page = sum of single-page parses.'"* This is a correctness-and-test-design-defining question, on the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: whether the multi-page reference block is shared across all pages (page N depends on 1..N-1) or pages are independent, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` + `infer_multi` line ranges.
- The resulting metamorphic property statement and the soundness verdict on cross-page KV-sharing recorded.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (multi-page section), the E-VERIFY metamorphic gate (§8.3), the E-P3/E-P4 KV-sharing spike (§9.5), and multi-page decode correctness in E-P1. On the Phase-1 critical path. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-3.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## STRUCTURED RECORD OF THE METAMORPHIC VERDICT (r2)
Because OQ-13's answer flips a SIGN on a test design (§8.3 forbids 'multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses' and the §9.5 KV-sharing optimization is only sound if pages are independent), record the verdict in a machine-readable cell the downstream gates read directly: `{oq: "OQ-13", cross_page_dependent: bool, reference_block_span: "all-pages-shared"|"per-page-independent", metamorphic_property_id, kv_sharing_sound: bool, cited_lines: [...]}`. E-VERIFY's metamorphic gate and the E-P3/E-P4 KV-sharing spike consume this cell, not the prose — a wrong sign here silently mis-designs a whole class of tests.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack

title: Snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source file under docs/truth-pack/
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The spec extraction and every OQ answer quote specific lines of specific source files. Those quotes are only trustworthy if the files are content-addressed and frozen. Plan §10 Phase -1: *"snapshot + SHA-256 every load-bearing source."* The hashes become the `fixture hash` provenance field every NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md / DISCREPANCIES.md entry carries (plan §8.4 artifact-graph fields: model source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, exact command + env, fallback/kill-switch state).

## WHAT TO DO
At the pinned commit (from PM1-pin-sources), snapshot and SHA-256 each load-bearing file, storing the snapshot + its hash + byte length under `docs/truth-pack/sources/` with a manifest `docs/truth-pack/SOURCE_HASHES.md`:
- `config.json` (top-level + `language_config`, `vision_config.sam_vit_b`, `vision_config.'clip-l-14-224'`, `projector_config`)
- `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB, vocab 129280, the DeepSeek vocab)
- `tokenizer_config.json`
- `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`
- `modeling_deepseekv2.py` (contains `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`, `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor`)
- `deepencoder.py`
- `conversation.py`
- `configuration_deepseek_v2.py`
- `model.safetensors.index.json` (total_size 6,672,212,480 bytes, 2710 tensors)
- `LICENSE` (MIT, Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu)
- (Optional secondary oracles) the `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-NVFP4` and `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-GGUF` tensor/scale listings for OQ-14 / OQ-10.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Content-addressed sources**: a `claim_id → (file_sha256, line range, quoted text)` index makes every spec claim reproducible and re-checkable; this is the backbone of the spec-first method (§8.6).
- **Hash the LICENSE too**: plan §2.2 — the MIT `LICENSE` (Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu) is what legally permits redistributing a quantized derivative; the verbatim notice must be embedded in `.focrq` `license_notice`. Freezing its hash proves what notice we are obligated to ship.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 6.67 GB safetensors shard itself is NOT committed (too large); record its SHA-256 from `model.safetensors.index.json` metadata + an out-of-band hash of the downloaded blob (this becomes the `.focrq` `source_sha256`, plan §5.2).
- Line numbers must be recorded against the *exact* snapshot — re-indenting or reformatting the source would invalidate every cited line.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every file above has a recorded SHA-256 + byte length in `docs/truth-pack/SOURCE_HASHES.md`.
- The safetensors blob's SHA-256 is recorded (out-of-band) and matches the future `.focrq` `source_sha256`.
- The snapshots are immutable copies (or git-pinned references) the spec/OQ beads can cite by line.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A CI hash-drift guard: re-hash each pinned source; fail loudly if any hash changes (shared with PM1-census-generator's drift guard).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-pin-sources (you hash at the pinned commit). Blocks PM1-census-generator, PM1-spec-extraction, and ALL OQ beads (every OQ quotes a hashed source by line).
close_reason: Resolved: SOURCE_HASHES.md

title: OQ-14: Enumerate the EXACT ~2196 NVFP4 linear modules and reconcile vs our ~2229 census
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-14)
Enumerate the EXACT set of ~2196 linear modules that NVFP4 quantizes (dump its scale keys) and reconcile against our ~2229-module census. Plan §2.6: the "2196" does NOT reconcile — our census of quantizable decoder linears is experts 11·64·3=2112 + attention 12·4=48 + shared 11·2·3=66 + dense-layer-0 3 (+ lm_head 1) ≈ 2229-2230, ~33 more than 2196. *"So NVFP4 is NOT quantizing the set we assume — it likely SKIPS attention q/k/v/o and/or lm_head and/or the shared experts."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
The `sahilchachra/Unlimited-OCR-NVFP4` tensor/scale listing (the hashed snapshot). Dump every quantized module's scale key and build the exact set. NVFP4 quantizes only linear modules to FP4 (group size 16, NVFP4A16, data-free PTQ), keeping vision tower, projector, embeddings, output layer, MoE router gate, and ALL norms in BF16, ~2.93 GB, and *"Reports OCR output identical to BF16"* (on the author's docs only; no published CER).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The §2.6 recipe SCOPE and, specifically, the validation status of two choices that go BEYOND the validated set (plan §2.6, AGENTS.md doctrine #2): **int8 on attention q/k/v/o** and **int8 on lm_head**. Both are *"explicitly OUR risk to retire by measurement (OQ-14)"*, gated behind a measured-CER kill-switch (`FOCR_INT8_ATTN`, `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`). Knowing whether NVFP4 quantizes attention/lm_head/shared-experts tells us which of our choices are validated-by-prior-art vs novel-risk.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
The core recipe (quantize decoder expert/dense FFN GEMMs; keep vision/projector/embeddings/router/norms high-precision) is converged. But Phase 2 stages quant precisely to isolate the beyond-validated choices (plan §10 Phase 2): 2a FFN/experts only → 2b attention q/k/v/o behind kill-switch → 2c lm_head behind kill-switch. OQ-14 tells us which stages carry prior-art cover.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The exact NVFP4-quantized module set enumerated from its scale keys, reconciled cell-by-cell against the PM1-census-generator quantizable-linear enumeration (account for all ~33 missing).
- An explicit per-class verdict: does NVFP4 quantize attention q/k/v/o? lm_head? shared experts? — marking each of our beyond-validated choices as prior-art-validated or our-CER-risk.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (NVFP4 snapshot) and PM1-census-generator (the ~2229 denominator). Feeds the E-P2 quant staging (2b/2c kill-switches) and the E-P4 int4 tier choices. Records the int8-attention / int8-lm_head validation status that DISCREPANCIES.md tracks.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack OQ_INDEX.md (OQ-14 (our set; NVFP4 cross-check deferred to Phase-2))

title: OQ-15: SAM windowed-attention window size (assumed 14) and the windowed-block position-embedding scheme
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-15)
What is the SAM windowed-attention window size (assumed 14) and the windowed-block position-embedding scheme? Plan §2.3.1 flags it [OPEN]: *"window=14 inherited from the SAM/DeepSeek-OCR lineage, NOT config-verified; only global_attn_indexes [2,5,8,11] is config-confirmed."* A wrong window/pos-embed silently corrupts vision features (surfaces only as a fuzzy L2 cosine).

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`deepencoder.py` (line-level). Read the SAM-ViT-B block construction: the literal window size for non-global blocks, how windows partition the 64×64 patch grid, and the windowed-block position-embedding handling (the learnable abs `pos_embed (1,64,64,768)`, bicubic-interpolated for non-1024/tiled inputs). Confirm global attention is only at blocks {2,5,8,11}.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
SAM window attention (`vision_sam.rs`) and the L2 parity gate. Plan §4.3: *"Windowed self-attention (window=14 [OPEN, OQ-15]) — confirm window size + pos-embed scheme from deepencoder.py first."* A wrong window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme silently corrupts vision features (plan §2.3.1).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.1: window attention processes the dense uncompressed tokens; global attention is reserved for the compressed set — keeps activations low for high-res. The window must partition the patch grid exactly as the source does. On the Phase-1 critical path (plan §14.3).

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: the literal SAM window size, the partition scheme over the 64×64 grid, and the windowed-block pos-embed handling (including bicubic interpolation for tiled inputs), cited to `deepencoder.py` line range.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (SAM section) and the E-P1 `vision_sam.rs` windowed attention + the f32 bicubic pos-embed interpolation kernel. On the Phase-1 critical path.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack
