title: Decide the oracle strategy (CPU-vs-GPU split) and capture one smoke fixture
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The whole parity ladder (L0-L5) and the perf claim G2 hinge on what reference we compare against. Plan §8.1 warns: *"The official infer() path is CUDA-oriented (.cuda() + CUDA autocast), so a CPU bf16 HF oracle is NOT guaranteed to run as-is (OQ-17)."* The resolution (plan §8.1) is to SPLIT the oracle: **(correctness)** golden fixtures come from the unmodified official model on a CUDA host, frozen once and committed — parity NEVER depends on CPU HF; **(performance)** the CPU baseline is separate — either a CPU-patched HF run (force tensors to CPU, disable autocast) that is PROVEN to reproduce the GPU oracle's tokens within the nondeterminism floor (§8.2), or, if CPU HF is infeasible, the perf claim is stated against the best CPU reference that actually runs (llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX Runtime / MLAS) and labeled as such. This bead IS the OQ-17 decision plus the Phase -1 smoke fixture.

## WHAT TO DO
1. Read `modeling_unlimitedocr.py` `infer()` — record the exact device/autocast lines (`.cuda()`, autocast dtype). (This is the OQ-17 source read.)
2. Attempt a CPU-patched HF run (force CPU, disable autocast) of `model.infer()` on one golden image and determine whether it reproduces the GPU oracle's token sequence within the oracle nondeterminism floor.
3. **Establish the oracle's OWN nondeterminism floor FIRST** (plan §8.2): run the oracle twice and at two thread counts over a small corpus; record the per-token divergence rate + first-divergence position as a committed fixture (the bf16 129280-vocab argmax is tie-prone across BLAS reduction order). L4 "exact" is defined only over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically; the L3 logit tolerance is DERIVED from this measured variance, NOT from the imported frankensearch `0.055`.
4. Decide and record in `docs/truth-pack/ORACLE_STRATEGY.md`: which path is correctness oracle (frozen GPU fixtures), which is perf baseline (CPU-patched HF if proven, else llama.cpp/ONNX/MLAS labeled), and the resulting meaning of G2.
5. Capture one smoke fixture: per-layer activation dumps (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector, per-decoder-layer hidden states, pre-lm_head, logits) + end-to-end decoded text for one golden image, with a `PROVENANCE.md` (`transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, git ref, exact command — plan §8.6).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Correctness fixtures are GPU-frozen and committed** — parity must never depend on a CPU HF run that may not exist (§8.1).
- **Nondeterminism floor measured before tolerances** (§8.2): a franken_ocr int8 divergence inside the oracle's own bf16 noise is NOT a bug; setting tolerances before measuring the floor would either over- or under-gate.
- **G2 honesty** (§1.1, §11): G2's "beats the CPU reference" is only honest against whatever CPU reference is PROVEN to run; this decision fixes that.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If CPU-patched HF cannot reproduce GPU tokens, the gauntlet (§9.3) targets llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX / MLAS and labels every ratio with the precision (focr-int8 vs torch-bf16 vs torch-int8) — a raw cross-precision ratio is meaningless (§9.3 precision annotation rule).
- The community GGUF requires llama.cpp PR #17400 (not upstream) — if used as the CPU perf reference, record that dependency.
- Use `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)` + seeded RNG for the gauntlet oracle bridge (§8.5), but the *correctness* fixtures capture the production (non-deterministic) path so the floor is real.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `ORACLE_STRATEGY.md` records the OQ-17 decision (CPU HF runs/reproduces-or-not) with the cited `infer()` device/autocast lines.
- The oracle nondeterminism floor is measured and committed (per-token divergence rate, first-divergence position) — the source of the L3/L4 tolerances.
- One smoke fixture (per-layer + end-to-end) is captured with full `PROVENANCE.md`.
- The perf-baseline choice (CPU-patched HF vs llama.cpp/ONNX) is recorded with its proof-of-equivalence (or its labeled-as-such caveat).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A fixture-provenance check: the smoke fixture carries `PROVENANCE.md` with the pinned runtime + git ref + exact command.
- A determinism-floor sanity test: re-loading the committed floor fixture and asserting the recorded divergence envelope is well-formed (used by E-VERIFY to derive L3/L4 tolerances).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-pin-sources (uses the pinned runtime) and PM1-hash-sources (cites the hashed `modeling_unlimitedocr.py`). IS the resolution of OQ-17 (depends_on OQ-17 read). Blocks E-VERIFY (the parity ladder uses these fixtures + floor) and the perf gauntlet (E-P3) which targets the chosen CPU baseline.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## RUNTIME REPRODUCIBILITY ARTIFACT (r2)
The correctness fixtures and PROVENANCE.md pin `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, `Pillow==12.1.1`, `pymupdf` — but a version string is not a reproducible environment. This bead should also freeze a **fully-resolved lockfile** (`docs/truth-pack/oracle-env/requirements.lock` with transitive pins + hashes, e.g. `pip freeze` / `pip-compile --generate-hashes` output) and record the exact Python + CUDA driver versions used to generate the GPU golden fixtures. Rationale: a future re-run of `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py` (E-P0) that resolves a different transitive dep silently changes the bf16 nondeterminism floor, invalidating the derived L3/L4 tolerances. The lockfile is the reproducibility anchor the floor measurement and every committed fixture inherit. Cross-reference: §10 Phase 0 builds `scripts/fetch_model.sh` — it MUST pin to the PM1-pin-sources verified HF commit, not a floating `main`.

## STRUCTURED-LOGGING REQUIREMENT (r2)
The nondeterminism-floor measurement must emit NDJSON: per run `{image_id, thread_count, seed, run_idx, n_tokens, first_divergence_pos, per_token_divergence_rate, max_logit_abs_diff, deterministic_prefix_len}`, and a summary `{corpus, n_images, floor_first_divergence_min, floor_divergence_rate_p95, derived_L3_logit_tol, derived_L4_exact_prefix_policy}` so E-VERIFY can mechanically derive the L3/L4 tolerances from the committed fixture rather than re-reading prose.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack OQ_INDEX.md (oracle)

title: Build the claim_id → source-line index tying every truth-pack assertion to a hashed source line
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §10 Phase -1 requires everything *"under docs/truth-pack/ with a claim_id → source-line index."* This index is what makes the truth pack auditable: every [VERIFIED] fact, every OQ answer, and every spec clause maps to (file_sha256, line range, quoted text). It is also the spine of the artifact-graph ledger (§8.4): NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md / DISCREPANCIES.md entries carry `claim_id`/`evidence_id` fields that resolve into this index.

## WHAT TO DO
Create `docs/truth-pack/CLAIM_INDEX.json` (+ a rendered `CLAIM_INDEX.md`) mapping each `claim_id` to: the source file path, its SHA-256 (from `SOURCE_HASHES.md`), the line range, the verbatim quoted text, and the [VERIFIED]/[REPORTED]/[OPEN] tag. Seed it with: all §2.2 identity/format/license facts; all §2.3.2 decoder config values; the R-SWA §2.3.3 mechanism; the §2.4 input/output contract; and a `claim_id` for each OQ-1..OQ-18 answer (each pointing at the line resolved by the corresponding OQ bead). Assign stable `claim_id`s (e.g. `CL-CONFIG-HIDDEN-1280`, `CL-RSWA-RING-W128`, `CL-OQ16-TOKENIZER`).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Stable claim_ids**: ledger entries (§8.4) reference them; renaming breaks provenance, so freeze the scheme.
- **Tag preserved**: [OPEN] claims start unresolved and flip to [VERIFIED] when their OQ closes — the index is the single status board for "is this still open?"

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A `claim_id` whose tag is still [OPEN] must block any kernel bead depending on it (the hard rule); the index is queried to enforce that gate.
- Every quote must be re-checkable against the hashed snapshot (line numbers are against the frozen file).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `CLAIM_INDEX.json` exists with one entry per VERIFIED fact and one per OQ answer, each with (file_sha256, line range, quote, tag).
- A query "list all still-OPEN claim_ids" returns empty at Phase -1 exit (every OQ resolved).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- `claim_index_links_valid`: every entry's (file_sha256, line range) resolves to real text in a hashed snapshot.
- `no_open_claims_at_exit`: assert no [OPEN]-tagged claim remains (the Phase -1 green gate).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (line citations), PM1-spec-extraction (spec clauses), and ALL OQ beads (each contributes one resolved claim_id). This is the aggregation point proving every OQ is answered with a pinned-source citation (the top E-PM1 exit gate).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## §8.4 LEDGER HAND-OFF (r2 — make the provenance contract explicit)
Multiple bodies state the §8.4 artifact-graph ledgers (`NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md` / `DISCREPANCIES.md` / `PERF_LEDGER.md`) carry per-entry fields **sourced from this truth pack**: `claim_id`, `evidence_id`, **model source commit + fixture hash**, CPU feature string, exact command + env, fallback/kill-switch state. This claim-index is the resolution target for the `claim_id`/`evidence_id` fields. To make the contract enforceable, this bead must additionally emit a small machine-readable **`PROVENANCE_FIELDS.json`** giving the canonical values every ledger entry copies: `{model_hf_commit, model_github_commit, safetensors_source_sha256, census_sha256, truth_pack_root}` — so the Phase-0 ledger-seeding task (E-P0) and every later ledger entry reference one authoritative source instead of re-deriving it. Add a test `ledger_provenance_resolvable`: assert every `claim_id` referenced by a ledger entry exists in `CLAIM_INDEX.json` (run in E-VERIFY once ledgers exist; the schema/contract is fixed here).
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack OQ_INDEX.md (claim-index)

title: OQ-1: R-SWA reference set m — visual-only or visual+prompt? Exact mask boundary
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-1)
R-SWA reference set `m`: does it include the running **prompt** tokens, or strictly the **visual** tokens? And what is the exact mask boundary between prompt vs generated tokens? Plan §2.3.3 notes the conflict: *"Paper says 'all reference tokens (visual tokens in OCR)' but Figure 2 caption says 'visual+prompt'."*

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` in `modeling_deepseekv2.py` (the hashed snapshot from PM1-hash-sources). Read how the reference (never-evicted) KV block is constructed: does it span only the vision-token positions, or vision+prompt-prefix positions? Determine the exact index boundary where the sliding 128-window begins.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The R-SWA attention kernel (`rswa.rs`) and prefill (building the reference KV block). The attention mask cannot be finalized without this — a wrong `m` boundary attends to the wrong tokens and silently corrupts decode (surfaces only as an L2/L4 parity drift). Plan §6.4: *"resolve the R-SWA boundary questions (§2.3.3 / OQ-1..3 and the multi-page span OQ-13) from SlidingWindowLlamaAttention before finalizing the mask."*

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
R-SWA is the one architectural novelty (plan §2.3.3): `o_t = Σ_{j∈N(t)} α_tj v_j` where `N(t) = {all reference tokens} ∪ {last n=128 generated tokens}`. The reference tokens are a frozen global KV — never evicted, excluded from state transitions. Whether `m` = visual-only or visual+prompt changes the reference-block length used for KV/buffer sizing (PM1-census-generator) and per-token attention compute `O(m+128)`.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A quoted, line-backed answer in EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md + CLAIM_INDEX.json: `m` = visual-only OR visual+prompt, with the exact reference-block index span and the sliding-window start boundary, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` line range.
- The [OPEN] tag for the R-SWA reference set flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources (cites the hashed `modeling_deepseekv2.py`). Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (R-SWA section), PM1-census-generator (m sizing), and the future E-P1 `rswa.rs` kernel. Closely coupled to OQ-3 (warm-up/ring semantics) and OQ-13 (multi-page span).
close_reason: Resolved: oq/rswa-attention.md

title: OQ-2: Is attention uniformly R-SWA across all 12 decoder layers (incl. layer 0)?
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-2)
Is attention uniformly R-SWA across all 12 layers (including layer 0)? Is there any layer that retains full (non-sliding) attention? Plan §2.3.3 hypothesis: *"attention is expected R-SWA everywhere, including layer 0 — the first_k_dense_replace=1 split affects only the MLP, not attention."* Confirm this from source.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`modeling_deepseekv2.py` — the decoder layer construction loop. Verify whether every one of the 12 layers instantiates `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` (vs some layers using a full-attention class), and confirm that `first_k_dense_replace=1` (layer 0 dense MLP) does NOT also make layer-0 attention full.

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The decoder attention dispatch (`decoder.rs` 12-layer loop). If even one layer is full-attention, the R-SWA constant-KV property and the per-layer ring-buffer allocation differ for that layer — a wrong assumption breaks both correctness and the KV-bound invariant the gauntlet monitors (§8.5: *"KV cache never exceeds L·(m+128)"*).

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §2.3.3: R-SWA *"replaces ALL decoder attention (every one of the 12 layers)."* The KV formula `C_RSWA(T)=L·(m+min(n,T))` uses L=12 uniformly; if a layer is exempt, L is effectively smaller for the ring and the buffer math changes.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: all 12 layers are R-SWA (or the explicit list of any exceptions), cited to the layer-construction line range.
- Confirmation that `first_k_dense_replace=1` affects only the MLP, not attention.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction and the E-P1 decoder loop. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-3.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack

title: OQ-3: Exact warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during the first 128 decode steps
description:
## THE EXACT QUESTION (plan §13, OQ-3)
What are the exact warm-up vs ring-overwrite mask semantics during the first 128 decode steps? Plan §2.3.3 reports the mechanism: *"records prefill_len on first decode, warms up until cache reaches prefill_len+W, then in-place ring overwrites at slot = prefill_len + ring_pos, ring_pos = (ring_pos+1) % W"* (W=128). Confirm and pin the exact arithmetic and the mask during warm-up.

## SOURCE FILE / LINE TO READ
`SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` in `modeling_deepseekv2.py`. Read: how `prefill_len` is recorded; the warm-up condition (cache length < prefill_len+W); the in-place overwrite slot formula; the `ring_pos` update; and crucially the attention MASK applied during the first 128 steps (does a partially-filled ring mask out unfilled slots, or attend to stale data?).

## WHAT THIS BLOCKS
The R-SWA ring-buffer KV cache implementation (`rswa.rs`) — the single most important kernel implication (plan §2.3.3: *"This is the single most important kernel implication."*). Getting the warm-up mask wrong attends to garbage ring slots before the ring fills, corrupting the first ≤128 generated tokens.

## BACKGROUND / WHY IT MATTERS
Plan §6.4: *"Write the new K,V into the ring at slot = prefill_len + (t % 128) (after warm-up)."* The pre-warm-up behavior (steps 0..127, ring not yet full) is exactly where a mask bug hides — it only manifests on the early output tokens and is easy to miss in aggregate CER.

## DELIVERABLE / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Quoted, line-backed answer: exact `prefill_len` recording, warm-up condition, slot formula, `ring_pos` update, and the warm-up-phase mask semantics, cited to `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention`.
- [OPEN] flips to [VERIFIED].

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on PM1-hash-sources. Feeds PM1-spec-extraction (the SlidingWindowLlamaAttention section) and the E-P1 `rswa.rs` ring buffer. Coupled to OQ-1/OQ-2.
close_reason: Resolved: docs/truth-pack

title: Docs: document native Windows install path (install.ps1 + winget?) in README/CHANGELOG once Windows binaries ship
close_reason: Done: README + CHANGELOG document the native Windows install path (install.ps1 one-liner) and that focr pull now works on Windows.

title: End-to-end CLI test script: focr ocr <golden image> --json → compare to frozen reference, full structured logging
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the headline e2e the user asked for: an end-to-end CLI test that runs the REAL `focr` binary on a golden image, emits `--json`, and compares against a frozen reference — with full detailed logging at every stage. It is the integration test that proves the whole pipeline (decode→preprocess→tokenize→vision→connector→prefill→decode→postprocess→emit) actually works end-to-end on the native path, not just per-op parity. No mocks: real binary, real golden image, real frozen reference JSON (per `/testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks`).

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` (the Rust e2e, model-gated) AND `scripts/run_e2e.sh` (the standalone CLI script):
1. **`scripts/run_e2e.sh`**: resolves the model (env `FOCR_MODEL_DIR`), runs `focr ocr tests/fixtures/golden_corpus/<doc>.png --json` for each golden doc, pipes output to a comparator that diffs against `tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json` after canonicalization (scrub timing/run-id/durations, sort bbox, normalize line endings). Emits a structured NDJSON summary per doc and a final pass/fail. Exit 0 on all-pass; non-zero on any diff. Skips-green (exit 0, logged) if no model resolvable.
2. **`tests/native_engine_e2e.rs`**: the same flow as a Rust test using TEST-model-gated-runner — invokes the real binary or `OcrEngine::recognize`, asserts `native_path_ran=true`, compares to the frozen reference (text exact where the bf16 oracle is deterministic over its reproduced prefix; logits/continuous values within the E-VERIFY documented tolerance), and asserts determinism (same image+args twice → byte-identical under greedy).
3. **Full per-stage logging**: at EACH pipeline stage emit a `TestLog` `stage` line with inputs/shapes/dtype/simd_tier/elapsed_us — e.g. preprocess logs the tile geometry (Base 1024×1024 vs Gundam) and the [-1,1] normalize; tokenize logs token count; vision logs the 256 image-feature tokens + the 273 placeholder-token total (16 image + 1 image_newline per row × 16 rows + 1 view_seperator) at base 1024 (OQ-18 census); prefill logs reference-block length `m`; decode logs per-N-tokens TPS and the no_repeat_ngram(35) window (128 single-image / 1024 multi-image); postprocess logs EOS strip + bbox /999 rescale.
4. **Cover both modes**: Base (1024×1024, crop_mode=false, multi-page) and Gundam (base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=true, single-page tiling) — each against its own frozen reference.
5. **Multi-page e2e**: a 10-page golden, comparing to the multi-page reference (which is cross-page DEPENDENT under R-SWA, OQ-13 — the reference is a single `infer_multi` oracle run, NOT a concatenation of single-page parses).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Real binary, real fixtures, no mocks** — an e2e that stubs the engine proves nothing.
- **`--json` is the compared surface** (stable, structured) — human markdown is a rendering of it.
- **Per-stage logging makes a failure diagnosable**: if the e2e diffs, the structured log pinpoints WHICH stage's shapes/tokens diverged, not just "output differs."
- **Both modes + multi-page** because the cost center and the token census differ (Base = 256 tokens/page; Gundam crops ≈ 100 tokens each; multi-page is cross-page dependent).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- **Determinism only holds under `temperature=0` greedy** (§7.3) — assert byte-identical only in that mode.
- **L4 'exact' is defined only over the prefix the bf16 oracle reproduces identically** (§8.2) — compare text exactly only up to the nondeterminism-envelope boundary carried in the fixture; beyond it, fall back to CER-within-budget.
- **Do NOT assert multi-page == sum of single-page** (§8.3, OQ-13) — the metamorphic property is the opposite (page order may change later-page output).
- Robot/JSON output may carry run-id/timing → scrub before compare (canonicalization from TEST-fixture-harness).
- Script must work both in CI (model absent → skip-green) and locally (model present → real compare).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `scripts/run_e2e.sh` runs the real `focr` over each golden, compares to frozen reference, exits 0 all-pass / non-zero on diff, skips-green without a model.
- `tests/native_engine_e2e.rs` asserts `native_path_ran=true`, frozen-reference match (text exact over the deterministic prefix; continuous within tolerance), and determinism.
- Both Base and Gundam modes covered; a 10-page multi-page e2e present.
- Every stage emits a valid structured NDJSON log line; a failing run produces a log that localizes the diverging stage.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The e2e itself is the test; additionally a self-test that a deliberately-perturbed reference makes the comparator FAIL (proves it actually compares).
- A self-test that the script skips-green without a model.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-model-gated-runner**: uses it to gate + prove the native path ran.
- depends_on **TEST-fixture-harness**: loads the frozen `<doc>_reference.json` + canonicalizes before compare.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the per-stage structured logs.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — error-path e2e (the §7.4 exit-code contract), piping + help smoke
- **Error-path e2e through the REAL binary (currently only the happy path is covered).** The §7.4 exit codes are a PUBLIC contract that agents branch on; the e2e must assert them END-TO-END by invoking `focr` and checking the process exit code AND the `robot run_error` payload code agree: (a) a missing/unresolvable model → **exit 3** with an actionable searched-dirs message; (b) a corrupt/truncated image → **exit 4**; (c) a corrupt or wrong-`format_version` `.focrq` → **exit 7**; (d) a stage budget exceeded (`FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_*_MS` set tiny) → **exit 5**; (e) a SIGINT/Ctrl-C mid-run → **exit 6** with finalizer cleanup. These are real binary invocations (no mocks) and several need NO weights (3/4/7 fire before the forward) so they run in the always-on no-weights lane — a rare chance for the e2e to be meaningful WITHOUT the 6.67 GB model.
- **Robot/piping + help/version smoke.** Add a smoke that `focr ocr <golden> --json | <jq-style parse>` yields one JSON object (data-only on stdout, diagnostics on stderr, exit 0) and that `focr --help` / `focr --version` / `focr robot schema` emit stable, parseable output — the agent-ergonomic surface the README/AGENTS.md promise. Scrub timing/run-id before any golden compare (canonicalization from TEST-fixture-harness).
- **Exit-code/robot parity assertion.** For each error case assert `process_exit_code == robot_run_error.payload.code == FocrError::exit_code()` (one source of truth, per P0-error-exit-codes) so the three can never silently diverge.
notes: 2026-06-25 Codex correction: any phrase like 'Base = 256 tokens/page' in this generated bead means 256 image-feature tokens only. For token/KV sizing use the OQ-18 census: base 1024 has 256 image-feature tokens plus structural slots (16 image_newline + 1 view_seperator = 273 before prompt), and the R-SWA reference block grows with page/input length up to the 32K cap. Do not size buffers from 256 alone.

title: Per-module unit-test scaffolding standard (happy/edge/error + structured logging) every module author follows
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
AGENTS.md Testing Policy: "Each module includes unit tests for happy path, edge cases, error handling." Without a documented, copy-pasteable standard, module authors (preprocess, tokenizer, vision_sam, vision_clip, decoder, rswa, moe, decode, postprocess, weights, robot, storage, etc.) each invent their own shape, logging drifts, and the unit-test floor erodes. This bead defines the ONE scaffolding standard + a template so every per-module test suite is uniform, richly logged, and reviewable.

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver `docs/UNIT_TEST_STANDARD.md` + a `tests/_template_module_tests.rs.txt` template:
1. **Three mandatory groups per module**: `happy_*` (nominal path on representative input), `edge_*` (boundary: empty input, max dims, the i32-overflow worst case at K=6848 for int8 GEMM modules, single-token decode, 40-page context), `error_*` (malformed input, missing weight, shape mismatch, format/version mismatch → asserts the right `FocrError` + exit-code mapping per §7.4).
2. **Structured logging in EVERY test** via `TestLog` (bead TEST-log-emitter): log `setup`, each `stage` with shapes/dtype/simd_tier/elapsed, and `result`.
3. **Determinism assertion** where applicable: same input twice → identical output (uses the shared helper from TEST-determinism-gate).
4. **SIMD-tier coverage note**: kernel modules must run their tests across available tiers (force via `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH`) and assert the scalar fallback is bit-identical to the active SIMD path (except AVX2, which carries its own overflow proof — see §6.6 ⚠).
5. **The overflow proof obligation** (AGENTS.md doctrine #6, §5.4): any module doing int8 i32-accumulation includes a test multiplying worst-case saturated operands at **K = 6848** (dense layer-0 `down_proj`) on every arch, asserting the i32 result equals an i64 reference and no path silently saturates. (Worst cases: S8S8 ≤ 110.4M, U8S8 ≤ 221.7M at K=6848; ≥9× headroom under i32::MAX=2,147,483,647 — but PROVEN, not assumed. Do NOT inherit frankensearch's k≤1536 bound.)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **A template, not just prose**, because authors copy-paste; the path of least resistance must be the correct shape.
- **Overflow proof is a UNIT-test obligation in the standard** so no int8 module ships without it (the proof is per-module, the standard makes it mandatory).
- **SIMD-tier coverage in the standard** so bit-identity (integer accumulation is exact/associative → SIMD == scalar exactly) is verified everywhere, and the AVX2 i16-saturation exception is explicitly called out as needing its own proof or a `DISCREPANCIES.md` entry.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The standard must not mandate the 6.67 GB model for unit tests — unit tests use tiny synthetic tensors; the model-gated path is for e2e only.
- `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` forcing must degrade gracefully on a host lacking an intrinsic (the scalar fallback always cross-compiles and runs).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/UNIT_TEST_STANDARD.md` + template exist; the three groups + logging + determinism + SIMD-tier + overflow-proof obligations are documented with concrete examples.
- At least one module (e.g. the int8 linear facade in `nn.rs`) ships a reference implementation of the standard as the worked example.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The worked-example module's test suite passes and emits valid structured logs (validated against TEST-log-conventions).
- The K=6848 overflow proof passes on every available arch in the worked example.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: the standard mandates logging via that emitter.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — shared SIMD-tier enumeration helper, snapshot + table-driven conventions
- **Provide a shared `for_each_available_arch_tier(|tier| …)` helper, don't make each author hand-roll `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH`.** The standard mandates kernel modules run tests across tiers and assert scalar==SIMD bit-identity, but offers no plumbing — every author re-implementing the tier sweep guarantees drift. Add (or reference, from `tests/common/`) one helper that enumerates the tiers available on the host (`scalar` always; `sdot`/`smmla`/`vnni256`/`vnni512`/`amx`/`avx2` as detected), forces each via `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH`, runs the supplied closure, and logs a `stage` line per tier with `simd_tier`. The AVX2 i16-saturation exception is encoded ONCE in the helper (it asserts equality-modulo-the-documented-DISC, not raw equality) so no author gets it wrong.
- **Module-level snapshot (insta) + table-driven conventions.** §8.6 uses insta exact-snapshots for `--json`/help; the unit standard should also bless insta module-level snapshots for structured outputs (e.g. a tokenizer's id sequence, a postprocess markdown assembly) and a table-driven `cases: &[(input, expected)]` shape for the `happy_/edge_/error_` groups, so edge enumeration is uniform and reviewable. Reference the proptest generators from TEST-r2-property-fuzz for the randomized edge cases (the K=6848 overflow operands, random valid/invalid `.focrq` headers) rather than ad-hoc literals.
- **Determinism + tier helper in the worked example.** The worked-example int8-linear module must demonstrate BOTH the determinism helper (TEST-determinism-gate) AND the tier-sweep helper, so the path of least resistance copies both.

title: Sampler no-ban ngram path avoids vocab-row clone
description: Optimize greedy decode n-gram masking so no-ban steps do not clone the full logits row. Current sampler.rs comment promises zero extra allocation on common no-ban steps, but sample() eagerly row.to_vec() once the generated sequence is long enough. Keep behavior identical: only allocate/mask when the sliding-window scan finds an in-range non-whitelisted token to ban, and preserve existing argmax/tie/NaN semantics. Document as a single-lever performance fix inspired by prior Frankensearch contiguous-layout wins: measure/prove small, avoid broad surfaces, and leave negative-evidence/perf ledger updates for measured benchmark lanes.
close_reason: Implemented sampler no-ban allocation fix. sample() now scans sliding-window n-gram bans through a shared visitor and only materializes a masked logits Vec on the first real in-vocab ban; no-ban decode steps argmax the borrowed row directly, preserving greedy semantics while avoiding a full 129280-f32 row copy. Kept the old in-place blocker as a cfg(test) semantic helper, added tests for no-mask and mask-present paths, and renamed DecodeOutput.token to token_id to remove UBS hardcoded-secret false positives while making the field clearer. No PERF_LEDGER claim made because this is a by-construction allocation removal, not a measured benchmark; benchmark attribution remains for the gauntlet/perf lane. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt --check; cargo +nightly test native_engine::sampler --lib; ubs --format=json --ci --only=rust src/native_engine/sampler.rs and src/native_engine/mod.rs (0 critical); git diff --check -- src/native_engine/sampler.rs; cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test.

title: Harden sampler logits Mat backing-length validation
notes: Fresh-eyes audit found sampler::sample checks Mat rows but not the public backing-buffer length before logits.row(0), so malformed [1, vocab] Mat values can panic instead of returning FocrError.
close_reason: Fixed sampler panic surface from malformed public logits Mat backing buffers. sampler::sample now checks rows*cols overflow and data.len() before borrowing row 0, returning FocrError instead of panicking. Added rejects_malformed_logits_backing_data_without_panicking regression test. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fmt --check; cargo +nightly test native_engine::sampler --lib (23 passed); cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test (full suite passed: 548 lib tests, integration tests, doc tests); ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/sampler.rs (exit 0, 0 critical).

title: Reject malformed R-SWA decode step tensors before mutating cache
description:
## Background / why
Fresh-eyes audit found that `rswa::attention` validates the logical `q` shape and only checks K/V backing lengths, then writes K/V into the ring cache before `decode_attention` validates the query slice length. A malformed public `Mat { rows: 1, cols: NUM_HEADS*HEAD_DIM, data: short_vec }` can therefore return an error after mutating cache state. K/V also accept inconsistent rows/cols as long as their data length matches.

## Scope
- Validate Q/K/V as exact one-row decode-step matrices before `RingCache::write_decode_step`.
- Ensure malformed Q fails before cache mutation.
- Add focused regressions for malformed Q backing data and K/V logical shape mismatch.

## Acceptance
- `attention` rejects malformed decode-step Mats with actionable `FocrError`s before mutating cache.
- Existing R-SWA behavior remains unchanged for valid inputs.
- Focused rswa tests, full cargo gates, and UBS pass.
close_reason: Fixed: rswa::attention now validates q/k/v as exact one-row decode-step Mats before any RingCache::write_decode_step mutation. Added regressions proving malformed q backing data and k logical-shape mismatch are rejected with cache.ring_len unchanged. Verified cargo +nightly fmt --check; focused cargo +nightly test native_engine::rswa --lib; cargo +nightly check --all-targets; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo +nightly test; ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/rswa.rs (exit 0, no critical findings).

title: Phase 4 — int4 group-quant refinement (the biggest decode bandwidth lever)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 4 is the int4 refinement round of franken_ocr — the pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for Baidu Unlimited-OCR (a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA, hidden 1280, 12 decoder layers, 64 routed + 2 shared experts, top-6, moe_intermediate 896, vocab 129280, MIT-licensed). Decode is **memory-bandwidth-bound**: a single-token decode GEMV streams ~500M active params/token. Per the plan §6.3, **int4 group-quantized weights (g=16-32) halve the bytes streamed per token vs int8 → up to ~2x decode throughput**; applied to the dominant **expert FFN weights** (the bulk of the 3B params AND the decode hot path) this is **the single biggest decode lever** and the headline CPU-performance strategy of the whole project. int8 (Phase 2) ships first as the ~lossless correctness oracle; int4 (this phase) pushes quantization as far as a *measured* CER bound allows.

The over-arching goals this serves: max CPU speed for THIS one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels; **correctness before speed (G1 > G2, always)**; portable single binary; agent-ergonomic. int4's win is *bandwidth + footprint*, not extra compute.

## WHAT TO DO (the epic scope)
Deliver, as child tasks:
1. `focr convert --quant int4` — per-group symmetric int4 (group size 16-32; NVFP4 used 16), attention `v_proj` and expert `down_proj` kept one tier higher (int8), Q4_K_M-equivalent ~4.5-4.9 bpw. Emits int4 tensor records into `.focrq`.
2. **int4 GEMM/GEMV kernels** — int4 weights **unpack to int8 in-register** and feed the SAME SMMLA / VNNI / SDOT MAC built in Phase 3 (there is NO CPU int4 matmul instruction; the win is the halved weight stream). Per-arch unpack + dispatch.
3. **AF-1 rate-distortion / water-filling per-tensor bit allocator** — `focr convert --optimize-bits --budget <GB>` emitting the `bit_allocation_table` in the `.focrq` header from measured per-tensor distortion curves D_t(b).
4. **Expected-loss-guided per-layer quant** — choose tier (int8/int4-g32/int4-g16) per layer/tensor by measured CER impact, not uniform.
5. **Dense-numeric/table/code exact-token-sensitive accuracy corpus + CER curve per bit-width** (tables/code/numbers/sub-superscripts).
6. **Footprint arithmetic demonstrated** — the converter prints realized per-section bytes; the target is a measured number, not assumed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **int4 only on the expert/FFN bulk; sensitive minority stays int8.** `v_proj` and expert `down_proj` kept one tier higher (the llama.cpp `_M` discipline, derived not guessed). `lm_head` int8 only behind its `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD` kill-switch. Router gate, ALL norms, vision tower, projector, embed_tokens stay BF16/F32 — quantizing them drifts expert selection / wrecks OCR (both prior-art quants keep them).
- **Target Q4_K_M-equivalent (~4.5-4.9 bpw), NOT sub-4-bit.** The accuracy cliff is BELOW 4-bit; OCR of dense numbers/tables/code is exact-token-sensitive and perplexity under-predicts this.
- **int4 unpacks to int8 in-register → same SMMLA/VNNI/SDOT MAC.** No new MAC instruction; reuse Phase 3's bit-exact int8 path as the arithmetic oracle.
- **Activations stay int8 dynamic per-row** — int4 activations are too lossy for exact-token OCR.
- **Per-tensor bit split is CHOSEN by AF-1 (water-filling), never uniform.** Deterministic fallback: uniform Q4_K_M-class allocation.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Below the 4-bit cliff is a catastrophic-regression zone; gate on CER over the dense-numeric/table corpus (AF-2 CVaR/tail), NOT mean perplexity.
- Footprint arithmetic may NOT close to <2.9 GB with int4 experts alone (NVFP4 keeping embeddings+lm_head+vision in BF16 already lands at 2.93 GB). int8 lm_head / embeddings are outside the validated recipe → gated by CER + kill-switch + DISCREPANCIES.md.
- Never trade a good int8 kernel for a naive one (NE-INH-5: naive fused forward regressed 3-10x).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 4 exit gates, plan §10)
- int4 end-to-end CER within the ledgered budget (NOT below the 4-bit cliff; Q4_K_M-class bar).
- size ≈ 1.8-2.9 GB, **demonstrated** by the converter's per-section print.
- no catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content (AF-2 CVaR_0.1 within ledgered budget of f32).
- every quant choice ledgered (DISCREPANCIES.md / NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md) with its measured impact.
- int4 kernel bit-identical to int8-via-int4-unpack scalar reference (integer accumulation is exact).
- parity ladder L0-L5 still green at int4 (logits within int4 tolerance, argmax/token exact where reference deterministic).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Each child task carries its own unit + parity + (where perf-relevant) bench beads. Epic-level integration: a model-gated e2e test that runs the full int4 forward on the golden corpus and asserts the Phase-4 exit gates, with structured NDJSON logging of per-bit-width CER, CVaR, EVT-p999, and realized footprint.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `E-P3` (Phase 3 SIMD kernels): int4 reuses the Phase-3 tiled SMMLA/AMX prefill GEMM + SDOT/VNNI decode GEMV (int4 unpacks to int8 and feeds them) and the per-arch weight pre-packing infrastructure.
- `E-ALIEN` (AF-1 family): the rate-distortion/water-filling allocator and AF-2 tail-risk CER gate are specified in the alien-artifact epic; this epic consumes them.

title: int4 loader: native_engine/weights.rs reads QInt4PerGroup records (byte-range index + inline scales + tier) with load-time census
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §5.2 makes the reader (`native_engine/weights.rs`) a **dependency-free byte-range index into one mmap/blob** that performs the **manifest census on load** (catch wrong/stale weights at load, not as garbage output). P4-convert-int4 WRITES `QInt4PerGroup` records and P4-int4-gemm CONSUMES packed K-panel tiles, but no bead OWNS the load-side read+validate of int4 records. Without it, the kernel has nothing well-typed to read and the format-safety guarantees (§5.4 provenance) are unenforced for int4. This bead closes that seam.

## WHAT TO DO
Extend the `.focrq` reader to fully support `QInt4PerGroup` (and the int8 tier-higher records alongside it), mmap-backed, zero-copy where possible:
- **Byte-range index the int4 record:** from the tensor_directory entry `{ name, dtype: QInt4PerGroup, shape, packing, byte_offset, byte_len, scales_offset, scales_len, group_size, tier }`, expose to the kernel: (a) the packed-nibble payload as a `&[u8]` slice over the mmap (two int4 per byte, in the `arch_target` packing), (b) the inline per-group `&[f32]` scales slice, (c) the `group_size` and `tier` (int4-g16 / int4-g32 / int8). Struct-of-arrays per §6.12 so a tile load is one contiguous stream.
- **Load-time manifest census (§5.2):** assert all 2710 tensors present with expected (name, shape); MISSING / SHAPE-MISMATCH / EXTRA → LOUD named diff, refuse to proceed (exit code 7 / format-mismatch path). Assert tier metadata matches the recipe (v_proj / expert down_proj / dense-0 down_proj are int8; vision/projector/embed/router/norms are BF16).
- **format_version + arch_target guards (§5.4):** refuse a `.focrq` whose `format_version` exceeds the binary's; on `arch_target` mismatch WARN and fall back to the generic packing path (the int4 unpack must have a generic decode that does not assume the arch repack). Verify `source_sha256` is present.
- **Scales sanity:** assert `scales_len == (K/group_size) * n_groups_rows * 4` for each int4 tensor (the per-group f32 count matches shape ÷ group_size); reject a record whose scales count is inconsistent with shape+group_size (a silent corruption guard).
- **mmap discipline (§6.12):** `mmap` read-only (no 6→3 GB copy), `madvise(WILLNEED)` the active region; the loader hands the kernel borrowed slices, never owns/copies the weight bulk.
- **Structured logging:** NDJSON per-tensor `{name, dtype, tier, group_size, k, n, payload_bytes, scales_bytes, arch_target, census_ok}` on load (verbose/robot mode) so a stale-weight or packing-mismatch localizes to one tensor.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Census on load, not on garbage output (§5.2)** — a wrong/stale `.focrq` must fail loudly at load, before any forward.
- **Generic-packing fallback on arch mismatch (§5.4)** — 'one canonical artifact, many packings'; a `.focrq` packed for x86-vnni loaded on aarch64 must still decode (slower) via the generic int4 unpack, not crash.
- **Borrowed slices, zero-copy** — the int4 bandwidth win is erased if the loader materializes int8 or copies the bulk; the loader only indexes.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The packing convention read here MUST match P4-convert-int4's writer AND P4-int4-gemm's unpack byte-for-byte (nibble hi/lo order) — this bead is the third corner of that triangle; a mismatch is silent garbage.
- The loader must not assume int4 group_size divides K for tensors carrying a documented remainder group (§5.2 remainder handling) — read the recorded handling rather than recompute.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` discipline: any mmap/unsafe lives behind the named module with a `// SAFETY:` and a safe-read fallback.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The reader indexes `QInt4PerGroup` records and exposes (packed payload, per-group scales, group_size, tier) as borrowed slices to the int4 kernel.
- Load-time census passes happy-path; injected MISSING/EXTRA/SHAPE-MISMATCH/wrong-tier produces a loud named refusal (exit 7).
- format_version-too-high refused; arch_target mismatch warns + falls back to generic packing; scales-count inconsistency rejected.
- No copy of the weight bulk (mmap borrow asserted); NDJSON load log emitted.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: round-trip a synthetic `.focrq` (write via P4-convert-int4 path → read here) and assert the kernel-facing slices reproduce the logical weights; census loud-refusal on each injected fault; format_version/arch_target/scales-count guards. Integration (model-gated): load the real int4 `.focrq`, assert census passes and NDJSON localizes per tensor; assert zero bulk copy (RSS does not jump by the payload size). NDJSON logging throughout. Pairs as the load-side mirror of P4-convert-int4-tests.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-convert-int4`: reads the records that bead writes; shares the packing convention.
- Feeds `P4-int4-gemm` (the kernel consumes the slices this loader exposes) and `P4-robot-quant-config` (introspection reads the loaded directory).

title: Agent-ergonomic quant-config introspection: focr robot surface for tiers, group sizes, bit_allocation_table, realized footprint
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
G5 (agent ergonomics) and §7.3 (robot/JSON/NDJSON contract) require a stable, self-describing machine surface. Phase 4 introduces several agent-relevant facts about a model that are currently buried in the `.focrq` header or printed only at convert time: per-tensor TIER (int8/int4-g16/int4-g32/bf16), group sizes, the AF-1 `bit_allocation_table`, the realized per-section footprint, and which out-of-recipe kill-switches (`FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`, embeddings-int8) were active. An agent choosing a model/config, or debugging a CER regression, must be able to READ what tiering and footprint a given `.focrq` actually has, in stable JSON — not by parsing convert stdout. No existing bead exposes this via `focr robot`.

## WHAT TO DO
Add a stable, versioned robot/JSON surface that introspects a loaded (or named) `.focrq`'s quant configuration:
- **`focr robot quant-config <model.focrq>` (or fold into `robot backends`/`robot describe-model`)** emitting versioned JSON: `{ format_version, arch_target, source_sha256, per_tensor_tiers: [{name, tier, group_size?, bits}], bit_allocation_table?: [...], footprint: {vision_bytes, embed_bytes, lm_head_bytes, router_norms_bytes, int4_expert_bytes, int8_minority_bytes, scales_bytes, total_bytes, total_human}, out_of_recipe: {int8_lm_head: bool, int8_embeddings: bool}, allocator: "uniform-Q4_K_M" | "af1-water-filling" }`.
- **Reflect the realized footprint** (from P4-footprint-arithmetic) and the **bit_allocation_table** (from P4-af1-bit-allocator) exactly as stored — single source of truth, no recomputation drift.
- **Stable schema:** add to `focr robot schema` so the contract is self-describing and frozen; scrub volatile fields (timing) per §8.6 golden-artifact discipline.
- **Cross-check:** the introspected total footprint equals the on-disk `.focrq` size byte-for-byte (the same invariant P4-footprint-arithmetic asserts at convert time, now also at read time).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Read-side single source of truth** — agents read the realized config from the artifact, not from a convert-run transcript that may be stale.
- **Stable versioned schema (§7.3, G5)** — a frozen robot contract is the project's agent-ergonomic backbone; the quant config is exactly the kind of fact agents need to reason about speed/accuracy trade-offs.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must not require loading the full weight bulk just to report config — read the header/tensor_directory only (cheap), unless `--with-footprint-recheck` is passed.
- Keep this strictly read-only and deterministic (golden-artifact testable).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr robot quant-config` (or the chosen subcommand) emits valid versioned JSON with per-tensor tiers, group sizes, the bit_allocation_table (when present), realized per-section footprint, out-of-recipe flags, and the allocator name.
- Schema is in `focr robot schema`; output is golden-artifact frozen (scrubbed); total footprint cross-checks the on-disk size.

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: synthetic `.focrq` header → expected JSON (insta golden); schema validates; footprint total == directory sum. Integration (model-gated): real int4 `.focrq` introspection reports the correct tiers + footprint + (if --optimize-bits) the allocation table. NDJSON/JSON contract test per §7.3.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-r2-int4-loader`: reads the tensor_directory/tiers the loader indexes.
- `P4-footprint-arithmetic`: surfaces the realized per-section footprint it computes.
- (soft) `P4-af1-bit-allocator`: surfaces the bit_allocation_table when present.

title: EPIC: franken_ocr Model Zoo — multi-model, CPU-hyper-optimized specialized vision-language runtime
description:
WORLD-CLASS PERFORMANCE MANDATE: every model in this zoo must be SUPER-OPTIMIZED to the same degree, level and intensity as the Baidu Unlimited-OCR runner — every kernel register-blocked and at peak, NOTHING naive, measured head-to-head at-or-near ONNX/MLAS on CPU. Full kernel spec in foundation task A7 (bd-3jo6.1.7); it propagates to every per-model decoder/perf bead.

Extend franken_ocr from a single Baidu Unlimited-OCR runner into a FAMILY of pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runners for specialized vision-language tasks, each transformed to the SAME extreme-performance bar as the Baidu model (int8-quantized decoder GEMMs + model-specific NEON SDOT/SMMLA-i8mm on Apple Silicon and AVX2/AVX-VNNI/AVX-512-VNNI/AMX on Intel/AMD, fused tape-free forward, full-core-parallel, no Python/CUDA/FFI at inference). Targets (all Apache-2.0/MIT so a quantized derivative is legally redistributable, matching the Baidu-MIT precedent): GOT-OCR2.0 (~0.7B; general OCR-2.0 = text/math-LaTeX/tables/charts/molecular-SMILES/geometry/sheet-music-kern; the foundation model and biggest surface), SmolVLM2 (256M-2.2B; photo description + VQA), OneChart (~0.2B; chart->data digitization), Polyphonic-TrOMR (tiny; optical music recognition), and opportunistic specialists TrOCR (handwriting) + pix2tex (math->LaTeX). Sub-epic A generalizes the engine into a model-agnostic graph runtime sharing the int8 kernel layer (without it each model is a fork); B-F are per-model integration epics that all depend on A. The Baidu Unlimited-OCR runner remains the default 'focr ocr' with no regression. Doctrine recap: #1 correctness>speed (parity gate first), #2 quantize decoder FFN/expert/projection GEMMs ONLY (vision/projector/router/norms stay high precision), #3 no hand-rolled wide-SIMD over scalar loops (use SDOT/VNNI matmul intrinsics + LLVM autovec glue), #5 no nested rayon-under-lock / no second asupersync runtime, #6 prove int8 i32 non-overflow at worst-case K, #9 two thin-shim binaries. Research basis: the model survey in this session (oemer's U-Net-segmentation pipeline was REJECTED as a poor kernel fit; end-to-end ViT-encoder + autoregressive-transformer-decoder models were chosen because they reuse franken_ocr's existing GEMM/attention int8 kernels).

title: Sub-epic A (FOUNDATION): multi-model engine generalization — model-agnostic graph runtime over a shared int8 kernel layer
description: Generalize franken_ocr from the single hardcoded Unlimited-OCR forward into a model-AGNOSTIC graph runtime while SHARING the int8 kernel layer. Without this every new model is a fork. Deliver: a ModelArch abstraction (vision encoder + projector + decoder + prompt template + tokenizer), a .focrq v2 container + model registry, generalized convert/pull, a CLI task/model selection surface, multi-tokenizer infra, a reusable dense (non-MoE) int8 decoder engine, shared vision conv/patch-embed + token-compression kernels, and generalized parity-ladder + e2e/perf + selftest harnesses. HARD CONSTRAINT: the existing Baidu Unlimited-OCR runner becomes the FIRST ModelArch impl with ZERO output/perf regression (its goldens, CER 0.0094, and selftest 24/24 must hold). Everything in sub-epics B-F depends on A. Meet the franken_ocr PERFORMANCE BAR (full kernel spec in EPIC bd-3jo6 + foundation A7): int8 decoder GEMMs via SDOT/SMMLA-i8mm (ARM) + AVX2/AVX-VNNI/AVX-512-VNNI/AMX (x86), fused tape-free full-core-parallel forward, vision high-precision (doctrine #2), worst-case-K overflow proof (#6), parity-first then perf (#1) with PERF_LEDGER/DISCREPANCIES/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entries.

title: A1: ModelArch abstraction + refactor Unlimited-OCR as the first impl (ZERO regression)
description:
PROGRESS (2026-06-30): LANDED (zero regression — goldens 34/34, lib 709, fmt/clippy clean) — (1) the additive ModelArch descriptor module native_engine::model_arch with the ModelArch trait, UnlimitedOcr as impl #1, and the registry()/arch_by_id()/default_arch() lookup, re-exported as franken_ocr::model_arch [commit 3a160d2]; (2) OcrModel::arch() + the forward dispatch SEAM (ensure_arch_implemented guard at the top of forward_pre, returns clean NotImplemented for any not-yet-built arch) + version-report license read from the descriptor [commit 332547e]; the UnlimitedOcr descriptor is asserted byte-equal to the live constants so it cannot drift. REMAINING (kept in_progress): abstracting the forward COMPUTATION itself into a ModelArch::forward-style trait method is intertwined with the shared decoder (A7) + vision (A8) kernels — the common forward interface is defined there, so the full forward refactor lands with A7/A8 + the first second-model (B), not before.

Introduce a ModelArch trait/enum describing a model's graph: vision encoder kind + config, projector, decoder kind + config, prompt template, tokenizer ref, and per-arch forward dispatch inside OcrModel. Refactor the existing hardcoded Unlimited-OCR forward (SAM-ViT-B -> CLIP-L/14 -> 2048->1280 projector -> DeepSeek-V2 MoE + R-SWA) into the FIRST ModelArch implementation. ACCEPTANCE: byte-identical goldens, CER 0.0094 vs baidu, selftest 24/24, and unchanged decode perf (PERF_LEDGER delta ~0). This is the keystone — nearly everything depends on it. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Generalized parity-ladder harness (per-model oracle fixtures + L0-L5 + measured budgets)
description: Generalize the conformance ladder per model: a gen_reference_fixtures path that runs each model's torch/transformers oracle (establishing its OWN nondeterminism floor first), then L0-L5 — preprocess exact, per-op cosine ~1, per-layer hidden states, logits within MEASURED quant tolerance, decoded tokens exact where deterministic, end-to-end metric (CER/TEDS/structural-AP/note-accuracy) within a documented per-model budget. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Generalized e2e + perf-benchmark harness (detailed logging + ONNX/transformers head-to-head, fairness controls)
description:
INCLUDE A CONCURRENCY WATCHDOG per model: a many_pages_without_deadlock-style test (images/pages >> the rayon pool) that HANGS on a nested-rayon-under-lock or second-asupersync-runtime regression (doctrine #5), like the base model's watchdog.

Per-model e2e test scripts with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights), plus a head-to-head perf bench vs ONNX/transformers using thread/allocator/precision FAIRNESS controls (never bench torch at default thread counts). Append every measurement to docs/PERF_LEDGER.md. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: 'focr robot selftest' generalization — per-arch int8-kernel parity + worst-case-K overflow
description: Extend focr robot selftest so each registered model's int8 kernels are proven bit-identical to a scalar oracle on the host, INCLUDING the worst-case-K i32-overflow case per arch (doctrine #6). Machine-readable verdict per model. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: A13: per-model docs + distribution runbook + 'focr models' discovery
description: Generalize the docs + distribution path so EVERY model in the zoo ships as polished as the Unlimited-OCR model. (1) DOCS: each model integration (B10/C9/D7/E9/F1/F2) must update README (Quick Example + a per-task command reference), the CLI built-in help/long-help, the installer first-steps examples (install.sh + install.ps1), and CHANGELOG — the same doc surfaces the base model and the --extract-figures feature carry. (2) DISTRIBUTION: generalize the model-distribution runbook (committed per-model SHA256 manifest + the user uploading the quantized weight parts to GitHub Releases + HuggingFace; asupersync HTTP pull via tls-webpki-roots) so 'focr pull <model-id>' works on a clean machine, cross-platform (incl. native Windows). (3) DISCOVERY: 'focr models' lists installed + available models (id, task, size, license, pull status) so users can find them. (4) LICENSE NOTICES: each model's redistribution notice (Apache-2.0/MIT) surfaced in --version/robot health like the Baidu-MIT notice. TESTS: docs lint + an installer/pull e2e per model (model-gated), and a 'focr models' golden.

title: .focrq v2 container + model registry (back-compatible with v1)
description: Extend the .focrq quant container with a model-id + arch tag + a per-tensor quant-policy table (which tensors are int8 vs high-precision, per doctrine #2 for THIS arch). Add a model registry mapping model-id -> arch + default manifest + cache subdir. v1 Unlimited-OCR blobs MUST load unchanged (back-compat unit test + census). TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Generalized 'focr convert' — any supported HF arch -> .focrq with its doctrine-#2 quant policy
description: Generalize focr convert to transform any supported HF model (selected by arch) into a .focrq, applying that arch's doctrine-#2 quant policy (decoder GEMMs int8; vision/projector/router/embed/norms high-precision). Per-arch byte-parity proof (the converted tensors round-trip bit-exactly vs a reference dump). TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Generalized 'focr pull' + per-model committed manifests + cache layout
description: Generalize focr pull to fetch any registered model by id: per-model committed SHA256 manifest, multi-part download + reassembly + byte verify, cache at ~/.cache/franken_ocr/models/<model-id>/. 'focr pull <model-id>'; default stays the Unlimited-OCR model. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: CLI task/model-selection surface (focr ocr|music|chart|describe + --task/--model)
description: Add the multi-task CLI surface: task subcommands (focr ocr | music | chart | describe | ...) and/or --task/--model flags that route an input to the right model-id + prompt template + tokenizer. 'focr ocr' with no flags STAYS the Unlimited-OCR default (no regression; update the root --help golden once, deliberately). Robot mode + -o/--extract-figures must compose with every task. TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Multi-tokenizer infrastructure (Tokenizer trait + Qwen2/SmolLM2/music/BART) + per-model id-exact conformance
description: Generalize tokenization behind a Tokenizer trait with impls for the model families (Qwen2 BPE, SmolLM2, the TrOMR music vocab, BART/SentencePiece for TrOCR). Token-id-EXACT conformance harness per model (the prerequisite for every downstream parity gate). TESTS IN SCOPE: per-module unit tests (happy path + edge cases + error handling) AND an e2e test script with great detailed NDJSON logging (model-gated skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path via a /nonexistent-model clean ModelNotFound; XFAIL documented phase gaps). A green bar must mean it actually works.

title: Shared int8 DENSE transformer decoder engine (Qwen2/Llama/SmolLM2) — the reusable kernel core
description:
WORLD-CLASS PERFORMANCE BAR — the SAME degree, level and INTENSITY of optimization as the Baidu Unlimited-OCR runner, where EVERY op runs at peak and NOTHING is naive (the enemy is "kernels below peak", not framework overhead — doctrine #4; a naive scalar-f32 fused forward regressed 3-10x). MANDATE: (1) REGISTER-BLOCKED int8 matmul micro-kernels — NEON SDOT + the tiled SMMLA/i8mm GEMM we BUILD on Apple Silicon/ARM64, and AVX2 + AVX-VNNI + AVX-512-VNNI (VPDPBUSD) + AMX tiles ONLY where AMX actually wins (AMX-f32 did NOT beat ONNX-int8) on x86-64 — all runtime-dispatched, each with a bit-identical scalar fallback. (2) LLVM autovectorizes ONLY the elementwise/norm/softmax/dequant glue — NEVER hand-roll wide-SIMD over scalar loops (measured ~5x SLOWER, doctrine #3). (3) Every Baidu-proven micro-trick: dequant-once weight cache, fused q/k/v GEMV, contiguous transpose, prefer SDOT over SMMLA where SMMLA is load-bound/half-rate (verified on M4), int4 bandwidth win on bulk weights where measured-lossless. (4) Fused, tape-free, ZERO-per-op-allocation forward; full-core-parallel (kernel-owned rayon pinned to PHYSICAL cores, ONE live forward, no nested rayon-under-lock / no 2nd asupersync runtime — doctrine #5); i32-accumulation non-overflow PROVEN at worst-case K per arch (doctrine #6). (5) HONEST MEASURED BAR (doctrine #8): at-or-near ONNX/MLAS on CPU, head-to-head with thread/allocator/precision FAIRNESS controls (never bench torch at default threads), driven by the 5-pass loop (claim+baseline -> ONE lever + BIT-EXACT proof -> rebench+Score -> keep/revert -> next hotspot). Parity FIRST then perf (#1): drift reverts + docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md; accepted divergence -> docs/DISCREPANCIES.md + kill-switch env var; every number -> docs/PERF_LEDGER.md.

title: C8: SmolVLM2 parity + e2e (caption/VQA quality) + perf
description:
PARITY LADDER + MEASURED BUDGETS: SmolVLM2 captioning/VQA is GENERATIVE/open-ended, so the PARITY GATE is the L0-L5 ladder vs the torch oracle — preprocess exact, per-op cosine ~1, logits within MEASURED quant tolerance, GREEDY token-exact on a held-out image+prompt set — NOT a downstream quality score. Downstream caption (CIDEr/BLEU) + VQA accuracy on a small held-out set are tracked as INFORMATIONAL regression signals with a guard, not the gate. title: Sub-epic E: Polyphonic-TrOMR (tiny, Apache-2.0) — optical music recognition (the RIGHT OMR move, not oemer)
description: Integrate Polyphonic-TrOMR (NetEase/Polyphonic-TrOMR; arXiv 2308.09370; Apache-2.0; tiny: 256-wide, 4+4 layers, 8 heads): a ViT encoder with a light ResNet stem (staff image 128x1280 -> 16x16 patches) + an autoregressive Transformer decoder with FOUR parallel heads (rhythm / pitch / accidental / is-note) + a consistency loss, emitting a music token sequence. Chosen over oemer (REJECTED: oemer is two U-Net segmentation CNNs + a large rule-based reconstruction engine — wrong kernel family, int8-hostile, mostly heuristic). TrOMR consumes one pre-cropped dewarped STAFF, so a staff-detection/dewarp front end (classical CV) is the only extra. Depends on sub-epic A. Meet the franken_ocr PERFORMANCE BAR (full kernel spec in EPIC bd-3jo6 + foundation A7): int8 decoder GEMMs via SDOT/SMMLA-i8mm (ARM) + AVX2/AVX-VNNI/AVX-512-VNNI/AMX (x86), fused tape-free full-core-parallel forward, vision high-precision (doctrine #2), worst-case-K overflow proof (#6), parity-first then perf (#1) with PERF_LEDGER/DISCREPANCIES/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entries.

title: Shared determinism gate helper (same-input-twice → byte-identical under greedy) reused across unit + e2e tests
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
G5 / §7.3 require: **deterministic under fixed sampling (`temperature=0` greedy) — same image+args → byte-identical output**. §8.2 mandates a separate determinism gate. Many beads (unit kernel tests, the e2e CLI test, the conformance harness, the robot contract) all need the same "run twice, assert byte-identical" check. Rather than re-implement it per bead, this builds the shared helper, so the determinism invariant is asserted uniformly and logged richly.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/common/determinism.rs`:
1. **`assert_deterministic(run_fn, n)`** — runs the supplied closure `n` times (default 2, optionally at two thread counts to also probe reduction-order stability), captures each output (decoded text, bbox tags, optionally logits), and asserts byte-identity across runs under `temperature=0`.
2. **Thread-count cross-check**: optionally run at two `FOCR_THREADS` settings and assert the discrete output is identical (the SIMD/parallel paths are bit-identical via i32 accumulation; f32 reduction-order differences must NOT cross an argmax tie within our own engine — distinct from the bf16 ORACLE's nondeterminism, which is measured separately in §8.2).
3. **Structured logging**: emit a `TestLog` `parity` line per pair with `metric:"token_exact"`, `value` (first-divergence position or `identical`), and the thread counts compared.
4. **Reusable across**: unit kernel tests (same input → same output), the e2e CLI test (TEST-e2e-cli-script), the robot contract (scrubbed NDJSON identical across runs), and the conformance harness.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **One helper, used everywhere** so determinism is asserted the same way and logged the same way.
- **Distinguish OUR determinism from the ORACLE's nondeterminism** (§8.2): our engine MUST be deterministic (byte-identical) under greedy; the bf16 reference oracle is NOT necessarily deterministic across thread counts — that envelope is measured separately and is NOT a bug. This helper asserts OUR determinism only.
- **Two thread counts** to catch a reduction-order-dependent argmax flip in our own code early.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Determinism only holds under `temperature=0` greedy — the helper must reject/skip non-greedy configs.
- Scrub timing/run-id before comparing robot NDJSON (use TEST-fixture-harness canonicalization).
- A flake here is a REAL bug (nondeterminism in our engine), not test noise — never paper over it with a tolerance.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `assert_deterministic` runs N times (and optionally 2 thread counts) and asserts byte-identity under greedy.
- Emits a structured `parity`/`token_exact` log line per comparison.
- A self-test with an injected nondeterministic source (e.g. a HashMap iteration order leaking into output) FAILS the gate.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Self-test: a deterministic fn passes; an injected-nondeterministic fn FAILS.
- Self-test: greedy-only enforcement (non-greedy config rejected/skipped with a logged reason).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs `FOCR_THREADS` + greedy `temperature=0` decode path from the scaffold.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the determinism `parity` lines.

title: Perf(structural): SAM vision attention per-query dot loops -> batched GEMM (vision_sam.rs:429-456); QK^T sgemm + decomposed rel-pos bias pass + probs@V sgemm. ~3-5x on global blocks. Parity-risky rel-pos decomposition -> verify bit-parity first
notes:
2026-06-25 Codex/WhiteCave: landed the first safe sub-lever for bd-3n16 without taking on the full parity-risky QK^T/probs@V GEMM rewrite. In src/native_engine/vision_sam.rs::attention, decomposed rel-pos H/W bias dot products are now precomputed once per query/head and then indexed inside the key loop. This preserves the old f32 summation order for each bias term while removing repeated q·Rh/q·Rw recomputation for every key. Added decomposed_rel_pos_bias_matches_direct_inner_loop_formula to prove exact equality against the old direct inner-loop formula, plus an ignored local release-perf probe sam_attention_relpos_bias_local_probe. Local synthetic evidence is recorded under artifacts/perf/bd-3n16/ with SHA256SUMS and a WIN row in docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md (not PERF_LEDGER because no pinned CPU-reference ratio yet): 14x14 SAM attention probe improved from avg 26.8490916 ms to 17.787375 ms (1.509x, 33.75% less wall time) with unchanged checksum -0.009587256237864494. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fmt --check; git diff --check on touched files; cd artifacts/perf/bd-3n16 && shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS; python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config patch.crates-io.asupersync.path='"/dp/asupersync"' test vision_sam::tests --lib -- --nocapture; same command check --all-targets; same command clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; same command test; timeout 240 ubs --only=rust --diff --jobs=1 --quiet --ci --report-json=/tmp/franken_ocr_ubs_bd_3n16.json . (0 findings). Remaining work for this bead: implement and prove the larger batched QK^T + probs@V GEMM path, with rel-pos bias added as a separate parity-checked pass.

2026-06-25 WhiteCave: tested and rejected a standalone layout-only follow-up for SAM attention after the rel-pos precompute win. The local experiment replaced the nested Q/K/V split loop with per-head contiguous slice copies and precomputed key ky/kx coordinates outside the logits loop. It preserved the synthetic probe checksum but regressed the 7-run release-perf probe from 17.53473214285714 ms to 18.066375 ms (+3.03%), so the source change was reverted and not committed. Added qkv_grid_hoist_baseline_7run.txt and qkv_grid_hoist_attempt_7run.txt under artifacts/perf/bd-3n16/, updated SHA256SUMS/README.md, and added a NEGATIVE(reverted) row to docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md with do-not-retry guidance. Pinned reference ratio remains in the gauntlet lane.

title: focrq reader accepts noncanonical QInt4 group sizes
description: native_engine::weights::Weights::qint4 validates QInt4 group_size only as nonzero and dividing k. The format docs and quant/int4.rs define valid group sizes as exactly 16 or 32, and FocrqBuilder now rejects noncanonical values. The reader still accepts group_size=2 fixtures, which can silently bless malformed .focrq artifacts. Harden the reader to reject non-{16,32} group sizes and update reader/tensor tests to use canonical groups or assert rejection.
close_reason: Hardened Weights::qint4 to reject noncanonical QInt4 group sizes outside {16,32}, matching docs/focrq-format.md, quant::int4, and FocrqBuilder. Updated reader/tensor fixtures to canonical group_size=16 and added a regression where group_size=8 divides k=32 but is rejected. Verified with qint4 lib slice, native_engine lib slice, rustfmt/diff checks, and focused UBS on weights.rs and tensor.rs.

So 'focr ocr --robot ' and 'focr robot run ' produce NO actual OCR result in the NDJSON stream (only --json non-robot and plain human modes emit the markdown). Found during the native-PDF fresh-eyes review.
