title: focr convert --quant int4: per-group symmetric int4 quantizer + .focrq records
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The offline weight transformation (plan §5) must gain an int4 path. int4 group-quantized expert weights are THE biggest decode bandwidth lever (§6.3): halving bytes-per-token on the expert bulk (the dominant decode cost) is up to ~2x decode throughput. This task adds the deterministic, data-free int4 quantizer to `focr convert` and the `.focrq` writer/reader records that carry int4 weights with inline per-group scales.

## WHAT TO DO
Extend `focr convert <safetensors> -o <.focrq> --quant int4` (plan §7.2, §10 Phase 4). Concretely:
- **Per-group symmetric int4 quant**, group size **16-32** (NVFP4 used 16). For each weight row, partition the K-dimension into contiguous groups of `g`; per group `scale = max|w|/7` (int4 symmetric range is [-8,7] or [-7,7] — pick the symmetric [-7,7] mapping, zero-point 0, and record the choice; document in the truth-pack/DISCREPANCIES if [-8,7] is needed for the cliff). Quantize to a 4-bit signed code; pack two int4 codes per byte.
- **Tier discipline (llama.cpp `_M`):** attention `.v_proj` and expert `.down_proj` are kept **one tier higher = int8** (per-output-channel symmetric, the Phase-2 scheme), NOT int4. All other decoder linears eligible for int4: `q/k/o_proj`, expert `gate_proj`/`up_proj`, shared-expert `gate/up/down` (down kept higher), dense layer-0 `gate/up` (down kept higher). `lm_head` int4 NOT default (it is int8 only behind `FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`; int4 lm_head is out of scope unless AF-1 + CER permits).
- **KEEP HIGH PRECISION (unchanged from §5):** entire vision tower (`sam_model.*` + `vision_model.*`), projector, `embed_tokens` (129280x1280), MoE router gate (`mlp.gate.weight`), ALL norms (RMSNorm/LayerNorm). Stored BF16 verbatim.
- **`.focrq` tensor record:** dtype `QInt4PerGroup`, fields `{ name, shape, packing, byte_offset, byte_len, scales_offset, scales_len, group_size, tier }` (§5.2). Scales are an inline per-group `Vec<f32>`. Tier metadata records whether the tensor is int4-g16 / int4-g32 / int8.
- **Tensor remap unchanged** (§5.3): HF dotted paths → internal layout; experts pre-packed cache-contiguous per `(layer, expert)`.
- **Deterministic, data-free PTQ:** pure function of input bytes; same source safetensors → byte-identical `.focrq` (assert via `source_sha256` + a content hash). No RNG, no calibration data in this base path (calibration enters only via AF-1, P4-af1-bit-allocator).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Group quant (not per-channel) for int4** because int4's dynamic range is tiny; per-group scales (g=16-32) recover the accuracy a single per-row scale loses. This is the Q4_K_M recipe.
- **`v_proj`/`down_proj` one tier higher** because they sit on error-amplifying paths (down_proj is the wide reduction; v_proj feeds the attention output) — empirically the tensors that wreck dense-token accuracy first.
- **Data-free** because NVFP4 proved data-free int4-class PTQ OCR-near-identical; avoids calibration-data provenance issues in the base path.
- **[-7,7] symmetric** keeps zero-point 0 so the int4→int8 unpack feeds the symmetric SMMLA/SDOT path directly without a zero-point fixup; note as a DISCREPANCIES entry if the cliff forces [-8,7].

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- OQ-14 (owned by E-PM1) enumerates the EXACT modules NVFP4 quantizes and reconciles vs our ~2229 census — **must be resolved before leaning on prior-art validation** for which tensors are safe at int4. Do NOT assume attention q/k/o or shared experts are validated at int4 until OQ-14 lands.
- Packing two int4 per byte must agree byte-for-byte with the unpack kernel (P4-int4-gemm) per arch — a packing/unpack mismatch is silent garbage. The packing layout is recorded in the `.focrq` header (`arch_target`) so the kernel loads contiguous tiles with zero runtime shuffle.
- Group size must divide the K-dimension of every int4 tensor cleanly, or carry a documented remainder-group handling. K-dims here: 1280 (q/k/o, gate/up, lm_head), 896 (expert down — but down is int8), 6848 (dense layer-0 down — int8). 1280 % 16 = 0, 1280 % 32 = 0 — clean. Record any tensor whose K is not a multiple of g.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr convert --quant int4` produces a valid `.focrq` with `QInt4PerGroup` records, inline per-group scales, tier metadata, and the MIT `license_notice`.
- Deterministic: same source → byte-identical output (content-hash asserted).
- Manifest census passes (all 2710 tensors accounted for; MISSING/SHAPE-MISMATCH/EXTRA = loud refusal).
- `v_proj`, expert `down_proj`, dense-0 `down_proj` stored int8; vision/projector/embeddings/router/norms stored BF16 verbatim.
- Dequant(int4 record) == original-quantized weights to within the int4 group-scale rounding (round-trip determinism test).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit tests in the converter: group partition correctness; symmetric scale = max|w|/7; pack/unpack round-trip (two int4 per byte); tier assignment table (v_proj/down_proj → int8); manifest census happy + missing + shape-mismatch; deterministic same-source content hash. Separate parity/round-trip bead: `P4-convert-int4-tests`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `E-P3`: reuses the Phase-3 per-arch weight pre-packing (`--arch`) and the int8 quantizer scheme for the tier-higher tensors.
- `E-PM1`: needs the pinned config, the token/shape census, and the MIT license string from the truth pack.
- `OQ-14`: determines which tensors are prior-art-validated vs OUR int4 CER risk.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — loader/robot seam, scales layout, packing triangle
- **The load-side reader is a separate deliverable (P4-r2-int4-loader).** This bead WRITES `QInt4PerGroup` records; the loader (`native_engine/weights.rs`, §5.2) READS them (byte-range index + inline scales + tier + load-time census). Keep the writer's directory fields (`scales_offset/scales_len/group_size/tier/packing`) exactly the shape the loader expects; they are validated round-trip in P4-r2-int4-loader's tests.
- **Packing triangle.** The nibble packing convention (two int4 per byte, hi/lo order, per-arch repack mirroring llama.cpp `q4_0_4x8`/`q4_0_8x8`) must agree byte-for-byte across THREE corners: this converter (write), P4-r2-int4-loader (read), P4-int4-gemm (unpack). Pin it once, record it in the header `arch_target`, and cross-test all three.
- **Scales SoA layout (§6.12).** Store the per-group f32 scales as a contiguous struct-of-arrays stream (separate `scales_offset` region) so a kernel tile load streams weights and scales each as one contiguous run; assert `scales_len` is consistent with `shape ÷ group_size` so the loader's sanity check can catch corruption.
- **Quant config is agent-introspectable (P4-r2-robot-quant-config).** Emit enough header metadata (per-tensor tier, group_size, packing, the realized footprint) that `focr robot quant-config` can report the model's quant config without loading the weight bulk.

title: Expected-loss-guided per-layer quant: choose tier by measured CER impact
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Plan §10 Phase 4 names 'expected-loss-guided per-layer quant (choose tier by measured CER impact)' as a core task, and §9.5 frames it decision-theoretically: state = per-tensor/per-layer bit choice; loss = measured CER impact (weighted toward dense numbers/tables where each token must be exact); constraint = total footprint; objective = minimize **expected end-to-end loss with an explicit TAIL bound** (the cliff is below 4-bit, so the optimizer respects a worst-case, not just mean, CER bound). This is the operational layer on top of AF-1's water-filling: AF-1 picks bits from cheap distortion proxies; this bead closes the loop with **actual measured CER** per layer/tier and re-tiers where the proxy mispredicted.

## WHAT TO DO
- For each decoder layer (1..11 MoE, 0 dense) and each quantizable tensor class, **measure the end-to-end CER impact** of dropping that tensor/layer to int4 (vs the int8 baseline) on the dense-numeric/table/code corpus (P4-dense-corpus), AF-2-weighted (CVaR-aware).
- Build a **per-layer tier ledger**: which layers/tensors tolerate int4-g16 / int4-g32 / int8, ranked by measured CER cost per byte saved.
- **Decision rule** (Alien-Artifact Contract requires: explicit state space, actions, loss matrix, confidence term, deterministic fallback, evidence ledger): state = tier per tensor; actions = {keep int8, drop int4-g32, drop int4-g16}; loss matrix = measured ΔCVaR-weighted-CER vs bytes saved; choose the footprint-feasible config minimizing expected loss subject to the AF-2 tail bound; **deterministic fallback** = the AF-1 uniform/water-filling allocation if the measured config violates the tail gate.
- Feed the result back to refine the `bit_allocation_table` (this is the measurement that validates/corrects AF-1's cosine-drop proxy).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Measured CER, not perplexity** — perplexity under-predicts exact-token failure on dense content (§6.3, §11). Weight the loss toward dense numbers/tables/code where each token must be exact.
- **Tail-aware (AF-2 CVaR)**, not mean — a per-layer choice that wrecks dense tables is unacceptable even at good mean CER.
- **AF-1 proxy first, measured CER to correct** — measuring full-CER for every (layer × tier) combo is expensive; AF-1's cheap cosine-drop curves prune the search, and this bead measures only the promising frontier configs.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Combinatorial explosion (12 layers × several tensor classes × 3 tiers): use AF-1 to prune; do not brute-force.
- The oracle's own bf16 nondeterminism floor (§8.2) bounds 'measured CER' resolution — a CER delta inside the oracle's noise is not a real regression. Establish the floor first.
- Every per-layer choice is a DISCREPANCIES/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE entry with its measured impact (Phase-4 exit gate).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- A per-layer tier ledger exists with measured CER-cost-per-byte per layer/tensor.
- The chosen config minimizes expected (CVaR-weighted) CER loss under the footprint budget and respects the AF-2 tail gate; deterministic fallback wired.
- Every quant choice ledgered with its measured impact (Phase-4 exit gate).

## TESTS REQUIRED
Unit: the decision rule picks the min-expected-loss feasible config from a synthetic loss matrix; fallback engages on a tail-gate violation. Integration (model-gated): the per-layer measurement loop produces a stable ledger and the chosen config passes the tail gate, NDJSON-logged. See `P4-expected-loss-per-layer-tests`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-af1-bit-allocator`: this refines/validates AF-1's allocation with measured CER.
- `P4-dense-corpus`: needs the exact-token-sensitive corpus + CER harness.
- `P4-tail-risk-gate`: the AF-2 tail bound is the constraint this optimizes under.

title: CLIP residual add helpers accept malformed Mat backing data
description: The CLIP vision helper add/add_in_place compare only Mat rows/cols before iterating data with zip. If a Mat has matching logical shape but malformed backing data, add can collect a truncated vector and panic in Mat::from_vec, while add_in_place can silently mutate the prefix and return Ok because zip stops at the shorter slice. Validate both operands' backing lengths before residual math, and ensure add_in_place checks before mutating so error paths leave activations unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed CLIP residual helper malformed-backing handling in src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs. add() and add_in_place() now validate both operands with ensure_mat_data_len after logical shape checks and before iteration; add_in_place validates before mutating so malformed rhs/lhs error paths leave activations unchanged. Added add_rejects_malformed_backing_data_without_panic and add_in_place_rejects_malformed_backing_data_before_mutating. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt; focused env CARGO_HOME=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-target TMPDIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/tmp CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo +nightly test native_engine::vision_clip --lib passed (35 passed); cargo +nightly fmt --check passed; git diff --check passed; cargo +nightly check --all-targets passed; cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo +nightly test passed (567 lib tests passed / 1 ignored plus all integration/doc tests green); ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/vision_clip.rs exited 0 with 0 critical findings.

title: Structured JSON-line test-logging conventions (every stage logs inputs/shapes/timings/parity-deltas) + schema validator
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The user explicitly wants **great detailed logging** throughout the tests, and the gauntlet's artifact graph (§8.4/§8.5) wants every claim reproducible and traceable. A free-for-all of `println!` is unusable. This bead defines the ONE structured NDJSON test-logging convention — the schema every unit, conformance, and e2e test emits — plus a validator that CI runs over captured logs to enforce it. It is the single most load-bearing piece of "logging as a feature" for this project.

## WHAT TO DO
Deliver `docs/TEST_LOGGING.md` (the convention) + a machine-readable JSON schema fixture + a Rust validator. The convention:
1. **One JSON object per line (NDJSON), data-only on stdout, diagnostics on stderr, exit 0 = success** (matches the project's robot/CLI conventions and the `cass`/`bv` style mandated in AGENTS.md).
2. **Mandatory fields per line**: `schema_version` (a `TEST_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION` const), `ts` (monotonic-relative, scrubbable), `test` (test fn name), `case` (subcase id), `event` (one of `setup|stage|parity|assert|skip|result`), `stage` (pipeline stage name where applicable: `decode_image|preprocess|tokenize|vision_sam|vision_clip|vision_bridge|connector|prefill|decode|postprocess`), `result` (`pass|fail|xfail|skip|skip_no_model`).
3. **Stage-detail fields** (when `event=stage`): `inputs` (named input descriptors), `shapes` (e.g. `[T,1280]`, `[256,768]`), `dtype` (`f32|bf16|i8|i4|u8`), `elapsed_us`, `simd_tier` (the dispatched tier: `smmla|sdot|vnni512|vnni256|amx|avx2|scalar`), `seed`.
4. **Parity-detail fields** (when `event=parity`): `gate` (`L0|L1|L2|L3|L4|L5`), `metric` (`cosine|max_abs_diff|cer|teds|formula_cdm|argmax_match|token_exact`), `value`, `tolerance`, `oracle_fixture`, `oracle_sha256`, `nondeterminism_envelope` (the measured oracle variance, §8.2), `pass`.
5. **Native-path proof field** (for model-gated e2e): `native_path_ran` (bool) + `fallback_target` (must be `/nonexistent` when proving native ran).
6. **The schema validator** (`tests/common/log_schema.rs`): parses each captured NDJSON line, validates required fields per `event` type, rejects unknown `event`/`stage`/`result` enums, and is itself exercised by a self-test with a deliberately malformed line.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Versioned schema** (`TEST_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION`) so the convention can evolve without breaking log parsers, mirroring `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` (§7.3).
- **`ts` and any duration fields are SCRUBBABLE** so logs can be golden-compared (the canonicalization in TEST-fixture-harness strips them).
- **`simd_tier` on every stage line** so a parity failure can be attributed to a specific dispatched path (the §6.6 catalog tiers) — critical because the AVX2 path is NOT bit-identical to the i32-exact paths (it saturates at i16) and must be visible in logs.
- **`nondeterminism_envelope` on parity lines** because L4 "exact" is only defined over the prefix the bf16 oracle reproduces identically (§8.2) — the log must carry the envelope so a divergence-inside-noise reads as not-a-bug.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Logs must be cheap to emit (no per-token allocation storms in the decode hot path under test) — buffer + flush, or emit only at stage boundaries, never per inner-loop iteration.
- Don't mix human decoration into the NDJSON stream (AGENTS.md robot-ergonomics rule).
- The validator must reject, not silently accept, an unknown enum — a typo'd `stage` is a real bug.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `docs/TEST_LOGGING.md` documents every field, every enum value, and the versioning rule.
- The JSON schema fixture + Rust validator exist and are wired so CI validates captured test logs.
- A malformed line (missing required field / unknown enum) is rejected by the validator (proven by self-test).
- `simd_tier`, `nondeterminism_envelope`, and `native_path_ran` fields are present and documented.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: validator accepts a well-formed line per `event` type.
- Unit: validator rejects a line missing a required field, with an unknown `event`/`stage`/`result`, or with a mismatched `schema_version`.
- Integration: capture logs from a real test run and validate the whole stream.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the scaffold + the `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` precedent + `tests/common/` location.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — correlation id, fuller stage taxonomy, panic-safe flush, error events
- **Per-invocation correlation id (`run_seq` / `trace_id`).** The current fields (`test`,`case`) do NOT correlate the multiple NDJSON lines a single pipeline invocation emits, and a test that runs the pipeline more than once (determinism = run twice; deadlock watchdog = N pages) produces interleaved lines with no way to group them. ADD a mandatory `run_seq` (monotonic per-test invocation counter) and an optional `trace_id` so all `stage`/`parity`/`result` lines of one forward are joinable. This is what makes a failing e2e log actually localizable to one page's one stage.
- **Extend the `stage` enum to cover the real §4.2/§6 internals.** The current list stops at coarse stages; for 'great detailed logging' at EVERY stage the enum MUST also admit the decoder-internal stages the hot path actually has: `embed` (token-embedding lookup, f32-preserving), `rmsnorm`, `rope`, `rswa_attn` (R-SWA ring + reference-block), `moe_router` (top-6 greedy), `moe_expert` (grouped SiLU-gated GEMM), `dense_mlp` (layers with no MoE), `lm_head` (129280-vocab GEMV / argmax-only-under-greedy), and `kv_cache` (ring-buffer bound check). Keep the enum CLOSED+validated (a typo is a bug) but make it complete, and document a per-decoder-layer `layer_idx` sub-field on these lines so a per-layer L2 hidden-state divergence names its layer.
- **Panic-safe flush.** Because logs may buffer (the 'zero hot-loop cost' rule), the convention MUST require a flush-on-drop / flush-on-panic so a CRASHING test still leaves its NDJSON up to the failure point on disk — otherwise the most important failure produces no log. Document the buffering+flush contract.
- **A first-class diagnostic/error event.** ADD `event="error"` (or reuse `result="fail"` with a mandatory `diag` payload: `error_kind`, `focr_exit_code` per §7.4, `message`) so a failing assertion or a caught `FocrError` is machine-parseable on the stream, not just a stderr blob — the validator must require the diag fields when `result="fail"`.
- **`simd_tier` AVX2 caveat in the doc.** Where the convention documents `simd_tier`, explicitly state that the `avx2` tier is the ONE non-bit-identical path (i16 saturation, §6.6) and that a parity line whose `simd_tier=avx2` is compared against an i32-exact reference must carry an `avx2_exception` flag pointing at its `DISC-NNN` — so the validator/reader never mistakes the documented AVX2 divergence for a bug.

title: Tests: int4 converter round-trip, determinism, tier assignment, census
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The int4 converter is the on-disk source of truth for every downstream int4 kernel; a silent packing or tier bug corrupts all OCR output. This bead is the comprehensive test+logging suite proving the converter is deterministic and correct, per the project's mandate for thorough tests with great logging.

## WHAT TO DO
Build a test module + a model-gated integration test for the int4 converter (P4-convert-int4):
- **Round-trip:** for synthetic and (model-gated) real tensors, `quantize_int4 → pack → unpack → dequant` reproduces the quantized weights to the int4 group-scale rounding error; assert max abs error ≤ 0.5*scale per group.
- **Determinism:** convert the same source twice → byte-identical `.focrq`; assert `source_sha256` + output content hash equal. Convert under different thread counts → identical (no RNG, no order dependence).
- **Tier assignment table:** assert `v_proj`, every expert `down_proj`, dense layer-0 `down_proj` are `QInt8PerChan`; `q/k/o_proj`, expert `gate/up_proj`, shared `gate/up` are `QInt4PerGroup`; vision/projector/embed_tokens/router/norms are BF16.
- **Packing/arch:** assert each `--arch` packing dequantizes to the same logical weights (CI cross-check, §5.4 'one canonical artifact, many packings').
- **Manifest census:** happy path passes; an injected missing/extra/shape-mismatched tensor produces a loud named diff and refuses to proceed (exit code 7 / format mismatch path).
- **Structured logging:** emit per-tensor NDJSON `{name, tier, group_size, k, n, scale_bytes, payload_bytes, max_round_trip_err}` so failures localize to a single tensor.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All unit tests green; integration test skips-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights (prove native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`).
- Round-trip error bound asserted per group; determinism byte-identical; tier table exact; census loud-refusal verified.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. Unit tests (synthetic tensors) + one model-gated integration test (real safetensors) with NDJSON logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-convert-int4`: tests the converter it depends on (every *-tests bead depends_on the thing it tests).

title: Audit: apply postprocess LaTeX colon-equals normalization globally
close_reason: Fixed SPEC-115 postprocess normalization: \coloneqq and \eqqcolon now normalize globally after span rewrites, not only when an other-label ref/det span exists. Added no-tag and image-only regression tests. Verified rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/native_engine/postprocess.rs; git diff --check -- src/native_engine/postprocess.rs; cargo test --lib native_engine::postprocess -- --nocapture (24 passed); ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/postprocess.rs (0 critical).

title: Connector: avoid per-row allocation in masked_scatter
description: masked_scatter currently does vision_features.row(feat).to_vec() for each masked placeholder row before copying into inputs_embeds. Because the public safe signature takes &mut Mat for inputs_embeds and &Mat for vision_features, callers cannot alias the same Mat without unsafe code. Scope: copy directly from the immutable source row slice, keep ordering/tie/numeric behavior identical, add no perf claim without a benchmark, and update tests/gates.
close_reason: masked_scatter now copies directly from source row slice; gates green.

comments.author: WhiteCave
comments.text: Implemented behavior-preserving masked_scatter allocation cleanup in src/native_engine/connector.rs. The loop now borrows vision_features.row(feat) directly and copies that slice into inputs_embeds.row_mut(row), removing one temporary Vec allocation per masked placeholder row. Safe-aliasing proof: public signature takes &mut Mat for inputs_embeds and &Mat for vision_features, so safe Rust callers cannot pass the same Mat as both operands; ordering, tie-breaking, floating-point values, and scatter count semantics are unchanged. No wall-clock performance claim or PERF_LEDGER row was made because this was not benchmarked; it is a code-inspection allocation cleanup. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt --check PASS; focused CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config patch.crates-io.asupersync.path="/dp/asupersync" test native_engine::connector --lib PASS (16 tests); git diff --check PASS; python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py PASS; br dep cycles --json PASS (0 cycles); same cargo check --all-targets PASS; same clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; same cargo test PASS (451 passed, 1 ignored doctest plus integration suites green). Required AGENTS UBS spelling ubs $(git diff --name-only) is unsupported by installed UBS v5.0.3 and exits usage 2; supported ubs --diff --ci PASS/exit 0 with 0 critical, 81 warnings from existing connector test unwrap/assert inventory, 9 info.

title: Verification gauntlet + conformance + golden (the conscience)
description:
# E-VERIFY — Verification gauntlet, conformance ladder, golden artifacts (the project's conscience)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is a pure-Rust, framework-free runner for ONE model (Baidu Unlimited-OCR, a DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). The over-arching goal is *max CPU speed for this one model via int4/int8 + fused-at-peak kernels* — but the iron law is **G1 > G2: correctness outranks speed, always**. This epic is the machinery that keeps every speed claim honest. It owns the gates that **P1 (fp32 parity), P2 (int8), P3 (SIMD kernels), and P4 (int4) all depend on**: a kernel that drifts the OCR output is reverted, no source landed, and memorialized — and these gates are what *detect* that drift.

The central methodology (plan §8): **Parity gate FIRST, perf second.** Apply `/porting-to-rust`, `/running-the-gauntlet-on-your-rust-port`, `/testing-conformance-harnesses`, `/testing-golden-artifacts`, `/testing-metamorphic`.

## WHAT THIS EPIC DELIVERS (the children)
1. The Python/HF reference oracle harness (`scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py`) — GPU-correctness, per-layer + e2e dumps, provenance.
2. The oracle nondeterminism-floor characterization (run-twice / two-thread-counts envelope) — sets all downstream tolerances.
3. The L0–L5 parity ladder gates (preprocessing→per-op→per-layer→logits→token→e2e).
4. Differential + metamorphic + golden-artifact suites (insta exact / fuzzy ULP logits / scrubbed NDJSON / canonicalized).
5. Tokenizer conformance (OQ-16) — token-id-exact, an L0/L4 prerequisite.
6. The `ConformanceTest` trait + coverage-accounting matrix + DISCREPANCIES/XFAIL discipline.
7. The three-pillar release certification (performance / conformance / surface-parity) with FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix.
8. The conformal lower-bound release ratchet (release decision uses the LOWER bound).
9. E-processes (Ville's inequality) for the load-bearing invariants (KV bound, overflow-free, determinism, SIMD==scalar).
10. The NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE / PERF_LEDGER artifact-graph ledger discipline.
11. The head-to-head gauntlet bench (`benches/gauntlet`) with thread/allocator/precision fairness.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Oracle split (OQ-17):** correctness fixtures come from the UNMODIFIED official model on a **CUDA host**, frozen once and committed — parity NEVER depends on CPU HF (the official `infer()` is `.cuda()` + CUDA-autocast oriented). The CPU perf baseline is separate.
- **Establish the oracle's own nondeterminism floor FIRST** before setting ANY tolerance. The HF reference is non-deterministic across torch thread counts / BLAS reduction order at the logit-tie level (bf16, 129280-vocab argmax). L4 'exact' is defined only over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically; L3 logit tolerance is DERIVED from measured oracle variance, NOT the imported frankensearch `0.055`.
- **Discrete output bit-exact, continuous values within documented tolerance** (the frankensearch `parity_logits_and_ranking_match_reference` shape).
- **Release uses the conformal LOWER bound, not the point estimate.** A change may only land if it raises the lower bound without lowering any per-category bound — the formal version of 'parity gate first.'
- **Convergent multi-round:** ≥10 full rounds, ≥2 consecutive clean rounds (<3 new genuine findings), every open hypothesis resolved.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A tokenizer mismatch corrupts EVERY downstream gate (OQ-16 is a prerequisite).
- Do NOT assert 'multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses' — R-SWA makes multi-page decode CROSS-PAGE DEPENDENT (OQ-13).
- Three pillars, NO victory-on-one-while-another-regresses.
- The oracle PyO3/subprocess bridge is test-only and NEVER linked into the shipping `focr` inference binary (preserves G3's no-FFI runtime claim).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (epic-level)
- All children complete; L0–L5 ladder all green for the f32 path (P1), int8 path (P2), and int4 path within its ledgered budget (P4).
- Release-readiness scorecard all-green: parity (L0–L5), surface-parity (FeatureUniverse), honest perf ratios (gauntlet), determinism, cross-platform build matrix, ledger completeness — converged (≥10 rounds, ≥2 clean).
- The release decision is taken on the conformal LOWER bound; no red parity cell, no unledgered divergence.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (Phase -1 Truth Pack: pinned source hashes, token census, OQ answers) for fixture provenance and the census the parity gates assert against. Children consume specific OQ research beads owned by E-PM1: OQ-16 (tokenizer), OQ-17 (oracle strategy), OQ-13 (multi-page span), OQ-14 (NVFP4 module set), OQ-18 (token census). Downstream, P1/P2/P3/P4 task gates depend on the L0–L5 ladder and the ledger discipline defined here.

title: Python/HF reference oracle harness (gen_reference_fixtures.py) — GPU correctness + per-layer + e2e dumps + provenance
description:
# Reference oracle harness — `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py`

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Every parity gate (L0–L5), every differential test, and every golden fixture is measured against the bf16 reference. This harness is the single producer of the golden truth. Without it there is no 'correct' to compare against, so it is the literal foundation of G1 (OCR accuracy matches the reference stack). It serves the over-arching goal by making 'correctness before speed' executable: no kernel can be declared correct without a frozen oracle output to diff against.

## WHAT TO DO
Write `scripts/gen_reference_fixtures.py` running a **pinned environment**: `torch==2.10.0`, `transformers==4.57.1`, `Pillow==12.1.1`, `pymupdf` (per the README — NOT the `4.46.3` recorded in `config.json`). It runs `model.infer(...)` (signature [VERIFIED]: `model.infer(tokenizer, prompt='', image_file='', output_path='', base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True, test_compress=False, save_results=False, eval_mode=False, max_length=32768, tps_interval=0, no_repeat_ngram_size=0, ngram_window=0, temperature=0.0)`) to capture golden outputs.

**Oracle split (OQ-17, decisive):** the official `infer()` path is CUDA-oriented (`.cuda()` + CUDA autocast), so a CPU bf16 HF oracle is NOT guaranteed to run as-is. Therefore:
- **(correctness)** golden fixtures come from the **unmodified official model on a CUDA host**, frozen once and committed — parity NEVER depends on CPU HF.
- **(performance)** the CPU baseline is a SEPARATE concern (handled by VERIFY-gauntlet-bench), not this harness.

**Dumps to produce (as `.npy`, loadable via the `fnp-io` NPY/NPZ pattern or our own reader):**
- Per-layer activation dumps: post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector, per-decoder-layer hidden states (all 12), pre-lm_head, logits.
- End-to-end outputs: decoded text + bbox tags for the golden image corpus.
- ALSO dump the **community NVFP4/GGUF per-layer activations as secondary oracles** (no framework runtime dependency — just frozen fixtures; used during bring-up).

**Determinism harness for the oracle:** set `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)` and capture the seeded RNG per call (the ML-System-class oracle-wiring requirement).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Pin the RUNTIME env (`4.57.1`/`2.10.0`), not the export-tag `4.46.3` — the README controls at runtime.
- Golden corpus must span the OUTPUT-LENGTH axis (sparse page, dense page, 10-page, 40-page) so downstream profiling/parity covers both vision-prefill-dominated and decode-dominated regimes (plan §9.1).
- The fixtures bundle a `PROVENANCE.md` (mandatory): `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, pinned HF git ref + GitHub git ref (from E-PM1 truth pack), exact command line, CUDA device string, and a SHA-256 of each emitted `.npy`.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- This harness and its PyO3/subprocess bridge are TEST-ONLY and must NEVER be linked into the shipping `focr` inference binary (preserves G3 no-FFI runtime).
- The model changed on 2026-06-24 (post-cutoff) — the fixtures must record the EXACT pinned source commit so a later model change cannot silently invalidate the golden set.
- Do NOT freeze fixtures before OQ-17 is answered (it decides whether the perf baseline can even use CPU HF).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `gen_reference_fixtures.py` runs reproducibly on a CUDA host, emits all listed per-layer `.npy` dumps + e2e text/bbox for the full golden corpus.
- Each fixture carries a `PROVENANCE.md` with pinned versions, git refs, exact command, SHA-256.
- A second run with identical seed/threads is byte-identical for the deterministic prefix (feeds VERIFY-oracle-nondeterminism-floor).
- Secondary NVFP4/GGUF activation dumps committed as frozen `.npy`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A provenance-validation test (in VERIFY-oracle-tests) that fails if any committed fixture lacks a complete `PROVENANCE.md` or a matching SHA-256.
- Structured logging: the harness emits one JSON line per dumped tensor (`{stage, shape, dtype, sha256, ms}`) so the run is auditable.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- **E-PM1**: needs the pinned HF/GitHub commit hashes and the load-bearing source snapshots for provenance.
- **OQ-17**: decides the oracle split (does CPU HF run?) — must be answered before the fixture strategy is finalized.

title: Tests: oracle harness provenance + reproducibility validation
description:
# Tests — oracle harness provenance & reproducibility

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The oracle is the root of all parity. If a fixture's provenance is wrong or unrecorded, every downstream gate is silently anchored to an unknown model version. This test bead proves the oracle harness produces traceable, reproducible fixtures.

## WHAT TO DO
- A test that walks `tests/fixtures/native/` and asserts every fixture directory has a `PROVENANCE.md` containing: `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, a pinned HF git ref, a pinned GitHub git ref, the exact command line, and a SHA-256 per `.npy`.
- A test that recomputes the SHA-256 of each committed `.npy` and asserts it matches the recorded value (catches accidental fixture corruption / mismatched commit).
- A reproducibility check: given the seeded RNG capture, re-running the deterministic-prefix portion (where a CUDA host is available; skip-with-SUCCESS otherwise) yields byte-identical tokens for that prefix.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS when neither weights nor a CUDA host is present, so CI stays green without the 6.67 GB weights.
- Structured JSON-line logging on every assertion (`{check, fixture, expected_sha, actual_sha, result}`).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All committed fixtures pass provenance + SHA checks.
- Test is green in CI without the model (skip-with-SUCCESS) and green with it.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the test bead. It must log per-fixture structured results.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the thing it validates).

title: Metamorphic test suite — oracle-free invariants (resize/rotation/whitespace/determinism); multi-page CROSS-PAGE dependence (OQ-13)
description:
# Metamorphic test suite

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Metamorphic testing (`/testing-metamorphic`) proves properties that must hold WITHOUT an oracle — invaluable for inputs not in the golden corpus and for catching bugs the oracle also has. It complements differential testing: differential checks 'same as reference', metamorphic checks 'self-consistent under transformations.'

## WHAT TO DO
Implement metamorphic relations that MUST hold:
- **Identity-resize invariance**: resizing to the same size is a no-op on recognized text.
- **90°-rotation / transpose relationships on bbox coords**: rotating the page transforms bboxes predictably.
- **Whitespace-padding invariance**: padding a page with whitespace must NOT change recognized text.
- **Base-mode determinism across runs**: same image → same parse.

**⚠️ CRITICAL — multi-page (OQ-13):** Do **NOT** assert 'multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses.' Under R-SWA the multi-page decode is **cross-page DEPENDENT**: in a single 32K pass, ALL pages' visual+prompt prefixes form one frozen reference block, so page N can attend to pages 1..N-1. The output is NOT a concatenation of independent single-page parses. The defensible metamorphic property is closer to the OPPOSITE: **changing page order / earlier-page content MAY change later-page output.** Lock the precise property only after OQ-13 confirms the reference-block span from `SlidingWindowLlamaAttention` / `infer_multi`.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Metamorphic relations are derived from the model's documented semantics (bbox /999 normalized coords, [-1,1] normalize, R-SWA reference block).
- The multi-page relation is deliberately phrased as a dependence-existence property, not an independence property, to avoid encoding a falsehood (the most dangerous metamorphic-test failure mode here).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Whitespace-padding invariance assumes the pad gray (127) is outside the recognized content — verify against the actual pad color.
- The multi-page property is GATED on OQ-13; until resolved, only the single-page metamorphic relations are asserted.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Identity-resize, rotation/transpose bbox, whitespace-pad, and Base-mode determinism relations all hold over a generated input set.
- The multi-page relation (post-OQ-13) asserts cross-page dependence, NOT independence.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- One metamorphic test per relation, structured-logged (`{relation, n_cases, violations}`).
- A generator producing transformed input pairs.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (needs a working e2e path to transform) and **OQ-13** (decides the multi-page metamorphic property; gating the §9.5 cross-page KV-sharing idea too).

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — scope boundary vs the fault suite
This suite covers ORACLE-FREE INVARIANTS under content-preserving transforms (resize/rotation/whitespace/determinism + the cross-page-dependence property). It does NOT cover malformed/adversarial INPUT — corrupt/truncated images, mid-decode cancellation, budget-exceeded — which is the FAULT leg of the conformance pillar (§8.5) and is owned by the new VERIFY-r1-fault-suite. Keep the boundary explicit so robustness coverage is not assumed to live here.

title: Golden-artifact suite — insta exact (ocr --json/help) / fuzzy logits+activations / scrubbed NDJSON / canonicalized cross-platform
description:
# Golden-artifact suite (`/testing-golden-artifacts`)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Golden artifacts freeze known-good outputs and diff forever — the cheapest, highest-signal regression catcher. They cover what differential/metamorphic do not: the exact CLI/robot surface contract and the per-layer numeric fingerprints. A snapshot diff catches a regression the moment it lands, with the exact before/after visible in the PR.

## WHAT TO DO
Apply the RIGHT pattern per artifact type (this is the load-bearing discipline of the skill):
- **exact (insta snapshots)** for `focr ocr --json` structure and CLI `--help` output.
- **fuzzy (epsilon / ULP)** for logits and per-layer activation tensors (consumes the per-op ULP table).
- **scrubbed (strip timing, run-id, durations)** for robot NDJSON event streams.
- **canonicalized (line endings, paths, sorted bbox)** for cross-platform stability.
- Frozen reference fixture `tests/fixtures/native/<doc>_reference.json` from the Python oracle is the bar.
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` workflow with **mandatory `git diff` review**; `*.actual` gitignored; **CI never auto-updates** goldens.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Wrong pattern = useless test: an exact snapshot of a timing-laden NDJSON stream flaps; a fuzzy snapshot of CLI help hides real regressions. Pattern-per-artifact is mandatory.
- Canonicalization (strip timing, sort bbox) makes the snapshot deterministic across runs/platforms.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Robot NDJSON has run-ids and durations that MUST be scrubbed or every run flaps.
- Cross-platform path/line-ending differences must be canonicalized or the 5-target matrix produces 5 different goldens.
- CI must NEVER auto-update goldens (that defeats the regression-catch); only a human-reviewed `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` + `git diff` lands a new golden.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr ocr --json` and `--help` have exact insta snapshots.
- logits/activations have fuzzy (ULP) goldens.
- robot NDJSON has scrubbed goldens; cross-platform outputs canonicalized.
- `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` documented; CI never auto-updates.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The golden tests themselves (insta + custom fuzzy/scrubbed comparators), structured-logged on mismatch.
- A test asserting `*.actual` is gitignored and that CI does not set `UPDATE_GOLDENS`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the reference JSON bar) and VERIFY-ladder-l1-l2 (the ULP comparator for fuzzy tensor goldens). Consumed-by VERIFY-ladder-l5.

title: ConformanceTest trait + coverage-accounting matrix (≥0.95 MUST) + DISCREPANCIES/XFAIL discipline + fixture provenance
description:
# ConformanceTest trait + coverage matrix + XFAIL discipline

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The conformance harness is the structured backbone that turns ad-hoc tests into an auditable coverage claim. `/testing-conformance-harnesses` requires every MUST/SHOULD clause of OUR spec to be enumerated and accounted, so 'we are conformant' is a measured statement (≥0.95 MUST coverage) rather than a vibe. This is what lets the release scorecard say 'conformance: green' with evidence.

## WHAT TO DO
- Define a `ConformanceTest` trait: `name / category / requirement_level{Must,Should,May} / run`.
- Build a **coverage-accounting matrix**: every MUST/SHOULD clause of our spec — the extracted `EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md` (THE SPEC, §10) — enumerated, with **≥0.95 MUST coverage required to claim conformance**.
- **Differential testing (Pattern 1) against the PyO3 oracle is the gold standard** registered here.
- **Fixture provenance is mandatory**: a `PROVENANCE.md` recording `transformers==4.57.1`, `torch==2.10.0`, the git ref, and the exact command.
- **Intentional divergences are XFAIL, never SKIP** — each an entry `DISC-NNN` in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` (reference behavior, our impl, MEASURED impact, kill-switch env var, resolution, review date).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- XFAIL (expected-fail, counted) vs SKIP (ignored, hides debt): a SKIP silently drops coverage; an XFAIL keeps the clause in the matrix as a known, ledgered divergence.
- The coverage matrix is computed from the SPEC, not the test list — so a missing test shows up as an uncovered MUST clause, not as a passing suite.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The spec (`EXISTING_UNLIMITED_OCR_STRUCTURE.md`) is produced in Phase 1 from the pinned source (E-PM1 + spec extraction) — the matrix cannot be complete until the spec is extracted, but the trait + matrix scaffold lands first.
- 'partial' coverage NEVER rounds up to 'present'; 'excluded' still counts as coverage debt.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `ConformanceTest` trait implemented; differential/golden/metamorphic suites register under it with categories.
- Coverage matrix enumerates every MUST/SHOULD clause; ≥0.95 MUST coverage reported to claim conformance.
- Every intentional divergence is an XFAIL with a `DISC-NNN` in `DISCREPANCIES.md`; fixture `PROVENANCE.md` present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A meta-test asserting the coverage matrix computes a MUST-coverage ratio and fails if < 0.95 (when claiming conformance).
- A test asserting every XFAIL maps to a real `DISC-NNN`.
- Structured logging of the coverage matrix as a committed artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the spec source) and VERIFY-oracle-bridge (the differential gold standard). The other suites (differential/metamorphic/golden) register under this trait.

title: Three-pillar release certification (perf / conformance / surface) with FeatureUniverse / SurfaceMatrix; no victory-on-one-while-another-regresses
description:
# Three-pillar release certification (ML-System-class gauntlet)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
franken_ocr is an ML-System-class port (the `/running-the-gauntlet-on-your-rust-port` class shared with frankentorch / franken_whisper). The release gate is the three-pillar gauntlet — the single check that prevents shipping a fast-but-wrong, or correct-but-incomplete-surface, binary. It enforces the project's deepest rule: you cannot win on one pillar while another regresses.

## WHAT TO DO
Run the gauntlet as the release gate with **three pillars, NO victory-on-one-while-another-regresses**:
- **(a) Performance** — honest per-stage ratios vs the reference (from VERIFY-gauntlet-bench / §9).
- **(b) Conformance** — same answer as the bf16 reference (L0–L5 ladder + metamorphic + fault), via the ConformanceTest matrix.
- **(c) Surface parity** — a **FeatureUniverse / SurfaceMatrix** accounting EVERY CLI surface, robot event, and modeling feature as `present | partial | missing | n/a | excluded`. **partial NEVER rounds up to present; excluded still counts as coverage debt.**

Produce the **release-readiness scorecard**: parity (L0–L5 all green), surface-parity (CLI/contract), honest perf vs reference (gauntlet ratios), determinism, cross-platform build matrix, ledger completeness. **A release cannot ship with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence.**

**Convergence rule**: ≥10 full rounds, ≥2 consecutive clean rounds (<3 new genuine findings), every open hypothesis resolved (per-pillar hypothesis ledgers). `convergence-tracker` gates it.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The FeatureUniverse is the formal answer to 'did we drop a feature' — every modeling feature (R-SWA, Gundam tiling, multi-page, all prompt modes), every CLI surface, every robot event is enumerated and scored.
- Convergent multi-round (not single-pass) because early rounds find shallow bugs; convergence requires the deep ones to stop appearing.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A green perf pillar with a regressed conformance cell is a FAIL, not a partial win — the no-cross-regression rule is absolute.
- The capacity certificate from asupersync (p95/p99 queueing, no nested-runtime / no rayon oversubscription) is a gauntlet artifact that must be present (proves the `many_pages` soak holds).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Scorecard all-green: parity, surface, perf, determinism, build matrix, ledger.
- FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix complete; no `present` that is actually `partial`; excluded items counted as debt.
- Convergence: ≥10 rounds, ≥2 consecutive clean rounds, all hypotheses resolved.
- No red parity cell, no unledgered divergence at ship.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A scorecard generator emitting the per-pillar status as a committed artifact.
- A `convergence-tracker` integration asserting the round/clean-round criteria.
- A test asserting the SurfaceMatrix enumerates every CLI subcommand + every robot event in the frozen schema.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (conformance pillar), VERIFY-conformance-trait (the matrix), VERIFY-gauntlet-bench (perf pillar), and VERIFY-conformal-ratchet (the release decision uses its lower bound). This is the Phase-5 release gate.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — the THREE conformance legs and the surface-matrix source
The conformance pillar (b) is **L0–L5 ladder + metamorphic + FAULT** (plan §8.5, verbatim). The fault leg is owned by the new **VERIFY-r1-fault-suite** (input-fault robustness: corrupt/truncated/zero-byte images, malformed prompt, mid-decode cancellation, budget-exceeded, missing-model) — a green conformance pillar REQUIRES the fault suite green, not just the ladder + metamorphic. The surface-parity pillar (c) scores against the enumerated **VERIFY-r1-surface-matrix** (FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix population), which is the source of truth this cert consumes — this cert ASSERTS the matrix is complete/no-partial-rounds-up; it does not build it. Add both as inputs when wiring the scorecard.

Focused checks passed: sh -n scripts/check.sh; python3 scripts/gauntlet_cert.py --self-test; python3 -m py_compile scripts/gauntlet_cert.py; git diff --check -- scripts/check.sh. title: Conformal lower-bound release ratchet — Beta-posterior × distribution-free conformal band; release decision uses the LOWER bound
description:
# Conformal lower-bound release ratchet

## BACKGROUND / WHY
A point-estimate parity score (e.g. '99.2% pass') is overconfident — it ignores sampling uncertainty and lets a noisy improvement masquerade as progress. The conformal lower-bound ratchet (the `/alien-artifact-coding` conformal family) is the FORMAL version of 'parity gate first': it makes the release decision on a statistically defensible LOWER bound, so a change only lands if it genuinely raises the floor. This is the alien-artifact math family that keeps the release honest under uncertainty.

## WHAT TO DO
- Compute the parity score as a **Beta-posterior per category × a distribution-free conformal band**.
- The **release decision uses the LOWER bound, NOT the point estimate** (`truncate_score` to 6 dp).
- **Ratchet rule**: a change may only land if it **raises the lower bound WITHOUT lowering any per-category bound.**
- Ship the galaxy-brain transparency card (equation · substituted values · plain-English intuition · validity assumptions · what would flip the decision) and an assumptions ledger.
- Provide a deterministic conservative fallback (the raw pass/fail point estimate) if the conformal machinery cannot be computed.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- LOWER bound, not point estimate: prevents shipping on lucky noise; this is the load-bearing decision rule.
- Per-category bounds (not just aggregate): a change that improves overall but regresses table parity is REJECTED — the per-category no-lowering rule encodes the no-cross-regression principle at the statistics layer.
- `truncate_score` to 6 dp: deterministic, reproducible decision boundary.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Requires a calibration set sized for the conformal band to be meaningful; too-small a corpus gives a useless (very low) lower bound — document the minimum.
- No adaptive controller ships without a conservative deterministic fallback (Alien-Artifact Engineering Contract).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Parity score computed as Beta-posterior × conformal band; release decision taken on the LOWER bound (truncated to 6 dp).
- Ratchet enforced: a landing change raises the lower bound and lowers no per-category bound.
- Transparency card + assumptions ledger + deterministic fallback present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A unit test of the lower-bound computation on synthetic pass/fail counts (known Beta posteriors).
- A ratchet test asserting a per-category regression blocks the land even if aggregate improves.
- Structured logging of the per-category bounds + decision.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-ladder-l5 (the per-category parity results it ratchets over) and VERIFY-conformance-trait (the category structure). Its lower bound is consumed by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert as the release decision input.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — minimum calibration-corpus size is a hard precondition, not advice
The GOTCHA 'too-small a corpus gives a useless (very low) lower bound' must be a CHECKED precondition: compute and COMMIT the minimum per-category calibration count needed for the conformal band to be meaningful (e.g. the smallest n at which the Beta-posterior + distribution-free band lower bound is not dominated by the prior), and have the ratchet REFUSE TO DECIDE (fall back to the deterministic raw pass/fail point estimate, ledgered) when any per-category corpus is below that n — rather than silently emitting a meaninglessly-low bound that blocks all landings. Add a test: a category with n below the minimum triggers the deterministic-fallback path, not a spurious red.
notes: 2026-06-25 CreamStork: landed 0b2703c wiring scripts/gauntlet_cert.py --self-test into scripts/check.sh as a supporting guard for the three-pillar release cert, conformal lower-bound ratchet, and Ville e-process invariant math. Focused checks passed: sh -n scripts/check.sh; python3 scripts/gauntlet_cert.py --self-test; python3 -m py_compile scripts/gauntlet_cert.py; git diff --check -- scripts/check.sh. Bead remains open/blocked by L5/conformance/release inputs. Repo gate still has a pre-existing cargo fmt --check blocker in native_engine files from de67e39; this slice did not touch those files.

title: E-processes (Ville) for load-bearing invariants — KV bound, int8 overflow-free, determinism, SIMD==scalar
description:
# E-processes (Ville's inequality) for the load-bearing invariants

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Four invariants, if EVER violated, silently corrupt output: the KV cache exceeding its bound (R-SWA correctness), an int8 i32 accumulator overflowing (wrong arithmetic), same-input nondeterminism (irreproducibility), and the SIMD path diverging from scalar (silent kernel bug). A fixed-sample test under-monitors an unbounded test stream. E-processes (anytime-valid, Ville's inequality) alarm on the FIRST genuine violation across an unbounded stream WITHOUT a Bonferroni penalty — the right tool for invariants that must hold forever. This is the same machinery the gauntlet uses for its statistical decisions, reused for safety invariants.

## WHAT TO DO
Monitor each invariant as an **anytime-valid e-process** (`p₀, λ, α` per hardware/software calibration), alarming on the first genuine violation across an unbounded test stream:
1. **KV cache never exceeds `L·(m+128)`** — L=12, n=128, m=reference length. The R-SWA ring buffer + reference block must satisfy `C_RSWA(T) = L·(m + min(n,T)) ≤ L·(m+n)`.
2. **int8 i32 accumulator never overflows** — the worst case is the dense layer-0 `down_proj` at **K = 6848** (U8S8 ≤ ~221.7M; it fits i32 but must be PROVEN, NOT assumed; do NOT inherit frankensearch's `k≤1536` bound).
3. **Same-input determinism** — same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
4. **SIMD path == scalar path bit-identical** — integer accumulation is associative, so SDOT/SMMLA/VNNI == scalar EXACTLY (tolerance is only for int8-vs-f32 drift, never for the SIMD path itself).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- E-process (not p-value): anytime-valid, no multiple-testing penalty across an unbounded stream — correct for 'must hold forever' invariants.
- The overflow invariant is a PROOF OBLIGATION (AGENTS.md doctrine #6): a unit test at worst-case K=6848 on EVERY arch, plus the e-process as the continuous monitor.
- SIMD==scalar is bit-identical (not cosine) because integer add is associative — any divergence is a definite bug, alarmed immediately.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Calibrate `p₀, λ, α` so a genuine violation alarms but hardware/measurement jitter does not produce false alarms.
- The KV-bound e-process must account for the logical reference block `m` GROWING with page count up to the 32K cap (R-SWA is constant generated-token KV, not page-constant memory) — the bound is `L·(m+128)` with growing `m`, not a fixed number.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Four e-processes implemented, calibrated, and wired into the test stream.
- Each alarms on an injected violation (a deliberately-broken fixture) and stays silent on the clean stream.
- The int8 overflow invariant additionally has a worst-case-K=6848 per-arch unit test (the proof obligation).
- The SIMD==scalar invariant has a bit-identical assertion per arch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- One e-process test per invariant, each with a clean-stream pass and an injected-violation alarm.
- The K=6848 overflow proof unit test (scalar + every SIMD tier).
- The SIMD==scalar bit-identical test per dispatched tier (SDOT/SMMLA/VNNI/scalar).
- Structured logging of the e-value per step (`{invariant, step, e_value, alarm}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-conformance-trait (registers under it). The overflow + SIMD==scalar invariants are consumed-by P2/P3 kernel beads (they re-prove these on every kernel change). The KV-bound invariant is consumed-by the R-SWA work in P1/P3.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (review pass 1)


## ADDENDUM (review r1) — the four invariants are RE-PROVEN by the kernel beads that can break them
This bead BUILDS the four e-processes + the K=6848 overflow proof + the SIMD==scalar bit-identical assertion. The invariants are consumed-by (re-proven on every change to) the kernels that can violate them: the int8 overflow + SIMD==scalar invariants are re-proven by every P2/P3 int8/SIMD GEMM bead (e.g. P3-smmla-prefill-gemm, P2-int8-attn, P2-int8-lmhead, P3-int8-attn); the KV-bound invariant is re-proven by the R-SWA work (P1-rswa-attn) and by AF-5/P3-numa-usl (the pool caps must not change the per-forward KV bound). When wiring, ensure those kernel beads reference this gate as the invariant they must keep, so a kernel that overflows i32 at K=6848 or whose SIMD path diverges from scalar trips the e-process immediately.

Focused checks passed: sh -n scripts/check.sh; python3 scripts/gauntlet_cert.py --self-test; python3 -m py_compile scripts/gauntlet_cert.py; git diff --check -- scripts/check.sh. title: NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE / PERF_LEDGER / DISCREPANCIES artifact-graph ledger discipline (claim_id, evidence_id, fixture hash, CPU feature, kill-switch)
description:
# Artifact-graph ledger discipline — NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE / PERF_LEDGER / DISCREPANCIES

## BACKGROUND / WHY
G7 (Honest) is a non-negotiable goal: every accepted divergence and every rejected optimization must be a reproducible, traceable ledger entry — never prose, never a remembered claim. This discipline is what stops the swarm from re-attempting a dead lever (the inherited NE-INH entries) and stops a 'win' from being declared without a head-to-head measured ratio + correctness proof. It is the conscience's record-keeping, and it serves the whole optimization program by making every speed claim falsifiable.

## WHAT TO DO
- `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md` + `docs/PERF_LEDGER.md` are **ARTIFACT-GRAPH ledgers, NOT prose.** Use the frankentorch row format: `date | WIN/NEGATIVE(reverted) | lever | MEASURED before→after vs reference (ratio) | bit-exact correctness proof | Disposition KEEP/REVERT | 'do not retry X unless Y' | per-lever W/L/N`.
- Additionally carry, per entry/row, the **FrankenSuite artifact-graph fields**: `claim_id`, `evidence_id`, **model source commit + fixture hash** (from the Phase -1 truth pack), the **CPU feature string** (the dispatched SIMD tier), the **exact command + env**, and the **fallback / kill-switch state**.
- Per-lever artifact dirs `artifacts/perf/<bead>/` with paired baseline/after gauntlet logs + SHA-256.
- `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` — every accepted numeric divergence: reference behavior, our impl, MEASURED impact, kill-switch env var, resolution, tests affected, review date.
- **A 'win' only counts with a head-to-head MEASURED ratio against a real reference AND a correctness proof.** Everything else lands in NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE, not PERF_LEDGER.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Artifact-graph (claim_id/evidence_id/fixture hash) not prose: every claim is reproducible and traceable to the EXACT model version it was measured against (the model changed 2026-06-24).
- The kill-switch / fallback state per row proves WHICH path was active when the number was measured (int8-attn on/off, SIMD tier).
- Inherited NE-INH-1..5 are PRIORS (re-confirm on this model's shapes), not franken_ocr measurements — keep them clearly separated.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- A lever that does not clear its measurement bar is REVERTED, not kept; the per-lever W/L/N tally makes a thrice-failed idea visibly dead.
- No fabricated results — the franken_ocr measurements section stays empty until a real head-to-head ratio with a correctness proof exists.
- Every perf commit re-states its parity receipt (text exact, max logit diff, deterministic).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The three ledgers exist with the full artifact-graph schema (claim_id, evidence_id, fixture hash, CPU feature, command+env, kill-switch state).
- A landed perf win has a paired baseline/after artifact dir with SHA-256.
- Every accepted divergence is a DISCREPANCIES entry with measured impact + kill-switch + review date.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A linter/CI check that PERF_LEDGER rows have all mandatory artifact-graph fields and a referenced `artifacts/perf/<bead>/` dir.
- A check that every kill-switch env var named in DISCREPANCIES exists in `error.rs`/the engine.
- A check that no PERF_LEDGER 'WIN' lacks a correctness-proof reference.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the model source commit + fixture hash come from the Phase -1 truth pack). Consumed-by every P2/P3/P4 perf bead (each lands its measured row here) and by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert (ledger-completeness is a scorecard cell).

Added required PERF_LEDGER correctness_proof column/enforcement, parsed real NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE measurement rows outside fenced templates, and require claim/evidence/provenance/command/fallback/proof/disposition/tally/agent/evidence-dir fields for future negative-evidence entries. Not closing because cargo fmt --check still fails on pre-existing native_engine formatting from de67e39, and ubs --diff --only=python . stopped producing output during Python scan and was interrupted after about 90s.

title: Head-to-head gauntlet bench (benches/gauntlet) vs the Phase -1 proven CPU reference — per-stage ratios with thread/allocator/precision fairness
description:
# Head-to-head gauntlet bench — `benches/gauntlet`

## BACKGROUND / WHY
G2 (CPU speed beats the proven CPU baseline) is meaningless without an apples-to-apples head-to-head. This bench is how G2 is PROVED — and, critically, how fake wins are PREVENTED (the hardened frankentorch lesson: never benchmark torch at @64, oversubscription inflates fake 'wins'). It shells out to the real reference and reports honest reference/focr ratios per stage, so the honest story is visible: decode faster, vision-prefill maybe slower in f32 v1.

## WHAT TO DO
`benches/gauntlet` shells out to the **Phase -1 proven CPU reference** (`FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD` / `FOCR_REFERENCE_BACKEND` env) per stage, parses elapsed, reports the **honest reference/focr ratio** (tag each row OK/warn/slower/'focr faster') — NOT a self-relative number. If CPU-patched HF cannot be proven equivalent to the CUDA oracle (OQ-17), the target is **llama.cpp GGUF / ONNX Runtime / MLAS** and is LABELED that way.

**Fairness controls — ALL MANDATORY:**
- **Thread parity**: pin `OMP_NUM_THREADS` / torch `set_num_threads(N)` EQUAL to focr's thread budget; **NEVER benchmark torch at @64** — measure at @8/@32.
- **Allocator fairness**: build focr with the SAME allocator posture used for the claim (mimalloc behind a feature), wired into the MEASURED binary, not merely mentioned.
- **Best-of-N with warmup discard**; report the min and the precision of each side.
- **Precision annotation per row**: focr-int8 vs torch-bf16 (and torch-int8 if available) — a raw ratio across different numerics is meaningless without it.
- **PER-STAGE ratios** (preprocess / vision-encode / prefill / decode-per-token): decode-per-token faster than the Phase -1 proven CPU reference is the GATING part of G2; vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is acceptable and recorded honestly.

Also compute the **roofline** per stage (compute floor = int8/int4 GEMM FLOPs ÷ peak int8 throughput; memory floor = bytes streamed ÷ DRAM bandwidth; take the max) and record it next to every measured ratio in PERF_LEDGER — treat any stage >~1.3× above its floor as a named, attackable lever.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Honest ratio vs a REAL reference, not self-relative — a win only counts head-to-head (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE law).
- Per-stage because the cost center MOVES with output length (sparse page = vision-prefill-dominated; 40-page = decode-dominated) — a single end-to-end ratio hides the truth.
- Roofline target makes the perf goal falsifiable: the reranker lesson is the gap is 'kernels below peak,' so each stage's distance-from-floor is the lever list.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Benchmarks must SKIP gracefully (exit 0) if the model fixture is absent.
- A benchmark-guardrail gate catches regressions vs a frozen baseline.
- OQ-17 decides whether the reference is CPU-HF or llama.cpp/ONNX — label accordingly; G2 'beats the CPU reference' is honest only against whatever CPU reference is PROVEN to run.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `benches/gauntlet` produces per-stage honest reference/focr ratios with thread/allocator/precision/best-of-N fairness controls all applied.
- Each row labeled with the reference identity (CPU-HF proven-equivalent, or llama.cpp/ONNX) and precision annotation.
- Roofline floor recorded next to each ratio in PERF_LEDGER.
- Decode-per-token ratio > 1.0 (faster) vs the proven CPU reference on the primary arches (the gating G2 part); vision-prefill recorded honestly.
- Skips gracefully without the model.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A bench-guardrail test asserting no regression vs the frozen `.bench-history` baseline (cv_pct reported; >5% = noise, ineligible; MT8 attribution to a named ≥0.1% frame).
- A fairness-control test asserting thread parity and allocator posture are actually applied (not just configured).
- Structured logging per row (`{stage, focr_ms, ref_ms, ratio, ref_identity, precision, roofline_floor, tag}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (the Phase -1 proven CPU reference + pinned source), **OQ-17** (which reference is proven to run), and VERIFY-ledger-discipline (where the rows land). Feeds the performance pillar of VERIFY-three-pillar-cert. Consumed-by every P3 SIMD-kernel perf claim.

Focused non-build checks passed: rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check benches/gauntlet_harness.rs; git diff --check -- benches/gauntlet_harness.rs docs/gauntlet/BENCH_HARNESS.md; test ! -e reports/bench/latest.json. comments.text: Fresh-eyes gauntlet follow-up: closed a real head-to-head fairness hole in benches/gauntlet_harness.rs. The harness already pinned FOCR_THREADS plus OMP/TORCH/RAYON/MKL/OPENBLAS/VECLIB/BLIS/NUMEXPR thread env vars before shelling out to FOCR_REFERENCE_CMD, but it accepted a measured timing envelope without proving what thread count the reference actually used. comments.text:

title: Determinism gate + many_pages_without_deadlock watchdog + asupersync capacity certificate
description:
# Determinism gate + concurrency watchdog + capacity certificate

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Two failure classes are catastrophic and silent: nondeterministic output (same image → different parse, breaking reproducibility and every golden) and a concurrency deadlock under many pages (the frankensearch deadlock saga burned 5 commits). This bead is the gate that proves franken_ocr is deterministic and does not hang — both are release-blocking properties distinct from oracle parity (L4 is 'match the reference', this is 'match yourself / don't hang').

## WHAT TO DO
- **Determinism gate**: a separate gate asserting same-input-twice → byte-identical output under greedy (independent of the oracle; this is self-consistency).
- **`many_pages_without_deadlock` CI watchdog**: pages ≫ pool — the test HANGS on regression. This is the durable fix the frankensearch deadlock saga converged on: single `OcrModel` behind a cache; SEQUENTIAL outer page/document loop; each forward fans out across all cores internally via the kernel's OWN rayon pool (pinned to physical cores, ONE live forward at a time). NEVER nest rayon under a held lock; NEVER nest a second asupersync runtime inside a task.
- **asupersync capacity certificate** (gauntlet artifact): p95/p99 queueing evidence + a proof of NO nested-runtime / NO rayon-pool oversubscription (the USL pool caps of §6.9 / AF-5 hold under the `many_pages` soak); streaming uses BOUNDED channels (backpressure, never unbounded growth).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Determinism is a SEPARATE gate from L4: L4 matches the (possibly noisy) oracle over its reproducible prefix; the determinism gate demands WE are byte-identical to ourselves — a stronger, oracle-independent property.
- The watchdog HANGS (rather than fails fast) on regression because a deadlock IS a hang — a timeout wrapper turns the hang into a CI failure with the offending thread dump.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- 'Streaming per-page' = streaming the OUTPUT of a sequentially-processed page, NEVER concurrent forwards (which oversubscribe rayon N×).
- The capacity certificate must prove the USL pool caps (decode GEMV peak ~8–16 effective cores bandwidth-bound; not 64) hold — oversubscribing 64 cores is β-dominated slowdown.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Determinism gate green: same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog passes (pages ≫ pool, no hang) under a timeout wrapper.
- Capacity certificate produced: p95/p99 queueing + no-nested-runtime + no-oversubscription proof under the `many_pages` soak; bounded channels verified.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A determinism test running each golden image twice and asserting byte-identical output.
- The `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog (pages ≫ pool) under a wall-clock timeout.
- A capacity-certificate test emitting p95/p99 + the oversubscription/nesting proof as a structured artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on the token/decode path being runnable (L4). Consumed-by VERIFY-three-pillar-cert (determinism is a scorecard cell; the capacity certificate is a gauntlet artifact).

title: Tests: L0-L5 ladder integration runner + per-gate structured scorecard fixture
description:
# L0–L5 ladder integration runner + scorecard

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The individual ladder gates (L0..L5) need a single integration runner that executes them in order, short-circuits sensibly (a failed L0 makes L1+ meaningless), and emits one structured scorecard the release process and per-commit parity-receipt consume. This is the operational face of 'L0–L5 parity all green' that appears in every phase exit gate.

## WHAT TO DO
- An integration runner `tests/conformance_harness.rs` that executes L0→L5 in order over the golden corpus, recording per-gate pass/fail + the measured metric (exact/cosine/logit-diff/CER/TEDS).
- Emit a structured scorecard fixture (`{gate, granularity, tolerance, measured, pass}`) consumed by the parity-receipt every perf commit re-states.
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing any fallback at `/nonexistent`.
- The runner is the single entry the phase exit gates and the three-pillar conformance pillar call.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- A single ordered runner (not 6 independent tests) so the parity receipt is one artifact and a lower-gate failure is reported clearly rather than as 6 confusing failures.
- Structured scorecard so the conformal ratchet and the three-pillar cert can consume per-category results directly.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Must re-prove on EVERY perf commit (the §9.2 loop re-proves L0–L5 each lever) — keep it fast enough to run in the optimization loop.
- Skip-with-SUCCESS must still PROVE the native path ran (the `/nonexistent` fallback trick) so a silently-skipped suite is not mistaken for a pass.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- The runner executes L0–L5 over the corpus and emits the structured scorecard.
- Per-commit parity receipt derivable from the scorecard (e.g. 'text exact, max logit diff 0.05, deterministic').
- Skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; native-path-ran proof present.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the integration test bead — it composes L0..L5 and asserts the all-green condition, with full structured logging.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on all four ladder gates (VERIFY-ladder-l0, -l1-l2, -l3-l4, -l5). Consumed-by VERIFY-determinism-gate (needs the token/decode path), VERIFY-three-pillar-cert, and VERIFY-conformal-ratchet.

title: Oracle nondeterminism-floor characterization (run-twice / two-thread-counts envelope) — sets all downstream tolerances
description:
# Oracle nondeterminism-floor characterization

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The HF reference is FREQUENTLY non-deterministic across torch thread counts / BLAS reduction order at the logit-tie level (bf16 arithmetic, 129280-vocab argmax). If we set parity tolerances WITHOUT first measuring the oracle's own noise, we will chase phantom 'bugs' that are just the oracle disagreeing with itself — or, worse, set tolerances so loose that real drift hides inside them. This task is the keystone that makes every L1/L3/L4 tolerance defensible rather than guessed. It directly serves 'correctness before speed': a franken_ocr int8 divergence INSIDE the oracle's own bf16 noise is NOT a bug, and we must be able to prove that.

## WHAT TO DO
Before setting ANY tolerance:
- Run the oracle **twice** over the full golden corpus, and at **two thread counts** (e.g. `torch.set_num_threads(8)` and `(32)`), with `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)` where it does not error.
- Diff the runs and record the **nondeterminism envelope** as a committed fixture: per-token divergence rate, first-divergence position per document, and the per-logit max-abs spread at matching positions.
- Derive: (a) the **L4 'exact' prefix** = the longest decoded-token prefix the oracle reproduces identically across all runs/threads, per document; (b) the **L3 logit tolerance** = derived from the measured oracle logit variance (NOT the imported frankensearch `0.055`).
- Commit `tests/fixtures/oracle_nondeterminism_envelope.json` and a `tolerances.toml` consumed by the ladder gates.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- L4 exactness is defined ONLY over the reproducible prefix; beyond it, the oracle is non-deterministic and we cannot demand bit-exactness.
- The per-op ULP tolerance table (default 4 ULP f32 matmul, 2 ULP elementwise) is the L1/L2 comparator — but the END-TO-END logit/token tolerances are derived here from measured variance, not a hand-guessed epsilon.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- If the oracle is wildly non-deterministic (large divergence rate), that is itself a finding to ledger — it may mean greedy temperature=0.0 still hits ties; record and pick the longest stable prefix.
- Must be re-run if the pinned model source changes (CI-guarded against drift via the E-PM1 census).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `oracle_nondeterminism_envelope.json` committed with per-document divergence rate + first-divergence position + logit spread.
- `tolerances.toml` committed: L1/L2 ULP table, L3 logit tolerance (derived), L4 exact-prefix bounds — all sourced to measured numbers.
- A short note in `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` if the floor reveals any structural nondeterminism.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A regression test asserting the derived tolerances are present and internally consistent (L3 ≥ measured spread, L4 prefix ≤ document length).
- Structured logging of the envelope computation per document.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (needs the oracle to run twice/two-thread). This task BLOCKS all the L1–L5 ladder gates, which consume `tolerances.toml`.

title: Test-only PyO3/subprocess oracle bridge with deterministic-algorithms + per-op ULP tolerance table + EngineIdentity
description:
# Test-only oracle bridge (PyO3 / subprocess) — ML-System-class wiring

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Frozen `.npy` fixtures cover the committed golden corpus, but per-op differential testing (Pattern 1 of `/testing-conformance-harnesses`) is the gold standard and needs a LIVE bridge to the pinned reference for ad-hoc inputs. This bridge is what lets a kernel author diff a single op against the oracle without regenerating the whole fixture set. It serves correctness-before-speed by making oracle comparison cheap enough to run on every kernel change.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a **test-only** PyO3 or subprocess bridge to the pinned reference (`torch==2.10.0`, `transformers==4.57.1`).
- Force `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)`; capture the seeded RNG per call.
- Provide a **per-op ULP tolerance table** as the L1/L2 comparator: default **4 ULP for f32 matmul, 2 ULP for elementwise** — this is the comparator, NOT a hand-guessed epsilon.
- Define `EngineIdentity::{Subject, Oracle}` and assert them **distinct** so the oracle is never accidentally compared against itself.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **NEVER linked into the shipping `focr` inference binary** — gated behind a test-only feature/dev-dependency so G3's no-FFI runtime claim holds.
- subprocess is acceptable if PyO3 linkage is fragile across the 5 build targets; the bridge only needs to run on the dev/CI host with the reference env.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The bridge must not leak a torch runtime into the release artifact — a build test asserts `focr` links no Python.
- ULP comparisons must account for the oracle's measured nondeterminism floor (VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor): a 4-ULP table is the per-op default but end-to-end tolerances come from the measured envelope.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Bridge runs per-op diffs against the reference with the ULP table comparator.
- `EngineIdentity::Subject != Oracle` asserted in the harness.
- Build test proves `focr` (release) links no Python/torch.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A smoke test diffing a trivial op (e.g. RMSNorm on a fixed input) Subject-vs-Oracle within the ULP table.
- A linkage test asserting the shipping binary is FFI-free.
- Structured logging of every op-diff (`{op, max_ulp, within_tolerance}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (the reference env) and VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (tolerance derivation).

title: L0 preprocessing parity gate — resized/normalized/padded tensor + tile geometry, EXACT
description:
# L0 — preprocessing parity gate (EXACT)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L0 is the bottom rung of the parity ladder. If preprocessing diverges, every downstream stage is fed a different tensor and all higher gates become meaningless. Preprocessing is deterministic integer/float arithmetic with no quantization, so it MUST be exact. This gate is a prerequisite for P1 (fp32 parity).

## WHAT TO DO
Gate the preprocess front end output against the oracle's preprocessed tensor, **exact**:
- **Normalize**: `image_mean = image_std = [0.5,0.5,0.5]` → pixels scaled to [0,1] then `(x-0.5)/0.5` → **[-1,1]**, CHW layout.
- **Pad to square with gray (127,127,127)** = `tuple(int(m*255))`, aspect preserved (`ImageOps.pad` equivalent).
- **Tile geometry**: `patch_size=16`, `downsample_ratio=4`, `num_queries = ceil((image_size//patch_size)/downsample_ratio)`. Two modes: **Base** (1024×1024, `crop_mode=false`) and **Gundam** (`base_size=1024`, `image_size=640`, `crop_mode=true`, global padded 1024 view `global_view_pos='head'` + 640×640 local crops via `dynamic_preprocess()` / `find_closest_aspect_ratio()` over `i*j ∈ [min_num,max_num]`).
- Ratio selection (`find_closest_aspect_ratio`), gray pad value, and the [-1,1] normalize must all match exactly.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- EXACT tolerance (not cosine): preprocessing is reproducible arithmetic, any drift is a bug.
- The tile census (token counts per mode/aspect-ratio) is asserted against the E-PM1 machine-readable census, the source of truth.
