## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Gundam multi-tile token totals per aspect ratio are **[OPEN, OQ-18/OQ-7]** — this gate cannot finalize the Gundam geometry assertions until those are resolved.
- Bicubic pos-embed interpolation (for non-1024/tiled inputs) is a separate frankentorch GAP (f32 build) — L0 covers the resize/pad/normalize; the interp correctness is checked at L1/L2.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Base-mode preprocessed tensor exact-equal to the oracle's (bitwise on the f32 array).
- Gundam-mode tile geometry + per-tile token counts match the census once OQ-7/OQ-18 are resolved.
- Gray pad value, [-1,1] normalize, ratio selection all exact.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit tests per preprocess submodule (resize/pad/normalize/tile) with golden tensors.
- An integration test feeding the golden corpus through preprocess and diffing exact vs oracle, with structured per-image logging (`{image, mode, tile_count, max_abs_diff, exact}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden preprocessed tensors) and VERIFY-tokenizer-conformance (the prompt side of the input contract must be token-id-exact first, an L0 prerequisite). The geometry assertions consume the E-PM1 census and are gated on OQ-7/OQ-18.

title: L1 per-op + L2 per-layer parity gates — cosine ≈ 1.0, max-abs-diff ledgered
description:
# L1 (per-op) + L2 (per-layer) parity gates

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L1/L2 catch kernel drift at the finest granularity the oracle exposes. A correct end-to-end answer can hide a compensating pair of per-op bugs; per-op/per-layer cosine pins each kernel to the reference. These are the gates every kernel bead in P1/P2/P3 re-proves on each change — they are the operational form of 'every fused op must stay at peak AND correct.'

## WHAT TO DO
- **L1 per-op**: each kernel's output vs the oracle activation. Tolerance **cosine ≈ 1.0 (≥ 0.9999 in f32; documented int8 tolerance)**. Comparator uses the per-op ULP table (4 ULP f32 matmul / 2 ULP elementwise) for the bridge path.
- **L2 per-layer**: per decoder-layer hidden state (all 12) and per vision-stage output (post-patch-embed, post-SAM, post-bridge/compress, post-CLIP, post-projector, post-connector). Tolerance **cosine ≈ 1.0; max-abs-diff LEDGERED** per layer.
- Both consume the frozen `.npy` dumps from VERIFY-oracle-harness AND can use the live VERIFY-oracle-bridge for ad-hoc inputs.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Cosine ≈ 1.0 (not bit-exact) because f32-vs-bf16 and int8-vs-bf16 are continuous-value divergences within a documented tolerance.
- max-abs-diff is LEDGERED (recorded) per layer so a slow drift across layers is visible, not just the final cosine.
- A wrong SAM window size or windowed-block pos-embed scheme (OQ-15) surfaces ONLY as a fuzzy L2 cosine drop — L2 must therefore log per-stage cosine so this silent-corruption failure mode is caught.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- int8 tolerances must sit inside the oracle nondeterminism floor (VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor) — an int8 divergence inside the oracle's own bf16 noise is not a bug.
- The projector concat order (OQ-6) and SAM window (OQ-15) are [OPEN]; a wrong answer passes L0 but fails L2 — so L2 must run per-vision-stage, not just on the final hidden.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every implemented kernel passes L1 cosine ≥ 0.9999 (f32) / within documented int8 tolerance.
- All 12 decoder-layer hiddens + all 6 vision-stage outputs pass L2 cosine ≈ 1.0 with max-abs-diff ledgered.
- Per-stage cosine logged so OQ-15/OQ-6 misimplementations are caught.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Per-op unit tests (one per kernel) diffing against the frozen oracle activation.
- A per-layer integration test producing a structured table (`{stage, cosine, max_abs_diff}`) for the full forward, committed as a golden-fuzzy artifact.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (per-layer dumps), VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (tolerance derivation), VERIFY-oracle-bridge (live per-op diffs). Consumed-by every P1/P2/P3 kernel bead.

title: L3 logits + L4 token parity gates — logits within derived tolerance + argmax match; decoded tokens EXACT on reproducible prefix
description:
# L3 (logits) + L4 (token) parity gates

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L3/L4 are where quantization risk is actually adjudicated. L3 bounds the continuous logit divergence; L4 demands the discrete decoded token sequence be exact where the reference is deterministic. This is the gate that says 'int8/int4 did not change what the model says.' It is the heart of G1 for the decoder and the gate P2/P4 must clear.

## WHAT TO DO
- **L3 logits**: pre-sampling logits within the int8/int4 quant tolerance DERIVED from the oracle nondeterminism floor (NOT the imported frankensearch `max_diff ~0.055` — that is a precedent value, the real number comes from VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor). **argmax MUST match** at every position where the reference is deterministic.
- **L4 token**: the decoded token sequence is **EXACT under greedy where the reference is deterministic** — and 'where deterministic' is defined ONLY over the reproducible prefix established by VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor.
- Replicate the exact sampler semantics: greedy / low-temp argmax, strip trailing EOS `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`, and `no_repeat_ngram_size=35` with a sliding `ngram_window` that DIFFERS by mode — **128 (single-image) vs 1024 (multi-image)** (these are first-class generation semantics; the `SlidingWindowNoRepeatNgramProcessor` bans repeated 35-grams within the window).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Discrete output bit-exact, continuous (logits) only within documented tolerance — the frankensearch `parity_logits_and_ranking_match_reference` shape.
- A separate determinism gate (VERIFY-determinism-gate) asserts same-input-twice → identical output; L4 is about matching the ORACLE, the determinism gate is about matching OURSELVES.
- The ngram window per mode is a generation semantic, not a CLI flag — the sampler must replicate the exact window or L4 diverges on repetition-prone content.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The 129280-vocab argmax has logit ties at the bf16 level; argmax-match is only demanded where the oracle itself is stable (the reproducible prefix).
- int8 on `lm_head` (P2 stage 2c) and int8 attention (2b) go BEYOND the validated NVFP4/GGUF set — L3/L4 are the gate that retires that risk (OQ-14); a regression reverts just that stage via its kill-switch (`FOCR_INT8_LMHEAD`, `FOCR_INT8_ATTN`).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- L3: logits within the derived tolerance; argmax matches on every deterministic position.
- L4: decoded tokens EXACT over the reproducible prefix under greedy.
- Every perf commit re-states its parity receipt (e.g. 'text exact, max logit diff 0.05, deterministic').

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A logits diff test producing `{position, max_logit_diff, argmax_match}` per token.
- A token-exact test over the reproducible prefix per document, structured-logged.
- An ngram-window test asserting the per-mode window (128 single / 1024 multi) bans the right 35-grams.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden logits/tokens) and VERIFY-nondeterminism-floor (derives the L3 tolerance and the L4 reproducible prefix). Consumed-by P2/P4 quant beads.
close_reason: Done (design/harness wave, committed): see docs/alien|gauntlet|conformance|testing + scripts/af*.py

title: L5 end-to-end OCR parity gate — decoded text + bbox on golden corpus; CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within documented budget
description:
# L5 — end-to-end OCR parity gate

## BACKGROUND / WHY
L5 is the top of the ladder and the only gate a non-expert can read: does franken_ocr produce the same document parse as the reference? It is the operational definition of G1. The lower gates can all be green and L5 still catch a postprocess bug (tag parsing, bbox rescale, markdown assembly). L5 is the gate that releases depend on.

## WHAT TO DO
- Compare decoded text + bbox tags on the frozen golden corpus.
- **exact-match where the reference is deterministic** (the reproducible-prefix discipline).
- Aggregate **CER / TEDS / Formula-CDM within a documented quantization budget**: target **int8 within noise** of bf16 (NVFP4's 'identical to BF16' as the bar); **int4 within a small ledgered budget**, with **no catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content**.
- Postprocess parity: EOS strip (`<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`), `<|ref|>…<|/ref|><|det|>…<|/det|>` regex parse, bbox coords `/999 → ×W/H` rescale, markdown assembly (HTML tables, LaTeX formulas, `<page>` separators for multi-page).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM are aggregate metrics with a DOCUMENTED budget, not exact — quantization is allowed a measured, ledgered tolerance, but exact-token content (dense numbers, tables, code, sub/superscripts) is held to a tighter tail bound (see VERIFY-tail-risk / AF-2).
- The metrics: CER (character error rate), TEDS / TEDS-S (table structure), Formula-CDM (LaTeX) — the reference's own reported strengths (printed text Text-Edit 0.038, tables TEDS 90.93, math Formula-CDM 92.61).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Multi-page is CROSS-PAGE DEPENDENT under R-SWA (OQ-13) — L5 multi-page comparison must be against the multi-page reference output, NEVER a sum of single-page parses.
- The full prompt-mode taxonomy (free OCR / layout / grounding / markdown) and whether bboxes are emitted in all modes is [OPEN, OQ-8] — L5 bbox assertions are gated on that.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Decoded text exact where deterministic; aggregate CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within the ledgered budget (int8 within noise, int4 within small budget).
- No catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content (checked via the tail-risk monitor).
- Postprocess (EOS, tag regex, bbox rescale, markdown) matches the reference.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- End-to-end test over the golden corpus producing a structured scorecard (`{doc, CER, TEDS, Formula_CDM, text_exact}`).
- A postprocess unit suite (tag regex, bbox /999 rescale, EOS strip).
- Model-gated: skip-with-SUCCESS without weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-harness (golden e2e outputs), VERIFY-ladder-l3-l4 (tokens must match before text can), and VERIFY-golden-artifacts (the frozen `focr ocr --json` snapshots). Multi-page assertions gated on OQ-13; bbox-mode assertions on OQ-8.

title: Tokenizer conformance (OQ-16) — token-id-exact byte-level-BPE vs LlamaTokenizerFast, the L0/L4 prerequisite
description:
# Tokenizer conformance (OQ-16)

## BACKGROUND / WHY
The tokenizer is the single highest-leverage correctness prerequisite: **a wrong token id corrupts EVERY downstream gate** (L0 input, L4 output, and everything between). The plan calls this out explicitly as an L0/L4 prerequisite that must pass before any decoder parity work. franken_ocr ships a pure-Rust byte-level BPE over `tokenizer.json` (no SentencePiece) and must be token-id-exact against `LlamaTokenizerFast`.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a dedicated exact-match conformance corpus: diverse Unicode, CJK, math, code, the DeepSeek glyph specials, and the structural specials `<image>` / `<|ref|>` / `<|det|>`.
- Round-trip `encode` → `decode` against `LlamaTokenizerFast` over `tokenizer.json` (9.98 MB, vocab **129280**, ~512 special tokens). [VERIFIED]: `LlamaTokenizerFast`, byte-level BPE; bos/eos/pad = 0/1/2; `add_special_token=false`, `image_token='<image>'`, `ignore_id=-100`.
- Verify: pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token handling, DeepSeek-glyph handling (e.g. `<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>`).
- **Freeze the reference id sequences as golden fixtures.**

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Token-id-EXACT (not fuzzy) — there is no tolerance for tokenization; an off-by-one id is a different model input.
- Pure-Rust HF-tokenizers-JSON path, no SentencePiece dependency (G3 single static binary).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The pre-tokenizer regex and byte-fallback are the classic divergence sources between a hand-rolled BPE and `LlamaTokenizerFast`; the corpus must exercise multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries, combining characters, and the DeepSeek glyph specials.
- OQ-16 (the exact byte-level-BPE encode/decode semantics: pre-tokenizer regex, byte-fallback, special-token + glyph handling) must be resolved against the pinned `tokenizer.json` first.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- 100% token-id-exact encode AND decode round-trip vs `LlamaTokenizerFast` over the full conformance corpus.
- Frozen reference id sequences committed as golden fixtures.
- All structural specials (`<image>`, `<|ref|>`, `<|det|>`) and DeepSeek glyphs handled exactly.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A conformance test iterating the corpus, asserting `encode(text) == reference_ids` and `decode(ids) == text` for round-trippable inputs, structured-logged (`{case, len, mismatch_pos|none}`).
- Edge-case unit tests: empty string, lone surrogate-prohibited UTF-8, special-token-in-text, CJK runs, LaTeX, code with whitespace.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-PM1** (pinned `tokenizer.json`) and **OQ-16** (the exact BPE semantics). This gate BLOCKS L0 and L4 (and therefore all decoder parity) — it is on the Phase-1 critical path.
close_reason: Done (batch-verified: cargo check green / py_compile / sh -n): tokenizer conformance corpus + generator

title: Differential test suite — our path vs oracle, per-op + end-to-end, on golden corpus + community quant artifacts
description:
# Differential test suite

## BACKGROUND / WHY
Differential testing (`/testing-conformance-harnesses` Pattern 1) is the gold-standard conformance method: run our path and the oracle on the same inputs and diff. It catches bugs that golden snapshots miss (because golden snapshots only cover the frozen corpus, while differential testing can run on ANY input via the live bridge). It is how we prove 'same answer as the bf16 reference' for arbitrary inputs.

## WHAT TO DO
- Build a differential harness diffing our path vs the oracle, **per-op AND end-to-end**, on: (a) the golden corpus, and (b) the **community quant artifacts** (NVFP4/GGUF per-layer activation dumps as secondary oracles).
- Per-op diffs go through the per-op ULP tolerance table (4 ULP f32 matmul / 2 ULP elementwise); end-to-end diffs go through the L3/L4/L5 tolerances.
- Wire into the `ConformanceTest` trait as `category=differential` tests.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Use the community NVFP4/GGUF dumps as SECONDARY oracles during bring-up — they confirm our quant path independently of the bf16 oracle, and the NVFP4 author reports 'OCR identical to BF16.'
- Differential testing is the gold standard for the ConformanceTest coverage matrix; intentional divergences become XFAIL (a `DISC-NNN`), never SKIP.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- NVFP4 quantizes only ~2196 linear modules and the exact set is [OPEN, OQ-14] — when using it as a secondary oracle, only compare the modules NVFP4 actually quantizes (its scale-key set), or the diff is apples-to-oranges.
- The bridge is test-only; differential e2e runs need the reference env (model-gated, skip-with-SUCCESS otherwise).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Per-op and e2e differential tests green against the bf16 oracle within tolerance on the golden corpus.
- Secondary-oracle (NVFP4/GGUF) diffs green on the modules they cover.
- Any intentional divergence recorded as an XFAIL + `DISC-NNN`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The differential harness itself, emitting structured per-test rows (`{op|e2e, oracle, max_diff, within_tol, xfail?}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on VERIFY-oracle-bridge (live diffs), VERIFY-ladder-l1-l2 (the tolerance comparators), and VERIFY-conformance-trait (the trait it registers under). OQ-14 gates the NVFP4 secondary-oracle comparison set.

title: Reject malformed SAM absolute-position embeddings before forward mutation
description:
## Background / why
Fresh-eyes audit found that vision_sam::abs_pos returns a raw Vec without validating the pos_embed backing length or checked output geometry. On the same-grid path a short/long pos_embed is silently zip-applied against the patch tokens, and on interpolation paths malformed buffers can panic during bicubic sampling.

## Scope
- Make SAM abs-pos construction return FocrResult and validate src/tgt geometry plus pos_embed length before use.
- Ensure forward_with rejects malformed pos_embed before mutating token activations.
- Add focused regressions for short same-grid pos_embed and overflow/invalid geometry.

## Acceptance
- Malformed SAM pos_embed returns actionable FocrError instead of panic or silent partial application.
- Valid same-grid and interpolation behavior remains unchanged.
- Focused vision_sam tests, cargo gates, and UBS pass.
close_reason: Fixed: vision_sam::abs_pos now returns FocrResult, rejects zero/overflow geometry, validates pos_embed len == src_h*src_w*dim before same-grid passthrough or bicubic interpolation, and forward_with now fails before applying malformed pos embeddings. Added regressions for same-grid/interpolation malformed source length and invalid/overflow geometry. Verification: cargo +nightly fmt --check passed; focused env CARGO_HOME=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/franken-ocr-cargo-target TMPDIR=/Volumes/USBNVME16TB/temp_agent_space/tmp CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo +nightly test native_engine::vision_sam --lib passed (32 passed, 1 ignored); scripts/check.sh under the same scratch env passed ledger/provenance checks, cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test (562 lib tests plus integration/doc tests passed; dependency-only frankensqlite unused_features warnings observed). The final script ubs --diff was interrupted after it spent several minutes tarring existing target-codex-verify/debug/deps outside its default target ignore; scoped ubs --only=rust src/native_engine/vision_sam.rs passed with exit 0 and no critical findings (warnings were existing test unwrap/assert/style inventories).

title: Phase 4 integration: end-to-end int4 forward meets all exit gates
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
This is the Phase-4 acceptance integration: it runs the full int4 forward (converter → int4 kernels → AF-1-allocated tiers → tail gate) on the golden corpus and asserts every §10 Phase-4 exit gate together, with comprehensive structured logging — the project mandate for a model-gated e2e proof that the whole int4 path works, not just its parts.

## WHAT TO DO
A model-gated e2e test (skip-with-SUCCESS without the 6.67 GB weights; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent`) that:
- Converts to int4 (AF-1-allocated tiers + uniform-fallback both exercised), runs the full forward on the dense-numeric/table/code corpus.
- Asserts the **Phase-4 exit gates (§10):** int4 CER within the ledgered budget (NOT below the 4-bit cliff, Q4_K_M-class); CVaR_0.1 within budget (AF-2); size ≈ 1.8-2.9 GB **demonstrated** (P4-footprint-arithmetic); no catastrophic regression on dense numeric/table content; every quant choice ledgered with measured impact.
- Asserts the **parity ladder still green at int4** (§8.2): L3 logits within int4 tolerance, L4 token exact where the reference is deterministic, L5 CER/TEDS/Formula-CDM within budget; bit-identical SIMD-vs-scalar on the int4 kernel.
- Asserts the **decode bandwidth win** is realized (P4-int4-gemm-bench ratios cited) WITHOUT a CER regression (G1 > G2: a faster int4 kernel that drifts OCR is reverted).
- Asserts **determinism** (§7.3): same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- **Structured NDJSON logging** of every gate: per-config CER/CVaR/EVT/TEDS, realized footprint, decode bytes-streamed, bit-identical result, first-divergence token, kill-switch states.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
All Phase-4 exit gates pass together on the golden corpus; parity ladder green at int4; bandwidth win realized with no CER regression; deterministic; every quant choice ledgered. The test skips-green without weights.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the integration/e2e bead. It composes the unit/parity/bench beads above into the phase acceptance proof.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on every Phase-4 deliverable: `P4-int4-gemm` (the kernel), `P4-af1-bit-allocator` (the allocation), `P4-expected-loss-per-layer` (the tiering), `P4-tail-risk-gate` (the gate), `P4-footprint-arithmetic` (the demonstrated size), `P4-dense-corpus` (the measurement substrate). It is the convergence point that proves the epic.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## REVIEW-2 ADDENDUM — int4 changes the perf model; re-measure pool sizing + NUMA; assert load path
- **Roofline shifts at int4 (§9.1).** int4 halves the decode weight stream, which RAISES the compute:load ratio. Re-measure the decode roofline at int4 (P4-int4-gemm-bench) — a stage that was memory-bound at int8 may sit differently at int4; flag any stage >~1.3x above its (new) floor as a named lever. Assert the bandwidth win is realized on the int4-tensor portion specifically, not diluted by the unchanged bf16/int8 bulk.
- **USL pool sizing (AF-5, §6.9) re-check.** The decode USL peak (α/β fit) was measured at int8 in Phase 3; int4's smaller per-token byte stream can move the peak. The e2e should at least RECORD whether the Phase-3 `pool_sizing` table still holds at int4 (a full refit is a perf bead, but a regression check belongs in the convergence proof).
- **NUMA blob shrink (§6.9).** int4 experts shrink the per-NUMA-node replicated weight footprint; assert replication/first-touch still holds for the int4 `.focrq` and the smaller blob does not change correctness.
- **Assert the full load path ran (P4-r2-int4-loader).** The e2e must prove the int4 loader's census + arch_target handling executed (point fallbacks at `/nonexistent`), not just the kernel — a stale-weight or packing-mismatch must surface here as a loud failure, never silent drift.

title: focr ocr -o/--output FILE: write .md (markdown) or .json (with bounding boxes) to a file natively (no stdout redirect needed)
close_reason: Fixed: default model resolver now probes quant-suffixed names (unlimited-ocr.int8.focrq/.int4.focrq) so a pulled model resolves with no --model (bd-3u6x); focr ocr -o/--output FILE writes .md or .json-with-bounding-boxes natively (bd-sreb). Engine recognize_with_layout entry points + cli output layer + unit/e2e tests + README/installer/help docs.

title: Hardening: OcrModel decode loop must reject empty decoder hidden rows before last-row slice
description:
## Problem
Fresh-eyes scan found `OcrModel::generate` in `src/native_engine/mod.rs` still uses `debug_assert!(hidden.rows >= 1)` immediately before slicing `hidden.row(hidden.rows - 1)` for the lm_head last-row optimization. Valid decoder output should always have at least one row, but malformed/future decoder wiring or a bad `.focrq` accessor would panic in release via usize underflow/out-of-bounds instead of returning a typed `FocrError`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Replace the debug-only assertion with a production checked helper that returns `FocrError::Other` when decoder hidden has zero rows.
- Keep the last-row optimization and valid shape behavior unchanged.
- Add a focused test for the empty-hidden rejection and last-row extraction behavior without fabricating model weights.
- Run focused native_engine/mod tests plus the standard local gates used for these hardening beads.

## Coordination
WhiteCave owns `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `src/native_engine/mod.rs` for this narrow slice. Avoid tokenizer (`bd-re8.8`) and active gauntlet/CI lanes.
close_reason: Implemented OcrModel decode-loop hardening in src/native_engine/mod.rs. Replaced the debug-only hidden.rows assertion before the lm_head last-row slice with OcrModel::last_hidden_row, which returns FocrError::Other when decoder::forward produces zero hidden rows and preserves the existing [1, hidden] final-row slice for valid outputs. Added focused tests for empty hidden rejection without panic and valid final-row extraction. Verification: rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 --check src/native_engine/mod.rs; cargo +nightly fmt --check; git diff --check -- .beads/issues.jsonl src/native_engine/mod.rs; br dep cycles --json; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex-verify cargo +nightly --config 'patch.crates-io.asupersync.path="/dp/asupersync"' test native_engine::tests::last_hidden_row --lib -- --nocapture; same config check --all-targets; same config clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; same config test (445 passed, 1 ignored; integration/doc tests green); python3 scripts/check_ledgers.py; timeout 240s ubs --diff --jobs=1 --quiet --ci . (0 findings).

title: Wire batched vision into recognize_batch_spine: compose forward_with_batched (SAM+CLIP) + group views by side, replace per-page vision_tower loop; batch vision across P admitted pages (defeat the Amdahl wall, cross-page)
Perf: native packed-int4 dot (no int8 materialization) before ANY int4 decode use
description: MEASURED 2026-06-26 (docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md bd-int4-lmhead): int4 lm_head regressed 5.8x (lm_head phase 2130->12278ms) because simd::int4::igemm_s4s8 UNPACKS nibbles to an int8 buffer in memory before the SDOT -> ~2.5x int8 memory traffic (read 0.5B packed + write 1B int8 + read 1B int8) vs int8's 1B. int4 is strictly slower than int8 for every decode GEMV with this kernel. To ever profit from int4's halved footprint at runtime, build a NATIVE packed-int4 dot: load packed bytes, unpack nibbles IN-REGISTER (NEON vshr/vand or AVX2), feed straight into SDOT/SMMLA accumulators, NEVER materialize an int8 buffer in memory. Unsafe SIMD island + bit-identical scalar fallback (doctrine #3,#6). NOTE low ROI: lm_head is ~14% of decode (perfect int4 <=~4% of a page); experts(45%)+attn(37%) are overhead/f32-bound where a bytes lever doesn't apply. Backlog until a higher-ROI lever is exhausted.
close_reason: Duplicate of bd-2ela (in-register int4->int8 unpack kernel). Measured evidence lives in docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md bd-int4-lmhead: current simd::int4::igemm_s4s8 unpacks to a MEMORY buffer (not in-register) -> int4 lm_head regressed 5.8x. bd-2ela IS the fix; bd-vm88 IS the roofline bench (answer: int4 loses with the memory-materializing kernel; decode is overhead/f32-bound, not bandwidth-bound, so int4 ceiling is ~4% of a page even done right).

title: Bench: int4 vs int8 decode GEMV bandwidth + throughput, roofline check
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
int4's entire thesis is **bandwidth**, so the win must be MEASURED, not assumed (NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE doctrine: a win only counts with a head-to-head measured ratio + correctness proof). This bench quantifies the decode bandwidth/throughput win on the expert-MLP shape and checks it against the memory roofline.

## WHAT TO DO
- Criterion bench over the expert SiLU-gated MLP decode GEMV (1280↔896 ×3) and the lm_head GEMV (1280→129280), comparing int4 vs int8 weights at decode (single-token) on each arch tier (SMMLA/SDOT, VNNI/AVX2).
- **Bytes-streamed counter:** assert int4 streams ~half the weight bytes of int8 on the int4-tensor portion.
- **Roofline (§9.1):** compute the memory floor (bytes streamed ÷ DRAM bandwidth); report how close int4 decode sits to it; flag any stage >~1.3x above its floor as a named lever.
- **Fairness controls (§9.3):** best-of-N, fixed thread/allocator/precision; same git state / same `target/` / same minute; `cv_pct` reported (>5% = noise, ineligible); MT8 attribution (the win names a specific ≥0.1% frame).
- Benches skip-green (exit 0) if the model fixture is absent.
- Write the honest ratio + roofline numbers to `PERF_LEDGER.md` with the artifact-graph fields (claim_id, evidence_id, model source commit + fixture hash, CPU feature string, exact command/env, kill-switch state).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- int4 decode GEMV streams ≤ ~half the int8 weight bytes (measured); decode throughput improves with int4 (memory-bound win, §12); ratio + roofline ledgered; correctness proof (P4-int4-gemm-tests) cited alongside the perf claim.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This IS the bench bead (criterion). Pairs with P4-int4-gemm-tests for the correctness half of the keep-gate.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- `P4-int4-gemm`: benches the kernel it depends on.

title: Phase 5 — CLI hardening + cross-platform release (production single binary, agent-ergonomics maxed)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Phase 5 is the productionization phase of franken_ocr — the pure-Rust, CPU-hyper-optimized runner for Baidu Unlimited-OCR (DeepSeek-OCR-derived 3B MoE VLM with R-SWA). The kernels and quant recipe (Phases 1–4) are done; Phase 5 turns the working engine into a **shippable, single static binary per target** that an AI agent (the primary user, per G5) can drive with zero context, and certifies the whole thing honest via the three-pillar gauntlet.

The over-arching goals this epic serves (plan §1.1):
- **G3 Pure-Rust single static binary, cross-platform**: one `focr` binary per target (linux x86-64/arm64, darwin x86-64/arm64, windows-msvc x86-64), no Python, no FFI, no network at inference time, no GPU required.
- **G5 Agent-ergonomic**: `robot` subcommand emitting versioned NDJSON with a self-describing `robot schema`, stable exit codes, `--json` everywhere, deterministic output under fixed sampling.
- **G6 Embeddable**: library API stays synchronous/blocking; runtime is an owned detail.
- **G7 Honest**: every accepted divergence ledgered; the release-readiness scorecard must be all-green before ship.

## WHAT TO DO (the Phase 5 task set, plan §10 Phase 5)
Goals: production single-binary release, agent-ergonomics maxed. Tasks:
1. Finalize subcommands / exit codes / env overrides (§7.2, §7.4, §7.5).
2. Robot contract tests + frozen schema (§7.3, AGENTS Agent-Ergonomics Requirements).
3. `doctor` — idempotent, reversible, capability reflection (§7.2, world-class-doctor-mode skill).
4. Per-page streaming polish (mpsc + std::thread, bounded sync_channel, recv_timeout ~40ms loop; §3.3, §7.3).
5. 5-target release build + `install.sh` + SHA-256 checksums (§7.6, /release-preparations, /installer-workmanship).
6. Agent-ergonomics audit (/agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools).
7. Release-readiness scorecard (§8.4).
8. Run the three-pillar gauntlet to convergence (§8.5) and produce the certification bundle (cross-ref E-VERIFY).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Two `[[bin]]` (`focr` short + `franken_ocr` long), both → `src/main.rs`; `fn main()` is SYNCHRONOUS (clap parse → install ctrlc ShutdownController → sync `run(cli)` → exit-code mapping). The asupersync runtime is owned by `OcrEngine` below main (§3.3, AGENTS doctrine #9). This is the franken_whisper template, proven.
- Robot mode is line-oriented NDJSON with a `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` const on every line; human decoration NEVER mixed with machine output in robot mode (AGENTS).
- **v1 is IMAGE-ONLY** — PDF stays off the CLI surface (§7.7): a pure-Rust raster at byte-parity with pymupdf@300DPI is unscoped and any pixel mismatch blows the L0 preprocessing parity gate.
- The release build has a **real gotcha**: franken_ocr uses local `../` path deps (ft-kernel-cpu, ft-core, ft-serialize, asupersync, fsqlite). Path deps break standard GH-Actions CI (release-preparations OP-6) — the 5-target build likely needs Path B (dsr/rch local build) or vendoring. Plan for this.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The gauntlet is the conscience: no victory-on-one-pillar-while-another-regresses; release cannot ship with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence (§8.4, §8.5).
- Cross-compile/nightly: nightly required for stdarch i8mm/dotprod, scalar fallback must cross-compile to every target; runtime SIMD dispatch → one binary per arch.
- Determinism gate: same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy; this is a hard gate for robot mode.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 5 exit gates, plan §10)
- All 5 targets build + smoke-test.
- Robot schema stable + self-describing (`robot schema` self-describes the contract; contract test green).
- Release-readiness scorecard all-green (L0–L5 parity, surface parity, honest perf, determinism, build matrix, ledger completeness).
- `install.sh` verified (curl-pipe install, checksum-verified, idempotent).
- Agent-ergonomics score above bar.
- Three-pillar gauntlet converged (≥10 rounds, ≥2 clean) and certification bundle produced.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4** (int4 refinement — the final quant tier must be locked so the release ships the production weights and the scorecard certifies the shipped numerics) and **E-VERIFY** (the verification gauntlet/conformance machinery — Phase 5 RUNS that machinery to convergence and packages its certification bundle; we do not rebuild it). Each child task carries its own finer-grained deps.

title: Finalize CLI subcommands, exit codes, and env overrides (the stable surface)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The `focr` CLI is the agent-facing contract (G5). Before we can contract-test the robot schema, audit ergonomics, or certify the release, the **command surface, exit-code dictionary, and env-override set must be frozen and complete**. This is the foundational task all other Phase-5 work nests on. Earlier phases shipped stubs (`convert`/`ocr`/`doctor` in Phase 0); Phase 5 finalizes them.

## WHAT TO DO
Finalize the exact subcommand table (plan §7.2), wired in `src/cli.rs` (clap-derive `Cli`/`Command`/`Args`, `to_request()` validation) and dispatched from sync `src/main.rs`:

| Subcommand | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `focr ocr <image>` | Parse a document IMAGE → markdown (human default) or `--json`. v1 IMAGE-ONLY. |
| `focr ocr --robot` (or `focr robot run`) | Streaming NDJSON events for agents |
| `focr convert <safetensors> -o <.focrq> [--arch ...] [--quant int8\|int4]` | Offline weight transformation |
| `focr robot schema` | Self-describing event/contract schema (versioned) |
| `focr robot health` | Diagnostics: model present? arch features? threads? |
| `focr robot backends` | Detected SIMD tiers (SMMLA/SDOT/VNNI/AMX/scalar), core count |
| `focr runs [--id\|--limit\|--format plain\|json\|ndjson]` | Query run history (fsqlite) |
| `focr sync export-jsonl\|import-jsonl` | Audit export/import (locked, atomic, one-way) |
| `focr doctor` | Idempotent self-check/repair |

**Exit codes (stable, documented in `src/error.rs`; plan §7.4):** `0` success · `1` generic error · `2` usage/CLI error · `3` model not found / not resolvable · `4` input decode error (bad image/PDF) · `5` budget/timeout exceeded · `6` cancelled (Ctrl+C) · `7` format/version mismatch (`.focrq`). The `robot run_error` event MUST carry the same code in its payload.

**Env overrides (read once via `OnceLock`; plan §7.5):** `FOCR_MODEL_DIR`, `FOCR_THREADS`, `FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS`, `FOCR_QUANT` (int8/int4/f32 select if multiple `.focrq` present), `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH` (override SIMD dispatch for testing), `FOCR_NO_REPEAT_NGRAM` (default 35). Each numerics-affecting lever is documented in its own doc-comment, defaulted by a measured A/B, and has an opt-out.

**Model resolution (`resolve_model(spec)`):** existing path OR short name searched across an ordered, env-driven list (`$FOCR_MODEL_DIR`, `~/.cache/franken_ocr/models`, …), failing with an **actionable** error listing every searched dir. `native_model_available()` does a cheap header sniff (magic bytes), no tensor load.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Exit `1` is NEVER used to mean "ran fine, no results" — that is exit `0` with an empty JSON array (agent-ergonomics Axiom 5). An agent must be able to write `case $? in 3) ...; 5) ...; esac` deterministically.
- `pdf` does NOT appear in the CLI surface in v1 (§7.7) — accepting it would over-promise; PDFs are rasterized out-of-band by the user.
- Every numerics-affecting env var is a documented, measured, opt-out-able lever (no silent numerics changes — G7).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Backwards-compat: we are pre-users with NO tech debt allowed (AGENTS) — finalize the RIGHT surface now, no compatibility shims.
- Stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics (Axiom 4) — no log line may leak into `--json`/NDJSON stdout. Honor `NO_COLOR`/non-TTY for any human decoration.
- `--version` must embed the MIT "Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu" license notice (license risk mitigation, §11).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every subcommand in the table is wired, parses, and returns the correct documented exit code on each error class.
- `error.rs` defines the exit-code enum; a unit test asserts the numeric value of each variant matches §7.4.
- All 6 env vars resolve through a single `OnceLock`-backed config path with a doc-comment each.
- `resolve_model` failure lists every searched directory verbatim in the error.
- `pdf` is absent from `--help` output.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: exit-code numeric stability test (asserts 0..7 mapping); env-override parsing (valid/invalid/unset → default); `resolve_model` happy/missing/ambiguous paths with the actionable-error assertion.
- Integration (P5-subcommands-tests): drive each subcommand via the built binary with structured logging of (argv, exit code, stdout/stderr split), asserting the exit-code contract end-to-end.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4**: the final `--quant int4` path and the `.focrq` format-version must be locked so the `convert` subcommand and `FOCR_QUANT`/exit-7 (format/version mismatch) reflect the shipped reality.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## ADDENDUM (review2): per-stage budget enumeration, OnceLock read-once contract, runs/sync handoff
- **`FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS` is a FAMILY, not one var — enumerate the closed set against §4.2.** The nine pipeline stages are: `load`/`preprocess`/`tokenize`/`vision_encode`/`connector`/`prefill`/`decode`/`postprocess`/`emit`. Document each `FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS` lever explicitly so an agent can discover the full set (e.g. via `doctor capabilities --json` / `robot health`), and add a unit test that every §4.2 `PipelineStage` variant has a corresponding parseable budget env var (no stage silently un-budgetable). An unknown stage name in the env var must fail with an actionable error listing the valid stage names.
- **OnceLock read-once is a CONTRACT, test it.** Every numerics-/budget-affecting env var is read EXACTLY ONCE via `OnceLock` at startup — no mid-run re-read (so a streaming run cannot change behavior partway). Add a test asserting a var mutated after first read does not change in-flight behavior. This is what makes determinism (§7.3) and the robot contract stable.
- **`runs` + `sync` finalization moved to P5-r2-runs-sync-surface.** This bead's subcommand TABLE still lists `focr runs` and `focr sync export-jsonl|import-jsonl` as part of the frozen surface, but their detailed behavior, output schema, `_meta` versioned migrations, and locked/atomic/one-way semantics + tests are owned by the new bead **P5-r2-runs-sync-surface** (depends on this one). If `sync` lock-contention needs an exit code beyond the §7.4 dictionary, DECIDE it here and update §7.4 deliberately (no ad-hoc codes).

title: Integration tests for CLI subcommand surface + exit-code contract
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The exit-code dictionary and subcommand surface are the part of the contract agents branch on. A regression here silently breaks every downstream agent script, so it needs an end-to-end test that drives the actual built binary (not just the parser), with the comprehensive structured logging this project mandates.

## WHAT TO DO
In `tests/` add a CLI-surface integration test that, for each subcommand and each documented error class, invokes the compiled `focr` binary and asserts:
- the exit code matches §7.4 (`0`/`1`/`2`/`3`/`4`/`5`/`6`/`7`),
- stdout is pure data (parses as JSON when `--json`/robot; no diagnostic lines),
- stderr carries diagnostics only,
- the actionable model-not-found error (exit `3`) lists every searched dir.
Emit one NDJSON log line per case `{case, argv, exit_code, stdout_bytes, stderr_first_line, pass}` so failures are diagnosable from logs alone.

Cover at minimum: `ocr` (missing image → 4; missing model → 3), `convert` (bad/old format → 7), usage error (unknown flag → 2), `robot schema`/`health`/`backends` (→ 0, valid JSON), `doctor` (→ 0 healthy), cancellation (SIGINT → 6, via a budgeted long run), budget exceeded (`FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_*_MS=1` → 5).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Model-gated: cases needing the 6.67 GB weights skip-with-SUCCESS when absent; prove the native path ran by pointing fallbacks at `/nonexistent` (AGENTS Testing Policy).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every documented exit code is exercised by at least one case and asserted exact.
- stdout/stderr discipline asserted for every `--json`/robot case.
- Test is green in CI without weights (model-gated cases skip-green).

## TESTS REQUIRED
This bead IS the test deliverable. It must produce the per-case NDJSON log and a summary line `{total, passed, skipped}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env** — it tests exactly that surface.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## ADDENDUM (review2): golden CLI help + --version license snapshot; runs/sync coverage handoff
- **EXACT golden snapshot for `--help` and `--version` (§8.6).** §8.6 mandates the EXACT (insta-style) golden pattern for CLI help text. Add a golden snapshot test that freezes the top-level `focr --help` and each subcommand `--help`, and asserts `focr --version` embeds the MIT **"Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu"** license notice (the §11 license-risk mitigation; this is the only place the notice is contract-tested). Canonicalize line endings/paths so the snapshot is cross-platform stable (§8.6 canonicalized pattern). A drift in help text or a missing license line is a CI failure, not a silent break.
- **`pdf` absence is asserted here too:** the golden `--help` snapshot is the durable proof that `pdf` never appears in the CLI surface (§7.7) — make that an explicit assertion, not just an eyeball check.
- **runs/sync coverage moved to P5-r2-runs-sync-surface.** This bead does NOT need to grow `runs`/`sync` cases inline; the dedicated bead **P5-r2-runs-sync-surface** owns finalizing + integration-testing those two surfaces. Keep this bead focused on the exit-code dictionary + the ocr/convert/usage/robot*/doctor/cancel/budget matrix already enumerated, plus the new help/version golden.

title: Release-readiness scorecard (all-green ship gate) tying every Phase-5 deliverable together
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The release-readiness scorecard is the single all-green ship gate (plan §8.4, §12). It is the convergent honest evaluation that a release cannot ship past with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence. It aggregates every Phase-5 deliverable into one go/no-go.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §8.4, §10 Phase 5 exit gates, §12 success metrics)
Produce the scorecard with these cells, each green/red with cited evidence:
- **Parity (L0–L5)** all green — per-layer cosine ≥0.9999 (f32), decoded text exact where reference deterministic, int8/int4 CER within ledgered budget (CVaR/tail bound per AF-2, not just mean), determinism gate green. (from P5-gauntlet-to-convergence)
- **Surface parity** — CLI/contract FeatureUniverse dashboard; PDF `excluded`, every other surface `present`. (from P5-gauntlet + P5-robot-contract-tests)
- **Honest perf vs reference** — gauntlet per-stage ratios in `PERF_LEDGER.md` with §9.3 fairness controls; decode-per-token beats the Phase −1 proven CPU reference. (from P5-gauntlet)
- **Determinism** — same image+args → byte-identical output under greedy.
- **Cross-platform build matrix** — all 5 targets build + smoke-test. (from P5-release-build-matrix)
- **Installer** — install.sh verified, checksums present. (from P5-install-sh-checksums)
- **Agent-ergonomics** — audit score above bar. (from P5-agent-ergonomics-audit)
- **Robot schema** — stable + self-describing + contract-tested. (from P5-robot-schema-frozen / P5-robot-contract-tests)
- **Doctor** — idempotent + reversible + ≥700 rubric, fixtures round-trip. (from P5-doctor-fixtures)
- **Deadlock watchdog** — `many_pages_without_deadlock` green. (from P5-streaming-deadlock-watchdog)
- **Ledger completeness** — every accepted divergence in `DISCREPANCIES.md`, every rejected lever in `NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`, certification bundle present. (from P5-certification-bundle)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The scorecard uses the conformal LOWER bound for the parity cell (not the point estimate) — the formal version of "parity gate first" (§8.5).
- A release cannot ship with ANY red cell or an unledgered divergence (G7) — the scorecard is a hard gate, not advisory.
- It binds the whole epic: it depends on every other Phase-5 deliverable so it cannot be green until they all are.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Correctness outranks speed (G1 > G2): a red parity cell blocks ship even if every perf cell is green.
- Vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 v1 is GREEN (acceptable per G2 reframing) as long as it's recorded honestly — the scorecard must encode that nuance, not flag it red.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (the Phase 5 exit gate)
- Every scorecard cell green with cited evidence (artifact path / test name / fixture).
- No unledgered divergence; certification bundle present and re-verifies.
- The scorecard is committed and reproducible (a script regenerates it from the artifacts).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A scorecard-validation script that reads each cell's evidence artifact and asserts green; fails if any evidence is missing/stale (structured log `{cell, evidence, status}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
This is the capstone: depends on **P5-gauntlet-to-convergence** + **P5-certification-bundle** (parity/perf/surface/ledger cells), **P5-install-sh-checksums** (installer cell), **P5-agent-ergonomics-audit** (ergonomics cell), **P5-robot-contract-tests** (robot-schema cell), **P5-doctor-fixtures** (doctor cell), and **P5-streaming-deadlock-watchdog** (deadlock cell). It is green only when all are.

title: Finalize + test `focr runs` and `focr sync export-jsonl|import-jsonl` (run-history + audit-export surface)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The §7.2 subcommand table (and the table inside `P5-subcommands-exit-env`) lists two surfaces that NO Phase-5 bead actually finalizes, specifies, or tests:
- `focr runs [--id | --limit | --format plain|json|ndjson]` — query run history from the fsqlite `RunStore` (§4.1 `storage.rs`, `_meta` versioned schema + forward migrations).
- `focr sync export-jsonl | import-jsonl` — locked, atomic, one-way audit export/import (§4.1 `sync.rs`).

`P5-subcommands-exit-env` finalizes the surface in the abstract but its TESTS REQUIRED and the integration bead `P5-subcommands-tests` enumerate cases for `ocr`/`convert`/usage/`robot schema|health|backends`/`doctor`/cancellation/budget ONLY — `runs` and `sync` are never driven, schema-checked, or contract-frozen. Because `P5-gauntlet-to-convergence` accounts EVERY CLI surface via the FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix as `present|partial|missing` (and `partial` never rounds up), these two land as coverage DEBT and would block the all-green release-readiness scorecard (`P5-release-readiness-scorecard`). This bead closes that hole so the surface-parity pillar is honestly `present`.

## WHAT TO DO
### `focr runs`
- Wire `focr runs` over the `RunStore` (fsqlite, NEVER rusqlite): `--id <run-id>` (one run), `--limit N` (most-recent N), `--format plain|json|ndjson`.
- Define and FREEZE the run-record schema (run id, input path, mode base/gundam, args summary, started_at, elapsed_ms per-stage totals, exit code, output summary, model source commit/fixture-provenance hash if available). `--format json` emits one JSON document; `--format ndjson` emits one object per run carrying a `schema_version` const (reuse the robot `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` discipline or a sibling `RUNS_SCHEMA_VERSION`).
- Empty history returns exit 0 + an EMPTY array/stream (NOT exit 1) — agent-ergonomics Axiom 5 (no-results is success).
- stdout is pure data under `--format json|ndjson`; stderr diagnostics only (Axiom 4).
- `_meta` versioned schema + forward migrations: opening an older-schema store auto-migrates forward (or fails with the §7.4 exit 7 format/version mismatch if not migratable), with a unit test for the migration path.

### `focr sync export-jsonl | import-jsonl`
- Implement the locked/atomic/one-way audit semantics (§4.1 `sync.rs`): export the run-history to JSONL under a file lock, atomically (temp-file + rename), one-way (export is the canonical direction; import is for restoring an audit trail, not bidirectional merge). Document the one-way contract explicitly so an agent never expects a two-way sync.
- Atomicity: a crash mid-export leaves either the old file intact or a complete new file — never a torn JSONL. Test with a simulated interruption.
- Idempotence: re-exporting identical history produces byte-identical JSONL (after scrubbing any timing/run-id non-determinism, per §8.6 scrubbed-golden discipline).
- Exit codes follow §7.4: lock contention → a documented code (reuse the doctor `5 concurrency lost` convention is NOT valid at top level — pick from the §7.4 dictionary, default `1` generic with an actionable message, or extend the dictionary deliberately and update `P5-subcommands-exit-env`).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- These are READ-side / audit surfaces; per the ergonomics audit (Axiom 8) every read-side command must have `--json`/structured output — `runs` already does, `sync` is inherently structured (JSONL).
- One-way is a deliberate safety choice: an audit export must not silently overwrite live history on import; import is additive/restorative and documented as such.
- Schema is frozen as a fixture so drift is a CI failure, mirroring the robot-schema discipline.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Model-gated: `runs`/`sync` operate on the RunStore, which exists WITHOUT the 6.67 GB weights (a stub/empty store) — so these tests run green in CI with no weights. Seed the store with a synthetic run record to exercise the formats.
- The fsqlite store and its `_meta` schema are owned by Phase-0 `storage.rs`; this bead FINALIZES the query/export surface on top, it does not re-architect storage.
- Do NOT let the JSONL export leak diagnostic lines into the data stream (Axiom 4) — it must round-trip through `import-jsonl` cleanly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `focr runs` with each `--format` emits the documented, version-stamped schema; empty history → exit 0 + empty array/stream.
- `_meta` forward-migration path tested (older store opens and migrates, or returns exit 7).
- `focr sync export-jsonl` is locked + atomic (interruption leaves a non-torn file) + one-way + idempotent (byte-identical after scrub); `import-jsonl` restores it.
- A frozen schema fixture (`tests/fixtures/runs_schema.json`) is committed and matches `runs --format json` output.
- Both surfaces are accounted `present` in the FeatureUniverse so the surface-parity pillar is all-green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: run-record (de)serialization round-trip; `_meta` forward-migration; empty-history → exit 0 + empty.
- Integration (drive the built binary): `runs --format plain|json|ndjson` (assert pure-data stdout, valid JSON/NDJSON, schema match); `sync export-jsonl` → `import-jsonl` round-trip (byte-identical after scrub); atomicity under simulated interruption; lock-contention behavior. Emit one NDJSON log line per case `{case, argv, exit_code, stdout_bytes, schema_ok, pass}` and a summary `{total, passed, skipped}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env** (it finalizes the surface these two commands live in, the exit-code dictionary, and the env/model resolution they share). Feeds **P5-gauntlet-to-convergence** (surface-parity pillar accounts these as `present`) and is audited by **P5-agent-ergonomics-audit** (read-side `--json` requirement).

title: Freeze the robot NDJSON event schema + self-describing `robot schema`
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Robot mode is the primary agent interface (G5). For agents to parse it durably it must be a **stable, versioned, self-describing** contract. This task freezes the event schema and makes `focr robot schema` emit the machine-readable contract that downstream agents (and the contract test) validate against.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.3, AGENTS Agent-Ergonomics Requirements)
- Define the NDJSON event stream in `src/robot.rs`: one JSON object per line, **every line carrying `schema_version`** (the `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` const). Event types and their fields:
  - `run_start` — run id, input path, mode (base/gundam), args summary, schema_version.
  - `stage` — stage name, seq, elapsed_ms, budget_ms (one of the §4.2 pipeline stages: load/preprocess/tokenize/vision_encode/connector/prefill/decode/postprocess/emit).
  - `page` — per-page text + bbox as it completes (the streaming unit).
  - `run_complete` — totals, output summary.
  - `run_error` — message + the §7.4 exit code in payload.
- Implement `focr robot schema` to emit the self-describing, machine-readable contract for ALL event types (compact single-line JSON, parseable by agents). This is the source of truth the contract test pins.
- Bump `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` deliberately; document the version policy.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Single-line compact JSON even for `robot schema` so simple agent parsers handle it (§7.3).
- Robot mode NEVER mixes human decoration with machine output (AGENTS): stdout is the event stream, stderr is diagnostics.
- The schema is frozen as a fixture so any drift is a CI failure, not a silent break (the `🧪 Pin-The-Contract-Test` operator from agent-ergonomics Axiom 8).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- `page` events carry decoded text + bbox coords (`/999 → ×W/H`); under multi-page R-SWA the pages are cross-page dependent (OQ-13) — the event stream must NOT imply pages are independent; it just reports them as they complete in the single sequential pass.
- Timing fields (`elapsed_ms`) make output non-deterministic — they must be scrubbed in golden/contract comparison (see P5-robot-contract-tests).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every emitted line carries `schema_version == ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- `focr robot schema` emits a complete, self-describing, single-line-JSON contract covering all 5 event types and their fields.
- A frozen JSON schema fixture (`tests/fixtures/robot_schema.json`) is committed and matches the `robot schema` output.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: schema-completeness test (every event type the engine can emit is present in `robot schema`).
- The contract test itself lives in P5-robot-contract-tests (separate bead).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env**: `run_error` must carry the finalized §7.4 exit codes, and the `robot run`/`robot schema`/`robot health`/`robot backends` subcommands must be finalized first.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## ADDENDUM (review2): the `stage` event must enumerate the §4.2 closed stage set
- The `stage` event's `stage` field is a CLOSED enum over the nine §4.2 pipeline stages: `load`/`preprocess`/`tokenize`/`vision_encode`/`connector`/`prefill`/`decode`/`postprocess`/`emit`. The self-describing `robot schema` MUST list this closed set (not just say 'a stage name string') so an agent can branch on stages deterministically, and the schema-completeness unit test must assert every §4.2 `PipelineStage` variant appears in the schema's stage enumeration. This also lets the `FOCR_STAGE_BUDGET_<STAGE>_MS` budgets and the `stage` events share one source-of-truth stage list.
- Clarify the `run_error` exit-code field: the schema must declare the value domain as the §7.4 dictionary `{0..7}` so the contract test (P5-robot-contract-tests) can assert membership, not just integer-ness.

title: Robot contract test: validate emitted NDJSON against the frozen schema fixture
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
A frozen schema is only enforced if a test fails when emitted events drift from it. This `robot_contract_tests.rs` is the enforcement (AGENTS: "a contract test validates emitted events against a frozen JSON schema fixture"). It is what lets agents trust the contract across versions.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.3, §8.6 golden-artifact: scrubbed NDJSON)
- In `tests/robot_contract_tests.rs`, run an actual `focr ocr --robot` (or `focr robot run`) over a small golden image, capture every NDJSON line, and validate each against the frozen schema fixture (`tests/fixtures/robot_schema.json`):
  - every line is valid JSON, exactly one object per line;
  - every line has `schema_version == ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`;
  - the event sequence is well-formed: `run_start` … `stage`* / `page`* … (`run_complete` XOR `run_error`);
  - each event's fields match the declared types in the schema.
- Apply the **scrubbed** golden pattern (§8.6): scrub timing (`elapsed_ms`), run-id, and durations before comparison so the snapshot is stable; keep structural fields exact.
- Also assert `run_error` payload carries a valid §7.4 exit code on a forced error case (e.g. `/nonexistent` model → 3).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Scrubbed (not exact) golden for NDJSON because timing/run-id are inherently non-deterministic; structure must be exact (§8.6).
- Model-gated: when weights are absent, drive the well-formedness checks with a forced-error run so the test still exercises the schema path and stays green (skip-with-SUCCESS for the full-OCR case).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Test fails if any emitted event field, type, or the event ordering drifts from the fixture.
- Test fails if a line lacks `schema_version` or carries the wrong version.
- Green in CI without weights (forced-error path validates schema).

## TESTS REQUIRED
This bead is the contract test. Logging: emit a structured summary `{lines_seen, events_by_type, scrub_applied, pass}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-robot-schema-frozen** (the schema + fixture it validates) and on **P5-streaming-polish** (the streaming path that actually emits `stage`/`page` events end-to-end).

title: Per-page streaming polish: bounded mpsc + std::thread worker, recv_timeout loop
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Streaming per-page results as they complete is what makes `focr` usable on dozens of pages in one pass (the "Unlimited" long-document story) and what feeds the robot `page` events. The streaming transport must be **bounded** (backpressure, never unbounded growth) and must NOT violate the deadlock rule that burned the sibling project for 5 commits.

## WHAT TO DO (plan §3.3, §7.3)
- Implement the proven transport: a worker `std::thread` runs the engine and pushes per-page results into a **bounded `std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel`**; `main` (or the robot emitter) loops on `recv_timeout(~40ms)` emitting events until `worker.is_finished()`, then drains remaining and `join()`s the worker.
- Thread a `Copy` `CancellationToken` (deadline + global `ShutdownController`) INTO the worker closure (cancellation is cooperative — `spawn_blocking` keeps running on drop), with `checkpoint()` at per-page and per-decode-step boundaries so Ctrl+C / per-page timeout aborts at the next boundary (exit 6 / exit 5 respectively).
- "Streaming per-page" = streaming the OUTPUT of a SEQUENTIALLY-processed page — never concurrent forwards. The outer page/document loop is sequential; each forward fans out across all cores internally via the kernel's own rayon pool.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE (the load-bearing deadlock lessons)
- **NEVER nest rayon under a held lock; NEVER nest a second asupersync runtime inside a task** (AGENTS doctrine #5). Single `OcrModel` behind a cache; sequential outer loop; one live forward at a time; small `blocking_threads` (e.g. `(1,2)`) is a guard, not the mechanism. Concurrent forwards would oversubscribe rayon N× — the exact bug the frankensearch deadlock saga converged on after 5 commits.
- Bounded channel = backpressure; an unbounded channel would let a slow consumer grow memory without limit. The runtime ships a **capacity certificate** (p95/p99 queueing evidence + proof of no nested-runtime / no rayon-pool oversubscription) as a gauntlet artifact (§3.3).
- `recv_timeout(~40ms)` lets main interleave emit + liveness checks without busy-spinning.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The kill condition is the `many_pages_without_deadlock` CI watchdog (pages ≫ pool) — it hangs on regression. This MUST stay green.
- USL pool caps (AF-5 / §6.9): the rayon pool is pinned to physical cores and capped at its USL peak, not `num_cpus`, so the single fan-out doesn't β-dominate on a 64-core host.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Streaming uses a bounded `sync_channel`; no unbounded buffering anywhere in the path.
- `page` events emit incrementally (first page emitted before the last page is decoded).
- Ctrl+C aborts at the next checkpoint with exit 6; per-page budget overrun aborts with exit 5.
- `many_pages_without_deadlock` watchdog green.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: bounded-channel backpressure (producer blocks when buffer full; no growth).
- The deadlock watchdog is P5-streaming-deadlock-watchdog (separate bead).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4**: the decode path streaming wraps is the final int4 production forward; streaming behavior must be measured on the shipped numerics.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## ADDENDUM (review2): bounded-channel capacity is the capacity-certificate input; USL pool-cap source-of-truth
- The bounded `sync_channel` CAPACITY is not arbitrary — it is the input to the §3.3 **capacity certificate** the gauntlet ships (p95/p99 queueing evidence, proof of no nested-runtime / no rayon-pool oversubscription). Document the chosen capacity and its rationale, and emit the queueing telemetry (occupancy, blocked-producer count) needed for that certificate from the streaming path itself, so `P5-gauntlet-to-convergence` can consume it as an artifact rather than re-deriving it.
- The rayon pool cap is pinned to physical cores AND capped at its **USL peak (§6.9 / AF-5)**, not `num_cpus`. That cap is owned by the Phase-3 §6.9 USL pool-sizing work (E-P3), not chosen here — this bead CONSUMES that cap. Make the dependency explicit so the streaming worker does not independently pick a pool size that contradicts the USL certificate. If §6.9's cap is not yet available, the streaming path must read it from a single config source, never hardcode.
- Reaffirm: cancellation via the `Copy` `CancellationToken` must `checkpoint()` at BOTH per-page and per-decode-step boundaries; add an explicit test that a per-decode-step Ctrl+C (mid-page) still aborts at the next decode checkpoint with exit 6 (not only at page boundaries) — otherwise a long single page is uninterruptible.

title: `many_pages_without_deadlock` CI watchdog (pages ≫ pool, hangs on regression)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The nested-rayon-under-lock / nested-runtime deadlock is the single most expensive recurring bug in the sibling lineage (the frankensearch deadlock saga, 5 commits). The durable fix is architectural (sequential outer loop, one live forward, kernel-internal fan-out), and the durable PROOF is a watchdog test that HANGS on regression so CI catches any reintroduction.

## WHAT TO DO (AGENTS doctrine #5, plan §3.3, §11)
- Add a `many_pages_without_deadlock` test that streams a number of pages **far exceeding the worker/blocking-thread pool** through the real streaming path (P5-streaming-polish), with a wall-clock timeout. If the engine ever nests rayon under a held lock or spins up a second runtime, the test hangs and CI fails (timeout → red).
- Assert: all pages stream to completion; no page is dropped; exactly one forward is live at a time (instrument a counter that must never exceed 1).
- Run it under the gauntlet `many_pages` soak to also exercise the capacity certificate (p95/p99 queueing, no oversubscription).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The test must use pages ≫ pool so any accidental concurrency or lock-nesting actually deadlocks (a small page count could pass by luck).
- Model-gated: can run with a tiny synthetic/stub forward so it stays green without the 6.67 GB weights while still exercising the orchestration topology.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Test green (completes well under its timeout) on the correct sequential-loop topology.
- Live-forward counter never exceeds 1.
- Test is wired as a CI gate (not opt-in).

## TESTS REQUIRED
This bead is the watchdog test. Structured log: `{pages, pool_size, max_concurrent_forwards, completed, elapsed_ms}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-streaming-polish** — it watchdogs exactly that streaming/orchestration topology.

title: `focr doctor` — idempotent, reversible, capability-reflecting self-check/repair
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
`doctor` is the contract with a future agent who has no context, no human nearby, and one shot to make the tool work again. franken_ocr's failure modes are concrete: model not resolvable, stale/wrong `.focrq` format version, bad perms on the model cache, mismatched arch SIMD expectations. `doctor` detects and (where safe) repairs them, leaving a durable artifact (world-class-doctor-mode skill; plan §7.2).

## WHAT TO DO (world-class-doctor-mode CLI spec + 24-axiom kernel)
Implement `focr doctor` with this surface:
- `focr doctor` — detect-only, exit `0` healthy / `1` findings present.
- `focr doctor --fix` — apply safe repairs; exit `2` partial / `3` failed+rolled-back / `4` refused unsafe.
- `focr doctor --dry-run` — disclose worst-case blast radius without mutating.
- `focr doctor undo <run-id>` — restore byte-for-byte from `.doctor/runs/<run-id>/backups/`.
- `focr doctor capabilities --json` — version, contract version, detector list, fixer list, exit codes, env vars, run-artifact `schema_version`.
- `focr doctor robot-docs` — paste-ready agent handbook printed to stdout.
- `focr doctor --robot-triage` — mega-command: `{summary, findings, actions_planned, recommended_command, capabilities_url}` in one call.

**Detect-then-fix, never fix-then-detect.** Detectors are pure functions (no side effects, no `mutate()` calls); they return a finding list and the runtime decides whether `--fix` was passed.

**Single-chokepoint mutation:** every disk write goes through one `mutate(path, op)` that (a) writes a verbatim backup into `.doctor/runs/<run-id>/backups/<path>` first, (b) computes before/after SHA-256, (c) appends `actions.jsonl` `{path, before_hash, after_hash, op, timestamp}`, (d) holds the file lock. A code-search test fails CI if any other path writes to disk under `--fix`.

**Concrete detectors/fixers for franken_ocr:** model-not-resolvable (detect: searched dirs empty; fix: none — emit actionable hint with the exact `FOCR_MODEL_DIR=...` to set); stale `.focrq` format version (detect: header version != current; fix: re-run `focr convert` is suggested, not auto-run — quant is expensive/irreversible-numerics, refuse with exit 4 + recommended command); model-cache perms (detect: unreadable; fix: chmod via mutate with backup of perms); orphaned `.doctor/runs` cleanup (idempotent).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Idempotence:** run twice → second run reports `no_actions_taken`; run mid-fix → next run completes or aborts cleanly, no torn writes, no orphaned backups.
- **Reversibility:** `doctor undo <run-id>` reads `actions.jsonl` in reverse, restores from backups, verifies post-restore hash == recorded `before_hash`, fails closed if any backup is missing.
- **Blast-radius containment:** doctor only writes inside documented paths (`.doctor/`, the model cache it owns); writes outside refuse with exit 4.
- **Exit-code contract** (doctor-skill canonical): `0` healthy, `1` findings, `2` partial fix, `3` failed+rolled-back, `4` refused unsafe, `5` concurrency lost (lock held), `6` online required. Documented in `capabilities --json`.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- No flag silently mutates; no flag implies `--fix`; no flag turns off backups; no flag bypasses `mutate()`.
- Doctor must work in a sandbox: no TTY, no network, no human (offline-only by default).
- Re-quantizing weights is NOT an auto-fix (irreversible numerics + expensive) — refuse with exit 4 and recommend the exact command.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (doctor skill bar: ≥700 on the 10-dim rubric)
- For every fixer: corrupt → `doctor --fix` → `doctor` (no flags) returns exit 0 → `doctor undo <run-id>` → byte-identical to corrupted state.
- A code-search test proves every disk write goes through `mutate()`.
- `doctor capabilities --json` returns a stable schema with `schema_version`, detector/fixer lists, exit codes, env vars.
- `doctor robot-docs` makes external lookup unnecessary; `doctor --robot-triage` returns the mega-command shape in one call.
- Idempotent: second `--fix` reports `no_actions_taken`.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The fixture round-trip suite is P5-doctor-fixtures (separate bead).
- Unit: `mutate()` chokepoint (backup written, hashes recorded, lock held); detector purity (no side effects); undo-is-idempotent.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env**: doctor's detectors reference the finalized model-resolution logic and `.focrq` format version, and its exit codes must not collide with the top-level §7.4 dictionary.

title: Doctor fixture suite: corrupt → --fix → healthy → undo → byte-identical, per failure mode
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The doctor skill's One Rule: if a fix can't be undone byte-for-byte from the artifact in `.doctor/runs/<run-id>/backups/`, it doesn't ship; if `doctor --fix` and `doctor undo <run-id>` aren't a true inverse pair, the doctor is broken regardless of what its scorecard says. The fixture suite is the proof.

## WHAT TO DO (doctor-skill: each fixer has a fixture)
- For every detector/fixer in P5-doctor, add `tests/doctor_fixtures/<failure-mode>/` reproducing the broken state (stale `.focrq` header, unreadable model-cache perms, orphaned `.doctor/runs`, etc.).
- A test per fixture asserts the full round-trip: corrupt → `focr doctor --fix` → `focr doctor` (no flags) returns exit 0 → `focr doctor undo <run-id>` → state byte-identical to the corrupted fixture (verify by content hash).
- Add the **idempotence** assertion: a second `focr doctor --fix` on an already-healthy state reports `no_actions_taken` (exit 0, no new mutations).
- Add a **crash-recovery** case: simulate a mid-fix interruption (partial `actions.jsonl`) and assert the next `doctor` run completes or aborts cleanly with no torn writes.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Fixtures live in-repo so the repair-from-broken-to-known-good proof travels with the code (doctor-skill dimension 10: test_coverage_of_repair).
- The refuse-unsafe cases (re-quantize → exit 4) assert that NO mutation occurred and the recommended command is printed.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every fixer has a fixture and a passing round-trip test (corrupt→fix→healthy→undo→byte-identical).
- Idempotence and crash-recovery cases pass.
- Refuse-unsafe cases assert zero mutation + correct exit 4 + recommended command.

## TESTS REQUIRED
This bead is the fixture suite. Structured log per fixture: `{fm, corrupt_hash, post_fix_exit, post_undo_hash, byte_identical, idempotent}`.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-doctor** — it tests exactly that doctor's detectors/fixers/mutate/undo.

title: 5-target cross-platform release build (.github/workflows/dist.yml) + smoke test
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
G3 demands one self-contained `focr` binary per target, no Python/FFI/network/GPU at inference. The 5-target release build is how we deliver that, and a smoke test on each ensures the binary actually runs (not just links).

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.6, /release-preparations)
- Finalize `.github/workflows/dist.yml` for the **5-target matrix**: `linux x86-64`, `linux arm64`, `darwin x86-64`, `darwin arm64`, `windows-msvc x86-64`. Use **musl for Linux** (static linking, portable binaries — installer-workmanship: never `gnu` for the installer's expected triples). Runtime SIMD dispatch means **one binary per arch** auto-selects the best int8 path (AMX/VNNI/SMMLA/SDOT) — no per-microarch builds.
- Produce, per target, a `tar.gz` (Unix) / `zip` (Windows) archive named with the Rust target triple, and a **SHA-256 checksum** (`SHA256SUMS.txt`).
- Add a **smoke test** per target: run `focr robot health`, `focr robot backends`, `focr robot schema`, `focr --version` on the built binary and assert exit 0 + valid JSON. (Full OCR smoke needs the 6.67 GB weights → model-gated; the no-weights smoke must still prove the binary runs and the native path is reachable.)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE (the path-deps gotcha — load-bearing)
- franken_ocr depends on sibling crates by **local `../` path** (ft-kernel-cpu, ft-core, ft-serialize, asupersync `>=0.3.5,<0.4`, fsqlite, image). **Local path deps break standard GitHub-Actions CI** (release-preparations OP-6: "Absolute local paths can't resolve in CI"). So the 5-target build likely needs **Path B (local build via `dsr`/`rch`)** or vendoring the path deps into the release build. Decide and document which; the gauntlet/scorecard must reflect the real build path.
- nightly toolchain is REQUIRED (stdarch i8mm/dotprod, portable_simd) — the matrix pins `rust-toolchain.toml` channel=nightly; the scalar fallback cross-compiles to every target so even targets lacking i8mm build and run.
- GH-Actions bot-created tags don't trigger downstream workflows (anti-recursion) — if `release-automation.yml` creates the tag but `dist.yml` doesn't fire, trigger it manually (release-preparations gotcha).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- musl-incompatible deps: if any path dep pulls a C-FFI or ort/ONNX-class dep that can't build on musl, feature-gate it or fall back to gnu for that target (OP-16) — but G3 says no FFI at inference, so this should not arise; verify.
- Budget time for CI iteration — prior sibling releases needed 3 rounds (clippy lint → static-binary verification bug → aarch64-musl issue).
- Windows has no musl path — use the msvc target; ensure the static-ish posture and that the smoke test runs on a windows runner.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 5 exit gate)
- **All 5 targets build** (green).
- **All 5 targets pass the no-weights smoke test** (`robot health`/`backends`/`schema`/`--version` → exit 0, valid JSON).
- Each target produces a named archive + a SHA-256 entry in `SHA256SUMS.txt`.
- The build path (GH-Actions vs dsr/rch local) is documented and reproducible.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Smoke test scripted per target (the test deliverable is part of this bead; structured log `{target, build_ok, smoke_ok, archive, sha256}`).
- Checksum verification asserted in the same run.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4**: the shipped binary must embed the final int4 quant path / `.focrq` format so the release artifact IS the certified product.

---
## REFINEMENT NOTE (deep-review pass 2)


## ADDENDUM (review2): license-in-version + determinism + scalar-fallback smoke assertions
- The per-target no-weights smoke test MUST additionally assert that `focr --version` output contains the MIT **"Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu"** notice (§11 license mitigation) — proving the shipped binary, not just the source, carries the attribution.
- Add a **scalar-fallback build/run assertion**: at least one target (or a `FOCR_FORCE_ARCH=scalar` smoke invocation) proves the pure-scalar path links and runs, so a target lacking i8mm/dotprod still ships a working binary (the §11 cross-compile mitigation: 'scalar fallback cross-compiles to every target').
- Add a **determinism cross-check seam**: the smoke harness should, where weights are available (model-gated), run the same tiny input twice and assert byte-identical output (the §7.3 determinism gate) on each target — determinism can break per-arch via reduction order, so the build matrix is the right place to catch it. Without weights, assert at least that `robot schema` output is byte-identical across two invocations (a cheap determinism proxy).

title: `install.sh` curl-pipe installer + checksum verification + --verify self-test
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The one-liner `curl|bash` installer is how most users (and agent hosts) get `focr`. It must detect OS+arch, download the right release asset, verify its checksum, place the binary on PATH, and self-test — all idempotently and safely (installer-workmanship: emulate `/dp/destructive_command_guard/install.sh` and `/dp/remote_compilation_helper/install.sh`, the gold standard).

## WHAT TO DO (plan §7.6, /installer-workmanship)
- Write `install.sh` with the canonical structure:
  - **Platform detection** (OS + arch → Rust triple; WSL detection): `linux-x86_64 → x86_64-unknown-linux-musl`, `linux-aarch64 → aarch64-unknown-linux-musl`, darwin x86-64/arm64, windows-msvc x86-64. **The triples MUST match the release asset names** from P5-release-build-matrix (installer-workmanship OP-15: a mismatch causes silent fallback to source compilation).
  - **Download** with proxy-array curl (`curl -fsSL "${PROXY_ARGS[@]}" "$URL"`), versioned-artifact URL with unversioned-latest and build-from-source fallbacks.
  - **Checksum verification** (SHA-256 via `sha256sum` or `shasum -a 256`) — NEVER skip unless `--no-verify`. This is the supply-chain gate.
  - **PATH setup** (check `:$PATH:`, offer `--easy-mode` to append `export PATH` to rc files; warn if not writable).
  - **`--verify` self-test**: post-install diagnostics — run `focr robot health` and assert exit 0.
  - **Idempotence**: re-running install.sh on an already-installed system re-runs config harmlessly, does not duplicate PATH entries.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Always musl for Linux (static, portable) — matches the release matrix.
- Checksum verification is mandatory (supply-chain) — the gold-standard installers never skip it.
- The installer carries the MIT "Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu" attribution awareness only insofar as it installs the binary; the notice lives in `--version` (handled in P5-subcommands-exit-env).

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Don't assume `~/.local/bin` is on PATH (often isn't) — check and offer `--easy-mode`.
- Target-triple mismatch is the #1 silent installer bug — audit installer triples vs actual release asset names as an explicit step.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 5 exit gate: install.sh verified)
- `install.sh` correctly detects OS+arch on all 5 target platforms and selects the matching asset.
- Checksum verification runs and aborts on mismatch.
- `--verify` self-test runs `focr robot health` and reports success.
- Re-running is idempotent (no duplicate PATH entries).
- Installer target triples audited == release asset names (zero mismatch).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A test/CI step that runs `install.sh` against the freshly-built release assets in a clean environment (per available platform) and asserts the binary is on PATH and `--verify` passes.
- A triple-audit check comparing install.sh's triple table to the `dist.yml` matrix.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-release-build-matrix**: the installer downloads and checksum-verifies exactly those release assets; the triples must match.

title: Agent-ergonomics audit + apply pass on the focr CLI (≥10 landed changes, ≥3 dimensions)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
G5 makes the AI agent the primary user. The agent-ergonomics skill is for ACTIVE improvement, not a polite scorecard — a `full` pass that ships <5 substantive surface changes hasn't earned the right to call itself done (the Ambition Charter). This pass hardens `focr`'s surface so the FIRST command an agent instinctively tries "just works."

## WHAT TO DO (/agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools, `full` mode)
