- Run the audit → apply → re-score → test loop on the `focr` surface (commands, flags, error messages, exit codes, output format). Land the highest-leverage fixes directly on the branch, each with a regression test in `audit/regression_tests/`.
- Required applied set (the skill's Ambition Bar): **≥10 substantive landed changes** (≥5 if franken_ocr is judged a small CLI), covering **≥3 of the 11 scoring dimensions**, and including at least one of EACH:
  - a **mega-command** (`focr --robot-triage`-style: `quick_ref + recommendations + commands + health` in one round-trip);
  - a **`capabilities --json`** or **`robot-docs guide`** surface (so an agent reads the contract from the tool, not an out-of-band doc);
  - **`--json` (or `--robot-*`) output** on at least one read-side command (every read-side command should have it — Axiom 8);
  - an **error-message rewrite** (3 required parts: what failed, where (`file:line`), the exact copy-pasteable command to type instead — "See --help" alone is failure);
  - a **typo / intent-inference handler** for the most common wrong invocation (Levenshtein-1: `--jsno`/`--jason` → "did you mean `--json`").

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Axiom 0 (first-try inevitability) is the tiebreaker: a change that improves another dimension at the cost of first-try success is the wrong change.
- Axiom 4: stdout is data, stderr is diagnostics — any leak is a dimension-0 violation (`focr X --json | jq` must work without `grep -v`).
- Axiom 16: any score >700 needs a concrete citation (`file:line` or `--help` excerpt + transcript) — no vibes scoring.
- This skill does NOT add features (those are filed as beads); it reshapes the surface.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Don't pile all changes onto one dimension (coverage of ≥3 dimensions required).
- A `--help` rewrite without a pinning regression test counts as half — every applied change ships a regression test that fails if reverted.
- Run the Ambition Bar "That's it??" self-prompt before declaring done.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (Phase 5 exit gate: ergonomics score above bar)
- ≥10 (or ≥5 for small-CLI) substantive landed changes, each ≥100 points on at least one dimension, each with a green regression test.
- ≥3 of the 11 dimensions covered.
- One each of: mega-command, capabilities/robot-docs, --json on a read-side command, error rewrite, typo handler.
- Re-score shows median uplift across audited surfaces ≥50 points (else re-enter Phase 4/5).
- The durable artifacts (scorecard, heatmap, recommendations, playbook, regression tests) persisted in the sibling `__agent_ergonomics_audit/` workspace.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The regression-test set in `audit/regression_tests/` (one per applied change) — these ARE the test deliverable; project test suite stays green.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-subcommands-exit-env** (the surface being audited), **P5-robot-schema-frozen** (robot/--json surfaces audited for stability), and **P5-doctor** (the doctor surface is part of the audited CLI; capabilities/robot-docs may overlap).

title: Run the three-pillar gauntlet to convergence (≥10 rounds, ≥2 clean) on the release candidate
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The three-pillar gauntlet is the conscience that keeps every speed/correctness claim honest and is run as THE release gate (plan §8.5, §10 Phase 5). franken_ocr is an ML-System-class port; the gauntlet machinery (oracle bridge, conformal ratchet, e-processes, FeatureUniverse) is built/owned by E-VERIFY — Phase 5 RUNS it to convergence on the release candidate and resolves every finding.

## WHAT TO DO (/running-the-gauntlet-on-your-rust-port, §8.5)
Drive the gauntlet's 16-phase convergent multi-round evaluation against the pinned reference until convergence, across all three pillars (no victory-on-one-while-another-regresses):
- **(a) Performance** — honest per-stage ratios vs the reference (§9): preprocess / vision-encode / prefill / decode-per-token, with the §9.3 fairness controls (thread parity — never benchmark torch @64; allocator fairness; best-of-N with warmup discard; precision annotation per row). Decode-per-token must beat the Phase −1 proven CPU reference; vision-prefill parity-or-slower in f32 is acceptable and recorded honestly.
- **(b) Conformance** — the L0–L5 parity ladder + metamorphic + fault, against the bf16 reference, within the oracle's own measured nondeterminism floor. Per-op ULP tolerance table (4 ULP f32 matmul, 2 ULP elementwise) as the comparator. Intentional divergences are XFAIL (never SKIP), each a `DISC-NNN`.
- **(c) Surface parity** — FeatureUniverse / SurfaceMatrix accounting EVERY CLI surface, robot event, and modeling feature as `present|partial|missing|n/a|excluded` (partial never rounds up; excluded still counts as coverage debt). PDF input is `excluded` (§7.7).

Monitor the load-bearing invariants as anytime-valid e-processes (Ville's inequality): KV cache never exceeds `L·(m+128)`; int8 i32 accumulator never overflows (worst case dense layer-0 `down_proj` K=6848); same-input determinism; SIMD path == scalar path bit-identical.

**Convergence rule:** ≥10 full rounds (Phases 5→10), ≥2 consecutive clean rounds (<3 new genuine findings each), every open hypothesis resolved. `convergence-tracker.sh` gates it and exits non-zero until reached.

**Release ratchet:** the parity score is a Beta-posterior per category × distribution-free conformal band; the **release decision uses the LOWER bound, not the point estimate** (`truncate_score` to 6 dp). A change may only land if it raises the lower bound without lowering any per-category bound.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- The oracle bridge (PyO3/subprocess to pinned `torch==2.10.0`/`transformers==4.57.1`) is test-only, NEVER linked into the shipping `focr` binary — preserves G3's no-FFI runtime claim. `EngineIdentity::{Subject,Oracle}` asserted-distinct.
- L4 "exact" is defined only over the prefix the oracle reproduces identically; the L3 logit tolerance is derived from the measured oracle variance, NOT the imported frankensearch 0.055.
- Honest bar (NE-INH-2/3/4/5): the gap is kernels-below-peak not framework tax; at-or-near ONNX on CPU + portable + bounded-KV is the realistic target — do NOT claim a win that isn't a measured head-to-head ratio with a correctness proof.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- Phase 11 (the convergence loop) is the most expensive — rch-offload heavily.
- A release cannot ship with a red parity cell or an unledgered divergence.
- Cross-page R-SWA dependence (OQ-13): the metamorphic gate must NOT assert "multi-page concat = sum of single-page parses"; the defensible property is the opposite (page order / earlier content may change later output).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- `convergence-tracker.sh` reports converged (≥10 rounds, ≥2 clean, all hypotheses resolved).
- All three pillars green: parity L0–L5 (conformal lower bound), surface parity dashboard, honest per-stage perf ratios with fairness controls.
- Every invariant e-process below `1/α` across the full test stream.
- Decode-per-token faster than the Phase −1 proven CPU reference on the primary arches; vision-prefill ratio recorded honestly.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- The gauntlet's per-pillar evidence bundles (oracle differential corpus, comprehensive-bench matrix, FeatureUniverse) — produced as artifacts, not ad-hoc.
- Re-state the parity receipt on the final perf state ("text exact, max logit diff X, deterministic").

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **E-P4** (the certified product is the int4 release candidate), **E-VERIFY** (owns/builds the gauntlet machinery this task runs), **P5-release-build-matrix** (the gauntlet runs on the actual built binary and feeds the build-matrix pillar), and **P5-robot-contract-tests** (surface-parity pillar accounts robot events as present/partial).

title: Produce the release certification bundle (FINAL_GAUNTLET_REPORT + RELEASE_CERTIFICATION_TEMPLATE + bundle JSONs)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
Convergence is the proof; the certification bundle is the durable, machine-readable RECORD of that proof that ships with the release and lets a future agent/maintainer re-verify the claim. Phase 5's deliverable (cross-ref E-VERIFY) is this bundle (plan §8.5, §10 Phase 5).

## WHAT TO DO (/running-the-gauntlet final artifacts + certification)
Produce the gauntlet's final-artifact triple + bundle:
1. **`FINAL_GAUNTLET_REPORT.md`** — executive summary; full findings table with severity; per-pillar remediation plan; unresolved-but-deferred list with retry-condition predicates; convergence-evidence appendix (round-by-round new-findings counts proving the loop converged); certification-bundle manifest.
2. **`PARITY_RUNBOOK.md`** — how to reproduce the parity verification.
3. **`RELEASE_CERTIFICATION_TEMPLATE.md`** — the strict-conformant-release.v1 template with the required-pass constants: `CERTIFICATION_MIN_VERIFICATION_PCT = 100.0`, `CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED_SUITE_PASS_RATE_PCT = 100.0`, `CERTIFICATION_MAX_HIGH_SEVERITY_COUNTEREXAMPLES = 0`, `CERTIFICATION_MAX_EVIDENCE_AGE_HOURS = 24`; evidence-bundle classes; gate/ratchet spec; persisted baseline.
4. **Certification bundle** — confidence-gate JSON, verification-contract JSON, release-certificate JSON, CI artifact manifest, benchmark summary, `scorecards.json`, critical-path report, ratchet state.

Also fold in the franken_ocr-specific ledgers as bundle evidence: `docs/NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md` + `docs/PERF_LEDGER.md` (artifact-graph rows with `claim_id`, `evidence_id`, model source commit + fixture hash from the Phase −1 truth pack, CPU feature string, exact command+env, fallback/kill-switch state) and `docs/DISCREPANCIES.md` (every accepted divergence with measured impact + kill-switch env var + review date).

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- Evidence age cap (24h) means the bundle must be generated from a fresh converged run, not stale logs.
- 100% verification + 100% suite pass + zero high-severity counterexamples is the strict gate — a single red cell blocks certification.
- The bundle is machine-readable JSON (not just prose) so re-verification is automatable.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- The bundle must trace every claim to the exact pinned model version (truth-pack source commit + fixture hash) — a claim without provenance is not certifiable.
- Per-lever artifact dirs `artifacts/perf/<bead>/` with paired baseline/after gauntlet logs + SHA-256 must be present for every landed perf claim.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All four artifacts present and internally consistent (manifest references resolve).
- Required-pass constants all met (100/100/0/<24h).
- Every accepted divergence appears in `DISCREPANCIES.md` with measured impact + kill-switch; every rejected lever in `NEGATIVE_EVIDENCE.md`.
- The bundle re-verifies (a check script re-reads the JSONs and re-asserts the gates).

## TESTS REQUIRED
- A bundle-validation step that parses every JSON, checks the required-pass constants, and asserts manifest references resolve (structured log `{artifact, present, gate, pass}`).

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
Depends on **P5-gauntlet-to-convergence**: the bundle is generated FROM the converged gauntlet evidence and cannot exist before convergence.

title: Harden connector Mat backing-length validation
notes: Fresh-eyes audit found connector APIs validate declared Mat shapes but not public backing-buffer lengths before row indexing and bulk copy. Patch will convert malformed Mat panic paths into FocrError returns.
close_reason: Fixed connector panic surfaces from malformed public Mat backing buffers. title: Robot NDJSON contract test harness (frozen JSON-schema fixture + validator over emitted events, scrubbed goldens)
description:
## BACKGROUND / WHY
The robot/NDJSON surface is the agent-first product (G5). §7.3/§8.6 + AGENTS.md ergonomics require: a stable versioned schema, `robot schema` self-describes the contract, and **a `robot_contract_tests.rs` validates emitted events against a frozen JSON schema fixture**. The gauntlet's surface-parity pillar (§8.5) accounts every robot event. This bead builds the shared contract-test plumbing (frozen schema fixture + validator + scrubbing) that the robot contract test and the E-VERIFY surface matrix both use. E-VERIFY owns the FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix scoring; E-TEST owns the schema-validation plumbing it rides on.

## WHAT TO DO
Build `tests/robot_contract.rs` + `tests/fixtures/robot/event_schema.json`:
1. **Frozen JSON schema fixture** describing every robot event type (§7.3): `run_start`, `stage` (name, seq, elapsed, budget), `page` (per-page text/bbox as it completes — streaming), `run_complete`, `run_error` (carrying the §7.4 exit code in its payload), plus `schema` / `health` / `backends` outputs. Every line carries `schema_version` == `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
2. **Validator**: parse each emitted NDJSON line, validate against the frozen schema, reject unknown event types / missing fields / wrong `schema_version`.
3. **`robot schema` self-description check**: assert `focr robot schema` output is itself valid, versioned, single-line-JSON-per-event, and DESCRIBES exactly the events the engine emits (no drift between the self-description and the actual stream).
4. **Scrubbed goldens** (§8.6): freeze a golden NDJSON stream for a golden run with timing/run-id/durations SCRUBBED; exact-compare after scrubbing (per `/testing-golden-artifacts` scrubbed pattern).
5. **Determinism**: under greedy, the scrubbed robot stream is byte-identical across runs (delegates to TEST-determinism-gate).
6. **Backends reflection check**: `robot backends` reports the detected SIMD tier (smmla/sdot/vnni512/vnni256/amx/avx2/scalar), core count, and the USL pool-sizing table (§6.9/AF-5) — assert it matches the actually-dispatched tier.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS + RATIONALE
- **Frozen schema fixture as the contract** so any unintended event-shape change is caught (agents depend on stability).
- **Self-description must match the stream** — a `robot schema` that lies about events is worse than none.
- **Scrub before golden-compare** because robot lines carry timing/run-id (otherwise every run diffs).
- **No human decoration in robot mode** (AGENTS.md): the validator rejects any non-JSON line on the robot stdout.

## CONSIDERATIONS / RISKS / GOTCHAS
- `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION` bumps must be intentional + reviewed; the contract test fails on an unannounced bump.
- Streaming `page` events arrive via the bounded `sync_channel` + `recv_timeout(~40ms)` loop — the test must drain the full stream, not just the first event.
- The `run_error` event must carry the same stable exit code (0/1/2/3/4/5/6/7, §7.4) as the process exit — assert both agree.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Every emitted robot line validates against the frozen schema; an unknown event / missing field / wrong `schema_version` FAILS.
- `robot schema` self-description matches the actually-emitted event set.
- Scrubbed robot golden compares byte-identical; determinism holds under greedy.
- `robot backends` reflects the real dispatched SIMD tier + core count + pool sizing.
- `run_error` exit code in payload == process exit code.

## TESTS REQUIRED
- Unit: validator accepts each event type; rejects malformed / unknown / wrong-version.
- Integration: full streamed run validated end-to-end (model-gated; stubbed-stream variant for CI without weights).
- Golden: scrubbed NDJSON exact-compare.
- Determinism: scrubbed stream byte-identical across runs.

## DEPENDENCIES NOTE
- depends_on **E-P0**: needs the robot NDJSON skeleton, `ROBOT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `robot schema/health/backends`, and the streaming `sync_channel` loop from the scaffold.
- depends_on **TEST-log-emitter**: emits the contract-test structured logs.
- depends_on **E-VERIFY**: the surface-parity pillar (FeatureUniverse/SurfaceMatrix) consumes this validator; the schema-as-contract is shared with the gauntlet's surface accounting.
